annotations.definition: An Agent leaves behind a Theme effectively rendering it no longer within their control or of the normal security as one's property.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who acts to leave behind the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity that is relinguished to no one from the Agent's possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent gives up the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Agent gives up the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The style in which the Agent gives up the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what expanse of time the Agent has given up the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Explanation denotes a proposition from which the act of abandonment logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The FE Depictive describes the Agent during the abandoning event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Agent leaves the Theme behind.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent abandons the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: A description of the event as a whole.

annotations.definition: A Location is filled or covered with the Theme. The Location is realized as the External Argument, and the Theme either as PP complement headed by with, in or of. NB: This frame does not include uses of adjectives like paved when they merely specify the Type of some location, as in "paved and unpaved roads".

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme covers or fills the Location. This FE most frequently occurs in a PP Complement headed by with or in.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: Location is the object or area that the Theme fills or covers. This frame element generally occurs as the External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree of abundance with which the Theme covers or fills the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes the state of the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the the Abounding_with event occurs.

annotations.definition: An Entity (generally food) is exposed to a Heat_source whose Temperature may also be specified. Generally, the Entity undergoes some sort of change as a result of this process.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity (often a food item) that absorbs heat.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: The Container that holds the Entity that absorbs the heat.
frameElementDefinitions.Heat_source: This FE identifies the object that directly supplies heat to the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the heat application occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is a substance through which heat is absorbed by the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the cooking event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the heat absorption takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The thing or state-of-affairs that leads to the Entity being heated and undergoing change.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature: The temperature that the Entity stays at, resulting in change.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Entity is exposed to heat.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason why a state of affairs is caused or maintained by an (implicit) agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Entity as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The final state of affairs after the absorbing heat event has finished.

annotations.definition: The Quantity of a Collection occurring in a Place has a particular Measure. The Measure of the Collection is referred to only in terms of how the Measure diverges from a norm for Collections of the specified type. Degree may further refine the relation of the Measure to the norm.

frameElementDefinitions.Collection: The Collection whose Quantity is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Quantity: The feature of an Collection which is under discussion. This FE is incorporated by all the lexical units in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Measure from the norm or from the value for another Collection.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Collection is in the state of having a particular Measure for the Quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Measure: The measure of the Quantity, which is incorporated by the lexical units.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Collection occurs.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Abuser repeatedly treats the Victim in a cruel and violent way, including physically harming or forcing the Victim to engage in sexual activity against their will. The Victim usually lacks the power to resist or fight back. A Type of abuse may be indicated. There may also be a Degree.

frameElementDefinitions.Abuser: The Abuser subjects the Victim to repeated verbal, physical, emotional or sexual attacks.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the recipient of the Abuser's actions.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type is used for the domain in which the Abuser mistreats the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree is the extent to which the abuse occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the abuse occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the abuse occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner is used for any description of the abuse event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Abuser that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Abuser accomplishes the abuse.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity used by the Abuser to accomplish the abuse.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Abuser intends to accomplish by performing the abuse.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that abuse occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often abuse occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the Abusing event began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: A Theme is or is not capable of entering or accessing a Useful_location because of/despite a Barrier.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme whose motion is blocked or free.
frameElementDefinitions.Useful_location: The place or thing that the Theme is headed towards, despite a potential or actual Barrier.
frameElementDefinitions.Barrier: An entity that (at least potentially) prevents the Theme from getting to the Useful_location.

annotations.definition: A Co_participant fills the same role as the Participant in an event or relation.

frameElementDefinitions.Participant: In this frame, Participant is the accompanied entity (person or object).
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: In this frame, Co-participant is the accompanying entity (person or object).
frameElementDefinitions.Participants: Two or more entities construed as symmetrically and usually equally participating in an event or relation.

annotations.definition: After a period in which the Agent has been working on a Goal, the Agent manages to attain it. The Goal may be a desired state, or be conceptualized as an event.

frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that lets them accomplish their Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The conscious entity, generally a person, that performs the intentional act that fulfills the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that allows them to achieve their Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent achieves their Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent achieves their Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state-of-affairs from which the accomplishment would be predicted.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Agent achieves the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The state or action that the Agent has wished to participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of accomplishing a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which Goal is accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that it takes for Goal to be accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The particular aspect or domain of the Goal that is achieved.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: A Wearer wears accessories, which are made of some Material and may have a Style. Most of the accessories are non-essential (clothing) items.

frameElementDefinitions.Style: This FE identifies the Style of the Accoutrement.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: This FE identifies the Material from which the Accoutrement is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE identifies a characteristic or description of the Accoutrement.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Accoutrement is worn or made.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: This FE identifies a part of the Accoutrement.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_location: This FE identifies the place on the body where a particular Accoutrement is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The individual or group who created the Accoutrement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which the Accoutrement was created.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: A name given to an individual Accoutrement to distinguish it from all other objects.

annotations.definition: An Agent is involved in an activity whose degree of success is dependent on a parameter of the action matching a particular set of values of a continuous variable or variables (e.g. quantity, location, time). The Agent, or metonymically the Instrument or Means action, is described in terms of the actual or expected Deviation between the location, time, or quantity in the activity and the location, time, or quantity which is necessary for the intended event.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual who tries to interact with the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Target: The Target is the entity whose attributes (esp. position) the Agent is attempting to match in an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt at interaction, incorporated in the LUs of the frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent attempts to hit a Target_value.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: How closely the expected or actual location that the Instrument interacts with corresponds to the Target_value.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when something hits or misses.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Agent manages or fails to interact with the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The object manipulated by the Agent in order to interact with the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_value: The value or values for a parameter that match the Agent's intent.
frameElementDefinitions.Deviation: Expected or actual deviation from the Target_value.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The particular aspect or domain in which something is accurate.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Agent, Means, or Instrument is accurate.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer introduces a New_idea into society.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer comes up with or conceptualizes the New_idea. It is normally expressed as an External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.New_idea: The New_idea is a concept not previously present in society which the Cognizer introduces.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Cognizer comes up with the New_idea.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Cognizer comes up with the New_idea.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Cognizer accomplishes the innovative achievement.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Cognizer creates the New_idea .
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Cognizer created the New_idea.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: The Field is the general domain of endeavor to which the Cognizer is contributing and of which the New_idea is a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity used by the Cognizer to achieve the introduction of the New_idea into society.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the creative event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Cognizer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Basis: The conceptual material from which the Cognizer invents the New_idea.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_appearance: The Location_of_appearance is where the Invention is first discovered by others.

annotations.definition: A Substance causes an Effect. The Substance is produced by a process understood as directed towards the Effect. The Name for the material that makes up the substance may also be given.

frameElementDefinitions.Effect: The change brought about by the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Substance: The internally undifferentiated physical entity that has an Effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Substance belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The name of the material that makes up the active Substance.

annotations.definition: This is an abstract frame for durative activities, in which the Agent enters an ongoing state of the Activity, remains in this state for some Duration of Time, and leaves this state either by finishing or by stopping. The Agent's Activity should be intentional. This frame is intended mostly for the inheritance of common FEs, and to provide the frame structure for the beginning, ongoing, finish, or stop stage of an Activity, each of which constitutes a subframe of this frame. This frame should be compared to the Process frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the Activity in which the Agent is engaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the amount of Time an Activity takes.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Activity occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Activity occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is engaged in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the activity which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: An Agent is in an unfinished part of an activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE identifies the Agent in an unfinished State of an activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent and Activity remain unfinished.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Activity remains unfinished.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The process that the Agent is performing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Activity is no longer in progress.

annotations.definition: An Agent has completed an Activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE identifies the Agent in a done state of an the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the Activity in which the Agent was engaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point at which the Agent is done with the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent finishes the Activity.

annotations.definition: An Agent finishes an Activity, which can no longer logically continue. This frame is a subframe of Activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the Depictive phrase describing an actor or an undergoer of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner an Agent is engaged in finishing an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of finished Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: This FE identifies the last Co-timed_event of the finished Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent finishes the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Activity finishes.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE identifies the Agent who has finished an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the Activity that the Agent has finished.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent finishes an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent finishes for an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the how near the agent is to finishing.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies an event during which the Agent finishes the Activity.

annotations.definition: An Agent is performing the portion of an Activity in which there is dynamic stability.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of Time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the name of the ongoing Activity in which an Agent engages.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the ongoing Activity occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent is engaged in an ongoing Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when an ongoing Activity occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for the Depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Agent performs an ongoing Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent engages in an ongoing Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: This FE identifies the Co-timed_event with which the Agent continues an ongoing Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent engages in an ongoing Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Agent pauses in the course of an Activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the Activity for which an Agent is pausing.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: An Agent pauses in the course of an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Agent pauses during the course of an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent pauses in the course of an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where an Agent pauses in the course of an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which an Agent pauses in the course of an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent pauses in the course of an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for the Depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Completeness: The extent to which the Agent pauses in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time of the pause in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Legal_basis: This FE describes the legal basis or precedent by which the Agent pauses the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: An Agent remains in a particular stage of an Activity, without progressing in the activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE identifies the Agent in the paused State of an activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point at which the Agent
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The overall event that the Agent is involved in.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Activity does not resume.

annotations.definition: An Agent prepares for an Activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: An Agent prepares for an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the Activity for which an Agent is preparing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where an Agent prepares for an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which an Agent prepares for an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Agent prepares for an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: Thsi FE identifies the Means with which an Agent prepares for an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent prepares for an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifes the length of Time during which an Agent prepares for an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for the Depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the amount of preparation the Agent puts into an activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This extrathematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate. Further, the target predicate should involve some sort of Agent that intends that the benefit go to the Beneficiary.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes the state of affairs denoted by the target as role fillers in other frames. The state of affairs reported by the target is understood to play a role in the state of affairs of the modifier.

annotations.definition: The Protagonist is in an initial state, before beginning to be involved in the Activity, but all pre-conditions for performance of the Activity have been satisfied. In many cases, the Protagonist is an intentional agent, but there are other uses in which the agent is deprofiled, and the Protagonist is an entity which is essential to the Activity. Alternatively, the Activity may be indicated by a Salient_entity which stands in for the Activity. This Salient_entity may also be an intentional agent, but need not be.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: This FE identifies the Protagonist in the initial state of an activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The action that the Protagonist is prepared to do.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: An entity which stands in metonymically for the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Protagonist is ready.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state or event that follows due to the ready state.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Protagonist remains capable of the Activity without beginning it.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: An Agent resumes participation in an Activity. Progress on the Activity is retained from before the interruption.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of Time for which the Activity is resumed
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity which is being resumed by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Activity is resumed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Activity is resumed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Activity which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also mark indications of salient characteristics of the Agent which affect the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent who resumes an Activity that they were engaged in earlier.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: his FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The consequence of the resuming of the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: n general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole.

annotations.definition: An Agent initiates the beginning of an ongoing Activity in which he will be continuously involved.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent starts an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent engages in a starting Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent starts an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the Depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Agent starts an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent starts an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the Activity that an Agent starts.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE identifies the being that starts the intentional Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: This FE identifies the first Co-timed_event of the started Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies an event during which the Agent starts the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole. There are two somewhat distinct subgroups of sentence modifiers that cover this function.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases. I
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: A consecutive event to the starting of the Activity of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the target operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.

annotations.definition: An Agent ceases an Activity without completing it.

frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent stopped the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent stops the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent stops the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for a Depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Agent stops the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the stopped Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE identifies Agent that stops the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the Activity that the Agent stops.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the length of Time during which the Activity is stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent stops the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: This FE identifies the last Co-timed_event of the stopped Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the completeness of stopping.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often. It is to be distinguished from Iteration, which pertains simply to the number of times an event occurs-Iteration expressions answer the question how many times.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies an event during which the Agent stops the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason the Agent stops the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: Against the background of a contextually salient non-real world, for instance a hypothetical, imagined, simulated, expected, projected, or dream world, an Instance of a Type encountered in a relation or event in the real world is presented as being different from the instance found in the non-real world. Of course, the real-world Instance of the expected Type may also be presented as not occurring, as in the last example below.

frameElementDefinitions.Instance: The real-world individual--a concrete or abstract thing or an event--which differs from its counterpart in a discourse-salient non-real world.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: A kind of entity, instances of which are expected to occur in both the real and the non-real world.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns an Addict's physiological or psychological compulsive dependence on an Addictant, the substance or behavior to which the Addict is addicted.

frameElementDefinitions.Addict: The Addict is physiologically or psychologically dependent on a substance or a behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Addictant: Addictant is the substance or behavior to which the Addict is dependent.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an Addict is addicted to the Addictant.
frameElementDefinitions.Compeller: Compeller is the part of a human's body or psyche that compels the Addict to seek out the Addictant.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer computes a Result on the basis of input Numbers by addition.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The person doing the calculation.
frameElementDefinitions.Numbers: The Numbers that are used in the calculation.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The outcome of the Cognizer's calculcations.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Cognizer operates on the Numbers.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Cognizer performs their calculation
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Cognizer hopes to bring about by calculating.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Cognizer that brings about their calculation of the Result.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A holistic description of the event (e.g. "the same way"), including depictions of the Cognizer in terms of how the Cognizer's state affects the entire action.

annotations.definition: A Speaker mentions a Specified_entity filling a Role, thereby providing information which supports a point of the Speaker.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the person who specifies the Role to be filled by the Specified_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the Speaker identifies the filler of a Role.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In agentive frames, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Speaker makes a statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Speaker to transmit the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Speaker during the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the statement is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The FE specifies the place where the statement takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Specified_entity: The entity that the Speaker identifies as filling a particular Role.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: A slot in a particular or generic event or relation whose filler the Speaker identifies.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Speaker intends to accomplish by performing the action indicated by adducing the Specified_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Speaker (or Medium) comes to said conclusions about the Specified_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The event of which the adducing is a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times an event is occuring (within a period of time).
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The circumstances under which the adducing takes place.

annotations.definition: A Figure is located adjacent to a Ground. This is equivalent to the Figure and Ground either constituting or being associated with a zone, with each zone in contact with the zone of the other.

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as located relative to a certain Ground location. The figure can be an entity or an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance between the Figure and the Ground. This FE requires an image schema with a vector that extends from the Ground, or a subpart of the Ground, in some direction, and the Distance is measured along this vector from the Ground to the profiled region.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure. This FE is filled by one of a closed class of adverbs: up, down, out (meaning 'beyond the boundary around the viewpoint'), off (meaning 'disconnect between landmark and figure'), back (meaning 'along a line from front to back of landmark, and relatively inaccessible'), and, more rarely, away, along, and dialectally, back down (meaning 'down and behind').
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the locative relation between Figure and Ground obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Figures: The Figures are items which mutually serve to identify the location of the other items.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: An indication of how accessible the Figure is to some participant in the speech context (either the speaker or the addressee, or some other highly salient viewpoint in a narrative). This FE normally describes availability for manipulation and use, but may simply describe how easily seen or difficult to see the Figure is. There is a closed class of items that can fill this slot, including only 'just' and 'right' indicating ease of access, and 'way', 'all the way', or (dialectally) 'clear' indicating distance and difficulty of access.
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'.

annotations.definition: An Agent changes a Part of a complex entity with regard to some Feature in order to achieve some Imposed_purpose affected by the Feature. The complex entity is often a device or mechanism, but may also be an abstract entity such as the economy. Also, the full entity may be used in place of the Part.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that controls the value of a Feature.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: The part of the complex entity which is changed in the process of adjusting.
frameElementDefinitions.Feature: The Feature with respect to which the Part is adjusted.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The state of the Part with respect to the Feature that the Agent brings about by making the adjustment.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Agent which affects the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the adjusting takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the adjusting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_value: The value associated with the Feature that the system ends up in.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity manipulated by the Agent to adjust the Part and Feature.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Agent hopes to bring about by adjusting.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the adjustment act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A characterization of the amount that the Final_value and Initial_value differ.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Agent has an Attribute which can take different Values, and the Agent selects and then begins to use some Value for that Attribute. The Attribute includes both physical (shape, color) and abstract (belief, practice) types. For some LUs in this frame, it is common for only the Value to be expressed, with the Attribute implicit. (Often this implicit attribute can be given second-layer expression on the Value. Note that such second-layer annotation should be assumed in the first two examples below.)

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent selects a Value for his/her Attribute and begins to use it.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The Attribute of the Agent can take on several Values, selected by the Agent .
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The Value, chosen and made effective by the Agent, specifies the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period when the Agent selects and begins to put into effect the Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the adoption of a position which is not classified under more specific descriptions, including comparisons to other events (similarly), rates of adoption (slowly), and descriptions of the Agent which also influence the action (eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent adopts the Value of the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: This FE identifies the role filled by a particular participant of the clause headed by the target. In so doing, it relates the state of affairs evoked by the target to another background state of affairs by indicating that one of the role fillers of the current frame also fills a second kind of role in the background, either generically or in some specific instance.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent has the Attribute which can take different Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which an Agent adopts a Value for an Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The instument through which an Agent adopts a Value for an Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the target operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.). This may or may not reflect the actual reality of the state of affairs.

annotations.definition: An Entity is judged to be sensually pleasing or intellectually interesting to a (generally implicit) Judge.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which a particular gradable Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the implicit value either from the norm or from the value for another Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular implicit value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity has a particular implicit value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Viewpoint: The individual who judges the Entity's aesthetics.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity is in the state of having a particular implicit value for the Attribute.

annotations.definition: A Speaker uses language to affirm or deny the truth of a Message to some Addressee. Instead of (or in addition to) a Speaker, a Medium may also be mentioned. Likewise, a Topic may be stated instead of a Message.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the sentient entity affirming or denying the truth of the Message (this can be spoken or written). It is normally expressed as the External Argument of the predicative uses of the target word, or as the Genitive modifier of the noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message identifies the content whose truth the Speaker is affirming or denying. It can be expressed as a clause or as a noun phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the truth of the Message is affirmed or denied.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place specifies the location where the Message is affirmed or denied.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Message is affirmed or denied.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner describes the way in which the Speaker affirms or denies a Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: Means expresses the act whereby the Speaker affirms or denies a Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Speaker while he/she affirms or denies the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies an event during which the Speaker affirms or denies the truth of the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, Event_description is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole - here, the Message event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: Frequency is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or physical state of the Speaker that leads to their affirmation or denial of the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Speaker to transmit the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject matter to which the Message pertains. It is rarely mentioned in constructions with verbal targets, and is much more common with nominal targets, where it is usually expressed as a PP Complement headed by "of" or about".

annotations.definition: An Entity has existed for a length of time, the Age. The Age can be characterized as a value of the age Attribute, or a Degree modifier may express the deviation of the Age from the norm. The Expressor exhibits qualities of the age of the Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which a particular age is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is a function of the amount of time that the Entity has existed.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: A quantity or other characterization of the Entity's age.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Age from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is at a particular Age.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity has a particular Age.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Frame Element Expressor marks expressions that indicate a body part, gesture or other expression of the Entity that reflect its age.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.

annotations.definition: This frame contains nouns denoting Aggregates of Individuals. The Aggregates may be described by an Aggregate_property.

frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: This FE identifies the people or things that form the Aggregate.
frameElementDefinitions.Aggregate: This FE identifies the collection of entities that are the Aggregate.
frameElementDefinitions.Aggregate_property: This FE describes a property of the Aggregate rather than the Individuals and is used to tag the adjective that modifies the aggregate noun when it is unambiguous.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to the Aggregate.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location or political locale that the Organization is contained within.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant Domain as it pertains to the Aggregate.

annotations.definition: An Entity is undergoing a change in age typically associated with some deterioration or change in state.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity which is undergoing aging.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity ages.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity undergoes the aging event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In agentive frames, it may indicate salient characteristics of an intentional Theme that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE indicates where the Entity is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The end Result of the Entity aging.

annotations.definition: A Speaker either agrees to or refuses to engage in a Proposed_action, proposed by an Interlocutor.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the person to whom a particular action has been proposed and who responds to the request or offer made.
frameElementDefinitions.Interlocutor: This is the person who proposed the Proposed_action to the Speaker and to whom the Speaker communicates their response.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: As with Medium in other communication frames, the physical entity or channel used to communicate can be expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Proposed_action: The Proposed_action is an action that the Speaker would perform if they were to accept or refuse the request or offer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Speaker that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Speaker responds to the request or offer made.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The response may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause, the Speaker mental or emotional state.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Speaker gives their verbal response to the proposed action.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Speaker makes their verbal response concerning the proposed action.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Speaker intends to accomplish by agreeing or refusing to perform the Proposed_action that is requested of, or suggested to them.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of response events.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role denotes the status that the Interlocutor requested to be granted.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: This FE identifies the subevent of the agreeing/refusing to act.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times (within a given period) that the agreeing/refusing to act occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness, and consists of any descriptions of the state of affairs denoted by the target as role fillers in other frames. The state of affairs reported by the target is understood to play a role in the state of affairs of the modifier.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Agriculturist cultivates Food. The location or Ground where the Food is cultivated by the Agriculturist is often expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Agriculturist: The Agriculturist is the agent cultivating Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Food: Food is the item cultivated by the Agriculturist.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is used by the Agriculturist as a tool to help obtain Food. (This tool is not limited to typical artifacts and can be hands, arms, and other atypical hunting and fishing instruments.)
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what length of time the Agriculturist attempted to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agriculturist cultivates Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agriculturist cultivates Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The greater geographic location where the Agriculturist cultivates Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the agricultural event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agriculturist intends to accomplish by cultivating Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Agriculturist cultivates Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: This is the the background or context for the Food to which the Agriculturist pays attention in his agricultural efforts.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates the subtype of agriculture that the Agriculturist is engaging in.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE appraises the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the agricultural event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Agent adjusts an Instrument to enable it to interact directly with a difficult-to-access Target_location. Usually, there is a Targeted entity at the Target_location which is mentioned in its place.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that manipulates the Instrument to enable interaction with the Targeted.
frameElementDefinitions.Targeted: The Targeted is the entity located in an indirectly accessible location relative to the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the aiming is achieved.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent takes aim.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent during the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Agent aims at the Targeted.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any generalized description of the details of the event in terms of comparison to another event or what would be expected of the details given some state of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time peiod over which the adjustment of the Instrument takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Agent attempts to interact with the Targeted
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is independent of the the aiming of the Agent, but contextualizes it.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The object manipulated by the Agent in order to interact with the Targeted.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_location: The location that the Agent wishes to ready the Instrument to access.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: The location from which the proto-agent acts or perceives. This does not include the location of a thing which is affected or perceived in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity is undertaken by the Agent and is directed towards the Targeted or Target_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: A larger event during which an aiming event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that provides an explanation for the Aiming event.

annotations.definition: An Alignment_match region has the same orientation as the relatively linear Ground. The Alignment_mismatch region is oriented perpendicularly to the Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Alignment_match: A region of space oriented the same as the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity whose location and orientation is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the Alignment_match and Alignment_mismatch regions.
frameElementDefinitions.Alignment_mismatch: A region oriented approximately perpendicular to the Ground

annotations.definition: After having worked separately for some time, two or more organizational or individual Members are now organized in an Alliance so that they may better pursue some common Purposes. The formerly separate Members may be presented separately as Member_1 and Member_2.

frameElementDefinitions.Alliance: The entity of which the individuals are Members.
frameElementDefinitions.Members: The persons or groups belonging to the Alliance.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which an Alliance is formed.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_existence: The interval during which the Alliance exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Member_1: That group or individual which has joined the Alliance which is grammatically more prominent when the Members are presented separately.
frameElementDefinitions.Member_2: That group or individual which has joined the Alliance which is grammatically less prominent when the Members are presented separately.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of some property of the Alliance.

annotations.definition: There exist a number of different possible Situations that may happen in the future. (In some cases, the Situation is concurrent or past, and only the knowledge of which has happened is in the future.) In most cases, there is an Agent involved who has to consider which of the possible Situations will or should occur. A Salient_entity which is deeply involved in the Situation may also be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Situation: Something that may happen in the future, or at least whose factual status is unresolved. This is often tagged in a second FE layer and generally contains the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: An individual involved in the Event. "You have a choice."
frameElementDefinitions.Number_of_possibilities: The number of different future Events under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: An entity intimately involved in the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Agent hopes to bring about which is associated with some of the possible Events but not others.

annotations.definition: In this frame, there is a Profiled_alternative that the speaker wishes to primarily focus on and which is mutually exclusive with a Contrasting_alternative. It is not possible for both to be true in the same context with the same participants, although the described situation may be true of the same entities at different times, or true of different entities at the same time. Frames inheriting from this one, e.g. Negation, may indicate which of the alternatives is imagined to be the case, but in other frames related to this one, e.g. Conditional_scenario and Alternatives, both alternatives are presented as possible, and which one is true remains an open question.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_alternative: A situation that cannot be true at the same time as the Contrasting_alternative.
frameElementDefinitions.Contrasting_alternative: A situation that cannot be true at the same time as the Profiled_alternative.

annotations.definition: These words refer to Parts merging to form a Whole. (The Parts may also be encoded as Part_1 and Part_2.) There is a symmetrical relationship between the components that undergo the process, and afterwards the Parts are consumed and are no longer distinct entities that are easily discernable or separable in the Whole.

frameElementDefinitions.Parts: The entities that merge to form a single Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The single, undifferentiated entity that is the result of the amalgamation.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_1: The prominent entity that combines with Part_2 to form a Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_2: The less focussed-upon entity that the Part_1 merges with.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the thoroughness of the mixing job, i.e. to what degree the resulting Whole is undifferentiated.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In this frame, it is also deemed to include modification indicating the speed of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Parts after the amalgamation.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Parts become amalgamated into a Whole
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the amalgamation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of Part_1 or Parts.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A Descriptor describes the target. Usually adjectives, used with noun LUs.

annotations.definition: A Recipient (who may or may not be agentive) ends up in possession of a Mass_theme (which may be conceptualized as a single mass entity, or may be a group of individual entities). Some lexical units (e.g. stockpile) specifically indicate an intentional Recipient. This frame differs from Gathering_up in that this frame refers specifically to the acquisition and (according to expectation) long-term possession of entities, rather than (as in the Gathering_up frame) simply talking about causing a group of entities to be located in the same place. In sum, Gathering_up concerns (co-)locations, but Amassing concerns possession.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The Recipient indicates the entity that ends up in possession of the Mass_theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Mass_theme: The collection of objects that ends up in the Recipient's possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location that the Mass_theme formerly occupied. Persons that begin in possession of the Mass_theme may metonymically stand in for the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place denotes where the Mass_theme is acquired.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional amassment is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation refers to the existing eventuality that the Recipient is responding to.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is the action of the Recipient which results in their possession of the Mass_theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the Amassing which is not covered by more specific FEs. It includes such specifications as comparisons to other events (similarly), rates of acquisition (slowly), and descriptions of the Recipient which also influence the action (eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state that either the Recipient or the Mass_theme end up in after the acquisition.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE describes the length of time over which amassing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which amassing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is used for the capacity in which the Recipient amasses stuff, and is expressed in as-PPs.

annotations.definition: The Temperature in a certain environment, determined by Time and Place, is specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where it is a certain Temperature .
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The temperature feature of the weather.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature: A quantity or other characterization of the Temperature of the environment.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Temperature from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which an ambient environment has a particular Temperature.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the ambient Temperature is as specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Weather: The meteorological conditions which determine the ambient temperature of a locale.

annotations.definition: A piece of Ammunition is a disposable artifact to be fired as a projectile in a weapon with the purpose of causing harm or damage.

frameElementDefinitions.Ammunition: This FE identifies the Ammunition that is designed to have a particular Use.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which a piece of ammunition is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Ammunition into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which an Ammunition comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Ammunition.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Ammunition.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Ammunition, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Weapon: The weapon that the Ammunition is used in.

annotations.definition: The absolute Value of a quantifiable Attribute of an item is determined by adding up the values for the Attribute of the individuals or parts that make up the item. The item is not expressed as a separate constituent but it is presupposed by the concept of Attribute. Alternatively, the Numbers form an implicit set whose sum is the Value associated with the set. In some cases what is reported is just the cardinality of the individuals that make up the item so that in effect the Value is a count. In other cases the identity of the Attribute can only be inferred from the Value. There is no cognizer on the scene in this frame. Instead, the frame focuses on the relationship between the Attribute or Numbers and the Value, which is stable and unchanging regardless of who performs the calculation.

frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The scalar property of a concrete or abstract entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_span: The span of time when the Attribute has the Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE indicates the time span during which the total of the Attribute is the Value, with the added information that it is (somehow) calculated at a regular interval coinciding with the transition between spans of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Numbers: A set of numbers that add up to the Value.

annotations.definition: The LUs in this frame refer to biological entities other than human beings and plants, which are labeled by the FE Animal.

frameElementDefinitions.Animal: The living organism belonging to the kingdom Animalia.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Animal not covered by another frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The location from which the Animal originated.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer, Expressor, or State has a feeling of annoyance as evoked by a Stimulus or concerning a Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part, gesture, or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer, Expressor, or State can be described as having said emotion.

annotations.definition: In the Appeal stage of the criminal process the Convict or his or her Representative applies to a higher Court to reverse the Decision of the lower court on certain Grounds.

frameElementDefinitions.Convict: The Convict is the individual convicted of the crime who may make an appeal on her or his behalf.
frameElementDefinitions.Representative: The Representative applies to a higher court on behalf of the Convict.
frameElementDefinitions.Decision: The Decision is the ruling handed down by the first court.
frameElementDefinitions.Grounds: The Grounds is the legal basis on which the appeal rests.
frameElementDefinitions.Court: The Court is the judicial establishment to which the Convict appeals.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which a Convict or Representative appeals.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: This frame is only for words that are used as the Titles of individuals. The Title is applied to a Name (surname or full name) and often designates their rank within a man-made institution (e.g. chief, king, bishop, brother) or general social status (mr., miss), or may indicate a kinship relationship (cousin, aunt). Pairings of Title and Name can be used as vocatives (see Attention_getting), while the Titles may not always be vocative on their own.

frameElementDefinitions.Name: The name of the person referred to by a Title.
frameElementDefinitions.Title: How a person is referred to within an Institution, often denoting their rank or status. Title is always marked on the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: The language, region, or domain in which the Title applies.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The sub-type of the Title.

annotations.definition: A Cook applies heat to Food, where the Temperature_setting of the heat and Duration of application may be specified. A Heating_instrument, generally indicated by a locative phrase, may also be expressed. Some cooking methods involve the use of a Medium (e.g. milk or water) by which heat is transferred to the Food. A less semantically prominent Food or Cook is marked Co-participant.

frameElementDefinitions.Cook: The Cook applies heat to the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Heating_instrument: This FE identifes the entity that directly supplies heat to the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the heat application occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Food: Food is the entity to which heat is applied by the Cook.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which the heat application occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which heat is applied to Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration is the amount of Time heat is applied to the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the substance through which heat is applied to the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co-participant is the gramatically less prominent of two foods to which heat is applied by the Cook.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature_setting: This FE identifies the Temperature_setting of the Heating_instrument for the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which heat application occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the cooking event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Cook that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Container: The Container holds the Food to which heat is applied.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: THe person for whose benefit the food is cooked.
