annotations.definition: A Selector, often an individual in a leadership role, appoints a Official to assume an official Role in an organization (which can be expressed as the Body).

frameElementDefinitions.Selector: The Selector is responsible for the appointment of the new official. Typically, it occurs as the External Argument of verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Official: The Official is a person appointed to a recognized position.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Frequently, the Official is referred to only by his/her role. However, the Role can be expressed separately from the leader, usually as either a secondary predicate or in a PP Complement (headed by as).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE indicates the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive indicates the state of one of the participants during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE is used for the Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Selector accomplishes the designation of the Official.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE is used for indicating the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This frame element describes the situation that brought about the new appointment.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: The Function is the purpose the Official will fufill.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the designation of the official takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the appointment takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Body: A group of individuals who are elected or appointed to perform some function, and to which the Official is assigned.

annotations.definition: An entity is integrated into a building as a Part of a (possibly unexpressed) Whole which may refer to the whole building or merely to a sub-part of the building.

frameElementDefinitions.Part: A Part of a larger architectural unit, the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: An architectural unit which includes the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A property relating to the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Orientation: This is a property of the Part that situates the Part within the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Part, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The Individual who creates the Part.

annotations.definition: This frame describes a certain type of mathematical scenario in which two numbers, labeled Term1 and Term2, interact via some mathematical operation.

frameElementDefinitions.Term1: The grammatically more prominent number in an arithmetic operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Term2: The grammatically less prominent number in an arithmetic operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Terms: Two more or elements of a commutative arithmetic operation. This frame element may be represented as Term1 and Term2.

annotations.definition: This frame describes types of arithmetic operations and statements which have the commutative property, i.e. the operation is bi-directional, e.g. addition and multiplication. The terms involved are either individually labeled Term1 and Term2 or grouped together as a single noun phrase labeled Terms.

frameElementDefinitions.Terms: Two more or elements of a commutative arithmetic operation. This frame element may be represented as Term1 and Term2.
frameElementDefinitions.Term1: The grammatically more prominent number involved in a commutative operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Term2: The grammatically less prominent number involved in commutative operation.

annotations.definition: This frame describes types of arithmetic operations and statements which are not commutative, i.e. the operation is uni-directional, e.g. subtraction and division. Because of the non-commutative nature, terms must be separated from each other in the form of Term_1 and Term_2 instead of a combined Terms FE.

frameElementDefinitions.Term_1: Term_1 is the first operand in the non-commutative arithmetic that is modified by Term_2 (e.g. subtracted or divided by Term_2).
frameElementDefinitions.Term_2: Term_2 is the second operand of the non-commutative arithmetic which modifies Term_1 (e.g. subtracts from or divides Term_1).

annotations.definition: An Asset, the safety of which is desirable, is protected from damage by a covering, the Armor. The Armor covers part or all of the Asset and is designed to absorb force from an oncoming Danger.

frameElementDefinitions.Armor: The Armor prevents the Asset from coming to harm.
frameElementDefinitions.Danger: An object or situation that could damage the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Asset: Something desirable which might be lost or damaged and which is protected by Armor.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description or characteristic of the Armor.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Armor protects the Asset, e.g., how resilient it is.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The Material which the Armor is primarily made of.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The place on the Asset covered by the Armor.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: A description of the subtype of Armor.

annotations.definition: At a court hearing, a Defendant is informed of the Charges against him or her, (usually) enters a plea, and a decision is made by a Judge as to the amount of bail, if any.

frameElementDefinitions.Defendant: The Defendant is charged with a crime by a Judge.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the arraignment occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the arraignment occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The Judge is the head of the court where the arraignment occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: Charges is the crime with which the Defendant is charged.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the arraignment takes.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the arraignment which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (openly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Judge that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: An Agent puts a complex Theme into a particular Configuration, which can be a proper order, a correct or suitable sequence, or a spatial position.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who causes the Theme to be in a particular Configuration.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: An entity that consists of parts which can be put into a configuration relative to each other and/or relative to their environment.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the arranging takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Configuration: A state that holds of the Theme or a relation that holds among the constituent parts of the Theme as a result of the arranging action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the arranging action which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). With agentive use, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action ( eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The place in which the Theme is put into a Configuration.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances identifies the situation in which an Agent arranges a Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent is trying to bring about by changing the Configuration of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity used by the Agent to change the Theme's Configuration.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Agent in order to arrange the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Arranging act has occured.

annotations.definition: Authorities charge a Suspect, who is under suspicion of having committed a crime (the Charges), and take him/her into custody.

frameElementDefinitions.Charges: Charges identifies a category within the legal system; it is the crime with which the Suspect is charged.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the arrest occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the arrest occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities charge the Suspect with commiting a crime, and take him/her into custody.
frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: The Suspect is taken into custody, under suspicion of having committed a crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates the subtype of arrest under which the Suspect is placed.
frameElementDefinitions.Offense: Offense identifies the ordinary language use of the reason for which a Suspect is arrested.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the arresting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Authorities that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which Authorities arrest a Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which Authorities arrest a Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_of_legal_authority: The Source_of_legal_authority describe the law which grants the power of arrest to the Authorities.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: A person that participates in the event along with the Authorities or the Suspect.

annotations.definition: An object Theme moves in the direction of a Goal. The Goal may be expressed or it may be understood from context, but its is always implied by the verb itself.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: Theme is the object that moves. It may be an entity that moves under its own power, but it need not be.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion. While Source expressions are possible in this frame, they are relatively infrequent. If present, they often express a general direction from which a Theme moves, rather than a landmark away from which it moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is any expression that tells where the Theme ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A Manner expression describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Theme arrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Theme is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Theme. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: Cotheme is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when arriving occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: The New_situation are the state of the Goal when the Theme arrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the state of the Theme upon arrival.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The time period within which the arrival repeats multiple times.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Theme arrives to the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE marks the state of affairs reported by the Arriving Event as understood to play a role in the state of affairs of the modifier.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Theme arrives at the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the arriving event occurs.

annotations.definition: A Perpetrator intentionally sets fire to his own or someone else's property, often in order to collect an insurance payment on this property.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a phrase that describes the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Offense: The illegal setting of fire to destroy something.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an action is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which arson is committed.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The action that the Perpetrator is trying to accomplish with the arson Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time that a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the arson occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the arson occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: The Perpetrator sets fire to a building in order to gain some benefit from so doing.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: Victim identifies the enity that is negatively effected by the arson.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frequency with which an act of arson is committed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: An Artifact is deliberately made or modified by an intelligent entity to be put to a certain kind of Use. Various properties of the Artifact may be specified, such as its Creator, Time_of_creation, Name, Type, and the Material of which it is composed. The Artifact may or may not be a physical entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Artifact: This FE identifies the entity or substance that is designed to have a particular Use. This can refer to physical artifacts, like a mug or computer monitor, or a digital artifact, such as a website or document.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which an artifact is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Artifact into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which an Artifact comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Artifact, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes either a state or characteristic of the artifact which is not subsumed by the core FEs.

annotations.definition: A Component has a distinct Function in the operation of a complex Artifact, which is not regarded as complete unless it is assembled from a number of different Components. Each Component may also be described by their Creator, Time_of_creation, or Material.

frameElementDefinitions.Component: An entity that is a part of an Artifact and serves a distinct Function.
frameElementDefinitions.Artifact: A deliberately made entity which fulfilling a purpose, whose complete form is composed of more than one Component.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: The role or purpose of the Component as it pertains to the Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The type of matter that the Component comprises.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The entity who creates the Component.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: When the Component was created.

annotations.definition: An Entity is evaluated for whether or not it belongs to a particular Reference_category in a context where its category membership is doubtful. The criterion or criteria that are relevant to evaluating Reference_category membership depend on the individual lexical units and/or on the context. Typically, either the material composition of the Entity, its method of creation, or the identity (and proper authority) of its creators are critical for proper category membership. Note that Entity also covers behaviors.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity that is being evaluated in terms of whether it is an instance of the Reference_category.
frameElementDefinitions.Reference_category: The type relative to which the Entity is evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Entity seems to belong (or not, if so specified by the lexical unit) to the category of the Reference_category is clear. Typically, this frame element is realized as an end-of-scale expression.

annotations.definition: This frame contains LUs that express the Form which a piece of Artwork represents. The Form may also represent the entire body of an Artist's work. The Form may be further characterized by the Time of some Artwork's creation or other Descriptors.

frameElementDefinitions.Artist: A person who creates an Artwork with a particular recognizable Form.
frameElementDefinitions.Artwork: A created artifact that is created by an Artist and has a unique Form as perceived by some judge.
frameElementDefinitions.Form: The Form is a style that characterizes an Artwork (or an Artist, metonymically) and can be recognized by a judge. This FE is usually incorporated into the target for nouns in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A description of when the Artwork was created.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Form that the Artwork resembles.

annotations.definition: In this frame a group of Individuals intentionally come together to form a Group. Alternatively, a grammatically more prominent Party_1 assembles with a Party_2, expressed as an oblique phrase. If this asymmetric construal is chosen, Purpose only relates to Party_1.

frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: The Individuals are self-movers who intentionally assemble together.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group describes the resulting formation of the Individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: Party_1 is the grammatically more prominent participant in the assembling event. It can refer to either a single individual or a larger party separate from Party_2 previous to the assembling.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: A grammatically less prominent participant in the assembling event. It can refer to either a single individual or a larger party separate from Party_1 previous to the assembling.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the assembling which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Individuals or Group that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the assembling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means descibes the way in which the Individuals accomplish the assembling.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the assembling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Group plans to engage in upon assembly.

annotations.definition: An Assessor examines a Phenomenon to figure out its Value according to some Feature of the Phenomenon. This Value is a factor in determining the acceptability of the Phenomenon. In some cases, a Method (implicitly involving an Assessor) is used to determine the Phenomenon's Value.

frameElementDefinitions.Assessor: The sentient entity that evaluates the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The entity whose Value according to a Feature is being measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The value (according to some scale) that the Phenomenon is deemed to have. The Value is taken to indicate the Phenomenon's acceptability.
frameElementDefinitions.Feature: The type of property or feature that the Phenomenon is being judged on.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: Some fact perceived by the Assessor which leads to their evaluation of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any secondary effect of the action or characterization of the Assessor which saliently affects the character of the assessment.
frameElementDefinitions.Method: An event or procedure that allows the Assessor to evaluate the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which assessment occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Assessor makes the assessment.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: An entity that the Phenomenon is compared to in the assessment.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal that the Assessor hopes to accomplish by assessing the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the assessing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Assessor to make the assessment.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: A person who participates along with the Assessor in the assessing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times assessing occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This extrathematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate. Further, the target predicate should involve some sort of Agent that intends that the benefit go to the Beneficiary.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world at a partiicular time and place.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The result of the assessing.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE denotes the length of time during which a continuous event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.

annotations.definition: An Individual is assigned a Station to remain at, usually in order to accomplish a task. The Station may have an intended use based on its features (e.g., a vendor's booth), or it may be temporarily assigned a use.

frameElementDefinitions.Station: The locale where the Entity is supposed to be. It is typically the designation of the nouns of Locale-derived frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: Salient parts that make up a Station.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that the Station is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Indicates the action (or causer) which brings the features of the Station about. This FE also covers expressions that indicate that there was no salient formation.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Station is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Station.
frameElementDefinitions.Related_event: An event related to the Station in some way other than (1) Formational_cause (which is a separate FE) or an intended use (which would place the instance in Locale_by_use), or (3) a defining event (which would place the instance in Locale_by_event).
frameElementDefinitions.Task: The activity that is carried out at the Station.
frameElementDefinitions.Responsible_party: The Responsible_party is tasked with remaining at the Station to perform a Task.

annotations.definition: A Helper benefits a Benefited_party by enabling the culmination of a Goal that the Benefited_party has. A Focal_entity that is involved in reaching the Goal may stand in for it. This Frame has unique Frame-to-Frame Relations: it inherits from and uses the Intentionally_act frame. This is atypical of FrameNet's frame relations.

frameElementDefinitions.Benefited_party: The Benefited_party receives a benefit from the action of the Helper.
frameElementDefinitions.Helper: The Helper performs some action that benefits the Benefited_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The desirable state of affairs that the Benefited_party is involved in and which is enabled by the Helper.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_entity: This FE identifies a Focal_entity involved in achieving the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Assistance occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Assistance occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which a Helper assists a Benefited_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action that the Helper undertakes in service of the Benefited_party's Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity used by the Helper in service of the Benefited_party's accomplishing their Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The aspect of accomplishing the Goal to which the Helper's assistance is relevant.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The measure to which the Helper's assistance brings the Benefited_party closer to accomplishing their Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time period during which the Helper gives assistence to the Benefited_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The situation that the Helper wishes to bring about by helping the Benefited_party to acheive the Goal. Note that this FE should normally be used only when this situation is separate from the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the questions how often. It is to be distinguished from Iteration, which pertains simply to the number of times an event occurs; Iteration expressions answer the question how many times.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: Perspective on Reciprocality in which Protagonist_1 acts on or is related to Protagonist_2. The relation or event is construed as asymmetrical, inasmuch as Protagonist_1 is presented as more agentive, if agency is involved (such as in conversation, participating), and Protagonist_2 is presented as more passive. In cases of comparison or other relations (e.g. resembling, being near), Protagonist_2 is construed as suitable as a reference for Protagonist_1, often because Protagonist_2 is more temporally stable and/or is more salient and widely known.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist_1: The profiled participant in a reciprocal event, encoded as the subject of an active-form sentence or as a by-PP in a passive.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist_2: The participant involved (subordinately) with Protagonist_1 in a reciprocal event. It is generally coded in a with-PP.

annotations.definition: An Agent has committed a Wrong in the past and is making Amends for it. The Degree to which the Amends are adequate may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent has committed a Wrong and is making Amends.
frameElementDefinitions.Wrong: Wrong identifies the act committed by the Agent for which Amends are made.
frameElementDefinitions.Amends: Amends marks expressions that indicate what the Agent does to compensate for the Wrong that was committed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE marks expressions that indicate the Degree to which Amends made are adequate for atoning the Wrong.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The situation that the Agent hopes to bring about by atoning.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the atonement which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent atones for the Wrong.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Agent expiates the Wrong.

annotations.definition: The Attaching frame covers two situations: a scene in which somebody causes one thing to be physically connected to something else; or a scene in which somebody causes two things to be connected to each other. In the first, the frame includes an Agent who attaches an Item to a Goal by manipulating a Connector, creating an asymmetric relationship between the Item and the Goal. In the second, the Agent attaches two Items to each other, where each serves as a Goal for the other, creating a symmetric relationship between the two Items. In both cases, the Connector remains to bind the two entities (either Item and Goal, or two Items), without creating a new entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent manipulates an object. Agent typically refers to animate beings, but is also extended to mechanical and other inanimate objects. It is most frequently expressed as the external argument of verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: This FE identifies the Item that the Agent attaches to the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an attaching event.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal identifies the location to which an Item is attached.
frameElementDefinitions.Connector: The Connector forms the bond that maintains the Item or Items in a fixed position; it is usually expressed in a with-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Items: This FE identifies the plural Items that are tied together, resulting in their mutual attachment.
frameElementDefinitions.Handle: Thie Handle is the point of attachment on the Item to which the Agent attaches.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent intentionally affects an Item or Items.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Agent intentionally affects an Item or Items.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent intentionally affects an Item or Items.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent intentionally affects an Item or Items.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent intentionally affects the Item or Items.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an Agent attaches an Item or Items. Typically, it transmits the force applied by the Agent, and is not left behind as a participant in the resultant Being_attached frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Item ends up attached.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times the Attaching event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the Attaching event began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time when the Attaching occurs.

annotations.definition: An Assailant physically attacks a Victim (which is usually but not always sentient), causing or intending to cause the Victim physical damage. A Weapon used by the Assailant may also be mentioned, in addition to the usual Place, Time, Purpose, Explanation, etc. Sometimes a location is used metonymically to stand for the Assailant or the Victim, and in such cases the Place FE will be annotated on a second FE layer.

frameElementDefinitions.Victim: This FE is the being or entity that is injured by the Assailant's attack.
frameElementDefinitions.Weapon: An entity used by the Assailant to cause damage to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Assailant: The person (or other self-directed entity) that is attempting physical harm to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Assailant that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Assailant that accomplishes the assault.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Assailant makes an assault on the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Assailant attacks the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Assailant is responding to that leads them to make an assault on the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Assailant attacks the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The starting-point of the motion of the Assailant or projectiles of the Assailant as they move toward the the Place that the Victim is occupying in the attack. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describing the state of the Assailant or Victim while the attack occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the attack on the Victim by the Assailant.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held. Form: once, twice, Cardinal expression + /it times, /it lots/a lot
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole. There are two somewhat distinct subgroups of sentence modifiers that cover this function.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the Assailant travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number (or description of the number) of times an attack takes place over a period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the attack lasts.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that is neither the goal of motion nor a landmark along the way.

annotations.definition: An Agent attempts to achieve a Goal. The Outcome may also be mentioned explicitly.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is what the Agent attempts to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Effort: This FE identifies the effort expended in an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an attempt is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent makes the attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time peiod over which the attempts take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Agent attempts the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is independent of the the attempt of the Agent to achieve the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The area of work to which the Attempt event belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: The conditions under which the the Agent attempts the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times the Attemping event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in desiring the Goal.

annotations.definition: This frame represents the core action of an Attempt, where an Agent has a Goal and performs some Means action that they believe will achieve the Goal. See its two perspectivized frames for further information.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes an attempt to achieve a Goal via some Means
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is what the Agent attempts to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an attempt is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent makes the attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time peiod over which the attempts take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Agent attempts the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is independent of the the attempt of the Agent to achieve the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: The conditions under which the the Agent attempts the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times the Attemping event occurs per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: An Agent attempts to interact with a relatively distant or indirectly accessible Target using some Instrument (which may be a body part, e.g. hand). The Agent's ability to manipulate the Instrument and put it in the Target location is in question. The most common types of distant interaction involve an Agent attempting to move the Instrument ballistically so that it contacts the Target (e.g. Shooting_scenario), or an Agent attempting to direct an Instrument that enables perception at a Target_location to be examined.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent tries to interact with the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Target: The Target is the entity located in an indirectly accessible location relative to the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent during the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an attempt is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent makes the attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time peiod over which the attempt or attempts take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Agent attempts to interact with the Target
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is independent of the the attempt of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The object manipulated by the Agent in order to interact with the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_location: The location that the Agent is attempting to access using the Instrument.

annotations.definition: An Agent attempts to achieve a Goal using some Means.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is what the Agent attempts to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: Note: this FE might not be in frame. It is temporarily here during the early stage of annotation. (JGK) The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an attempt is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent makes the attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time peiod over which the attempts take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Agent attempts the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is independent of the the attempt of the Agent to achieve the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: Note: this FE might not be in frame. It is temporarily here during the early stage of annotation. (JGK) The area of work to which the Attempt event belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE denotes the number of times an event is attempted per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: A Seeker is trying to obtain Food and is seeking it within the context of a Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Seeker: The Seeker is the agent hoping to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Food: Food is the sought-out item by the Seeker.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is used by the Seeker as a tool to help obtain Food. (This tool is not limited to typical artifacts and can be hands, arms, and other atypical hunting and fishing instruments.)
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what length of time the Seeker attempted to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Seeker makes the attempt for Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an attempt at achieving a Food obtainment.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The greater geographic location where the Seeker attempts to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Food seeking action occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Seeker intends to accomplish by seeking Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Seeker attempts to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the amount of effort the Seeker puts into an attempt to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: This is the entity to which the Seeker pays attention. It is referred to as Ground because it serves as the background or context for the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: An Agent attempts to obtain a non-renewable resource, often solid minerals or metals, but also oil, water, and other valuable substances.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes an attempt to obtain minerals.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means action performed by the Agent which they hope will let them obtain the Mineral.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an attempt is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time period over which the attempts take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Mineral: The valuable resource which the Agent attempts to acquire.

annotations.definition: The Speaker expresses through language his wish to get the Addressee to act in some way that will help to bring about events or states described in the Content. There is no implication that the Addressee forms an intention to act, let alone acts.

frameElementDefinitions.Content: The wish for action on the Addressee's part that the Speaker expresses.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The person that the Speaker seeks to influence to act.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person who intends through the use of language to get another person to act.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: What the communication is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The written or spoken material that contains a message that potentially influences the Addressee towards the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an action is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a phrase that describes the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an action is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the lobbying occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (independent of the event).
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group is the set of Speakers and Mediums within which the Attempt_suasion is occuring.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Speaker attemtps susaion on an Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Speaker attempts to persuade the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: An entity which the Speaker believes should participate in the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The role in which the Salient_entity is used to try to persuade the Addressee.

annotations.definition: An Agent has a particular Goal in mind, and then uses some Means that they believe will bring about the Goal. The Agent succeeds or fails inasmuch as the result of the Means action matches the Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is what the Agent attempts to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means action performed by the Agent which they hope will bring about their Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an attempt is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time period over which the attempts take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Agent attempts to achieve the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent attempts to achieve the Goal.

annotations.definition: An Agent goes to an Event and is present in a relatively non-participatory way.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who chooses to make himself present at the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the deliberate set of happenings given at a particular time and place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Agent went about going to the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: Through what strategies and efforts the Agent made possible his attendance at the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent attends the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Agent attends the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Under what conditions the Agent goes to the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason why the Agent goes to the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes the event as a whole.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns a Perceiver's state of readiness to process and consider impressions of a Figure within a Ground. It is often unknown to the Perceiver whether or not the Figure exists within the Ground. Alternatively, the Expressor may be expressed as showing signs of the Perceiver's state of attentiveness.

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The individual that pays attention to the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The sensory field or subset of a sensory field that the Perceiver is attending to.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The entity that the Perceiver is specifically focussing on within the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of attention that the Perceiver is paying to the Figure or Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The situation within which the Perceiver is alert.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of a Perceiver that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: An entity (or event) associated with a Perceiver that gives evidence for a Perceiver's attentiveness.

annotations.definition: This frame covers terms used to get someone's attention, including Interjections (e.g. hey, yo) and certain terms of address (Address_term), the latter of which may serve the function of attention getting.

frameElementDefinitions.Address_term: This FE identifies the Address_term as used to get someone's attention, and may be a proper name.
frameElementDefinitions.Interjection: Interjection identifies the attention getting element.

annotations.definition: The lexical units in this frame are descriptions of a Cognizer's Attitude about or outlook on a State_of_affairs.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The person whose Attitude about a State_of_affairs is being expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Attitude: The outlook or viewpoint expressed by the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: An event, topic, or situation about which the Cognizer has an Attitude.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree of the Attitude description.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time interval during which the Cognizer has the Attitude about the State_of_affairs.

annotations.definition: A Proposition is attributed to a Speaker or a Text.

frameElementDefinitions.Proposition: The information attributed to the Speaker or Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: A sentient entity that has communicated the information encoded by the Proposition.
frameElementDefinitions.Text: A written or verbal collection of statements, which imparts the information of the Proposition.

annotations.definition: An Entity has a particular Attribute with some Value. This frame is primarily for inheritance.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which a particular Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: A quantity or other characterization of the Entity's state with respect to the Attribute.

annotations.definition: An Agent has the means to affect a Theme along the lines of a certain Domain. There is an imbalance of influence or power within a certain Domain that favors the Agent (often due to some Source) over the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person or organization with more influence or power.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The person or organization with less influence or power.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: A description of the area in which an Agent can affect a Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: A principle factor which gave the Agent greater power or influence over the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Describes the Agent 's influence or power in some way. Descriptors are typically adjectives modifying nominal targets.

annotations.definition: An Agent avoids an Undesirable_situation under certain Circumstances. That situation may be an activity (e.g. going to school), or an entity (perhaps) metonymic for such an activity or situation (e.g. the curb). In this frame, the situation is undesirable from the Agent's point of view (e.g. evade), and contrasts with scenes in which the situation is specifically undesirable to someone else (e.g. forbear) or from the point of view of an external morality (e.g. abstain).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent avoids an Undesirable_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the avoiding occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the avoiding occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose avoiding occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Undesirable_situation: This FE identifies the situation that the Agent avoids.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which avoiding occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which avoiding occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances under which the Undesirable_situation is avoided.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which avoiding occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which avoiding occurs.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer has a piece of Content in their model of the world. The Content is not necessarily present due to immediate perception, but usually, rather, due to deduction from perceivables. In some cases, the deduction of the Content is implicitly based on confidence in sources of information (believe), in some cases based on logic (think), and in other cases the source of the deduction is deprofiled (know).

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person whose awareness of phenomena is at question. With a target verb or adjective the Cognizer is generally expressed as an External Argument with the Content expressed as an Object or Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The Content is the object of the Cognizer's awareness. Content can be expressed as a direct object or in a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The source of awareness or knowledge which can be expressed in a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Some verbs in this frame allow a Topic to be expressed in about-PPs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Cognizer knows or thinks something.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: Expressor is the body part that reveals the Cognizer's state to the observer.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is the category within which an element of the Content is considered.
frameElementDefinitions.Paradigm: This frame element identifies the Paradigm which serves as the basis for the Cognizer's awareness.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Cognizer is aware of the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why or how it came to be that the Cognizer has awareness of the Topic or Content.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer has a piece of Content in their model of the world due to deduction, inference, or similar means of arriving at information from perceivables.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person whose awareness of the Content is at issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The object of the Cognizer's awareness.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: A body part that reveals the Cognizer's relation to the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Content may be understood as pertaining to a Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the state of awareness holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The Cognizer's source of awareness about the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Cognizer knows or thinks something.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The category within which the Content is known.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Cognizer has awareness (or lack thereof) of the Content.

annotations.definition: Some Content is part of the mental model of a Cognizer. The Cognizer arrives at the mental model not by direct perception but by reasoning from perceived phenomena. Alternatively, the Role of a particular object is known to be applicable to that object.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person who has (or lacks) awareness of the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The fact or idea which the Cognizer is aware of.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the state of awareness holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The Cognizer's source of awareness about the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The category whose applicability to the Content is known.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Cognizer has awareness (or lack thereof) of the Content.

annotations.definition: This frame covers a step in a criminal process in which a Judge decides whether the Accused should be released on bail, and, if so, what the amount of the bail should be.

frameElementDefinitions.Accused: At the time of bail setting, the Accused is held without bail/bond, released on his/her own recognizance, or required to give the Court a specific amount of money for bail.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The Judge, as head of the Court, determines the Accused's bail.
frameElementDefinitions.Status: Status idenitifies the decision made with regard to bail. The possible statuses are "without bail/bond", "on one's own recognizance", or a specific amount of money required for bail.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the bail setting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the bail setting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the bail setting which is not covered by more specific FEs, including, secondary effects (openly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Judge that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The method used by the Judge to set the bail.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Judge sets bail.

annotations.definition: A piece of information about a Landmark is presented as more accessible and useful for determining or communicating a similar type of information about the Trajector. In this construal of a situation, there is a (more) profiled element, the Trajector, and an un- or less profiled element, the Landmark. In combination with a schematically-defined Profiled_region (with a pre-defined relationship to the Landmark), the Landmark serves as the basis for understanding the status or location of the Trajector.

frameElementDefinitions.Trajector: The entity whose status or location is (generally) unknown, and whose status or location is established by examining the Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark: The entity, generally presupposed, whose status or location is known or knowable and which thus serves as a basis for understanding the status or location of the Trajector by locating it in a Profiled_region which has a particular relation to the Landmark.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The region which has a particular shape, orientation, size, and location (literal or metaphorical) with respect to the Landmark. The shape of the Profiled_region is virtually always incorporated in the target. This shaped region usually includes the location of the Landmark, as seen with in.prep, at.prep, on.prep, but may also do exactly the opposite, as with away from.prep.

annotations.definition: Two (or more) people (the Parties, also encodable as Party_1 and Party_2) have an agreement. Both sides are construed as having a commitment to an Obligation--the relationship is understood to be symmetrical or reciprocal. Instead of a specific mention of the Obligation, a Topic expression may be used to indicate the domain covered.

frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: The individual or individuals that form the more prominent (i.e. agentive) party of the agreement, as compared with Party_2. That is, Party_1 is generally encoded as the External Argument of verbs, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to Party_1. Thus Manner is construed to be that of Party_1, not necessarily that of Party_2 or the group of Parties as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: The semantically (and grammatically) less prominent participant (or group of participants) in the agreement. It is generally encoded within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by with).
frameElementDefinitions.Obligation: An expression of the commitment of the Parties .
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is a description of the domain covered by the agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: As with other frames in the Communication domain, the Medium of communication may be expressed. Medium is the physical entity or channel used to record or negotiate the Obligation.
frameElementDefinitions.Parties: The group of individuals portrayed as equally involved in having an agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where an agreement was made.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the agreement was made.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the agreement not specified by the other FEs, including particularly salient characteristics of the parties.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes a characteristic or description of the agreement_on_action.

annotations.definition: The Cognizers have a similarity (or dissimilarity) in their Opinion. The Cognizers may be expressed separately, with Cognizer_2 being the basis for establishing the Opinion of Cognizer_1. Rather than a specific Opinion, a phenomenon about which a similar or differing Opinion is held, the Topic, may be specified. A specific Opinion may also go unmentioned when the Opinions of the Cognizers are understood as answers to a Question.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizers: This FE marks constituents that express the set of Cognizers whose similarity of opinion is at issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer_1: When there is an asymmetry, Cognizer_1 is the individual characterized by their similarity in opinion to Cognizer_2, whose opinion is assumed to be known. Cognizer_1 is often an external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer_2: When the Cognizers are expressed separately, Cognizer_2 is the one whose opinion is assumed to be known; it serves as a basis for establishing the opinion of Cognizer_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Question: This FE denotes an implicit question in response to which the Cognizers give their Opinions, which are then assessed for their similarity. The Question may concern the identitiy of a particular entity, as in the first example below, or it may be of the yes/no-type, as in the second example below.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Cognizers are similar in their Opinions.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Cognizers are in agreement or disagreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the general area in which the Cognizers are in agreement or disagreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE is for descriptions of the agreement state not covered by more specific FEs, especially characterizations of the state as a whole or of one of the Cognizers or Cognizer_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the agreement or disagreement logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describing the state of the Cognizers
frameElementDefinitions.Opinion: A state of affairs believed by the Cognizers.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: A phenomenon that the Cognizers are considering with respect to their Opinion.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state-of-affiars under which aggreement occurs (as independent of the act of agreement).

annotations.definition: A Protagonist has become aware of a possible Danger (a dangerous event or a Dangerous_entity that might participate in an event harmful to the Protagonist) and is ready to engage in an Activity in response to that Danger.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: This FE identifies the Protagonist who is prepared to undertake some action in response to a Danger.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The action that the Protagonist is prepared to do.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Protagonist is on alert to the potential Danger.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state or event that follows due to the ready state.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Protagonist remains ready to perform the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Protagonist is wary of the possible Danger and ready to perform an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Danger: A dangerous event or situation which teh Protagonist is aware of and is ready to respond to.
frameElementDefinitions.Dangerous_entity: An entity of which the Protagonist is aware and which poses a danger.

annotations.definition: A Part is considered to be a member of the set described by the Total. The relationship between Part and Total subset-superset rather than part-whole (as it is in the frames Part_whole and Including).

frameElementDefinitions.Total: The complex multi-part entity of which a constituent Part is specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: A subset of the Total.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE identifies the frequency with which the Part is included in the Total.
frameElementDefinitions.Contrast_set: This FE indicates other totalities that the Part may also be a subset of.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: This FE indicates the sub-location of the Total in which the Part is included. [MAY BE UNNECESSARY]
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the inclusion event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the subset relation holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place at which the subset relation holds.

annotations.definition: A Target_symbol in a Target_representation system is presented as equivalent to a Source_symbol in a Source_representation system in the ability to express a particular Content.

frameElementDefinitions.Source_symbol: A symbol or set of symbols representing the Content in the Source_representation format.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_representation: The representation system in which the Content pre-exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the translation equivalence holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the set of conditions in which the translation is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_symbol: A symbol or set of symbols representing the Content in the Target_representation format.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_representation: The representation system within which the Target_symbol is equivalent in its signification to the Source_symbol within the Source_representation system .
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The information that is meant to be represented by the Source_symbol and Target_symbol.
frameElementDefinitions.Equivalence: An assessment of how equivalent the Source_representation and the Target_representation are.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: A text in which the Target_symbol is used to render the Content of a Source_symbol.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist has (or does not have) a Weapon. The Degree to which the Protagonist is armed may also be described. The Protagonist is normally a human but may be a device that has subsidiary weapons.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of Weapons that the Protagonist has (relative to some standard).
frameElementDefinitions.Weapon: The destructive device that the Protagonist has with them.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The individual that possesses a Weapon.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where a Protagonist carries a Weapon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Protagonist carries a Weapon.

annotations.definition: A Winner has victory over an Loser.

frameElementDefinitions.Winner: The individual or individuals who beat the Loser.
frameElementDefinitions.Score: A quantity whereby progress is measured during the game, and Rank and Prizes are awarded to the Winner afterward.
frameElementDefinitions.Margin: The amount by which the Winner 's and Loser 's Scores differ.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: The rank (according to Score) that a Winner acheives.
frameElementDefinitions.Prize: A socially and/or monetarily significant entity which is given to a Winner according to Score or Rank.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Winner or Loser that makes the beating event possible.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Winner or Loser which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of a Winner or Loser that also affect their action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Winner has victory over the Loser.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Winner has victory Loser.
frameElementDefinitions.Venue: This FE is for the named venue of a competition, particularly when the place has been built for the purpose of a competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why the Winner has victory over the Loser.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when winning events began to be repeated to when they stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Loser: The individual or individuals that get beat by the Winner.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the Winner beats the Loser.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state-of-affairs that prevails as a result of the Winner beating the Loser.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times the Winner beats the Loser.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the winning event operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness, or a description of the state-of-affairs introduced by the winning event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A qualitative measure of how much the Winner beat the Loser.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Competition: The event in which the Competition competes.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Winner beats the Loser.

annotations.definition: An Entity ends up with some Final_quality--a new fact about the Entity. Alternatively, based on a cluster of changes of characteristics, the Entitynewly meets the conditions for being a member of a Final_category.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: >When the change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: How long the Entity remains in the Final_category, Final_situation, or Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In cases where the Entity is intentional, Manner may indicate salient characteristics of an intentional Entity that also affect the event (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity which undergoes a change, newly ending up in the Final_category or taking on a new Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_quality: A description of a characteristic of the Entity after the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_category: The category that the Entity ends up in after the change. Typically, this entails taking on a number of new characteristics.
frameElementDefinitions.Transitional_period: The period during which the Entity is in transition.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: This FE describes the state of the Entity before change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE marks the set of conditions under which the Entity enters the Final_category or takes on the characteristic of the Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_category: This FE describes the category of the Entity before a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: A description of the kind of entities associated with instances of the Entity and saliently affected by its change.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Any eventuality or entity which brings about the change of the Entity.

annotations.definition: A New_member becomes a member of a socially-constructed Group.

frameElementDefinitions.New_member: The New_member becomes a member of a (socially-defined) Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the joining occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the New_member joins the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the New_member joins the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the New_member becomes part of the group.
