frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a New_member that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the New_member becomes a member of a Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group is a socially-constructed entity composed of members.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A factor that explains why the New_member joins the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The capacity or role in which the New_member is placed upon joining the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the New_member when they join.
frameElementDefinitions.Reciprocation: This FE denotes an event paired with that of the main clause, which explains why the event of the main clause is expected. In particular, reciprocation is used to indicate an action that is being rewarded or punished in the main clause, or an action that is paired with the action in the main clause as the other side of an agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action.

annotations.definition: An Item comes to be attached to a Goal, with a Connector forming a bond between the Handle of the Item and the Goal (or some part of it). Alternatively, multiple Items may form a bond between each other.

frameElementDefinitions.Connector: Connector identifies the instrument used to attach, usually in a with-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Handle: Handle identifies the point of attachment on the Goal to which the Item attaches.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: Item identifies the entity that comes to be attached to the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Items: Items identifies the plural items that are tied together, resulting in their mutual attachment.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal identifies the location to which the first Item is attached.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an attaching event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Item comes to be attached to a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the attaching event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the attaching event takes place.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame have to do with a Cognizer adding some Phenomenon to their model of the world. They are similar to Coming-to-believe words, except the latter generally involve reasoning from Evidence.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person who becomes aware of a Phenomenon. It is normally expressed as an External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: Thm Phenomenon is the entity or situation in the world of which a Cognizer becomes aware. It is normally expressed as an Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground is the background or context against which a Cognizer becomes aware of a Phenomenon. It is normally expressed by a locative PP:
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State indicates the state or situation of the Phenomenon at the time when the Cognizer becomes aware of it. It is normally expressed by a Predicate expression (e.g. a PP or a participial VP):
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The Evidence is some fact that allows the Cognizer to become aware of something. It is normally expressed with PP-from:
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which becoming aware is occurring.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifes the Manner in which becoming aware occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifes the Means by which the Cognizer becomes aware of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic indicates the general field within which the Phenomenon is found.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Cognizer becomes aware of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal that the Cognizer has in mind, which they believe to be accomplishable by finding the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Cognizer uses an Instrument to (enable themselves to) become aware of the Phenomenon. In some cases, the Instrument may appear in subject position.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: his frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE is minimally distinct from Duration and Duration_of final_state in that the clause described is conveyed to be iterative.

annotations.definition: The Becoming_detached frame covers two situations: a scene in which one thing comes to be physically detached from something else; or a scene in which two things come to be disconnected from each other. In the first, the frame includes an Item that detaches from a Source, creating an asymmetric relationship between the Item and the Source. In the second, the Items detach from each other, where each serves as a Source relative to the other, creating a symmetric relationship between the two Items. The Items that were formerly attached re-appear as the two separate or potentially separate entities that they were throughout.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: This FE identifies the Item that detaches from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source identifies the location to which an Item was attached before getting detached.
frameElementDefinitions.Items: This FE identifies the plural Items that are becoming detached from each other, resulting in their separation. Notice that when the FE Items occurs, neither Item nor Source can occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the detaching event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Item becomes detached.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Item becomes detached from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Item ends up detached.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole.

annotations.definition: An Entity loses moisture with the outcome of being in a dry state.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity is the thing that loses moisture and becomes dry.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The intensity with which the Entity becomes dry.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Entity becomes dry.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity becomes dry.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Entity becomes dry.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The situation in which the Entity is becoming dry.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: This FE indicates that the visiting is a particular instance in a series of repeated visiting events.

annotations.definition: A Whole separates into Parts, or one part of a whole, called Part_1, becomes separate from the remaining portion, Part_2.

frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole is a single entity or an aggregate of entities which separates into Parts.
frameElementDefinitions.Parts: This refers collectively to the Parts resulting from separation of a Whole:
frameElementDefinitions.Part_1: Part-1 is the first of two Parts mentioned.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_2: Part_2 is the second of two Parts mentioned. This is usually a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Criterion: This frame element expresses a property of the Parts or components of the Whole which correlates with the separation. Criterion is typically expressed as a PP headed by by, or as a complex PP of the form on the basis of....
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the undergoers of the separation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intermediate action or event that enables the separation.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the separation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the separation occurs.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Speaker ceases speaking, sometimes about a particular Topic. An Expressor may also be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker has previously been speaking but has now stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject which the Speaker had been speaking about.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location where the becoming silent occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the becoming silent occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Expressor is the Speaker's voice or other communicative noise emitted by the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the quietening occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: An Entity that is previously not visible to a Perceiver becomes visible.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The thing that the Perceiver is able to see or recognize.
frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The person who is able to see the Entity. This FE is often null-instantiated as it is deprofiled in this scene.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of how the Entity becomes visible that is not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The physical location in which the Entity becomes visible.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A temporal description of the event of the Entity becoming visible.

annotations.definition: Artists work at various tasks that further the completion of a film, play, television show, or other similar Production. Artists never appear on stage or in front of a camera. Some (e.g., a director) may interact with the actors (on-screen participants) of the Production. Often times a particular Production is made for distribution by a Distributor, by a particular Studio, or in a specific Medium. The Artists may be categorized into Type or work in specific Medium.

frameElementDefinitions.Artist: The Artist works off-stage to help make the Production.
frameElementDefinitions.Production: The Production is the final product put out by the off-stage (and on-stage) participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An Artist may be categorizedy by Type.
frameElementDefinitions.Distributor: Often (especially with film or television) the Production is advertised and transmitted to the audience by the Distributor.
frameElementDefinitions.Studio: This FE indicates the particular orgainzaion that makes the Production.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This FE indicates the Medium in which a particular Artist works or a Production is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the time when the Production is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE indicates the location of production.
frameElementDefinitions.Nationality: This FE indicates the Nationality, ethnicity, or primary working location of the Artist.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which an Artist works on a Production.
frameElementDefinitions.Performer: In screen performances, the Performer is filmed as he or she acts out a role with respect to the larger Production
frameElementDefinitions.Score: The music which may be performed in the performance of the Production, either in isolation or accompanying actions and speech.
frameElementDefinitions.Scene: A Scene is a sub-part of a larger production. In film, scenes are sometimes recorded independently, and later edited into the final movie.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Artist creates the Production.

annotations.definition: An Agent is described as pursuing an Activity, expending some effort. The Agent may be active in a particular Role. Alternatively, the Activity itself may be described as having an Agent who is expending effort. In some cases, the Activity may involve a Salient_entity which may stand in for it.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the amount of Time for which an Activity is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE identifies the name of the ongoing Activity in which an Agent engages.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Activity is pursued.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent is engaged in an ongoing Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Activity is pursued.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Activity which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that affect the action (presumptuously, deliberately).
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent engages in an ongoing Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: An entity acted upon by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: This FE identifies the role filled by the Agent which is saliently associated with an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Agent or Salient_entity is active.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole. There are two somewhat distinct subgroups of sentence modifiers that cover this function.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often. It is to be distinguished from Iterations, which pertains simply to the number of times an event occurs-Iteration expressions answer the question how many times.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.

annotations.definition: An Asset is in a state where it is exposed to or otherwise liable to be affected by a Harmful_event, which may be metonymically evoked by reference to a Dangerous_entity. Words expressing relative safety (i.e., lack of risk) are also in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Asset: Something judged to be desirable or valuable which might be lost or damaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the actual level of security from the expected value given the Asset and the state indicated by the target itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Harmful_event: An action that may occur or a state which may hold which could result in the loss of or damage to the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Dangerous_entity: A concrete or abstract entity which may come to cause the loss of, or damage to the Asset either due to its participation in a Harmful_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A particular location in which the Asset is secure. Frequently, it is to be inferred that the characteristics of the location protect the Asset from a potential Harmful_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Asset enjoys the specified level of security from a Harmful_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the state of security or insecurity of the Asset logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation under which the Asset is safe or unsafe.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain in which the Asset has a degree of safety.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.

annotations.definition: An Item is attached by a Handle, via a Connector, to a Goal, or Items are attached to each other. There may be a Dependent_state, which depends crucially on Item(s) being attached.

frameElementDefinitions.Connector: Connector identifies the instrument used to attach and is usually expressed in a with-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Handle: Handle identifies the point of attachment on the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: Item identifies the entity that comes to be attached to the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Items: Items identifies the plural items that are tied together, resulting in their mutual attachment.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal identifies the location to which the Item(s) is attached.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_state: Dependent_state identifies the state of the Item or Items which depends on the existence of the attachment.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Item is in an attached state to a Goal, or two Items are attached to each other.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist is awake at some Time and Place. This entails the Protagonist having a higher general level of awareness of the surroundings than when asleep.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The entity that is not sleeping.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Protagonist is awake.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Protagonist is awake.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Protagonist is awake.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Protagonist being awake which is not covered by more specific FEs including secondary effects (quietly, noisily), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).

annotations.definition: This frame concerns the inception of the life of a Child, generally human, at a particular Time and Place. The Relatives may also be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Child: Child identifies the new self-motile creature.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the birth occurs, generally expressed as a date or a year.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Child enters the world.
frameElementDefinitions.Relatives: The individuals that the Child is related to, including the male and female individuals that come together to produce a Child (the parents), as well those who are related to them by birth or marriage.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Child as it enters the world.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The way in which the Child leaves the womb; this FE is typically only filled by phrases such as by natural birth or by Caesarian.

annotations.definition: In this situation, a Trajector is contained inside a Landmark. This frame serves only to relate the Containing frame (with LUs such as contain.v) with the Interior_profile_relation frame (with LUs such as inside.prep).

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The interior region of the Landmark, within which the Trajector is to be found.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark: The container which serves as a reference point for the Trajector which is surrounded by it.
frameElementDefinitions.Trajector: The entity which is to be located with respect to the Landmark.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Trajector is contained within the Landmark.

annotations.definition: An Item is detached from a Source, or Items are detached from each other.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: Item identifies the entity that is detached from a Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Items: Items identifies the plural items that are detached from each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source identifies the location from which the Item(s) is detached.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Item is in an detached state relative to a Source, or two Items are detached from each other.

annotations.definition: An Item is in a state of dryness.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: The Item exists in a state of dryness.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Item is dry.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: Subregion identifies the affected part of the larger whole.

annotations.definition: An Employee has a Position doing work in a particular Field, or doing work on a particular Task, for which an Employer gives Compensation to the Employee.

frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Employee receives for performing a Task.
frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The Employee is obligated to perform a Task in order to receive Compensation.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The Employer gives Compensation to an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Employee works for an Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Place_of_employment: This FE identifies the Place_of_employment of the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Employee is in employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: The Employee is obligated to perform the Task to recieve Compensation from the Employer .
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time period during which an Employee works for an Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: This FE identifies the Field in which one works.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the amount of Time for which the Employee continues being employed.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: Rank is the position in a hierarchy that an Employee occupies within the organization of the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies the Type of the profession.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis describes the conditions of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period, or payment basis.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Employee works.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Employee works.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE gives a description of the Being_employed event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: A Theme is held in captivity by an Agent or Cause.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity which is confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Holding_location: The area within which the Theme is confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent prevents the Theme from moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the general Place where the Theme is held captive.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Theme is held captive.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the state of being captive which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Theme is held captive.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Theme remains in captivity.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An inanimate force which causes the Theme in be unable to move.

annotations.definition: An Item belongs to a Category, as (often implicitly) conceived of by a Cognizer.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: This is the person who performs an act of categorization. Typically, this frame element is expressed as an oblique.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: This is the entity which is is an instance of a particular Category. Typically, the Item is expressed as the external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Category: This frame element expresses a general type or class of which the Item is considered an instance. Typically, it is expressed in a prepositional phrase headed by as or under.
frameElementDefinitions.Criteria: This frame element expresses properties of the Item which is used as a basis for categorization. Criteria are the general dimensions along which Items can potentially differ from one another and hence, fall into different Categorys. For example, a paint sample can be classified by color or by finish (matte or gloss); if they are classified by color, one can be classified as orange and another as red. The Criteria FE is typically expressed as a PP headed by by, or as a complex PP of the form on the basis of...:
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which an Item (metaphorically) is construed as being in a Category.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act or state of affairs by which the Item (perhaps metaphorically) is interpreted as being in a Category.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate when a certain Categorization holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the set of conditions in which the categorization holds.

annotations.definition: A Controlling_entity has control of a Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable.

frameElementDefinitions.Controlling_entity: A thing or person whose behavior controls a Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_entity: An entity, usually a thing, that is influenced in its behavior by a Controlling_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_situation: A situation whose temporal or other characteristics are influenced by a Controlling_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_variable: A scalar variable, whose value depends on the behavior of a Controlling_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Controlling_entity effects the Dependent_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the controlling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the controlling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Controlling_entity that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.

annotations.definition: A particular Binding_principle is (or is not) operative, that is, any obligations, restrictions, and any other aspects of the Binding_principle are (or are not) in effect. The Binding_principle can be expressed as being in effect for a particular Duration, or at a particular Time or Place, or under certain Circumstances.

frameElementDefinitions.Binding_principle: The Binding_principle is the law, contract, principle, or other (set of) obligations that is (or is not) operational.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Binding_principle is or is not in effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Binding_principle is in effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place at which the Binding_principle is or is not operational.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state-of-affairs under which the Binding_principle is/isn't operational.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A cause, reason, or other Explanation for the status of the Binding_principle
frameElementDefinitions.Obligated_party: The individual or group who is obligated to follow the Binding_principle.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: A Device or machine is in (or out of) service. Note that being broken or functional is a separate issue from being turned on or off. This latter notion is what the present frame is concerned with. The Time when and the Place where the Device is in use may be specified, as may be the Purpose a backgrounded human User had in turning the Device on or off.

frameElementDefinitions.Device: The Device or machine that is in or out of operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when a Device is in or out of operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place in which the Device is or is not operating.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end.

annotations.definition: A Prisoner is punished for committing a Crime by being confined to a Prison for a specified period of time.

frameElementDefinitions.Prisoner: The Prisoner is held in custody as a punishment for having committed a crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Prison: The facility where the Prisoner is held.
frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities hold the Prisoner in custody.
frameElementDefinitions.Crime: The crime of which the Prisoner is guilty, resulting in imprisonment.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the general Place where the Prisoner is held captive.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Prisoner is in prison.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the state of being imprisoned which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Prisoner is in custody.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Prisoner remains in jail.

annotations.definition: A Part is profiled as being a subset or constituent subpart of a Whole. This is in contrast to Inclusion frame, wherein the same relation is seen from the point of view of the Whole.

frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole is the larger entity that includes the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: The Part is either a subset or a constituent part of the Whole.

annotations.definition: A Theme is in a stable position with respect to a Location.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: An entity which is at a particular Location
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location (generally presumed to be known to the interlocutor) with respect to which the location of the Theme can be understood.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_state: A state (of the Theme) that results as a consequence of the Theme being situated at the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Theme is at the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: A salient entity that the Theme is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: This frame element is used to denote phrases that specifically entail an entity which is conceived as moving relative to another entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A Depictive expression describes a state or condition of the Theme independent of its location.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns entities conventionally being referred to by particular names.

frameElementDefinitions.Name: Name identifies the linguistic expression used to refer to Entities.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity is the person the speaker intends to refer to by using a particular name.
frameElementDefinitions.Language: Language is used for a person or thing to whose name the Name of the Entity is taken or in honor of.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The FE Type denotes sub-type of names.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Describes the Name in some manner.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: The context is the region or domain in which the name applies.
frameElementDefinitions.Etymological_root: The source language or inspiration for the Name.

annotations.definition: A Dependent state-of-affairs has a Requirement as a prerequisite for obtaining or occurring.

frameElementDefinitions.Dependent: The state-of-affairs which cannot hold without the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Requirement: The entity or state-of-affairs that must be present or obtain in order for the Dependent to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Dependent depends on the presence of the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Requirement is necessary for the Dependent.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: The conditions under which the the Requirement is necessary.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The field of endeavor whose nature imposes the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place denotes a location in which the specified relation between Requirement and Dependent holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_individual: The individual who needs to have the Required_entity or for the Requirement state of affairs to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Required_entity: An entity that has to be present for the Dependent state of affairs to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: An characteristic or feature of the Requirement or Required_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: Under some Condition, usually left implicit, a Responsible_party is required to perform some Duty. If they do not perform the Duty, there may be some undesirable Consequence, which may or may not be stated overtly.

frameElementDefinitions.Responsible_party: The person who must perform the Duty.
frameElementDefinitions.Duty: The action that the Responsible_party is obligated to perform.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: This FE indicates the Conditions under which the Responsible_party is obligated to perform the Duty.
frameElementDefinitions.Consequence: The Consequence will result if the Duty is not performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Responsibly_party is obligated to fulfill the Duty.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A location in which the Responsible_party has a specified Duty .
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: Under some Condition, usually left implicit, a Duty needs to be fulfilled by a Responsible_party. If the Duty is not performed, there may be some undesirable Consequence for the Responsible_party, which may or may not be stated overtly. Compare this frame to the Being_obligated frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Condition: This FE indicates the Condition under which the Duty is required of the Responsible_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Consequence: The Consequence will result if the Duty is not performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Duty: The action that the Responsible_party is obligated to perform.
frameElementDefinitions.Responsible_party: The person who must perform the Duty.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Duty is mandatory.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A location in which the Duty obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The positive consequence of the Duty being fulfilled.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why the Duty is necessary for the Responsible_party.

annotations.definition: An Object with an understood function is or is not in a state ready to perform that function.

frameElementDefinitions.Object: The entity with an understood purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Artifact exceeds or falls short of the minimum requirements for being operational.

annotations.definition: This frame describes the experience of expecting and bearing a child, from the perspective of the parent(s).

frameElementDefinitions.Mother: This frame element refers to the parent who is physically pregnant.
frameElementDefinitions.Father: This frame element refers to a parent who contributed to the conception of a child but is not physically carrying a child.
frameElementDefinitions.Parents: This frame element refers to the collective body expecting or carrying a child.
frameElementDefinitions.Offspring: This frame refers to the conceived offspring borne by the Parents.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This frame element refers to the length of time the Parents have been pregnant.

annotations.definition: In this frame, an Entity or Behavior is suspicious or otherwise subject to question. The general implication is that the Entity or Behavior provides evidence for some (as yet unproven) socially undesirable conclusion about a (usually implicit) Social_actor. (Such conclusions often include the idea that some individual is lying, or that a crime was committed.)

frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The Behavior is the state of affairs or action of the social actor that is regarded as susipcious.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Behavior is suspicious.
frameElementDefinitions.Social_actor: An individual that is suspected of wrongdoing because of the Behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity metonymically stands in for a Behavior that saliently involves the Entity.

annotations.definition: A Phenomenon is related to some cognitive action, the Endeavor, of a Cognizer. In many LUs, the Cognizer is null-instantiated and implied as being the speaker or another participant of the Endeavor.

frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: Something that is profiled in the mind of the Cognizer as being related to mental Endeavors.
frameElementDefinitions.Endeavor: A cognitive action (eg discussion, decision) performed by the Cognizer to which the Phenomenon is connected in some way.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: A participant in the mental Endeavor to which the Phenomenon is related. This frame element is often null-instantiated and may be inferred from the Endeavor.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A description of how related the Phenomenon is to the Endeavor.
frameElementDefinitions.Specification: The knowledge or situation that mediates the relevance of the Phenomenon to the Endeavor.

annotations.definition: An organic item, the Patient, has undergone the natural process of rotting and decaying with a possible expression of the Degree to which the rotting has occurred.

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the organic matter that has decayed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which rotting has occurred.

annotations.definition: An Entity meets the pre-conditions for participating in an Activity that is judged to be difficult for it to participate in, either due to inherent properties of the Entity or temporary Circumstances. A Degree modifier may indicate by how much the Entity exceeds or falls short of the minimum requirements.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity whose meeting or falling short of pre-conditions is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity that the Entity may participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Entity exceeds or falls short of the minimum requirements for participating in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Entity meets or fails to meet the pre-conditions.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An entity, event, or state that causes the Entity to meet or fail to meet the pre-conditions for participating in an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Relevant_feature: A characteristic or behavior of the Entity that is considered when judging whether the Entity might participate in the Activity.

annotations.definition: An Item is in a state of wetness with the possibility of the wetting Liquid being mentioned, along with the Degree of wetness.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: The Item takes up the Liquid through its pores.
frameElementDefinitions.Liquid: The Liquid is a fluid or gas that permeates the Item. It most frequently occurs in a PP Complement headed by with.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Item is wet.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation brings about the state of wetness, and is usually expressed in a from-prepositional phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.

annotations.definition: An Assailant, a group of armed forces, surrounds the Victim, a military location and the people within it, in an attempt to cut the Victim off from the outside, with the goal of either forcing their surrender or weakening them so that an invasion would be successful. A successful siege is often followed by the Assailant taking control of (the land held by) the Victim.

frameElementDefinitions.Assailant: The Assailant is a group of armed forces (or, metonymically, their commander or the organization they represent) that encircles, surrounds, or otherwise cuts off the Beseiged in an attempt to force surrender.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the fortification or geopolitical entity that is being attacked.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Assailant that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Assailant that accomplishes the assault.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Assailant makes an assault on the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Assailant attacks the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The starting-point of the motion of the Assailant or projectiles of the Assailant as they move toward the the Place that the Victim is occupying in the attack. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Assailant attacks the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Weapon: An entity used by the Assailant to cause damage to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the Assailant travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Victim is under attack from the Assailant

annotations.definition: Compared to its Competitors, a Standard is at the high end of a scale with respect to some Variable. The Competitors are frequently not realized syntactically.

frameElementDefinitions.Standard: The Standard is the entity that is located closest to the end of the scale evoked by some Variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Variable: The Variable is a property that evokes a scale along which the Standard is located closest to one extreme in comparison to its Competitors.
frameElementDefinitions.Competitors: The FE Competitors denotes the entities that are exceeded by the Standard with regard to the Variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The spatial or temporal context in which the Standard is not matched by any Competitors.

annotations.definition: A Billing_authority communicates in written form that the Debtor owes the Owed_party (often the same as the Billing_authority) some amount of Money for certain Goods (including services) provided by the Owed_party or to clear a debt for a Bad_action by the Debtor against the Owed_party.

frameElementDefinitions.Debtor: The person (or organization) that owes the Owed_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor or time, for example) which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the billing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction, or as recompense for the Bad_action.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Owed_party: The Owed_party expects a specific amount of Money from the Debtor, either because the Debtor received Goods or because the Debtor performed a Bad_action against the Owed_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by or per-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the billing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Seller that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Billing_authority: The individual who communicates to the Debtor the Money that they owe.
frameElementDefinitions.Payment_status: Describes the relation between the invoice and the associated payment: unpaid, paid, overdue, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Bad_action: An earlier action by the Debtor causing harm (directly or indirectly) to the Owed_party, for which the Owed_party expects compensation, and therefore bills the Debtor.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that denote large ecological areas as well as smaller locations characterized by the type of life present--in other words, geography locations as defined by biota.

frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: Container_possessor is the location of which the Locale is a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Formational_cause brings about the features of the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Locale: This FE identifies a stable bounded area, and is typically the designation of the nouns of Locale-derived frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: This FE indicates the Names of Locales.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: Relative_location identifies a place in respect to which a Locale is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description, characteristic, or property of the Locale which is not covered by more specific FEs.

annotations.definition: This frame has to do with scientific taxonomy. The lexical units in this frame include the seven major classifications in which organisms are grouped based on common biological characteristics. These classifications are labeled Rank in this frame. The Members or Subtype of the Rank is often expressed with the use of the target-denoting noun.

frameElementDefinitions.Rank: The Rank is the taxonomic category to which the Member belongs. It is also always the target-denoting noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Member: The living organism which belongs to the expressed Rank.
frameElementDefinitions.Property: A property of the Member of the Rank.

annotations.definition: The LUs in this frame refer to biological entities labeled by the FE Organism. An Organism is described as something that can be alive, or have naturally occuring biological processes and functions; however the concept of life is often used metaphorically for non-organic entities which resemble or act as if they have organic life.

frameElementDefinitions.Organism: An organic that has naturally occuring bioligical processes and functions.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterization of the Organism not covered by other FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The location from which the Organism originated.

annotations.definition: A trigger mechanism brings about a change in a biological system.

frameElementDefinitions.Trigger: The force that brings about the change in the Systemic_effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Systemic_effect: The biological entity that is affected by the Trigger.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Location where the Systemic_effect takes place.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer is in a State where a biological urge is signaling the need to perform a certain action such as eating, drinking, stopping exertion, sleeping, regurgitating, or having sex. The Duration, Time, Place, or Degree of intensity associated with the occurrence of the urge may also be indicated. In this frame, the Experiencer does not necessarily want to perform the action that would fulfill the urge, and there is no specific focus associated with the urge.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree identifies the extent to which the Experiencer receives the biological signal.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer receives the biological signal and possesses the internal experience which is a reaction to the phsyical signal.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Experiencer feels the biological signal. Time may also allow inference about the Reason for the biological urge.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place is where the Experiencer feels the biological signal.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: Expressor is used for a gesture, expression, or body part indicating that the Experiencer is feeling a certain urge.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration identifies the amount of time for which the Experiencer feels the urge.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Experiencer has the biological urge.
frameElementDefinitions.State: State identifies the Experiencer's state of being.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: Parameter is the particular biological realm in which the Experiencer feels the urge.

annotations.definition: An Offspring is born to a Mother and Father, collectively referred to as Parents. This event happens at a particular Time and Place.

frameElementDefinitions.Offspring: The living being that is produced by the Parents.
frameElementDefinitions.Mother: The female being that produces the Offspring.
frameElementDefinitions.Father: The male being that aids in the production of the Offspring.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the birth of the Offspring takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Offspring is born.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the birthing event takes place.

annotations.definition: A Traveller boards a Vehicle that they intend to use as a means of transportation either as a passenger or as a driver.

frameElementDefinitions.Traveller: The Traveller is the entity which boards the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Vehicle is the intended means of conveyance of the Traveller.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion outside of or off the Vehicle expresses the frame element Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes a property of the motion not related to its trajectory.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path describes the trajectory of the Traveller's motion onto the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The amount of time the Traveller spends on the Vehicle after boarding it.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Traveller or Vehicle during the boarding.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the boarding of the Vehicle takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: A theme that rides in the same Vehicle as the main Traveller. This may be an entity the Traveller transports or a co-traveller moving under their own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Traveller boards the vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Traveller boards the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns the decorations that human beings have on or apply to their bodies.

frameElementDefinitions.Body_location: This FE identifies the location on the body where the decoration takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Decorated_individual: This is the individual whose body is decorated.
frameElementDefinitions.Decoration: Decoration is the decorative item that adorns the body.
frameElementDefinitions.Decoration_descriptor: Dec_desc identifies a descriptor of the Decoration.
frameElementDefinitions.Picture: This is the frame element that describes what shape the Decoration represents.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: This FE identifies the Use to which a body decoration is put.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type designates different kinds of body decorations. Often this FE will label part of a multi-word or compound word target.

annotations.definition: This frame covers descriptions of an entire human body, viewed as a gestalt (as opposed to descriptions based on salient body parts). The Frame Elements are Individual and Figure.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which a body has a particular characteristic.
frameElementDefinitions.Individual: This FE identifies the individual (or individuals) being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: This FE identifies the Figure or form being described by the target.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with words that describe a person based on a salient part of their body.

frameElementDefinitions.Individual: This FE identifies the Individual (or individuals) being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: This FE identifies the Figure or form being described by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the body description applies.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Body_mark is present on a Possessor's body. It may be located at an Attachment and have a Descriptor. A Cause may also be indicated. It may be a temporary condition or a permanent one.

frameElementDefinitions.Attachment: The Attachment is the larger body part to which the Body_mark is attached, or of which it is a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This Frame Element is used for a characteristic or description of the Body_mark. it identifies a modifier of its head noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: This Frame Element denotes the Possessor of the Body_mark.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the event that brings about the presence of the Body_mark.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: A specific kind of the particular Body_mark.
frameElementDefinitions.Constituents: Salient constituents that make up the Body_mark.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for motions or actions an Agent performs using some part of his/her body. A number of words in this frame occur as blends with Communication, in which the action has an Addressee. For example,

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent uses some part of his/her body to perform the action. Agent occurs as the External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: With some verbs in this frame, the Body_part involved in the action is specified by the meaning of the verb and cannot be expressed separately. For example,
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: Body movement may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause, the Agent's mental or emotional state. Internal_cause is expressed in a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.External_cause: Cause is something in the external environment that causes the Agent to move part of his/her body. Cause is usually expressed in a PP Complement:.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: Addressee only occurs in the Body-movement frame in sentences which are blends with Communication. Where it occurs, it is typically a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is the place from which the Body_part is moved.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path identifies the trajectory of motion of the Body_part without specifying a start or end point.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is any expression that identifies the endpoint of movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: Area identifies any expression describing a region in which the motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path. (This FE should be contrasted with Place.)
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the body movement occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: This FE identifies the Message that the body movement conveys.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the body movement occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: This FE specifies the subregion of the body that is involved with movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose of the body movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognate_event: This FE identifies the event noun of the verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time during which the movement of a Body_part occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location within which the movement of a Body_part takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: An alternative description of the Body_movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Coordinated_event: An event that the Agent perceives and responds to by moving in coordination with it.

annotations.definition: This frame covers words for Body_part(s) (BP) belonging to a Possessor (Poss), which may be characterized by a Descriptor (Desc). The location of the BP may be identified in terms of its Attachment or its Orientational_location. A Subregion of a BP may also be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: This Frame Element denotes the Possessor of the Body_part.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This Frame Element is used for a characteristic or description of the Body_part. Note that Descriptor is not an FE of the same character as other FEs. Specifically, rather than filling a slot, it identifies a modifier of its head noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: This Frame Element is the Body_part.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: This FEidentifies the Subregion of the Body_part referred to by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Orientational_location: This Frame Element describes the location of the Body_part with respect to the inherent orientation of the body (or the larger body part) the Body_part is attached to.
frameElementDefinitions.Attachment: The Attachment is the larger body part to which the BP is attached.

annotations.definition: A Financial_instrument (a bond or insurance) fulfills a condition at a certain Time after which the Issuer of the Financial_instrument is legally obligated to pay the Holder a monetary Value, based on the instrument's original Principle and the Interest rate.

frameElementDefinitions.Financial_instrument: The bond, insurance, or debt security which a Holder buys from an Issuer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Financial_instrument reaches maturity.
frameElementDefinitions.Issuer: The entity which is obligated to pay the Holder after the Financial_instrument matures.
frameElementDefinitions.Holder: The entity which owns the Financial_instrument and is owed money from the Issuer.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: What the Financial_instrument is worth after maturity.
frameElementDefinitions.Principle: The amount that the Holder originally paid to own the Financial_instrument.
frameElementDefinitions.Interest: The growth rate on the Principle that results in the final Value of the Financial_instrument.

annotations.definition: The Borrower takes possession of a Theme with the knowledge that the Borrower will have to give the Theme back to the Lender after a Duration of time. This frame differs from the Lending frame in that this frame profiles the Borrower in active sentences, whereas the Lending frame profiles the Lender.

frameElementDefinitions.Borrower: The person who receives the Theme from the Lender for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Borrower has possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Lender: The person or institution who gives the Theme to the Borrower for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that the Borrower receives from the Lender for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The aim of the Borrower which they believe will be accomplished by possessing the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the borrowing even occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_transfer: The means by which the transfer of the Theme took place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Borrower gets the Theme from the Lender.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Borrower gains possession of the Theme from the Lender.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that describe a Boundary between two Entities. In many cases, the Boundary is the border between an Entity and some unnamed space, generally conceived of as empty.

frameElementDefinitions.Boundary: The region which is between the Entities or between the Entity and empty space.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The entity that has a Boundary.
frameElementDefinitions.Entities: The joint construal of both Entities next to the Boundary.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: The relative location of the boundary in respect to the Entity or Entities.

annotations.definition: This is an abstract frame for bounded areas, used mostly for FE inheritance.

frameElementDefinitions.Bounded_area: This FE identifies a stable Bounded_area. It typically serves as the denotation of nouns.
frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: This FE identifies the salient Constituent_parts that make up a bounded area.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: Relative_location identifies the place in respect to which a bounded area is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: This FE is used for the Name of a bounded area.
frameElementDefinitions.Owner: This FE identifies the Owner, generally a person, of the bounded area.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: This FE identifies the Formational_cause (action or agent) that brings about the features of the bounded area.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of which kind of Bounded_area the entity is.

annotations.definition: An Exterior, Surface, Boundary, and Interior are picked out relative to the Ground. This Ground may either occupy only the Boundary (in which case the Interior is negative space) or the Ground may fill the Interior.

frameElementDefinitions.Interior: A region of space located inside a Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the Interior, Surface, Boundary, or Exterior regions.
frameElementDefinitions.Surface: The region located outside of, but in contact with, the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Boundary: The region associated with the boundary of the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Exterior: The region which is not located inside or in contact with the Ground.

annotations.definition: A Speaker talks about a Topic, which may be an action performed by the Speaker or an entity or state of affairs that they are associated with in order to suggest that the Speaker is worthy of admiration. The Speaker may give an explicit Reason why the Topic redounds to their credit. Alternatively, a Message frame element can be used to directly describe what the Speaker says, leaving the more specific Reason for admirability to be inferred. Instead of the Speaker, the action may be attributed metonymically to the Medium, or possibly the Expressor.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the person who produces a message about a Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the Speaker communicated a message.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is a positively judged entity or state of affairs with which the Speaker is associated and about which they talk.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Speaker that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Speaker makes a boastful statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Speaker to transmit the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Speaker during the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the statement is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The FE specifies the place where the statement takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A characteristic of the entity or state of affairs denoted by the Topic which explains why the Speaker is proud of the association with the Topic referent.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: A description of the state of affairs that redounds to the Speaker's credit
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is used for the capacity in which the Topic entity or state of affairs is judged, and is expressed in as-PPs.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: Expressor is the body part or action that conveys the feeling of pride of the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Occasion: The Occasion is the reason a public statement is given.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE indicate that the particular Statement event referred to is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. Most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or physical state of the Speaker that leads to their speaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs.

annotations.definition: A Whole breaks apart into Pieces, resulting in the loss of the Whole (and in most cases, no piece that has a separate function).

frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole goes into Pieces which were not (necessarily) definable subparts prior to breaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Pieces: The Pieces result from the breaking apart of the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Criterion: This frame element expresses a property of the Parts or components of the Whole which correlates with the locations (e.g.) of fragmentation on the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event or participants not covered by more specific Frame Elements.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the breaking apart takes place.w
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the breaking apart takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action, event, or state that enables the event of breaking apart.

annotations.definition: An single Subpart breaks off from a Whole.

frameElementDefinitions.Subpart: The part of the Whole that is conceived as being broken or taken off from the remaining part of the whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The piece that is presented in a less prominent position, and which is the landmark or remaining whole from which Sub_part breaks off.
frameElementDefinitions.Criterion: This frame element expresses a property of the Parts or components of the Whole which correlates with the locations (e.g.) of fragmentation on the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the breaking off of the Sub_part happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the breaking off occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event or participants not covered by more specific Frame Elements.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action, event, or state that enables the event of breaking off.

annotations.definition: An Agent brings about the end of a Theme's captivity at a Location_of_confinement in contravention of the plans or desires of a captor.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the individual who ends the confinement or captivity of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the person or object in motion released by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the release which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Agent which brings about the release.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation identifies the reason for which the Agent releases the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose identifies the purpose for which the Agent release the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Theme ceases to be confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time identifies when the Agent release the Theme .
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_confinement: The location to which the Theme's motion was formerly inhibited.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the Releasing event occurs.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes Air to move in a direction, usually specifying either in or out of the body.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent's breathing causes the motion of the Air. Agent is generally the External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Air: Air is the substance that the Agent causes to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE identifies the point at which the Air ends up as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Agent breathes.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means of breathing.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source identifies the starting point of the Air.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: This FE identifies the Path of the motion through locations after leaving the Source and before reaching the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the degree to which an Agent is breathing.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a phrase describing a participant in a breathing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the breathing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE idenitfies the Duration of time that an Agent breathes.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance that the Air travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity that allows the Agent to breathe, either a natural part (the lungs, say) or a device (like a respirator).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which breathing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: This FE designates internal events/states that cause the Agent to exhibit the specified type of breathing. Further, the internal state of the Agent is perceivable to others because of the breathing event.
frameElementDefinitions.External_cause: This FE denotes an event or state external to the Agent that causes them exhibit the specified manner of breathing but not as a conscious response to the External_cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances are expressions indicating prevailing physical conditions or states of affairs concurrent to the breathing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by breathing in the specified manner.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns the movement of a Theme and an Agent and/or Carrier. The Agent, a person or other sentient entity, controls the shared Path by moving the Theme during the motion. In other words, the Agent has overall motion in directing the motion of the Theme. The Carrier may be a separate entity, or it may be the Agent's body. The Constant_location may be a subregion of the Agent's body or (a subregion of) a vehicle that the Agent uses.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is a sentient being who physically controls the movement of the Theme via the carrier, accompanying the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal identifies the endpoint of the path.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source indicates the beginning of the path along which the Theme travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path along which carrying occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The objects being carried.
frameElementDefinitions.Constant_location: The Constant_location expresses the location of the Theme relative to the Carrier. This relationship holds for the entire length of the carrying event.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: Area is used for descriprion of a general area in which the carrying action takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a phrase describing the Carrier, Agent or Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance the Theme is carried.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Carrier or Agent carries the Theme .
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the carrying or transporting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Carrier or Agent carries the Theme .
frameElementDefinitions.Carrier: The Carrier provides support for the Theme. Movement of the Carrier results in movement of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The actions the Agent takes to move the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the general area that serves as a setting for the specific motion of the Agent and Theme (with Source, Path, or Goal).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time in which the carrying event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This FE describes the particpant for whom the bringing is beneficial.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is used to indicate the location of a participant in cases where the multiple participants of an event are not co-located in the typical fashion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co_participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the same event as the primary protagonist, regardless of whether the protagonist, and hence the Co-participant, is more agent- or more undergoer-like.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE is minimally distinct from Duration and Duration_of final_state in that the clause described is conveyed to be iterative.
