annotations.definition: This frame describes assembly or construction actions, where an Agent joins Components together to form a Created_entity, which is profiled, and hence the object of the verb.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent builds a Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument(s) with which an Agent builds a Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the building occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the building occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent builds a Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Components: This FE identifies the Components that form the Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Created_entity: Created_entity identifies the entity that is created in the act of building.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the act of building.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifes the phrase describing the participant in the act of building.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE idenitifies the Manner in which an Agent builds a Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This extrathematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate. Further, the target predicate should involve some sort of Agent that intends that the benefit go to the Beneficiary.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: This frame includes words that name subparts of buildings that can be occupied by people.

frameElementDefinitions.Building_part: The place or area specified inside a structure. The Building_part of the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The entirety of a building whose Building_part is discussed.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Building_part was constructed.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE designates the kind or Type of Building_part.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE describes where the Building_part is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of some property of the Building_part.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words which name permanent fixed structures forming an enclosure and providing protection from the elements.

frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The Individual that created the Building.
frameElementDefinitions.Building: The entity built by the Builder which has some Function.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: The use for which the Building is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The Name used to refer to the Building.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Building, including components, ingredients, etc
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which the Building comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Building, including architectural style.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of some property of the Building.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The building's location.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Building is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The person or other legal entity that owns or has possession of the Building.

annotations.definition: An Agent performs an Action incorrectly. The affected Patient may be mentioned metonymically for the Action.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which a bungling event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifes a phrase describing a particpant in an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Agent bungles.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent bungles.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent performs an action incorrectly.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: Patient is the entity affected by the Agent's bungling of an Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the bungling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an Agent bungles.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the bungling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: This FE identifies the Action that the Agent performs incorrectly.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The type of action that the Agent habitually bungles.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause places a Theme under a surface (often the ground) into a Goal location.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent intentionally buries the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that is buried.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the initial location of the Theme, before it is buried.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The FE Goal is the location where the Theme ends up.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of the placing action which is not directly related to the trajectory of the Theme's motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree indicates the degree to which the burying occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the Agent or Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an act whereby the Agent achieves the burying of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the burying.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the amount of time for which the Theme stays in the Goal location.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place indicates where the burying occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation that the Theme is buried.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Time is when the burying occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is the general area where the Theme is buried.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: The Cotheme is buried along with the Theme
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion of the theme into the ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which buring occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: A non-animate force which buries the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by performing the burying action.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The Beneficiary derives a benefit from the occurrence of the burying event.

annotations.definition: A business locale, the Entity, ceases to provide services and closes.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity is rendered unable to continue its regular operations.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is named as one of the causes of failure.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time of the failure.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place of the failing entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances that caused failure. Compared to Explanation, this describes situations or environments that lead to failure, not a specific reason for failure.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes the way in which the Entity failed.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The Containing_event is an event within which the failure occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A Depictive describes something about the Entity.

annotations.definition: A Proprietor owns or runs a Business which provides a Product (which may be goods or services).

frameElementDefinitions.Product: Product identifies the product or service provided by the business.
frameElementDefinitions.Proprietor: Proprietor is the owner or chief participant in the business.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Descriptor indicates other characteristics of the business besides its Product and Proprietor.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place in which the business is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Business: Business identifies the company or establishment. This may be the target itself or the Business_Name if it is an appositive not set off with commas (i.e. restrictive):
frameElementDefinitions.Business_name: The name of the Business when it appears in an appositive set off by commas (non-restrictive):
frameElementDefinitions.Service_provider: When a Business is described in terms of the professionals who work to produce the product or service, use Service_provider.

annotations.definition: A hidden Cache of a Resource is kept safe and available for later Use. The existence, location, and other information about the Cache is kept secret by the Possessor.

frameElementDefinitions.Cache: This FE denotes the Cache of Resources.
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The goods or commodity that is being stored in a Cache.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The future Use for the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The entity that possesses the Cache.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Cache itself, rather than the Resource.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame name the different parts of the calendric cycle, both man-made and natural. The Unit (e.g. Tuesday) specifies some time period as part of a specific larger temporal Whole (Tuesday of next week), or may be resolved to an exact time span by a Relative_time (next Tuesday).

frameElementDefinitions.Relative_time: Relative_time is used for the word or words that locate the time with respect to an identifiable reference point.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: Name is used for the name of a day (month, etc.). Note that only the word (or words) that is the name is (are) tagged, not the target word.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: Whole is used for the larger unit of which the target word names a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of intervals of the specified type.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: The calendric unit that is denoted. LUs in this frame all denote the Unit.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_event: An event saliently associated with a particular calendric unit.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_period: The time-expression that is used to establish when the Trajector_event occurred. This FE, when present, always occurs on the second layer.
frameElementDefinitions.Trajector_event: An event that is located by the Calendric_unit expression.

annotations.definition: This frame contains adjectives and nouns that describe the truth or sincerity of communication. These words, analogously to behaver-evaluating words, can apply either to Speakers or to the Messages they produce. They can occur with about-PPs expressing Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker produces the Message, the truth or sincerity of which is at issue. It is normally expressed as the External Argument of predicative uses of the target word, or as the Nominal Head of prenominal uses.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the Message is communicated. This FE often remains unexpressed. When expressed, it normally takes the form of a PP Complement headed by with.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: Message is the communicative content whose truth or sincerity is at issue. This FE does not normally co-occur with the other FEs in this frame. When it does occur, it is normally the External Argument of a predicative use of the Target word, or the modified Nominal Head in a prenominal use.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject matter to which the Message pertains. It often occurs without the Message FE in predicative uses of the target word. It is normally expressed as a PP Complement headed by "about".
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which a communication is candid--some are more candid than others.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE indicates the manner of candidness.

annotations.definition: An Entity meets the pre-conditions for participating in an Event. A Degree modifier may be included to indicate by how much the Entity exceeds or falls short of the minimum requirements.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity whose meeting or falling short of pre-conditions is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event that the Entity may participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Entity exceeds or falls short of the minimum requirements for participating in the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Entity meets or fails to meet the pre-conditions.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An entity, event, or state that causes the Entity to be meet or fail to meet the pre-conditions for participating in an Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Relevant_feature: The characteristic or behavior of the Entity that is considered when judging whether the Entity might participate in the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The particular role that the Entity plays in the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Entity is capable of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE expresses how often the Entity is able to do the Event.

annotations.definition: An Entity has the ability to contain a Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity whose ability to contain items is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Entity exceeds or falls short of the minimum requirements for holding the items.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Entity meets or fails to meet the pre-conditions for containing.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An entity, event, or state that causes the Entity to be able or unable to hold the items.
frameElementDefinitions.Status: The status of the capability (e.g., innately, with modifications).
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Entity has the ability to contain the Theme, often expressed with a quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of one of the participants, typically the Theme but possibly the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: This FE denotes a sub-part of the Entity which has a particular capacity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose of the Entity's specified capacity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Entity has or had a certain capacity.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns Stock, a purchasable and sellable asset representing a Shareholder's share in a company (the Issuer). Stock entitles the Shareholder to profits of the Issuer company, as determined by the governing board of the Issuer company. LUs in this frame can denote the type of capital stock investment into a business (common or preferred) or the investment itself. Common stock is also known as ordinary stock or voting stock.

frameElementDefinitions.Stock: The investment that a Shareholder makes into a business, differing from property and assets.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The the kind of Stock, of which there are two: preferred or common. Holders of preferred stock are entitled to the first dividend that a company pays out, while holders of common stock are granted voting rights in corporate decisions.
frameElementDefinitions.Shareholder: The creditor that has invested into a business by purchasing some Type of its Stock.
frameElementDefinitions.Issuer: The entity that issues its Stock to the Shareholder.
frameElementDefinitions.Amount: The Amount is the number of shares, though any measure prase will suffice.

annotations.definition: This frame presents a situation where there is a certain Number of instances of an Entity or a certain Number that quantifies a Unit. Numbers are used to count any kind of Entity that has distinct individuals, or alternatively, to count Units whose own function is to quantify some property, with the Number associated with each word of this frame differentiating groups, individuals, parts of individual Entitys of different sizes, or to differentiate the quantity of the Units. A countable Entity prototypically occupies its own space separately from other instances of the Entity, and each instance of the Entity has specific kinds of parts or substance or characteristics as specified by the kind of Entity it is described as. Units (e.g., hours, inches, dozens) have the function of quantifying some property and as a result, naturally occur with a Number to say how the current property compares to the standard quantification of the Unit. Less prototypical numbers, which occur much more frequently with Units rather than Entitys, describe situations with less than a whole Unitor Entity (half.n), or the lack of a Unit or Entity (zero.num).

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: This FE identifies the Entity enumerated by the Number.
frameElementDefinitions.Precision: This FE identifies the Precision to which an Entity approximates a Number.
frameElementDefinitions.Multiplier: How many times larger the group of Entitys is than the base Number.
frameElementDefinitions.Composite_number: The total number of instances of the Entity. This FE should be used when more basic number expressions are used together to form a Composite_number whose value is the sum of the constituent numbers. It is annotated on a second FE layer. For cases where a number is used to multiply another number, use Multiplier.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: A Unit expresses some quantification of some property of an entity, event, or situation by providing a base value of the property combined with a Number expressing how the current value for the property relates to the Unit.

annotations.definition: A Distributor sells, lends, or otherwise distributes a class of Goods. In the prototypical case, the Distributor is a retailer who generally keeps some type of Goods on site for the purpose of selling it to a buyer. Extensions include lending Goods (e.g., libraries, movie rental outlets), organization-internal distribution (as in a hospital), or other non-commercial distribution centers. The Goods are constrained to be a type of item (e.g., canned goods, antique dressers) rather than a particular or unique item (e.g., Smith's last self-portrait, that guitar I saw here last week). Note that a Distributor may carry some particular goods, but may not have it on hand at that exact moment.

frameElementDefinitions.Distributor: The Distributor maintains a store of Goods for the purpose of selling or otherwise distributing them.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The Goods are kept on hand and distributed by the Distributor.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Distributor carries the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place indicates where the carrying of Goods occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the storing of goods which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of a Distributor that also affect the action (deliberately, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE indicates the length of time that the Distributor carries the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame involve an Undesirable_event which affects the Patient negatively. No agent need be involved.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Undesirable_Event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Undesirable_Event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The entity that experiences the Undesirable_Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE indicates the Circumstances under which a warning applies. (Proper only to betide.)
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The cause of the Undesirable_Event.

annotations.definition: A Fuel, some flammable object or substance, ends up on fire. In this frame, the cause of the fire is not the primary concern, but rather the fact that a fire started. In rare cases, only the Fire is mentioned and the associated Fuel is not.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Fire begins to burn.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Fire begins to burn.
frameElementDefinitions.Fire: The total material currently involved in combustion, including both the parts of the Fuel currently undergoing chemical alteration and the partially ionized gas that results from this process.
frameElementDefinitions.Fuel: The entity that is begins to undergo rapid chemical alteration leading to damage or destruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Fire event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A description of why the event happened.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer construes an Item as belonging to a certain Category. In this process, the Cognizer may either passively perceive the Item and note that it fits the Criteria for a Category, or, alternatively, actively examine the Item for certain Criteria that define a Category (or set of Categorys). Most members of the frame can also describe a resulting cognitive state of the Cognizer with respect to the Item and Category which no longer requires the current perception or consideration of the Item. A few LUs (e.g. peg.v, pigeonhole.v) do not readily allow stative interpretation, and a few LUs (e.g. deem.v, regard.v) only allow stative interpretation.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: This is the person who performs an act of categorization. Typically, this frame element is expressed as an External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: This is the entity which is construed or treated by the Cognizer as being an instance of a particular Category. Typically, the Item is expressed as an Object.
frameElementDefinitions.Category: This frame element expresses a general type or class of which the Item is considered an instance. Typically, it is expressed in a prepositional phrase headed by as or under.
frameElementDefinitions.Criteria: This frame element expresses properties of the Item which is used as a basis for categorization. Criteria are the general dimensions along which Items can potentially differ from one another and hence, fall into different Categorys. For example, a paint sample can be classified by color or by finish (matte or gloss); if they are classified by color, one can be classified as orange and another as red. The Criteria FE is typically expressed as a PP headed by by, or as a complex PP of the form on the basis of...:
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Cognizer categorizes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate when a certain Categorization was made.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the set of conditions in which the categorization holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Cognizer categorizes the Item in the given way.

annotations.definition: A Cause causes an Effect. Alternatively, an Actor, a participant of a (implicit) Cause, may stand in for the Cause. The entity Affected by the Causation may stand in for the overall Effect situation or event.

frameElementDefinitions.Cause: A force, process, or event that produces an effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Affected: Agents in a joint or reciprocal action (e.g. exchange)
frameElementDefinitions.Effect: A positive or negative evaluation of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Actor: An entity which participates in a Cause, perhaps volitionally and perhaps not.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any holistic description of the event, including overall depictions (the same way) and descriptions pertaining to the Actor's influence on the character of the event (eagerly, quietly).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action through which the Actor or Cause accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the action denoted by the target occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.

annotations.definition: A Cause brings about an Effect that would not have occurred otherwise, simultaneously preventing an Averted_situation, which is what would have occurred without the Cause. The Effect and the Averted_situation are thus mutually exclusive alternatives prior to the Cause event. In most cases, either the Effect or the Averted_situation is clearly most prominent; this is especially true in the case where the Cause is the action of an Agent, who normally conceives of their action as either bringing about a desirable Effect or avoiding an undesirable Averted_situation.

frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that produces an effect. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Effect: The Effect is the event which results from the Cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Averted_situation: The situation that would be the case if the Cause event did not occur or the Cause state were not the case.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Cause occurs and Effect takes place or the Averted_situation does not occur as expected.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which at which the Cause occurs and Effect takes place or the Averted_situation does not occur as expected.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause affects a Body_part of an Experiencer so that the quality of the Experiencer's perceptual experience of the Body_part is qualitatively changed.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person causing a bodily experience in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the being or entity whose experience changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result identifies the result of the Agent's action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree is the degree to which the Experiencer's bodily experience is affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place identifies the place where the bodily experience is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Time identifies the time when the experience-causing event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: >The Body_part identifies the location on the Experiencer's body where the bodily experience takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE is any object manipulated by the Agent to cause the experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Purpose identifies the state-of-affairs that an Agent hopes to bring about by causing an experience in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies any Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action or the affected entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies the event within which the bodily experience is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A fact or action somehow related to the Experiencer that the Agent responds to by causing an experience in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: Iterations refers to the number of times the Agent or Cause causes an experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause marks expressions that indicate some non-intentional, typically non-human, force that affects the Experiencer's Body_part.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion_bodypart: The Subregion_bodypart defines the precise location on the Body_part in which the experience occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the causal event and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Cause_bodily_experience event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of the Cause_bodily_experience event to its end.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: This FE indicates that the Cause_bodily_experience event is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the Cause_bodily_experience event) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: A description presenting a separate aspect of the experience-causing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the Cause_bodily_experience event began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause causes an Entity to change, either in its category membership or in terms of the value of an Attribute. In the former case, an Initial_category and a Final_category may be expressed, in the latter case an Initial_value and a Final_value can be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity that effects a change in the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_category: The category of which the Entity is a member before the Agent causes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_category: The category that the Entity belongs to after it has undergone the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_value: The value that the Attribute assumes as a result of the change that the Entity is caused to undergo.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_value: The value that the Attribute has before the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the change of the Entity is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the initial and final categories or values differ from each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event causing the change of the Entity which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in bringing about the change in the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: A characteristic of the Entity for which a value can be assigned or determined by calculation, measurement , or observation.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The abstract or concrete thing or situation that the Agent causes to change.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event that causes a change in the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by effecting a change in the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent (or event resulting from the Cause) that produces a change in the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the change of the Entity is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The particular role that the Agent plays in the Cause_change event.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Agent or Cause changes the consistency of a Patient from its Initial_state to a Result. This may take place under certain Circumstances for a Purpose or Reason in a certain Manner. A Change_agent can also be used. Note that this frame contrasts with Cause_change_of_phase in that this frame describes causation of a change in a Patient within a particular phase.

frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances at which the consistency change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Patient changes consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: This is the original state of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Patient's consistency is changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the the Patient changes consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Patient's consistency is changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result is the result of the change of consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed at which the change of consistency occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Patient changes consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: Patient is the entity that undergoes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent causes the change of consistency in the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which the Agent changes the consistency of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent changes the consistency of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent changes the consistency of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-Agent which brings about the change in the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Change_agent: The Change_agent is the substance used to change the consistency of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: This FE marks expressions that indicate a Container in which the Patient is changing consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Hot_cold_source: The Hot_cold_source is the source of the heat or coolness.

annotations.definition: A Cause or Agent causes a Patient to undergo a change of phase. The Result of the change may be given, along with the Initial_state and the Circumstances under which the change can occur. Note that this frame contrasts with Cause_change_of_consistency in that this frame describes causation of a change of a Patient between different phases (i.e. solid to liquid or frozen to "unfrozen").

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent causes the Patient to change phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which the Agent intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Agent intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent intentionally affects the Patient
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient undergoes a change of phase brought about by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where change of phase occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Agent affects the Patient,
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which the change of phase occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances under which the phase change occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Agent changes the phase of the Patient
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: This is the original state of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result is the result of the change of phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed at which the change of phase occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-Agent which brings about the change in the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: This FE identifies the Container that holds the Patient whose phase is being changed .
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Agent changes the phase of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes the state of the Agent or Patient at the time of phase change.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is the substance which surrounds the Patient and facilitates its phase change.
frameElementDefinitions.Hot_cold_source: The Hot_cold_source is the source of the heat or cool.

annotations.definition: This frame consists of words that indicate that an Agent or a Cause affects the position of an Item on some scale (the Attribute) to change it from an initial value (Value_1) to an end value (Value_2). The direction of the change (Path) can be encoded as well as the magnitude of the change (Difference). The rate of change of the value (Speed) can optionally be indicated as well as a second scale (Co_Variable), which the values are correlated with.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who causes an entity (Item) to change its positon on a scale with respect to some property (Variable).
frameElementDefinitions.Difference: The distance between the orginial value (Value-1) and the final value (Value-2) of the entity on the scale (Variable).
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The scale along which the Item ranges in value.
frameElementDefinitions.Value_1: The initial value of the entity (Item) along the scale.
frameElementDefinitions.Value_2: The final position on the scale that the entity (Item) assumes.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-variable: The Co-Variable is the scale that the (dependent ) Variable is measured against.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any expression that says something about the movement of the entity (Item) along the scale between its initial (Value-1) and its final position (Value-2).
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate at which the entity's positon on the scale changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity whose position along a scale is being changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent that brings about the change in the position of the Item on the scale.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The force that causes the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Agent changing the value of an Attribute which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent changes the value of an Attribute.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Patient to be more strong, often resulting in something that is more secure and safe.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent fortifies the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity that is being made stronger.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The resultant state of the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by an Agent that enables them to act upon the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent makes the Patient stronger.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Agent fortifies the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which causes the Patient to become stronger.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances of the world at the time of the event (which do not bear directly on the event itself).

annotations.definition: An Agent acts to cause a Experiencer to feel a certain emotion.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who's action's cause an emotion in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer feels an emotion as caused by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The tool used by the Agent to cause an emotion in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by an Agent that enables them to act upon the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: In what way the Agent causes an emotion in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes the event as a whole, often as a VPing.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE describes the reason for the state of affairs described by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: For how long instances of an Agent causing the Patient an emotion has repeatedly occured.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Agent causes emotion in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occasion or happening that evokes emotions in the Experiencer. This Event occurs as the direct result of a sentient being's actions.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type designates the kind of emotion that the Agent causes the Experiencer to have.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical or abstract setting in which the Agent causes the Experiencer to feel an emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent causes the Experiencer to feel the emotion.

annotations.definition: An Agent or non-human Cause causes an Item to change its physical size. The Size_change of an Item may be explicitly indicated or characterized in terms of Initial_size and/or Result_size. In the default case, expansion is along all dimensions, but sometimes the Dimension of expansion is incorporated or made explicit.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: With some words in this frame, an Agent who causes the change in size of the Item may be present. The Agent typically occurs as the External Argument of a verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The Item is frequently the External Argument of verbs or the NP Object if an Agent is expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_size: Initial_size is rarely expressed without Result_size also being explicit. Initial_size generally occurs in a PP-from Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Result_size: Result_size is typically expressed in a PP Complement headed by to.
frameElementDefinitions.Size_change: Size_change identifies the amount by which the size of an Item changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: Change of size may be limited to or described in terms of a single Dimension.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent brings about the expansion of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-variable: The feature that the size of the Item is varied in step with.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: This FE identifies the Group in which an Item changes size.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: Rate identifies the rate of change of the value.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is a non-human entity which brings about the change in size.
frameElementDefinitions.Elapsed_time: The Elapsed_time is the amonut of time in which the change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent causes the Item to expand.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent cause the expansion of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent causes the expansion of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which the Agent causes the expansion of the Item.

annotations.definition: An Agent or a Cause causes a Fluid to move from a Source to a Goal along a Path or within an Area.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The animate protagonist who causes the Fluid to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event or force that causes the Fluid to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Fluid: The entity that changes location and moves in a fluidic way. It is not necessarily in a liquid state but is construed that way.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The location that the Fluid occupies initially.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is the location the Fluid ends up in as a result of the motion
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The trajectory along which the Fluid moves, exclusive of the Source and Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The setting in which the Fluid's movement takes place along an unspecified Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). With agentive uses, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the fluidic motion event is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in causing the Fluid to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: A state of the Fluid that results from the motion event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The entity manipulated by the Agent which causes the Fluid to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Agent which brings about the motion of the Fluid.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Agent is attempting to bring about by causing motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance travelled by the Fluid.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe situations in which an Agent or a Cause injures a Victim. The Body_part of the Victim which is most directly affected may also be mentioned in the place of the Victim. In such cases, the Victim is often indicated as a genitive modifier of the Body_part, in which case the Victim FE is indicated on a second FE layer.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: Agent is the person causing the Victim's injury.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the being or entity that is injured. If the Victim is included in the phrase indicating Body_part, the Victim FE is tagged on a second FE layer (see 3rd example).
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result identifies the result of the Agent's action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree is the degree to which the Agent causes harm to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent acts upon the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place identifies the place where the harm causing event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Time identifies the time when the harm-causing event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The Body_part identifies the location on the body where the bodily injury takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE is any object used to cause the injury.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Purpose identifies the purpose for which a harm-causing action is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies any Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies the event in which the harm is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A fact or action somehow related to the Victim that the Agent responds to by causing harm to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: Iterations refers to the number of times the Agent causes harm to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause marks expressions that indicate some non-intentional, typically non-human, force that inflicts harm on the Victim
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion_bodypart: The Subregion_bodypart defines the precise location on the Body_part which is harmed.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the Cause_harm event and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Cause_harm event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of the Cause_harm event to its end.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: This FE indicates that the Cause_harm event is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the Cause_harm event) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: A description of the harm event which significantly displays a different aspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the Cause_harm event began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes an Impactor to make sudden, forcible contact with an Impactee, or manipulates two (or more) Impactors so that they make mutual forcible contact.

frameElementDefinitions.Force: The Force is the amount of force in the course of the impact.
frameElementDefinitions.Impactee: The Impactee is the entity which is hit by the Impactor.
frameElementDefinitions.Impactor: The Impactor is the entity that hits the Impactee because of the Agent's efforts.
frameElementDefinitions.Impactors: The Impactors are the multiple entities that collide.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The FE Manner identifies the manner in which an Agent intentionally affects a Impactee.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the impact.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This FE indicates the Speed of the Impactor (or Impactors at impact.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: Subregion identifies the part of the larger whole which was hit.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the impact occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent causes an impact.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an Agent intentionally affects an Impactee.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The FE Means identifies the means by which an Agent intentionally affects a Impactee.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The FE Purpose identifies the purpose for which an Agent intentionally affects the Impactee.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE identifies the Period_of_iterations during which the Agent repeatedly causes impacts.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause marks expressions that indicate some non-intentional, typically non-human, force that causes an Impactor to make sudden, forcible contact with an Impactee.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Theme to move from a Source, along a Path, to a Goal. Different members of the frame emphasize the trajectory to different degrees, and a given instance of the frame will usually leave some of the Source, Path and/or Goal implicit. The completion of motion is not required (unlike the Placing frame, see below), although individual sentences annotated with this frame may emphasize the Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the one whose action causes the motion of a Theme. The Agent is generally realized as the External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The FE Theme is generally an NP Object.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The FE Source is the starting point of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The FE Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The FE Goal is the point at which the Theme ends up as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The FE Distance is any expression characterizing the extent of motion of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The FE Area is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path. Locative setting adjuncts may also be assigned this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of a participant during the action
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The FE Means indicates the action that is taken that results in the motion of the Theme
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The FE Manner is any expression which describes a property of the motion of the Theme which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: Subregion identifies the part of the Theme that makes contact with the resulting location. When Subregion is expressed, there usually is no Goal expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: Cause marks expressions that indicate some non-intentional, typically non-human, force that causes the Theme to be set in motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity saliently involved in the transmission of force from an Agent to a Theme. It may be the Agent's bodypart, or some kind of device.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the general area that provides a setting for a specific motion (with Source, Path, or Goal).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the motion of the Theme occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: A resultant state that the Theme is in at the end of its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Handle: The Agent's manipulation of the Theme may be further specified as being localized to some part of the Theme, a Handle. The Handle is usually expressed as a PP
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: A previous state that the Theme was in before the beginning of its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the main clause logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the action requires.

annotations.definition: An Agent (or inanimate Cause) affects a Set so that the number of entities that make up the Set changes from an Initial_number to a Final_number. Alternatively, the situation can be described in terms of an Initial_state and a Final_state. In some cases the Attribute (i.e., number) or Difference in number may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who causes the Set to increase in number.
frameElementDefinitions.Difference: The distance between the orginal value (Initial_number) and the final value (Final_number) of the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The scale along which the Set ranges in, i.e., number.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_number: The initial number of entities in the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_number: The final number of entities in the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-variable: The Co-Variable is the scale that the amount of the Set is measured against.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any expression that says something about the movement of the entity (Set) along the scale between its Initial_number and its Final_number.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate at which the entity's positon on the scale changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Set: The entity whose amount is being changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent that brings about the change in the number of the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the proliferating of the number of a Set is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the proliferating occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The force that causes the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Agent changing the number of the Set which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent changes the number of the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: The state of the Set before it is affected by an outside Agent or Cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_state: The state of the Set after it is affected by an outside Agent or Cause.

annotations.definition: In this frame, an Agent changes the temperature of an Item. A Temperature_goal can specify the desired temperature. A Temperature_change can also be indicated. The Temperature_start indicates the initial temperature.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent performs the intentional act which changes the temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which the Agent changes the temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The Item undergoes the temperature change.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature_goal: The Temperature_goal is the temperature to which the Agent is heating or cooling the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature_change: The Temperature_change is the amount of difference between the intial and goal temperatures.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature_start: Temperature_start is the initial temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Agent changes the temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent changes the temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent changes the temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent changes the temperature of the Item and what the Agent hopes to accomplish.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent changes the temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result is the resultant state of the Agent changing the temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that changes the temperature. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Agent changes the temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: This FE identifies the Container that holds the Item whose temperature is being changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the Depictive phrase describing the Agent or Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Hot_Cold_source: The Hot_Cold_source is the source of the heat or coolness.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances at which the temperature change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Item approaches (an implicit) Temperature_goal, which is often construed to be the ambient temperature.

annotations.definition: These words refer to an Agent joining Parts to form a Whole. (The Parts may also be encoded as Part_1 and Part_2.) There is a symmetrical relationship between the components that undergo the process, and afterwards the Parts are consumed and are no longer distinct entities that are easily discernable or separable in the Whole.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: In causative uses of these verbs there is an Agent responsible for amalgamating the Parts. The Agent is generally the External Argument (or the object of a PP-by in passives).
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: This FE identifies Whole as the entity resulting from combination of Parts. When overtly expressed, it is usually a PP Complement (often headed by into).
frameElementDefinitions.Parts: This FE identifies the Parts entities being combined, and is often expressed in a single plural NP, usually the direct object of a verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_1: This FE identifies the first of two Parts mentioned. It is most often the NP-Object of a verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_2: This FE identifies the second of two Parts mentioned. It is usually a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the amalgamating occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the thoroughness of the mixing job, i.e. to what degree the resulting Whole is undifferentiated.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the amalgamating occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE is used for indicating the Result of amalgamating.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Parts during the amalgamation. Often it refers to the proportion of the Parts to one another.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the Agent seeks to bring about by the Amalgamation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location that the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the amalgamation occurs.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Dryee (either a surface or an entire entity, inside and out) to become dry. This should not include examples like "drying tears" or "drying spills" as these are in the Removing frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the intentional act causing the Dryee to become dry.
frameElementDefinitions.Dryee: The Dryee is the entity which has the water removed from it.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an Agent intentionally affects the Dryee.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an the Agent dries the Dryee.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent intentionally affects the Dryee.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent intentionally affects the Dryee.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which Agent intentionally affects the Dryee and what the Agent hopes to accomplish.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent intentionally affects the Dryee.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that produces the state of dryness. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the length of Time that the drying takes.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature: This FE identifies the Temperature at which heat is applied to the Dryee.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Dryee ends up dry.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause makes a New_member part of Group. The Group may be represented by an individual Existing_member if it implies the existence of a set of members.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is a volitional animate being who causes the New_member to become part of the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Existing_member: Something that is already a member of the Group and represents the Group as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.New_member: An item that becomes a part of the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group is anything that can be conceptualized as a complex collection of parts or ingredients, including through metonymy like containers or locations, which New_members can join.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that is responsible for causing a New_member to enter a Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the inclusion of a New_member.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies when the inclusion of the New_member to the Group occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Agent acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies what the Agent hopes to accomplish by including a New_member to a Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which a New_member is entered into a pre-existing Group.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause makes a Patient sharper or duller. An Instrument or Means may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the sharpening (or dulling) action.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent causes the Patient to become more or less sharp.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that interacts with an Patient, causing it to change in sharpness.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent sharpens the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Agent sharpens the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent sharpens Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity that is sharpened.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Agent changes the sharpness of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state of affairs that the Agent is responding to by executing the sharpening act.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Patient's sharpness is affected by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that produces the state of sharpness. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The resulting configuration of the Patient.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Patient to be wet with a Liquid. In this frame, the Liquid can be a liquid or gas. Patients are porous objects, but not hollow (like containers).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is a volitional animate being who causes the Patient to become wet.
frameElementDefinitions.Liquid: The Liquid is a fluid or gas that permeates or covers the Patient. It most frequently occurs in a PP Complement headed by with.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient takes up the Liquid through its pores or has the Liquid covering its surface.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner or way in which the Agent wets the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an the Agent wets the Patient
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent wets the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent wets the Patient and what the Agent hopes to accomplish.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent wets the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an Agent intentionally wets the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that produces the state of wetness.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Agent wets the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE describes the reason for the state of the Cause_to_be_wet event.

annotations.definition: A Cause event brings about and potentially maintains the burning of Flammables. Often, an Agent stands in for the intentional action the brings about the fire, or an Actor stands in for the action that brings about the fire unintentionally.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that intends a fire to burn and brings it about.
frameElementDefinitions.Flammables: The Flamables is the the object that burns as a result of the Cause, Agent, or Actor.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The cause of the fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Kindler that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event brought about by the Cause or the Kindler that actually makes the Flammables catch fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Kindler as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Actor: The individual who, while actively performing an action, unintentionally causes the Flammables to burn.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause causes a Process or State to continue.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual who causes the Process to continue or the State to be maintained.
frameElementDefinitions.Process: A Process that is maintained by the Agent or Cause.
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State that is maintained by the Agent or Cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Agent maintain the State or Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The event by which an Agent or Cause maintains the State or Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event that causes the Process or State to be maintained.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by maintaining the State or Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that maintain the State or Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Process continues.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Process continues.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause causes a Process or State to end.

frameElementDefinitions.Process: A Process which is ended by the Agent or Cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Agent that causes the Process or State to end.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Process or State is caused to end.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for why the Process or State is ended.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Process or State is ended.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Process or State is brought to an end.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of the event that causes the Process or State to end.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner of causing the Process or State to end.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual who causes the Process or State to end.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event that causes the Process or State to end.
frameElementDefinitions.State: A State which is ended by the Agent or Cause.

annotations.definition: An Agent intentionally seeks to bring about an internal mental or emotional state in the Experiencer. This frame in closely related to Experiencer_obj.

frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent intentionally tries to bring about an internal mental or emotional state.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is an External Argument of the target word and purposefully arouses an emotional state.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person the Agent causes to have a particular emotional state.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This Frame Element is for expressions that elaborate the manner in which the Agent affects the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Resulting_action: What the Agent intends to make the Experiencer do by causing a certain experience and the Experiencer actually does.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent affects the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent affects the Experiencer
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which the Agent brings about the emotional state.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent brings about the emotional state in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Experiencer ends up feeling an emotion.

annotations.definition: An Agent suddenly and often violently separates the Whole_patient into two or more smaller Pieces, resulting in the Whole_patient no longer existing as such. Several lexical items are marked with the semantic type Negative, which indicates that the fragmentation is necessarily judged as injurious to the original Whole_patient. Compare this frame with Damaging, Render_non-functional, and Removing.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The conscious entity, generally a person, that performs the intentional action that results in the Whole_patient being broken into Pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that interacts with a Whole_patient to accomplish its fracture.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the fracturing which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
