It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the breakage.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole_patient: The entity which is destroyed by the Agent and that ends up broken into Pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent breaks the Whole_patient into pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent causes the break-up of the Whole_patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in performing the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent breaks up the Whole_patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Pieces: The fragments of the Whole_patient that result from the Agent's action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the fracturing is completed.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Whole_patient which is directly affected by the breakage.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Resistant_surface: The Resistant_surface is the surface against which a Agent presses the Whole_patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which leads to the fragmentation of the Whole_patient.

annotations.definition: An Operator maneuvers onto land a Vehicle which typically does not operate on land, e.g., an airplane or boat.

frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Vehicle ends up stationary on land.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion. While Source expressions are possible in this frame, they are relatively infrequent.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is any expression that tells where the Vehicle ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A Manner expression describes a property of landing which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Operator lands a Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: Cotheme is a second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Operator lands teh Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: The New_situation are the state of the Goal when the Vehicle arrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the state of the Operator or Vehicle upon arrival.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The time period within which the landing event repeats multiple times.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the landing event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Operator lands the Vehicle at the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE marks the state of affairs reported by the landing event as understood to play a role in the state of affairs of Event_description
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Operator lands the Vehicle at the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the landing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Operator: The Operator maneuvers the vehicle onto land. A skilled pilot would never set a plane down in weather like this.

annotations.definition: An Agent manipulates a Sound_maker or a Cause causes the Sound_maker to emit a sound.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that manipulates a Sound_maker so that it makes Noise.
frameElementDefinitions.Sound_maker: The entity that produces the Noise, as a result of the action of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion is the sublocation of the Sound_maker from which the sound is emitting.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: The location from which an Agent or Cause acts to project sound from a Sound_maker toward an (implicit) perceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of one of the primary participants (either Agent or Sound_maker) during the course of the sound-making event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action directly due to the Agent or Cause that results in the Sound_maker emitting noise..
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The inanimate force of nature that causes the Sound_maker to produce noise.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs or action that the Agent wishes to bring about by performing the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the noise is produced.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the sound is produced.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times the Sound_maker makes noise per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: Used for expressions that indicate the number of times Sound_maker makes noise.

annotations.definition: An Agent works on a Project so that it reaches a more advanced and desirable state.

frameElementDefinitions.Project: The object or idea that undergoes change due to the efforts of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Prior_state: The former state of the Project.
frameElementDefinitions.Post_state: The state of the Project following an advance.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the progress occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The rate at which the change happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the progress, generally a description comparing events (the same way); in addition, it may describe characteristics and states of the Agent that also affect the development.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period over which the Agent develops the Project.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of advancement that the Project has attained.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant dimension of development.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that brings about the development of the Project.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the the Project progresses.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent progresses the Project.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Agent which enables the advancement.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Theme to move with respect to a certain Fixed_location, generally with a certain Periodicity, without undergoing unbounded translational motion or significant alteration of configuration/shape.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: Theme identifies the object involved in motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is the way in which the motion in place is instigated.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Periodicity: The number of times the Theme returns to a state in a given duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Fixed_location: The point or set of points that define the limits of motion for the Theme. For spinning motions, it is the axis, for vibrating it is a boundary, for swinging it is a point.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent performs the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Angle: Angle identifies the amount that the Theme turns.
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: The Agent's manipulation of an Theme may be further specified as being localized to some part of the Theme, a Locus. This FE is usually expressed as a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Bodypart_of_agent: This FE identifies the part of the Agent's body being used to manipulate the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: Cause identifies the non-agentive force that causes the motion in place.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This frame element is used for all path-like expressions, that express the Direction of the swinging, vibrating or rotating.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event.

annotations.definition: An Agent, Actor, Entity, or Medium causes a Phenomenon to be perceived by a Perceiver. With an Actor, Entity, or Medium, the Perceiver is usually unspecified.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent intentionally creates a situation in which the Perceiver observes or experiences the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon is an entity or idea that is perceived by the Perceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The Perceiver is made to have a perceptual experience by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The instrument or method by which the Perceiver was shown the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the way in which the Agent presents the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: Frequency describes how often the Agent presents the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent causes the perception of the Perceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose of the Agent in creating the perception of the Perceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Actor: An Actor is a sentient being who is exhibiting, possibly uninintentionally, some Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: An object that has some association with a Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium through which a Phenomenon is expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: A Co-participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the same event as the Actor or Co-participant Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.

annotations.definition: An Agent acts so as to cause some process to begin again after an interruption or suspension.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: A sentient being acts with the intention of causing a process to begin again.
frameElementDefinitions.Process: The process which begins again after a suspension.
frameElementDefinitions.Hiatus: The period of time during which the process is suspended.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the process is restarted.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that causes the process to resume. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent causes the Process to resume.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The intentional act performed by the Agent that results in the resumption of the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the manner in which the Agent causes the resumption of the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of time for which the Process continues after being started by the Agent.

annotations.definition: An Patient, which is an organic substance, undergoes the natural process of decay due to a Cause (or Agent).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent intentionally brings about the decay of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient undergoes decay.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of causing the Patient to rot.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by an Agent, or a sub-event centered around a Cause that leads it to cause the Patient to rot.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Cause causes the Patient to rot.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Cause acts upon the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which causes the Patient to rot.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Agent may use an instrument to bring about the rotting.

annotations.definition: A Cause, animate or inanimate, causes a process, the Effect, to begin.

frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that produces an effect. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Effect: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate the process that the Cause initiates.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Effect occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is the way in which the Cause causes the process to begin.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE is for the Time when causing to start occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical or abstract setting in which the Cause results in the Effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: To what end the Cause caused the Effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: This FE identifies the first Co-timed_event of the started Effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Cause causes the Effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the target operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why or the way in which the Cause causes the Effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world at the time when the Cause produces the Effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: A consecutive event to the starting of the Effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the Depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause causes a Sleeper to transition from the Sleep_state to wakeful consciousness.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Sleeper: The person or being that is sleeping.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an Agent intentionally affects a Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent intentionally affects a Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent wakes the Sleeper.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which an intentional act is performed. Mary woke him because he looked troubled.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that produces an effect. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Sleep_state: This describes the state out of which the Sleeper is awakened.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Sleeper ends up awake.

annotations.definition: An Entity ceases to exist.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Concrete or abstract entity that does not exist after the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This is the Place where the new Entity ceases to exist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifes the Time when a new Entity ceases to be.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE labels the Explanation of the destruction or disappearance of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Components: This FE identifies the components into which the Entity dissipates.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: This FE identifies the length of time before the Entity comes into existence again.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The aspect of the Entity which no longer exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE identifies the Period_of_iterations during which habitual or iterative actions are performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Entity disappears.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The completeness of the disappearance of the Entity

annotations.definition: This frame concerns a Cognizer's certainty about the correctness of beliefs or expectations. It only includes uses where a Cognizer is expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person whose certainty or uncertainty is at issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the entity that a Cognizer has a certain/uncertain belief about.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The Content denotes the mental content that the Cognizer is certain or uncertain about.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE indicates the Degree to which the Cognizer is certain or uncertain.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The FE Manner denotes the manner of the certainty or uncertainty.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Expressor is the body part or action by a body part that conveys the mental state of the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Cognizer is certain.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Useful_location to become accessible (or non-accessible) to a Theme to a certain Degree. Instead of the Useful_location, the scene may be described with reference to a Portal (or part of the Portal, e.g., a door) to the Useful_location.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent blocks access to the Useful_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Useful_location: The Useful_location is the place the Theme is headed towards and the Agent has changed the accessibility of.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is headed towards, or has an intention or tendency to head towards, the Useful_location, but is potentially blocked due to actions of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Useful_location is or is not accessible.
frameElementDefinitions.Portal: The Agent may regulate access to the Useful_location by regulating access to a Portal, including a part of the portal that could block potential Themes, such as doors or gates.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action carried out by the Agent to bring about the change in accessibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent changes the accessibility of the Useful_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The general Place where the blocking or allowing of access occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Agent changes the accessibility of the Useful_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: A portion of the Useful_location, usually its edge, where accessibility is blocked.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Agent uses the Instrument to block access, often by placing the Instrument such that it cannot be traversed.

annotations.definition: A Theme that is in motion assumes a new Direction in which it moves.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: This is the object which moves. In many sentences this FE will be expressed as something which moves under its own power. However, this FE is not required to be an animate mover, so it is different from a self-mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal (see below) expresses the frame element Path, excluding directional expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Any expression which indicates where the Theme ends up as a result of the change of direction.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In agentive frames, it may indicate salient characteristics of an intentional Theme that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event involving the Theme that results in the Theme moving in the specified way.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the Theme changes direction.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: This is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique:
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A phrase describing the state of the Theme during the act of changing direction.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the setting where the change of direction during the Theme's motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The configuration formed by the Path that the Theme traverses during the change of direction.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The new direction in which the Theme continues its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the change of direction takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Theme wishes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_motion: The device or bodypart that serves to move the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A factor which explains the change of direction of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at the time the change of direction occurs and which the change of direction is taken to be a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that a change of direction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: A larger conceptualization that includes the change of direction as an integral part. The change of direction expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action in this larger conceptualization.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: An unexpected event that affects the Theme, which is enabled by, and occurs after, the change of direction.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the change of direction event itself and any of its participants, and is merely peripherally related to the event as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Angle: The angle between the old and the new Direction in which the Theme moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The situation that results from the change of direction

annotations.definition: In this frame, an Agent or Cause changes the duration of an Event. The Event will then take place for a New_duration, rather than the Initial_duration. This can be done with by certain Means, in a certain Manner or to a certain Degree.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity that changes the duration of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.New_duration: The new duration of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_duration: The planned duration of the Event before the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the change in duration of the Event is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the initial and final durations differ from each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event causing the change of the Event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in bringing about the change of duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event's duration is changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event that causes the change in Event duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by changing the duration of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent (or event resulting from the Cause) that produces a change in the Event's duration.

annotations.definition: In this frame, an Agent or Cause changes the timing of an Event. The Event will then take place at the Destination_time or with respect to a Destination_event. This can be done with by certain Means, in a certain Manner or to a certain Degree.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the animate entity which brings about the change in time of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-sentient force which brings about the change of time for the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occurrence which changes time.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the time change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means describe the way in which the time change is accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the extent to which the time change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Destination_event: The Destination_event is the occasion which the timing of the Event occurs with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Destination_time: The Destination_time is the time at which the Event actually occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Interval: The Interval between the Landmark_time and the Destination_time may be expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_time: The Landmark_time is when the Event was originally intended to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the reason or explanation for why the Event occurs at a different time.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Agent intends to bring about by changing the time the Event will take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the delay occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the expected time of the Event changes.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Patient changes its consistency. There may be a Initial_state and a Result. Note that this frame contrasts with Change_of_phase in that this frame describes a change in a Patient within a particular phase (solid, gas, liquid).

frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE indicates the Result of the change of consistency of the Patient
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: This is the original state of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: Patient is the entity that undergoes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the the Patient changes consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Patient changes consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Patient changes consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed at which the change of consistency occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances at which the consistency change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Patient changes consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Patient changes consistency.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns the appointment of a New_leader or removal from office of an Old_leader. The Selector brings about the change in leadership, for example, by electing or overthrowing a leader. Some words in the frame describe the successful removal from office of a leader (e.g. depose, oust), others simply the attempt (e.g. uprising, rebellion).

frameElementDefinitions.Selector: The Selector (most commonly a group of people) is responsible for a change in leadership. Typically, it occurs as the External Argument of verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Old_leader: With words denoting removal (or attempted removal) from office of a leader, the Old_leader is typically the direct object of verbs, or occurs in a PP Complement with noun targets, frequently headed by the preposition against.
frameElementDefinitions.New_leader: With words denoting appointment to a position, the New_leader is typically the object of verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Frequently the Old_leader or New_leader is referred to only by his/her role. However, the Role can be expressed separately from the leader, usually as either a secondary predicate or in a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Old_order: The FE Old_order identifies the political order that existed before the change of leadership occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE indicates the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive indicates the state of one of the participants during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE is used for the Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Selector accomplishes the change of leadership.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE is used for indicating the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This frame element describes the situation that brought about the change of leadership.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: The Function is the purpose the New_leader will fufill.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Body: A group of individuals who are elected or appointed to perform some function of governance.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Patient undergoes a change of phase. Note that this frame contrasts with Change_of_consistency in that this frame describes a change of a Patient between different phases (i.e. solid to liquid or frozen to "unfrozen").

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: Patient is the entity that undergoes a change of phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The FE Result indicates the result of the change of phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: This is the original state of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Patient changes phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed at which the change of phase occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Patient changes phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Patient changes phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which the Patient changes phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances at which the phase change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.

annotations.definition: The sequence of events in which the Patient begins as one state of matter and ends up as another.

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity that undergoes a change of phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The FE Result indicates the result of the change of phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: This is the original state of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Patient changes phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed at which the change of phase occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Patient changes phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Patient changes phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which the Patient changes phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances at which the phase change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.

annotations.definition: This frame describes a general situation in which an Owner either increases or decreases their amount of a Possession. The increase or decrease may either be physical or metaphorical.

frameElementDefinitions.Owner: The Owner is the entity that contains within itself or controls the Possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Possession: The Possession is the physical or metaphoric object that increases or decreases in quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Possession undergoes a change in quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Possession undergoes a change in quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree of the change in quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the event of increasing or decreasing in quantity logically follows.

annotations.definition: In this frame, the temperature of an Item changes. An Initial_temperature, Final_temperature, or Temperature_change can also be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity whose temperature changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_temperature: The temperature that the Item has prior to the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_temperature: The temperature of the Item after the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature_change: The amount of difference between the Initial_temperature and the Final_temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Hot_cold_source: The Hot_cold_source is the source of the heat or coolness
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event or state-of-affairs that leads to the change in temperature of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: This FE identifies the Container that holds the Item whose temperature is being changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Item during the temperature change.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The span of time in which the Item is changing temperature.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the change in temperature occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Item changes temperature.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: A portion of the Item which is affected by the temperature change.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Item changes temperature.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances under which the temperature change occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Item that results from the temperature change.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature: The scalar property that the Item possesses. This FE is rarely expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the change in temperature takes place.

annotations.definition: A Device or machine is put in (or out of) service either by a volitional Agent or by a Cause event or force acting on the Device. The Time when and the Place where the Device is put in or out of use may be specified, as may be the Purpose of the human Agent in changing the operational state of the Device.

frameElementDefinitions.Device: The Device or machine that is in or out of operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when a Device is put into or out of operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place in which the Device is put into or out of operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who changes the operational state of the Device.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: Cause identifies the non-agentive force that causes the Device to begin to operate.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The FE Purpose denotes a state of affairs that the Agent wants to bring about by putting the Device into operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the event of the Device's being caused to operate or cease to operate logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In agentive uses, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Device is active after the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action done by the Agent in order to cause the Device to being to operate.

annotations.definition: This frame consists of words that indicate the change of an Item's position on a scale (the Attribute) from a starting point (Initial_value) to an end point (Final_value). The direction (Path) of the movement can be indicated as well as the magnitude of the change (Difference). The rate of change of the value (Speed) is optionally indicated. Another scale (Correlate), which the values are correlated with, is indicated if it is not the default correlate (namely, absolute time).

frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The Attribute is a scalar property that the Item possesses.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity that has a position on the scale.
frameElementDefinitions.Difference: The distance by which an Item changes its position on the scale.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any expression that gives information about points on the scale that the Item traverses between the starting and ending point of its movement along the scale.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_value: The position on the scale where the Item ends up.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_value: The initial position on the scale from which the Item moves away.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate of change of the Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Correlated_variable: The Correlated_variable is an ordered progression of positions or values (most often a sequence of spatial locations) that the (dependent) Attribute is measured against. The Correlated_variable is metaphorically mapped onto the progression of time, and in the absence of this FE, changes are simply measured against (forward-directional) time.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the change of position takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result. of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group in which an Item changes the value of a Attribute in a specified way.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is the time-frame in which the change of position occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Value_range: A portion of the scale, typically identified by its end points, along which the values of the Attribute fluctuate.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_correlate: The value, location, or state-of-affairs that corresponds to the Initial_value of the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_correlate: The value, location, or state-of-affairs that corresponds to the Final_value of the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A location where Attribute is measured
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: A description that presents the Item's state before the change in the Attribute's value as an independent predication.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_state: A description that presents the Item's state after the change in the Attribute's value as an independent predication.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the quantity change takes place and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The situation or state-of-affairs that results in the change in quantity described in this instance of the frame.

annotations.definition: The frame represents the result state of the Entity after event represented by the Undergo_change frame. The intial states of the Undergo_change frame Initial_quality and Initial_situation are also FEs of this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The entity which has undergone change.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist changes the overall position and posture of the body.

frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the change in posture which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Protagonist that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The individual whose posture changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Protagonist's state throughout the time they are changing posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The place or setting in which the Protagonist's change of posture takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Protagonist changes posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The final state of the Protagonist after the change of posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is used for expressions that indicate the trajectory of motion without referring to the Source or the Goal of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE is used for any expressions that indicate the endpoint of the posture change. Often this is the place that provides support for the posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is used for expressions that indicate the starting point of the change of posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE is used for expressions that indicate a line from the deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that is neither the Goal of the posture change nor a landmark along the way of the moving part of the body. Often Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the Protagonist, or the orientation imposed by an implicit observer.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which a Protagonist assumes a posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is used for expressions that indicate the extent to which the body (part) moves during the change of posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Point_of_contact: The part of the body that provides support to the Protagonist in holding the posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Protagonist whereby they achieve the change of posture.

annotations.definition: An Agent changes a Patient's ability to resist literal or figurative attack. This frame differs from Cause_change_of_strength only in that the Patient is restricted to acting as a defensive force, i.e., it can resist but not exert force.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes the Patient more resistant to attack.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity that is being made more resistant.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The resultant state of the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by an Agent that enables them to act upon the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent makes the Patient more resistant.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Agent makes the Patient more resistant.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which causes the Patient to become more resistant.
frameElementDefinitions.Attack: This FE describes the literal or figurative Attack that the Agent is strengthening the Patient for.

annotations.definition: An Agent changes Tools, from the Old_tool to a New_tool. The Tools may include vehicles, services providers, or more proto-typical implements.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that changes their situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent makes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The aim of the Agent when they carry out the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent acts intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Agent which brings about a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Agent's change which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Tools: The entities controlled by an Agent to achieve some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Old_tool: The tool that the Agent had been using.
frameElementDefinitions.New_tool: The tool that the Agent ends up using.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that the action goes from start to finish.

annotations.definition: The lexical units in this frame have to do with an Entity being in a State of order or disorder. Often the Entity is unexpressed in this frame and is, therefore, marked as a null instantiation.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The object or phenomenon which exists in the State of disorder. It is common for the Entity to be null instantiated in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State in which the Entity exists. Usually the State will be the target-denoting noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the State exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity exists in the State.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time in which the Entity exists in the State
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Entity exists in the State.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterization of the State not covered by a more specific frame element.

annotations.definition: A group of people (the Interlocutors or Interlocutor_1 and Interlocutor_2 together) have a conversation. No person is construed as only a speaker or only an addressee. Rather, it is understood that both (or all) participants do some speaking and some listening--the process is understood to be symmetrical or reciprocal. In this frame, the purpose of the conversation is generally social, rather than specifically to decide something or exchange information, or to quarrel (see Discussion and Quarreling).

frameElementDefinitions.Interlocutors: The group of individuals involved in chatting.
frameElementDefinitions.Interlocutor_1: The individual or individuals that form the more prominent (i.e. agentive) party of a conversation, as compared with Interlocutor_2. That is, Interlocutor_1 is generally encoded as the External Argument of verbs, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to Interlocutor_1. Thus a Purpose (or Means, Depictive, or Manner) is construed to be that of Interlocutor_1, not necessarily that of Interlocutor_2 or the group of Interlocutors as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Interlocutor_2: The semantically (and grammatically) less prominent group of participants in a conversation. It is generally encoded within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by with).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the chatting goes on.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The general subject that the statements of the Interlocutors in the conversation pertain to.
frameElementDefinitions.Language: The language(s) or dialect(s) that a conversation is conducted in.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_communication: The device used by the Interlocutors to communicate.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: What the Interlocutors (or Interlocutor_1) hope to accomplish by chatting.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which a chat takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where a chat takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The way that the Interlocutors succeed in having a conversation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the conversation which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Interlocutors (or Interlocutor_1) that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the state of the Interlocutors (or Interlocutor_1) during the conversation.

annotations.definition: This frame covers the descriptions of tastes and smells, and has one main frame element, Source. The frame element Sensory_attribute occurs mainly with the words taste, flavour, and smell.

frameElementDefinitions.Perceptual_source: Perceptual_source is used for the entity that is being described. A recipe can stand in for a Perceptual_source which is a kind of food.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which a chemical sense is described.
frameElementDefinitions.Sensory_attribute: Sensory_attribute is used for words that describe the perception itself.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that describe how potent a chemical is.

frameElementDefinitions.Chemical_entity: The entity for which potency is being evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the potency from the norm expressed by the target LU.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time during which the Chemical_entity is in the state of having a particular potency.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Chemical_entity has the ascribed potency.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Chemical_entity has the ascribed potency.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer decides upon the Chosen (either an item or a course of action) out of a set of Possibilities . The Cognizer may have an Inherent_purpose or a Larger_purpose for the Chosen. Often an Explanation, which serves as the basis of the choice, is given.

frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive labels an independent descriptor of a Cognizer in a choosing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer chooses the Chosen out of the Possibilities.
frameElementDefinitions.Chosen: Chosen identifies the entity or course of action which is selected from among the Possibilities.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Cognizer chooses the Chosen.
frameElementDefinitions.Possibilities: A Cognizer makes a choice from among a set of Possibilities. Possibilities are commonly expressed via an oblique phrase which indicates the alternative or alternatives, or by a subordinate clause (choosing to do it or choosing not to do it), typically headed by "whether or "if".
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which bthe Cognizer accomplishes the choosing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the choosing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Larger_purpose: This FE identifies the Larger_purpose for which the Cognizer chooses. It exemplifies what the Cognizer hopes to accomplish by choosing the particular Chosen.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Cognizer chooses the Chosen.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the selecting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of a Cognizer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic occurs quite rarely in this frame and usually only as a PP Complement of a noun target:
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The Cognizer intends the Chosen to fulfill an Inherent_purpose .
frameElementDefinitions.Contrast: The hypothetical Chosen that is less desirable for the Cognizer.

annotations.definition: An Entity is understood to be experienceable within a particular set of delimited Circumstances, which may be more narrowly expressed by a Time, Place.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: An object or event that is described as being available under delimited Conditions.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A temporal characterization of the circumstances of the existence of an Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A spacial characterization of the Circumstances of the existence of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: A description of the state of the world in which the Entity can be experienced.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Properties of the Entity as it exists in the stated Circumstances.

annotations.definition: A Defendant is officially summoned to appear in Court by an Authority (such as the police) based on certain Charges. Alternatively, there is often an option for the Defendant to pay a Fine (thereby admitting their guilt) instead of going to Court.

frameElementDefinitions.Defendant: The Defendant is the individual or entity summoned to appear in Court, or to pay a Fine instead.
frameElementDefinitions.Court: The Court where the Defendant is summoned to appear.
frameElementDefinitions.Authority: The Authority serve a citation or summons to a Defendant, requiring that the latter appear in Court or pay a Fine.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: Charges identifies the infraction or offense for which the Defendant is summoned.
frameElementDefinitions.Fine: Fine is the monetary amount that the Defendant may pay in certain citation cases, instead of him or her having to appear in Court.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place is the location where the Defendant is issued the citation or summons.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Defendant is issued the citation or summons.

annotations.definition: A Claimant asserts his or her right to possession of a piece of Property (either real, such as land, or private). The Claimant may be acting on the behalf of a Beneficiary.

frameElementDefinitions.Claimant: The Claimant asserts rights or priviliges, typically ownership, over some Property.
frameElementDefinitions.Property: The Property is declared to be under the ownership of the Claimant.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The Beneficiary is declared the owner of the Property by the Claimant, or the person or group in whose name or favor the Claimant is acting.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The Role of the land claimed.

annotations.definition: An Executive_authority exercises their power to reduce or remove negative consequences for someone who has been labeled an Offender who participated in an Crime.

frameElementDefinitions.Offender: The individual who is accused or convicted of a Crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Crime: An action deemed criminal by law.
frameElementDefinitions.Executive_authority: The constitutionally specified authority who has the power to grant clemency.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Executive_authority is responding to in granting clemency.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which clemency is granted.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Executive_authority that accomplishes the granting of clemency.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the act of clemency which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of an Executive_authority that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Executive_authority intends to accomplish by granting clemency.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Executive_authority grants clemency.

annotations.definition: An Agent manipulates a Fastener to open or close a Containing_object (e.g. coat, jar). Sometimes an Enclosed_region or a Container_portal may be expressed. Since the Manipulator is syntactically omissible, many verbs in this frame incorporate the Fastener.

frameElementDefinitions.Containing_object: This FE identifies the item that is closed by the agent with the fastener. Lee buttoned his coat.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent opens/closes the Containing-object.
frameElementDefinitions.Fastener: This FE identifies the fastener that the agent manipulates.
frameElementDefinitions.Enclosed_region: This FE identifies the region enclosed by the container-object that is closed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manipulator: This FE identifies the instrument or part of the body that manipulates the Fastener.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_portal: This FE identifies the active zone of the container that may be expressed as the direct object of the target verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Container_portal/Containing_object ends up closed.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity manipulated by the Agent that accomplishes the closure of the Containing_object or the Container_portal.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the closing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Agent that accomplishes the closure of the Container_portal or Containing_object.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the closing event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent closes the Container_portal or the Containing_object.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The resultant state of the Closure action.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The person for whom the Closure event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE describes the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the Closure event and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.

annotations.definition: This frame refers to clothing and its characteristics, including anything that people conventionally wear. The Wearer wears a Garment that has a certain Style, is made of a certain Material, and can be otherwise characterized by a Descriptor. In some cases, the Body_location (where the garment is worn) and Subregion of the clothing may also be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Garment: This FE identifes the clothing worn.
frameElementDefinitions.Wearer: The Wearer is the person wearing the clothes, or for whom the clothes are intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Style: This FE identifes the design, or cut, of the clothing.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: This FE identifes the material from which the clothing is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE identifes any description of the clothing besides Style and Material.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: This FE identifies a part of the clothing.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: This FE identifes the Use for which a Garment is worn or was made.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_location: Body_location identifies the place on the body where the clothing is worn.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The individual who created the Garment.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which the Creator makes the Garment.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The name given to a specific Garment. Note this is not the name of a general class of clothing items, but rather an individual item. This is accordingly VERY RARE.

annotations.definition: The Subparts of Clothing items worn by a Wearer and made of a certain Material. The Subpart may itself have parts, which we label Subregion:

frameElementDefinitions.Clothing: This is the Clothing of which the target word is a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Subpart: This is a Subpart of the Clothing item.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The Material from which the Subpart is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: A part of the Clothing item.
frameElementDefinitions.Wearer: The person who is wearing the clothes, or for whom the clothes are intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_location: This is the part of the body were the garment is worn.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE identifes any description of the clothing besides Material.

annotations.definition: Two or more individuals have a relationship by virtue of belonging to the same organization or institution. They may or may not be personally acquainted, and may or may not have chosen to have the relationship.

frameElementDefinitions.Participant1: The profiled member of a co-association relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Participants: The members of a Co-association relationship (collectively).
frameElementDefinitions.Participant2: The backgrounded member(s) of a Co-association relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place at which the Co-association relationship exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time during which the co-association relationship exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Institution: The institution or organization that the Participants have in common.

annotations.definition: A person, the Cognizer, thinks about a Topic over a period of time. What is thought about may be a course of action that the person might take, or something more general.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: With a target verb, the Cognizer is usually expressed as an External Argument, with the Topic appearing as an Object NP, a gerundive verbal Complement, or a PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: With a target verb, the Topic is usually expressed as an Object NP, a gerundive verbal complement, or a PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The FE Degree indicates the degree to which the cognizing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The FE Manner indicates the way in which the cognizing is being done.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Cognizer that makes them able to cogitate.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physical or abstract setting in which the Cognizer considers the Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Cognizer is trying to bring about by thinking.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Cognizer considers the Topic.

annotations.definition: A concept, Concept_1, is related causally or collocationally to another concept, Concept_2 by means of some evidence. They may be related only under certain Circumstances or from a certain Point_of_view. The two concepts may be expressed collectively as associated Concepts. Any cognizer is deprofiled.

frameElementDefinitions.Concept_1: The concept which is linked to Concept_2. Generally expressed as the external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Concept_2: The concept which is tied to Concept_1. Generally expressed as a PP complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Point_of_view: The point of view from which the connection exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the connection exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree of connection between the Concepts.
frameElementDefinitions.Specification: A further specification on the linkage.

annotations.definition: A Phenomenon (an external or cognitive event or situation) comes into the awareness of an Experiencer, resulting in a perceptually punctual change in the Experiencer's conception of the Phenomenon or entities and facts associated with it.

frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon is the entity that evokes a cognitive reaction for the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer in the entity who experiences the cognitive impact brought about by the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Judgment: The Judgment is the characterization of the cognitive impact or evaluation of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The physical or abstract Circumstances that determine the Judgment of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The extent to which the cognitive impact of a Phenomenon affects the Judgment ascribed to it.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with with the Experiencer experiences the Phenomenon.

annotations.definition: A State_of_affairs (a state or event) either holds or occurs, and is presented as being causally independent of other (implicit) states or events that might otherwise have been expected to be causally related to the State_of_affairs. The Time or Place may also be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: A State_of_affairs (that is, an event or state) that holds independently of (potentially) causally-related States of affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the State_of_affairs holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the State_of_affairs holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the target word not covered by a more specific FE.

annotations.definition: Partner_1 and Partner_2 or a group of Partners work together in some Undertaking. The Partners are taken to be of equal status even when expressed disjointly.

frameElementDefinitions.Partner_1: When the parties in a collaboration are expressed separately, this Frame Element marks constituents that express the one that is grammatically more prominent, i.e., the one that is the subject of an active clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Partner_2: This Frame element marks constituents expressing the grammatically less prominent, typically oblique, party in a collaboration.
frameElementDefinitions.Undertaking: This frame element marks constituents that express the project in which the Partners are collaborating.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which Partner_1 collaborates with Partner_2 or in which the Partners collaborate.
frameElementDefinitions.Partners: This frame element marks expressions that express the parties that work together in an Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Partners are engaged in their collaboration.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the collaboration occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the amount of time the Partners collaborate.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE marks expressions that indicate to what extent the Partners are cooperating.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action taken by the Partners that lets them accomplish the collaboration.

annotations.definition: A Profiled_region is specified as entirely or largely coinciding with that of a Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: A region of space located at a Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the Profiled_region.

annotations.definition: A group of Colonists from a Homeland migrate to and proceed to settle in a New_area. The New_area then becomes their permanent home. The New_area and Homeland are in some way construed as being part of the same organization or system. In the case of (people from) nation-states colonizing other areas, the colony and nation-state are considered a single political entity. In the extended sense of living things in general colonizing or settling a new area, the original Homeland must still inhabited by some of the same group. In this sense Colonization is distinct from migration or exodus.

frameElementDefinitions.Colonists: A group of people who move together to an area outside their Homeland for the purpose of settling.
frameElementDefinitions.Homeland: The Homeland is where the Colonists are originally from.
frameElementDefinitions.New_area: The Colonists move to the New_area, inhabiting it and (in the case of nation-states) extending the control of the Homeland.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the colonizing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the group of Colonists spreads across or inhabits the New_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the colonizing is done.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Colonists, Homeland, or New_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the colonization event logically follows.

annotations.definition: A Color serves as a landmark in color-space, either a point-like landmark (e.g. burnt sienna) or a broader region (e.g. blue). Especially when the Color designates a broad region in color-space, it may be defined with a specific Type, further specified by comparison to the color of a Comparand, modified by a Color_qualifier, or evaluated with a Descriptor. All color words are generally used to specify the color of some (physical) Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: This FE identifies the Entity described by the Color.
frameElementDefinitions.Color: A position in color-space, generally used to describe the quality of some Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category of a Color is indicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparand: A physical entity (or type of physical entity) whose color is similar to the designated color. When this FE occurs with adjective targets, (a relatively rare circumstance) the Comparand also serves to specify a Type, which should be labeled on the second FE layer.
frameElementDefinitions.Color_qualifier: A phrase that indicates the direction in color-space from the center of the region designated by the Color.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Some characteristic or judgment of a Color implicitly imposed by a speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The state of affairs that the Color is dependent on.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The closeness of the Entity 's color to the center of a Color prototype.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Entity that has the indicated Color.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The aspect of the Entity which is under discussion, namely color.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that describe specific aspects of a Color. The Degree to which the Color being discussed deviates from the prototype may be marked. The description may also target a specific Aspect of color.

frameElementDefinitions.Color: The color that is being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The level to which the Color is representative of a specific quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Described_entity: The entity whose Color is being described.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist comes to suffer from a Disease, often implicitly acquired from a Source.

frameElementDefinitions.Source: The individual or environmental reservoir of a Disease that the Protagonist was exposed to.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Protagonist contracts the Disease.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Protagonist contracts the Disease. It is different from the Source (usually marked with "from") in that it does not necessarily mean that it was contact with the Place that actually led to infection.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event as a whole in comparison to other events ("the same way") or in terms of details of the event that affect the overall event ("rapidly").

annotations.definition: A Binding_principle, previously only existing as a concept or formulation, enters an "active" phase or state. Often, the enactments of rules and regulations are described with this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Binding_principle: This FE describes the Binding_principle which comes into effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which an action is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time for which the Binding_principle is ongoing after it has come into effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that the specified type of Event begins.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for why Event occurs.

annotations.definition: In this frame a group of Individuals meet to form a Configuration. The Individuals or the Configuration may be expressed as the external argument of the predicates in this frame. In this frame, it is ambiguous whether or not the Individuals intentionally come together. In an alternative construal, a grammatically more prominent Party_1 congregates with a Party_2, expressed as an oblique phrase. If this asymmetric construal is chosen, Purpose relates only to Party_1.

frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: The Individuals are self-movers who come together.
frameElementDefinitions.Configuration: The Configuration describes the resulting formation of the individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means descibes the way in which the Individuals accomplish the congregating.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the congregating occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the congregating occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Configuration plans to engage in upon assembly.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The position in a series of iterations that the current congregating event occupies .
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Individuals come together to form an Configuration.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of congregating which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Individuals or Configuration that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: A Topic of a discussion that may occur between the Individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: Party_1 is the grammatically more prominent participant in the congregating event.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: A grammatically less prominent participant in the congregating event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
