annotations.definition: An Entity comes into existence at a particular Place and Time which may take a certain Duration_of_endstate, have a Cause, or be formed from Components.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Concrete or abstract entity that did not exist before the Time of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This is the Place where the new Entity comes into being.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifes the Time when a new Entity comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE labels the Explanation of the formation of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Components: This FE identifies the components out of which the Entity forms.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: This FE identifies the length of time that the Entity exists after coming to be.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The form in which the Entity comes to be.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE identifies the Period_of_iterations during which habitual or iterative actions are performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Entity comes to be.

annotations.definition: A person (the Cognizer) comes to believe something (the Content), sometimes after a process of reasoning. This change in belief is usually initiated by a person or piece of Evidence. Occasionally words in this domain are accompanied by phrases expressing Topic, i.e. that which the mental Content is about.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: Cognizer is the person who comes to believe something.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: Words in this frame may occur with a PP headed by from which expresses the Evidence on which knowledge or belief is based:
frameElementDefinitions.Content: With a target verb, the Content is usually expressed as a finite clausal Complement or an Object NP, and may sometimes be expressed by a PP:
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Some verbs in this frame may occur with postverbal Topic expressions: They found out about us! More generally verbs in this frame may occur with quantificational nouns followed by Topic expressions:
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Cognizer comes to believe the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Cognizer which enables them to figure something out.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Cognizer discovers the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the text created by the Cognizer to communicate that they came to believe a specified Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Cognizer realizes the Content.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame have to do with a Cognizer creating a new intellectual entity, the Idea. These words are similar to words of physical creation such as build and make, and in some cases may be understood as metaphorically based on such words. However, the Ideas in this frame are predominantly conceptual in nature. Compare this frame with Achieving_first. In that frame, the fact that the idea is new to society is emphasized, whereas Coming_up_with refers to an agentive Cognizer using mental effort to figure something out, without any implication of the impact of the idea.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: Cognizer is the person who comes up with or conceptualizes the Invention. It is normally expressed as an External Argument: Kim devised a new recipe.
frameElementDefinitions.Idea: Invention is the intellectual creation of the Cognizer. It is normally expressed as an NP Object, but sometimes as a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Some of the words in this frame frequently occur with a constituent expressing the Purpose for which the Idea is intended. Normally this constituent is a PP Complement headed by for or a to-marked VP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Words in this frame sometimes occur with a constituent that expresses the intellectual material from which the Idea is created. This frame element is characteristic of physical creation verbs like make; its presence gives sentences in this frame an especially strong metaphorical quality. The Material FE is normally expressed by a PP Complement headed by from or out of:
frameElementDefinitions.Amount_of_progress: The extent to which the Cognizer succeeds in coming up with the idea.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of a focal participant during the time the invention takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A state of the Cognizer that directly and palpably influences the overall action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Cognizer that contributes to the creation of the Idea.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_appearance: (This FE belongs in Achieving_first, not here.)
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Cognizer comes up with the Idea.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Idea is created.

annotations.definition: This frame describes a particular compounding construction (a pattern of coinage) by which more complex words can be created by joining an Honored_entity to a Dedicated_type to describe a particular Dedicated_entity which has been officially named for the Honored_entity. The Dedicated_type and Honored_entity FEs each form part of the Lexical Units (annotated on the second FE layer), and the Dedicated_entity is the denotation of the whole. Since each of the FEs is a part of the Lexical Unit, there is no assignment of Grammatical Function or Phrase Type. The Dedicated_type should (in principle) also be annotated as a LU in a separate frame. Targets are added opportunistically to this frame in the course of annotating other frames. For example, the Day of Armistice Day should also be annotated in the Calendric_unit frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Honored_entity: This FE identifies the person, subject, or event that is being honored.
frameElementDefinitions.Dedicated_type: This FE identifies the type of entity that had been dedicated to honor the Honored_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Dedicated_entity: The entity which has been dedicated to the Honored_entity.

annotations.definition: These are words describing a basic commercial transaction involving a Buyer and a Seller exchanging Money and Goods, taking the perspective of the Buyer. The words vary individually in the patterns of frame element realization they allow. For example, the typical pattern for the verb BUY: Buyer buys Goods from Seller for Money.

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer wants the Goods and offers Money to a Seller in exchange for them.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor or time, for example) which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The Seller has possession of the Goods and exchanges them for Money from a Buyer.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the purchasing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Buyer that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual intended by the Buyer to receive the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the commerce event began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Buyer's intended purpose for the Goods.

annotations.definition: Subframe of Commerce_money-transfer in which the Seller comes to have the Money.

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer wants the Goods and offers Money to a Seller in exchange for them.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor or time, for example) which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The Seller has possession of the Goods and exchanges them for Money from a Buyer.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the collection event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Seller that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: The subframe of the Commercial_transaction in which the Seller gives the Goods to the Buyer (in exchange for the Money).

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer wants the Goods and offers Money to a Seller in exchange for them.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor or time, for example) which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which a commercial transaction occurs. Abby bought the car with cash. Robin paid for the car by check.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The Seller has possession of the Goods and exchanges them for Money from a Buyer.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the transfer not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Buyer, Seller, or Exchangers that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Buyer, Seller, or Exchangers wish to bring about by participating in the exchange.
frameElementDefinitions.Exchangers: The Buyer and Seller considered jointly.

annotations.definition: The subframe of the Commercial_transaction frame which involves the transfer of Money from the Buyer to the Seller (in exchange for the Goods).

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer wants the Goods and offers Money to a Seller in exchange for them.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor or time, for example) which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The Seller has possession of the Goods and exchanges them for Money from a Buyer.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the transfer not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Buyer, Seller, or Exchangers that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Buyer, Seller, or Exchangers wish to bring about by participating in the exchange.
frameElementDefinitions.Exchangers: The individuals participating in the commercial exchange, otherwise encoded as Buyer and Seller.

annotations.definition: This frame involves Buyers paying Money for Goods. In this frame the Money is the direct object, and is mapped to the theme of the transfer.

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer has the Money and wants the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor or time, for example) which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which a commercial transaction occurs. Abby bought the car with cash. Robin paid for the car by check.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The Seller has the Goods and wants the Money.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the paying event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Buyer that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: Commerce is a situation in which a Buyer and a Seller have agreed upon an exchange of Money and Goods (possibly after a negotiation), and then perform the exchange, optionally carrying it out with various kinds of direct payment or financing or the giving of change. The Seller indicates their willingness to give the Goods in their possession to a Buyer who would give them some amount of Money. The Seller may have already decided on the amount of money that they would require, in which case it is called the Asking price. The Buyer also indicates their willingness to give an amount of money called an Offer to a Seller who would give them the Goods. Normally the process is begun by the Seller. The means by which the Seller indicates their wish to engage in an exchange are various, ranging from putting a price tag on an item on a store shelf, to advertizing, to communicating directly and specifically with a possible Buyer. In some cases, however, the process may be initiated by the Buyer indicating to a possible Seller that they would like to make an exchange.

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer has the Money and wants the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The Seller has the Goods and wants the Money.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: Goods is anything including labor or time, for example, which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: A state of affairs that the agent intends to bring about as a result of participating in the Commercial Transaction.

annotations.definition: These are words describing basic commercial transactions involving a buyer and a seller exchanging money and goods, taking the perspective of the seller. The words vary individually in the patterns of frame element realization they allow. For example, the typical patterns for SELL: SELLER sells GOODS to BUYER for MONEY.

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer has the Money and wants the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor or time, for example) which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The Seller has possession of the Goods and exchanges them for Money from a Buyer.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the selling event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Seller that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Seller after the sale has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the commerce event began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Buyer's intended purpose for the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Back: This FE indicates that the selling act reverses an earlier separate act in which the current Seller bought the Goods from the current Buyer.
frameElementDefinitions.Relay: This FE indicates that the selling act constitues a re-selling by the current Seller of Goods that they bought earlier from a third person who is not the Buyer of the current selling act.

annotations.definition: These are words that describe basic commercial transactions involving a Buyer and a Seller who exchange Money and Goods. The individual words vary in the frame element realization patterns. For example, the typical patterns for the verbs buy and sell are: BUYER buys GOODS from the SELLER for MONEY. SELLER sells GOODS to the BUYER for MONEY.

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer wants the Goods and offers Money to a Seller in exchange for them.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor or time, for example) which is exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: Price or payment per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The Seller has possession of the Goods and exchanges them for Money from a Buyer.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: The Unit of measure of the Goods according to which the exchange value of the Goods (or services) is set. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.

annotations.definition: A Speaker makes a commitment to an Addressee to carry out some future action. This may be an action desirable (as with promise) or not desirable (as with threaten) to the Addressee. Some of the words in this frame allow an Addressee to be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the person who commits him/herself to do something. Speaker is usually the External Argument of predicative uses of the target or the Genitive modifier of a noun: Jo made a promise to me. Jo promised me she would cook dinner. Jo's promise to cook dinner came to nothing.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Speaker's commitment can be made to an Addressee. With those words which allow this frame element to be expressed, Addressee usually occurs as a PP Complement headed by to or the (Indirect) Object of verbs: Kim made a promise to me. Kim promised me that everything would be okay.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: An expression of the commitment made by the Speaker expresses the frame element Message. Message is expressed as a finite or non-finite clausal Complement or an NP Object: I swore that I would never make the same mistake again. The owner finally consented to sell the business. They threatened revenge.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic expressions are not very frequent in this frame. They can occur with noun targets. Jo made endless promises about working harder at school.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: As with other frames in the Communication domain, the Medium of communication may be expressed. Medium is the physical entity or channel used to transmit the Message. Bob threatened the workers in Spanish.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the commitment is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Speaker makes the commitment.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Speaker makes the commitment.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Speaker intends to accomplish by taking on the commitment.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE is used to indicate length of time from when Commitment event began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: A Perpetrator (generally intentionally) commits a Crime, i.e. does something not permitted by the laws of society.

frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: The individual that commits a Crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Crime: An act, generally intentional, that has been formally forbidden by law.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The action that the Perpetrator was trying to accomplish in the Crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Perpetrator that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Perpetrator in order to accomplish the Crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Perpetrator is responding to in their Crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Crime was committed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Crime occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument used in committing the crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frequency with which a Crime is committed.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that describe a set of individuals which possess some Commonality, generally a common attribute.

frameElementDefinitions.Entities: This FE denotes the people in possession of the Commonality. This FE is usually genetive, the object of a postnominal PP, or null instantiated (INI), and null instantiaton often implies the public at large, as in "the common good".
frameElementDefinitions.Commonality: This FE denotes an object or attribute which is jointly posessed by the Entities.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity_1: One of the possesors of the Commonality. Can be singular or plural.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity_2: One of the possesors of the Commonality. Can be singular or plural.

annotations.definition: A Speaker communicates a message stating an Item's membership in a Category, i.e., asserts that the Item has the defining characteristics of the Category. Alternatively, the message is conceived as communicated by a Medium.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The individual that communicates the message concerning the Category of an Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Category: The class of entities with characteristics that match those of the Item according to the portrayal of the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity that the Speaker portrays as belonging to a Category.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action taken by the Speaker through which they communicate that the Item fits into a particular Category.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The piece of text in which a Speaker communicates their categorization of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point in time at which the Speaker makes a characterization of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Speaker characterizes the Item
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the categorization is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Criteria: The characteristics of an Item that fit the defining characteristics of a Category, and thus serve as the basis of the Speakers characterization of the Item .
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of a Speaker that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.

annotations.definition: A Communicator conveys a Message to an Addressee; the Topic and Medium of the communication also may be expressed. This frame includes no specification of the method of communication (speech, writing, gesture, etc.). This frame and the frames that inherit the general Communication frame can add elaboration to the Medium in a variety of ways (in French, on the radio program, in a letter) or to the Manner of communication (babble, rant, shout, whisper). There are also frames related to Communication that either do not inherit all of the FEs of this frame or do not inherit them in a straightforward manner (such as Conversation, in which Communicator and Addressee alternate roles, and are often expressed by a single, plural NP).

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The sentient entity that uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a Message to the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee receives a Message from the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: Message is a proposition or set of propositions that the Communicator wants the Addressee to believe or take for granted.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject matter to which the Message pertains. It is normally expressed as a PP Complement headed by "about", but in some cases it can appear as a direct object.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Amount_of_information: The amount of information exchanged when communication occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes the state of the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Communicator communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Communicator communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the communication takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration is the length of time during which the communication takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE describes the number of times per unit of time that the Communicator conveys a Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the act of communication occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which a Communicator communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Quote: The Quote is the concrete record of a communication act.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe Manners of verbal communication. All of them can occur with quoted expressions.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: Speaker is the sentient entity that produces a Message or communicates about a Topic. It is expressed as the External Argument of verbs:
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: Addressee is the person to whom the Speaker is communicating. When expressed, the Addressee occurs as a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Message: Message is the content which is communicated by the Speaker. The Message may be a direct quote, a finite complement clause or an NP Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is the subject matter of the communicated Message. It is normally expressed as a PP Complement headed by about and, in this frame, is frequently preceded by a quantificational noun which is treated as referring to the Message:
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This frame element expresses the medium of communication, such as the language used or the specific type of text in which the Message occurs. It is normally expressed as a PP-complement headed by on or in:
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive is used for any phrase describing a participant in an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Back: This FE indicates that the communication act denoted by the target is in response to an earlier communication act in which the roles of Speaker and Addressee were reversed. This is conceptually akin to the Communication_response frame in which the targets themselves denote the responsive nature of the act.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the communicative event lasts.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the communication event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the communication event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that the Speaker performs in order to communicate in the particular way described by the target.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns Communicators communicating with each other with the aid of a Means of communication such as a telephone.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: This FE is the person who uses the Means of communication to deliver the Message to the Addressee. The Speaker is normally expressed as the External Argument of target verbs, or as the Genitive modifier of nouns.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: Addressee is the person to whom the Message is communicated. When this FE is expressed, it often appears in a prepositional phrase introduced by to, or as the direct object, or as the first object in a double object construction.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: This FE identifies the content that the Communicator communicates to the Addressee. It can be expressed as a clause or as a noun phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is the subject matter to which the Message pertains. It is typically expressed as a PP Complement headed by about.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This Frame Element is for expressions that elaborate the manner in which an action is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This Frame Element refers to the Instrument of Communication that is used by the Communicator (and Addressee). Each LU in this frame incorporates the Means frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for using the means of communication.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Communicator or Message during the communication.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for types of noise which can be used to characterize verbal communication. It inherits from Communication (possibly more specifically Communication_manner) and the Sound_emission frame (which simply characterizes basic sounds of whatever source, including those made by animals and inanimate objects). As such, it involves a Speaker who produces noise and thus communicates a Message to an Addressee.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: Speaker is the sentient entity that delivers the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee refers to the one toward whom the Communication_noise is directed. Addressee generally occurs in to or at PPs.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: Message has a number of different realizations in this frame, including that-clauses, direct quotes, infinitival complements, for-to phrases, and NP Objects.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic occurs in PP Complements, usually headed by about.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is used for a physical entity or channel that transmits the Message. It is normally expressed as a PP Complement headed by on or in.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the state of the Speaker during the communication.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner describes the manner or style in which the Communication_noise is delivered.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The Communication_noise may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause, the Speaker's mental or emotional state. Internal_cause is expressed in a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Speaker communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Voice: The Voice describes the Speaker's utterance or other message-encoded production.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Back: This FE indicates that the communication act denoted by the target is in response to an earlier communication act in which the roles of Speaker and Addressee were reversed. This is conceptually akin to the Communication_response frame in which the targets themselves denote the responsive nature of the act.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time where the Communication noise event occurs.

annotations.definition: This frame deals with a Speaker communicating a reply or response, a Message, to some prior communication or action, the Trigger.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the sentient entity that communicates the reply or response. It is expressed as the External Argument of predicative uses of a Target verb or as the Genitive modifier of a Target noun:
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: This is the person to whom the response is communicated. When expressed, this FE occurs as the direct object of a Target verb, or in a prepositional phrase introduced by "to":
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message is the FE that identifies the content of what the Speaker is communicating to the Addressee. It can be expressed as a direct quote or a clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic occurs quite rarely in this frame and usually only as a PP Complement of a noun Target:
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: As with Medium in other Communication frames, the physical entity or channel used to communicate can be expressed, usually as a PP Complement headed by "on" or "in":
frameElementDefinitions.Trigger: The Trigger is the prior communication or action to which a response is given. It can occur as an NP Object or a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by the Speaker achieves the communication of the response Message .
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The response may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause, the Agent's mental or emotional state. Internal_cause is expressed in a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Speaker makes a verbal response.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Speaker makes their verbal response.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Speaker intends to accomplish by making a verbal response.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration of the communication response.

annotations.definition: An Executive_authority exchanges one penalty, the Original_punishment, for another less severe one, the New_punishment. The Offender is who received the Original_punishment is usually expressed as a modifier.

frameElementDefinitions.Offender: The individual who was convicted of a crime and given a sentence (the Original_punishment).
frameElementDefinitions.Executive_authority: The constitutionally specified authority who has the power to commute punishments.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Executive_authority is responding to in commuting the Original_punishment.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Original_punishment is commuted.
frameElementDefinitions.Original_punishment: The punishment the Offender was originally sentenced to.
frameElementDefinitions.New_punishment: The new reduced form of punishment that the Executive_authority orders for the Offender.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the commutation which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of an Executive_authority that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Executive_authority commutes the Original_punishment.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Executive_authority that accomplishes the commutation.

annotations.definition: LUs in this frame describe a commutative operation of arithmetic, e.g. addition and multiplication, involving two numbers, labeled Term1 and Term2 or, collectively, Terms. When referencing the process, the numbers can be vague and are often omitted through indefinite null instantiation. There may also be a Calculator who applies the operation.

frameElementDefinitions.Term1: The grammatically more prominent number involved in a commutative operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Term2: The grammatically less prominent number involved in commutative operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Terms: Two more or elements of a commutative arithmetic operation. This frame element may be represented as Term1 and Term2.
frameElementDefinitions.Calculator: An entity that uses the arithmetic operation.

annotations.definition: LUs in this frame describe a stative relationship between two numbers,Term1 and Term2 or, collectively, Terms, via a commutative arithmetic operation. The numbers are specifically expressed, along with the Result of the operation.

frameElementDefinitions.Result: The number or role of a number that comes from the interaction of Term1 and Term2 via a commutative operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Term1: The grammatically more prominent number involved in a commutative operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Term2: The grammatically less prominent number involved in commutative operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Terms: Two more or elements of a commutative arithmetic operation. This frame element may be represented as Term1 and Term2.

annotations.definition: Item-1 and Item-2 are compatible with each other if they can exist or function together in some context without problems, conflict, or other undesirable situation. The set of Item-1 and Item-2 may be expressed jointly as Items. The Degree to which the Items are compatible may also be indicated, as may be a Parameter along whose lines the Items are compatible.

frameElementDefinitions.Item_1: The Frame Element Item_1 marks the grammatically more prominent of the two entities, i.e.the subject in active clauses, when they are expressed separately.
frameElementDefinitions.Item_2: The Frame Element Item_2 marks the grammatically less prominent of the Items when they are expressed separately.
frameElementDefinitions.Items: This FE is used when the Items are expressed jointly in a phrase, either a plural or a coordinated NP.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This Frame Element is used for expressions that indicate the extent to which two Items are compatible.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: This Frame Element is used for expressions that indicate a Parameter or respect in which the Items are said to be compatible, not in conflict.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with the idea that people (Participant_1, Participant_2, or Participants) participate in an organized, rule-governed activity (the Competition) in order to achieve some advantageous outcome (often the Prize). Rank and Score are different criteria by which the degree of achievement of the advantageous outcome is judged.

frameElementDefinitions.Participant_1: This FE identifies the first (or only) participant in a competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Participant_2: This FE identifies the second participant in a competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Participants: This FE is used for plural NP participants in a competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Competition: This FE is used for the name of the competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Score: This FE is used for the score in a game.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: This FE is used for the ranked results of a competition, and is translatable into a numerical value.
frameElementDefinitions.Prize: This FE indicates a prize won in a competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Venue: This FE is for the named venue of a competition, particularly when the place has been built for the purpose of a competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place. A constituent tagged as Place may require annotation on a secondary FE-layer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way the Participants compete.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action the Participants (or Participant_1) are involved in that constitutes a Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time when the Competition occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE is used to describe the length of time over which competition occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE is used to describe the intended outcome of the competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the intensity of competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE expresses how often the Competition occurs.

annotations.definition: A Complainer communicates their negative emotional reaction to some state of affairs in a Complaint to an Addressee. As an alternative to the specific Complaint, the Topic of the speaker's Complaint may be specified. Likewise, instead of (or in addition to) the Complainer, a Medium may be stated.

frameElementDefinitions.Complainer: The Complainer is the sentient entity that produces the Complaint (whether spoken or written).
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the Complaint is communicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Complaint: The lamentable situation that the Complainer is communicating to the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject matter to which the Complaint pertains.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the complaining event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Complainer that affect the action (presumptuously, bitterly, vociferoursly ).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: Frequency is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Complainer makes a complaint.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or physical state of the Complainer that leads to their complaining.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Complainer to transmit the Complaint.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Complainer while making the Complaint.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the complaint is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE specifies the location where the complaining takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE indicate that the particular complaining event referred to is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. Most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Occasion: The Occasion is the reason a public statement is given.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically followes. This often means that the Explanation causes the complaint, but not in all cases. In particular, there are cases when the connection is a bit weaker than normal for causation. In these instances, cause is mediated by some actor responding in a complex way to the situation. Such cases are not paraphrasable in the language of direct causation.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: An event or state which results from the complaining.

annotations.definition: A Whole, an entity with a substructure, is assessed for whether or not it has all the necessary parts that are defined for it or for an entity of its kind. The entity can be a concrete physical thing, an abstract thing, or a process with goals or sub-goals.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: An expression indicating a deviation of the actual level of completeness from the level indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The entity that is being assessed for having all its necessary parts or not.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The interval of time when the completeness of the entity is assessed.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns Acts and State_of_Affairs for which Protagonists are responsible and which either follow or violate some set of rules or Norms.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist's behavior is in or out of compliance with norms.
frameElementDefinitions.Act: This FE identifies the Act that is judged to be in or out of compliance with the Norms.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: A State_of_affairs may violate a law or rule.
frameElementDefinitions.Norm: This FE identifies the rules or Norms that ought to guide a person's behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the compliance ocurrs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies any description of the Actor or Undergoer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the compliance occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the compliance is (or is not) accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of compliance.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the act of compliance or non-compliance occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for complying with the Norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: A speaker marks a Conceded_state_of_affairs as being or implying a contradiction to the Main_assertion. Some LUs specify the Main_assertion, others the Conceded_state_of_affairs, as referring to the preceding discourse (i.e. DNI), while yet others are conjunctions joining the two.

frameElementDefinitions.Conceded_state_of_affairs: A proposition or set of propositions that the speaker wants the addressee to believe or take for granted. This statement is not asserted, but presupposed by the speaker of the sentence, and is mentioned to contrast with the Main_assertion. It would or could seem to predict a situation different than the Main_assertion, but, for reasons left implicit, this normal prediction is contradicted by the fact that the Main_assertion and the Conceded_state_of_affairs are both simultaneously true.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: An entity which metonymically stands in for an associated Statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Main_assertion: The statement that the speaker wishes to assert or offer as their main contribution to the discourse, which is be modified or hedged in some way by the Conceded_state_of_affairs. The force of the contribution of the Main_assertion is presented as undiluted by the fact that something different from the Main_assertion would normally by predicted, based on the facts in the Conceded_state_of_affairs.

annotations.definition: A Patient has a Medical_condition that can be understood by its Symptoms. This frame has been constructed to list all the ways to speak of relating a Patient, a Medical_condition, and Symptoms, including LUs that are also related to much more general frames like Causation (e.g. cause.v).

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The person who has a Medical_condition associated with Symptom.
frameElementDefinitions.Medical_condition: A holistic description of the medical state of the Patient (or a part of the state of the Patient), which may or may not indicate the cause of a deviation from normal.
frameElementDefinitions.Symptom: A deviation from normal function or feeling which is noticed by a Patient or physician, indicating the presence of a Medical_condition
frameElementDefinitions.Group: Specific type of people in which a Medical_condition is normally found
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When a Symptom or Medical_condition occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where a Medical_condition presents.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The commonality of a Medical_condition or Symptom
frameElementDefinitions.Extent: The degree to which a Medical_condition is associated with a Symptom(s).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Explanation for why a Symptom(s) or Medical_condition(s) occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Influence: Genetic, Biological, and Environmental Influences affect Medical_conditions

annotations.definition: A Consequence is presented as occurring if the Profiled_possibility occurs. See Conditional_scenario for further information.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_possibility: The situation presented explicitly as a possibility, contrasting with the implicit Negated_possibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Consequence: The situation that follows from the Profiled_possibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Pragmatic_marker: Pragmatically emphasizes a speaker's assumption of the truth of the Profiled_possibility for the conversation's duration. This FE does not occur for all LUs in the frame, and is closed-class, limited to the word 'always'.

annotations.definition: In this scene, two mutually exclusive possibilities are presented, the Profiled_possibility and the Opposite_possibility, each of which has a consequence associated with it, the Consequence (associated with the Profiled_possibility) and the Anti_consequence (associated with the Opposite_possibility). Usually, the Consequence and Anti_consequence are also mutually exclusive, but in some cases (especially with "even if") the same Consequence results regardless of whether one considers the Profiled_possibility or the Opposite_possibility. Neither of the possibilities is asserted and neither can simply be merged with a factual understanding of the world. Both possibilities are presented as uncertain, and the Profiled_possibility may be further characterized with a neutral stance towards it, or with a doubtful, negative stance, depending on the specific lexical unit introducing the conditional and what verb forms are used in the Profiled_possibility.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_possibility: The situation presented explicitly as a possibility, contrasting with the implicit Opposite_possibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Opposite_possibility: The implicit situation that is mutually exclusive with the Profiled_possibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Consequence: The situation that follows from the Profiled_possibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Anti_consequence: The situation that follows as a result of the Opposite_possibility.

annotations.definition: An Agent acts in a certain Manner either generally or under some particular Circumstances. The conduct may also be directed specifically towards an Affected_party.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE marks expressions that denote the person whose conduct is being evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE marks expressions that characterize the way that the Agent behaves.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE marks expressions that indicate a set of Circumstances under which the Agent acts in a particular way.
frameElementDefinitions.Affected_party: A person to whom behavior is directed by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE marks expressions that indicate why an Agent behaved in a certain way.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent behaves in a certain way.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Agent exhibits their behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The way in which the Agent behaves in the specified manner.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain of human behavior in which the Agent acts in a certain Manner
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the (usually 'good' or 'bad') conduct of the Agent deviates from some standard or norm
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: An explicit point to which the conduct of the Agent is compared.

annotations.definition: A Benefactor confers a Beneficial_situation to a Beneficiary. Unlike in Commerce_scenario, the recipient is not necessarily required to pay for the good or service that they receive. Therefore, the element of benevolence is profiled. Dual FE-layer annotation:

frameElementDefinitions.Benefactor: The Benefactor is the person or thing that gives an advantage to the Beneficiary.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The Beneficiary is person or thing that receives an advantage from the Benefactor.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficial_situation: The Beneficial_situation is the item or action that is given to or done for the Beneficiary by the Benefactor. A change of possession can, but does not have to occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Beneficiary gains an advantage from the Benefactor.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain describes the type of advantage that is conferred.
frameElementDefinitions.Subset: A Subset of beneficial situations is selected from the set of beneficial situations in a specific domain, though the domain need not be explicitly named.

annotations.definition: An Agent becomes involved in an Issue which has negative consequences for them. The Agent will normally respond to resolve the Issue.

frameElementDefinitions.Issue: This FE identifies the Issue that the Agent deals with.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who is acting to deal with the Issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the intentional act occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the reason why the Agent confronts the issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent acts intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the way in which an Agent goes about facing the Issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain within which the Agent acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the Agent does the Act in a given period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result describes the resulting outcome of the confronting act.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the state of affairs expressed by the target, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE gives a description of the confronting event as a whole.

annotations.definition: An entity is integrated into a building as a Part of a (possibly unexpressed) Whole which may refer to the whole building or merely to a sub-part of the building. The Part itself connects locations within the building to each other, Connected_locations. If a particular point of view is adopted in the context of real or imagined motion, then one of the locations can be construed as Goal and the other as Source.

frameElementDefinitions.Part: A Part of a larger architectural unit, the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: An architectural unit which includes the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A property relating to the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Orientation: This is a property of the Part that situates the Part within the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Part, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The Individual who creates the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is one of the Connected_locations treated as source.
frameElementDefinitions.Connected_locations: Two or more named locations that are connected by the architectural Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The Direction is the direction or directions the Part of connecting architecture runs.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is one of the Connected_locations treated as a goal

annotations.definition: The Connector is an artifact created to affix a Connected_item or to bind onto a Fixed_location and is primarily so used. A more specific Use may also be specified. Certain words in this frame (e.g. bond, chain in the phrase in chains) indicate specifically that the Connector is being used to immobilize something. Connectors have a wide variety of physical forms: long, thin, and flexible for tying (ropes, bonds, withies); solid, with a shape corresponding to holes in the entities to be connected (bolts, nails, pegs); and liquid (glues, adhesives, pastes).

frameElementDefinitions.Connector: Connector identifies the entity or substance that forms the bond that maintains the Item in a fixed position; it is usually expressed in a "with"-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Connected_item: This FE identifies the entity that is attached to the Connector in such a way as to stabilize their position or to immobilize them.
frameElementDefinitions.Fixed_location: This FE identifies the location to which the Connector is attached.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The items that a Connector usually attaches or the type of activity the attaching is generally for.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Material identifies the material of which the Connector is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type designates the type of Connector. Often the Type labels part of a multi-word or compound word target.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to a Connector.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The individual or group that creates a Connector.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which the Connector comes to be.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Connector.

annotations.definition: This frame describes a Theme losing its autonomy and perhaps sustaining material damage as the result of a successful invasion on the behalf of a Conqueror.

frameElementDefinitions.Conqueror: The Conqueror is the entity that overtakes the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the land, government, and its people that is seized by the Conqueror.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the intensity and extent of the conquering event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Conqueror takes over the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action by which the conquering event is made possible through the efforts of the Conqueror.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Conqueror takes over the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Conqueror seizes the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason why the Conqueror seized the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The tool used by the Conqueror to seize the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: Frequency is the number of times the Conquering event occurs per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: A Profiled_region occupies the space in contact with the Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: A region of space in contact with a Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the Profiled_region.

annotations.definition: A Communicator (whose Location may be indicated) directs a Communication to an Addressee at a particular Address. Note that no actual successful communicative act is implied, only the successful completion of acts which would establish the communication. (In other words, Mari can e-mail Jon without Jon ever reading it.) NB: The Address or Location_of_communicator may be a virtual address, such as an e-mail or telephone number.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a message to another person.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physical or abstract setting in which the message is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The person that receives the message from the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Address: This FE is used for a (metaphorical) place in a system of communication where Communicators and Addressees can access the system.
frameElementDefinitions.Communication: The information that the Communicator wishes to impart to the Addressee, often to get a particular response. In this frame the Communication is not necessarily received by the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for the Communicator contacting the Addressee. Often, this FE is used to identify a PP-for which indicates what the Communicator is attempting to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: This is the subject matter to which the message pertains. It is typically expressed as a PP Complement headed by "about".
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: The location occupied by the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the intentional act occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent acts intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Intermediary: The Intermediary is the person who facilitates the contact.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive notes the state of one of the participants in the contacting event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times a Communicator contacts the Addressee per unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the nature of the action (e.g. "the same way"), including especially internal states of the Communicator that also influence the way the action is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Communicator that enables them to make contact.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause affects a Goal location by moving a Theme, but without necessarily filling or covering it. This frame is for Inheritance purposes.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the actor who instigates the filling.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the physical object or substance which changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location where the Theme begins its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is the trajectory of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the area or container being filled. Goal is generally the NP Object in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Agent of the filling action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the resultant state after the filling has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Agent fills or covers the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the filling takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent fills the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion is the smaller area of the Goal where the filling or covering occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which brings about the filling of the Goal.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause partially or totally changes the state of a Source object, towards having less of the Theme. This frame is primarily for Inheritance.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the entity that does the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme, when overtly expressed, invariably occurs in a PP Complement headed by "of".
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the region or container which is emptied of something. In this frame, the Source occurs as an NP Object.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is any expression which tells where the Theme ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depicitive phrase describing either the Agent or Theme of the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the Agent emptying the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This is the object with which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-sentient force which instigates the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the emptying takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the emptying takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: This frame covers entities which are prototypically conceived of and created to fulfill the function of containing--i.e. Containers that contain objects or substances, the Contents. This can be expressed as a complement , and includes potential or intended contents. A Container may also have an Owner, a current Relative_location, Material from which it is constructed, a method of Construction, and a Type. Note the connection between words in this frame and those in the Filling, Emptying, and Fullness frames.

frameElementDefinitions.Container: Container is an object which is conventionally used as a container. Target words in this frame are tagged with this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Contents: Contents is the object(s)/stuff contained in the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Material identifies the material of which the Container is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type designates the type of Container. Often the Type labels part of a multi-word or compound word target.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: Relative_location is the location of the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Construction: Construction indicates the action (or causer) which creates the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Owner: Owner identifies the owner of the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: Use identifies the purpose for which an artifact is constructed and the activity with which it is associated, or the Contents protypically contained in the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: This FE identifies a Part of the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor describes some aspect of the Container.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Container holds within its physical boundaries the Contents.

frameElementDefinitions.Container: The Container is the physical object which holds within its boundaries the Contents
frameElementDefinitions.Contents: The Contents is the substance or objects contained within the physical bounds of the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Container holds the Contents.

annotations.definition: In this situation, a Container has within it some Contents. The Container has an Interior, a space which is potentially empty, but instead is occupied with the Contents.

frameElementDefinitions.Container: The non-point-like entity which has a Boundary which (largely or completely) separates the Interior region from the Exterior region. It normally also has a Portal which links the Interior and Exterior regions.
frameElementDefinitions.Interior: The (finite) region which is inside the Boundary of the Container, disjoint from the Exterior, and overall closer or included in the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Portal: A (relatively small) region which goes through the Boundary, connecting the Interior and Exterior of the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Contents: Thing or substance located within the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period or moment in which the Contents occupy the Interior of the Container.

annotations.definition: The answer to one open question, the Outcome, is dictated (partially or completely) by the answer to another open question, the Determinant, i.e. if the answer to the Determinant is known, the answer to the Outcome can be predicted. The Degree of predictive power and the Circumstances under which the dependency holds can also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: An open question that is (to some degree) predictable from the Determinant.
frameElementDefinitions.Determinant: An open question that determines the Outcome.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of predictive power supplied by the Determinant with respect to the Outcome.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The conditions under which the Determinant dictates the Outcome. Oftentimes it is expressed in a fashion similar to Time and Place in other frames, but such cases are actually metonymic for the prevailing conditions of the Time and/or Place.

annotations.definition: A State_of_affairs has held and continues to hold up to the Reference_occasion.

frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: A state which holds prior to and up through the Reference_occasion.
frameElementDefinitions.Reference_occasion: The time or event (normally speech time) with respect to which the State_of_affairs is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Event takes place despite the existence of an Adversity that would otherwise support Contrary_circumstances or results.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event that occurs despite an Adversity that would seem to contradict the possibility of its occurence.
frameElementDefinitions.Adversity: The Adversity is a state of affairs or entity that hinders or negatively affects the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Contrary_circumstances: The Contrary_circumstances are the circumstances that would normally be expected to occur given the presence of the Adversity.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer feels bad about a choice, an Action, or a failure to do something, which they now consider to have been a mistake or wrong. The emotional state of the Experiencer may be showing in gestures or facial expressions, viz. an Expressor.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The FE Experiencer indicates the person who experiences the emotion about their prior action.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The FE Action marks expressions that indicate a prior action that the Experiencer has come to feel bad about.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The FE Degree marks expressions that indicate how badly the Experiencer feels about their prior action.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The FE Expressor marks expressions that indicate a gesture or body part which is displaying the Emotional_state of the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Authenticity: The FE Authenticity marks expressions that indicate whether the Experiencer genuinely feels contrite or just acts as if they did.
frameElementDefinitions.Emotional_state: The FE Emotional_state is modified by an adjective that indicates the Experiencer is contrite.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Experiencer or the Expressor is contrite.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Experiencer or Expressor has contrition.

annotations.definition: A Controlling_entity, Controlling_situation, or Controlling_variable control a Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable. The latter, dependent, element or some aspect of it is not just influenced, but determined by the controlling element.

frameElementDefinitions.Controlling_entity: A thing or person whose behavior controls a Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_entity: An entity, usually a thing, that is influenced in its behavior by a Controlling_entity or an Controlling_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Controlling_variable: A scalar variable (with unspecified value) which has an effect on the Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_situation: A situation whose temporal or other characteristics are influenced by a Controlling_entity, Controlling_situation, or Controlling_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_variable: A scalar variable, whose value depends on the behavior of a Controlling_entity, a Controlling_situation, or a Controlling_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Controlling_situation: A situation whose temporal or other characteristics control a Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Controlling element affects the Dependent element.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the controlling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the controlling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Influencing_entity that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: This frame contains items which, when manipulated, alter the function of some device, internal or external.

frameElementDefinitions.Control: This FE identifies the entity or substance that is designed to have a particular Use. This is often an incorporated FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which an control is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Control into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which an Control comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Control.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Control, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Controlled_entity: The entity which the Control controls.

annotations.definition: Through a Message, a Speaker (sometimes encoded metonymically by a Medium) communicates the relative importance of the state of affairs denoted by the Message.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a Message to another person.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the audience to which the Speaker communicates the Message.
