frameElementDefinitions.Message: The proposition whose denotation the Speaker presents as important.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, in particular secondary effects (loudly), general descriptions comparing events (the same way), and salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Speaker communicates the importance of the state of affairs denoted by the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Speaker communicates a Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times the Speaker conveys a Message whose denotation they present as an important fact to be aware of.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree of importance that the Speaker conveys the Message's denotation as having.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Speaker delivers the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE is minimally distinct from Duration and Duration_of final_state in that the clause described is conveyed to be iterative. Period_of_iterations can co-occur with either the peripheral FE Duration or the extra-thematic FE Duration_of_final_state (although these cannot occur together).
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.

annotations.definition: A Convoy consists of several Vehicles which move together to transport Cargo from a Source to a Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Vehicles: The constituent vehicles that make up the Convoy.
frameElementDefinitions.Convoy: The group of vehicles transporting the Cargo.
frameElementDefinitions.Aggregate_property: This FE describes a property of the Convoy rather than the Vehicles and is used to tag the adjective that modifies the aggregate noun when it is unambiguous.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to the Convoy.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant Domain as it pertains to the Convoy.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The Possessor of the Convoy. Often a nation or organization that wants the Cargo transported.
frameElementDefinitions.Cargo: The Cargo are the goods and/or individuals transported by the Convoy. This FE also covers the intended use of the transported goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the beginning of the path along which the Convoy travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path along which the Convoy travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the endpoint of path along which the Convoy travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction in which the Convoy travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Support: People and possibly other elements of the convoy who support and defend the transport of the Cargo are the Support. In military contexts, this typically consists of soldiers and military vehicles who provide defense for the conveying Vehicles and Cargo.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The general Place where the Convoy is operating.

annotations.definition: This frame describes food and meal preparation. A Cook creates a Produced_food from (raw) Ingredients. The Heating_Instrument and/or the Container may also be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Produced_food: The Produced_food is the result of a Cook's efforts.
frameElementDefinitions.Heating_instrument: This FE identifies the Heating_Instrument with which the Cook prepares the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: This FE identifes the Container that holds the food being produced.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the cooking creation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the cooking creation is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the cooking creation is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: Recipient identifies the person for whom the food has been prepared.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the cooking creation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the cooking creation performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Ingredients: This FE identifies the Ingredients which are altered by the Cook to create the Produced_food.

annotations.definition: Blend of Rewards_and_punishments and Bodily_injury in which the Agent performs some action intended to cause Bodily harm to the Evaluee as a consequence of some action or belief of the Evaluee (the Reason).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that is taken that results in punishement/reward.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: Evaluee is the person or thing about whom/which a judgment is made. With verbs this FE is typically expressed as Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Reason: Typically, there is a constituent expressing the Reason for the Judge's judgment. It is usually a for-PP, e.g.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: This FE is the location on the body where the bodily injury takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Severity: This frame element marks a quantity related to the seriousness of the punished behavior.

annotations.definition: A particular piece of Information is evaluated for its accuracy. The Degree of correctness may also be expressed. The Source of information may also metonymically stand in for the Information.

frameElementDefinitions.Information: The Information matches or does not match the facts of the world.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Information matches or does not match the facts of the world.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The aspect of reality which is accurately modeled by the Information.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The aspect of the Information which accurately models reality.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Information is correct.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the agent who holds the correct/incorrect information.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame, which typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Source is correct/incorrect.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Information or the Source is correct.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physcial context that the Information is found in.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the target operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The outcome of the correctness if the Source or Information.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_act: The event which communicates the Information.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic about which the Source has accurate information.

annotations.definition: In this frame the Patient, an inorganic item, undergoes a chemical process which renders it less useful, desirable or stable.

frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: Initial_state is the original state of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifes the Duration of time for which the Patient corrodes.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Patient corrodes.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Patient corrodes.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the change of phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Patient corrodes.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient undergoes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Patient corrodes.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This FE identifies the Speed at which the Patient corrodes.

annotations.definition: In this frame, the Patient, an inorganic item, undergoes a chemical process caused by another entity, which renders it less useful, desirable or stable. The process may or may not be under the control of an Agent.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Cause corrodes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Cause corrodes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: This FE identifies the Initial_state of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Cause corrodes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Patient is corroded.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the caused corrosion.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Cause corrodes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is corroded by the Cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This FE identifies the Speed at which the Cause corrodes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion identifies the part of a larger whole that is affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Cause corrodes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Cause corrodes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Patient is corroded.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: Cause identifies an animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that corrodes the Patient. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that corrodes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Agent may use an Instrument to cause the corrosion.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that necessarily indicate the motion of two distinct objects. The Theme is typically animate and is expressed the same way a Self-mover is expressed in the Self-motion frame--i.e. as the subject of a target verb. The Cotheme may or may not be animate and is typically expressed as a direct object or an oblique. Source, Path, Goal, and the other frame elements common to motion words also regularly occur with the words in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: This is the entity, frequently a living being, which moves in relation to the Cotheme. Normally the Theme frame element is expressed as an external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: This frame element is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique:
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source marks any expression which implies a definite starting-point of the motion of the Cotheme. In prepositional phrases, the object expresses the starting point of motion. With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path marks phrases that describe the Theme and Cotheme's trajectory of motion and which are neither expressions of the Source nor the Goal of motion. The notion Path also includes directional expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Any expression which tells where the Cotheme ends up as a result of the motion expresses the frame element Goal. Note that if the Cotheme is animate, the Theme need not also end up in the same place. Some particles imply the existence of a Goal which is understood in the context of utterance.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Phrases marked with this frame element describe a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of the Theme's motion. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Any expression which characterizes the extent of the motion of the Theme and Cotheme expresses the frame element Distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: Area marks expressions which describe a general area in which the motion of Theme and Cotheme takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path. Locative setting adjuncts of motion expressions may also be assigned this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This frame element marks a depictive phrase describing the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: An Event in which the Cotheme is involved. Usually this is some kind of travel.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This frame element expresses the Means by which a (volitional) Theme moves in relation to the Cotheme.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Phrases marked with this frame element mark resultant states that the Cotheme can reach due to the motion event.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: This frame element denotes the means of conveyance on which the Theme is traveling. The Mode_of_Transportation can be the Theme's own body or a vehicle. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: Phrases that describe the rate of motion of the Theme are marked Speed.
frameElementDefinitions.Road: Phrases that denote a physical path on which the motion of Theme and Cotheme takes place are marked Road.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which a Theme moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Following_distance: The Following_distance is the distance between the Theme and Cotheme.
frameElementDefinitions.Handle: The Handle is active zone of the Cotheme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location which provides a setting for a defined motion (that is one with Source, Path or Goal).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Some event or state of affairs that leads to the Theme following the Cotheme.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction in which the Theme and Cotheme move.

annotations.definition: An Assailant physically attacks a Victim (which is usually but not always sentient), causing or intending to cause the Victim physical damage. The attack is a response to an earlier attack by the Victim on the current Assailant (or by actors associated with the Assailant or Victim). A Weapon used by the Assailant may also be mentioned, in addition to the usual Place, Time, Purpose, Reason, etc. Sometimes a location is used metonymically to stand for the Assailant or the Victim, and in such cases the Place FE will be annotated on a second FE layer.

frameElementDefinitions.Victim: This FE is the being or entity that is injured by the Assailant's attack.
frameElementDefinitions.Weapon: An entity used by the Assailant to cause damage to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Assailant: The person (or other self-directed entity) that is responding to a prior attack by attempting physical harm to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Assailant that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Assailant that accomplishes the assault.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Assailant makes an assault on the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Assailant attacks the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Assailant is responding to that leads them to make an assault on the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Assailant attacks the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The starting-point of the motion of the Assailant or projectiles of the Assailant as they move toward the the Place that the Victim is occupying in the attack. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describeing the state of the Assailant or Victim while the Attack occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the attack on the Victim by the Assailant.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held. Examples include once, twice, [Cardinal expression] + times, a lot.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole. There are two somewhat distinct subgroups of sentence modifiers that cover this function.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the Assailant travels.

annotations.definition: A Questioner asks a Witness (or defendant) questions in the context of a court procedure.

frameElementDefinitions.Questioner: The individual who asks a Witness questions.
frameElementDefinitions.Witness: A person who is present in a courtroom in order to give some form of evidence, has been sworn in, and is being examined by a Questioner.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The subject matter to which the examination pertains.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the examination occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the examination takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Questioner examines the Witness.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose of the examination.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Questioner examines the Witness.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: How long the examination takes place.

annotations.definition: A particular Activity, performed conventionally or habitually by more than one Practitioner within a Culture, is described in terms of its method.

frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate the Activity that is being performed habitually or conventionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Practitioner: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate whose Activity is at issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate the Place in which the Activity is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE marks expressions that indicate attributes, traits, or evaluations of the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Culture: A Culture within which the Activity is performed.

annotations.definition: A Creator creates an artifact that is typically an iconic Representation of an actual or imagined entity or event. The Representation may also be evocative of an idea while not based on resemblance.

frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Representation into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which a Representation is created.
frameElementDefinitions.Representation: The entity that is created to represent either iconically or abstractly.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Creator that also affect the action (deliberately, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Role: This FE identifies a Role that one of the role fillers of the current frame also fills in a background context, either generically or in some specific instance.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Creator as being in some state during the creation of the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_representation: The physical location that holds the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Creator intends to accomplish by creating the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Creator produces the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the creation of the Representation ogically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The entity which the Creator uses to create the work of art.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Creator which brings about the creation of the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Creator or the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Reference: A representation of the entity depicted which the Creator uses as a reference in the creation of the Representation. For instance, another piece of art, a photograph, or his/her memory.

annotations.definition: A Creator produces a physical object which is to serve as a Representation of an actual or imagined entity or event, the Represented.

frameElementDefinitions.Representation: The entity produced by the Creator to evoke in an audience the idea of the Represented.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Representation into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which a Representation is created.
frameElementDefinitions.Represented: The entity--which may be a thing, an action or a state--that is represented by the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive_of_represented: A characterization of the state in which the Represented entity is presented.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Creator that also affect the action (deliberately, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Role: This FE identifies a Role that one of the role fillers of the current frame also fills in a background context, either generically or in some specific instance.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Creator as being in some state during the creation of the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The physical Material that is used in the production of the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Creator intends to accomplish by creating the Representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the artist produces the representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The entity used by the Creator to make a Representation of the Represented entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Creator that allows them to make a Representation of the Represented entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: Iterations is the number of times the Creator creates the representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Reference: A representation of the Represented which the Creator uses as a reference in the creation of the artwork. For instance, another piece of art, a photograph, or his/her memory.

annotations.definition: A Cause leads to the formation of a Created_entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Components: This FE identifies the Components that are attached together to form a Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Created_entity: This FE identifies the entity that the Agent intentionally creates.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies a depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action. We wandered around naked. Cicadas screech unseen from within.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Creator intentionally creates a new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which a Creator intentionally creates a new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place ehere the Agent intentionally creates the new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifes the Time when an Agent intentionally creates a new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that produces an effect. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the instrument with which an Agent intentionally creates a Created_entitiy.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: A secondary agent with whom the Creator intentionally creates the Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The Creator creates a created entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Creator intentionally creates a new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Creator's intended purpose for the Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The Beneficiary benefits in some way from the creation of the Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The Role that the Created_entity takes (or is intended to take, if there is a Creator).
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual who is intended to receive the Created_entity.

annotations.definition: A (putative) Crime is committed and comes to the attention of the Authorities. In response, there is a Criminal_investigation and (often) Arrest and criminal court proceedings. The Investigation, Arrest, and other parts of the Criminal_Process are pursued in order to find a Suspect (who then may enter the Criminal_process to become the Defendant) and determine if this Suspect matches the Perpetrator of the Crime, and also to determine if the Charges match the Crime. If the Suspect is deemed to have committed the Crime, then they are generally given some punishment commensurate with the Charges.

frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: The individual which is under suspicion of having committed the Crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: The individual that commits a Crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Crime: An act, generally intentional, that matches the description that belongs to an official Charge.
frameElementDefinitions.Charge: A description of a type of act that is not permissable according to the law of society.
frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The group which is responsible for the maintenance of law and order, and as such have been given the power to investigate Crimes, find Suspects and determine if a Suspect should be submitted to the Criminal_process.

annotations.definition: This frame describes the process that involves the inquiry and determination by an authority, the Investigator, of the circumstances surrounding an Incident perpetrated by a Suspect.

frameElementDefinitions.Investigator: The Investigator is the person or agency that investigates the Suspect of a crime (Incident).
frameElementDefinitions.Incident: The Investigator attempts to determine the exact circumstances surrounding the Incident, which is typically a criminal act (murder, robbery) or the result of such an act (death, loss of money).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Investigator investigates the Incident.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of the investigation.
frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: This FE denotes the Suspect that is investigated by the Investigator.

annotations.definition: A Suspect is arrested by Authorities on Charges related to an Offense, then is arraigned as a Defendant. If at any time the Defendant pleads guilty, then the Defendant is sentenced, otherwise the Defendant first goes to trial. During trial, the Defense and Prosecution present arguments on behalf of and against the Defendant, respectively. If the Jury comes up with a guilty verdict at the end of the trial, the Defendant is sentenced. In the end, the Defendant is either released or is given a Sentence by a Judge at the sentencing.

frameElementDefinitions.Court: This FE identifies the Court involved in a trial.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Defendant: The Defendant is charged with an Offense.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The Judge heads the Court where arraignment occurs and the case is tried.
frameElementDefinitions.Prosecution: This FE identifies the attorney(s) prosecuting the Defendant.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: This FE identifies the Charges brought against the Defendant.
frameElementDefinitions.Defense: The Defense represents the interests of the Defendant.
frameElementDefinitions.Jury: This FE identifies the people who are charged by the Court to listen to the testimony, evidence, and arguments, and come to a consensus about the guilt or innocence of the Defendant.
frameElementDefinitions.Offense: This FE identifies the Offense which the Defendant is accused of committing.
frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities charge the Suspect with commiting a crime, and take him/her into custody.
frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: The Suspect is taken into custody by the Authorities, under suspicion of having committed a crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Witness: A person who is present in a courtroom in order to give some form of evidence, has been sworn in, and is being examined by the Prosecution or Defense.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the person (or other sentient being or group) that has in some way been harmed or wronged by the Defendant.

annotations.definition: This frame deals with a Healer treating and curing an Affliction (the injuries, disease, or pain) of the Patient, sometimes also mentioning the use of a particular Treatment or Medication. This frame differs from Medical_intervention in that this frame deals only with cases in which the Patient is cured of the Affliction, not just treated for the Affliction.

frameElementDefinitions.Healer: The Healer, anyone who treats or cures the Patient, occurs as the External Argument of verbs:
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: This is the sufferer of the injury, disease or pain and can occur as an NP Object in this frame. However, Patient may not be expressed as a separate Frame Element but frequently occurs as the possessor of the Affliction, an example of FE Conflation: (See section on Conflation.)
frameElementDefinitions.Affliction: The Frame Element Affliction is generally the NP Object of a verb, frequently incorporating the Patient as a possessor, as described above:
frameElementDefinitions.Treatment: The FE Treatment is used for treatments and means, distinguished by separate FEs in FN-1.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Medication: The injested, applied, injected, etc. substance designed to cure the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Reciprocation: The Reason for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The Body_part is the specific area of the Patient's body which is treated.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time that the Patient recieves the Treatment.

annotations.definition: A Behavior is classified as entrenched for a Protagonist or a Society. The Behavior may be associated with a particular Domain of experience and described with a Descriptor. This indicates that the Behavior is commonly performed by the Protagonist or members of the Society.

frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate the entrenched Behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate whose entrenched Behavior is at issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate the area of human experience to which the Behavior belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE marks expressions that indicate attributes, traits, or evaluations of the entrenched Behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE marks expressions that indicate a region where the Behavior is entrenched.

annotations.definition: An Agent cuts a Item into Pieces using an Instrument (which may or may not be expressed).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person cutting the Item into Pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the Item is being cut into Pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the slicing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Item is being sliced into Pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the slicing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Agent that accomplishes the slicing.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The item which is being cut into Pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the Item being sliced into Pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which the Item is being cut into Pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Pieces: The Pieces are the parts of the original Item which are the result of the slicing.

annotations.definition: An Entity comes into being, exists for some time, and ceases to be.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Concrete or abstract entity that did not exist, then came into existence, then (potentially) ceased to exist.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist is born or sprouts, lives, dies, and then is dead.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: Protagonist identifies the being or entity that is dead or alive.

annotations.definition: An Agent affects a Patient in such a way that the Patient (or some Subregion of the Patient) ends up in a non-canonical state. Often this non-canonical state is undesirable, and some lexical units (marked with the Negative semantic type) specifically indicate that the Patient is negatively affected. Some lexical units of this frame indicate by what means the Patient is altered (e.g., scrape.v) and in some cases give some indication of the detailed effect on the Patient (e.g., score.v, chip.v), and in other cases, both are specified (tear.v). For another set of lexical units, the action and its result are much less specified (e.g., damage.v). Compare this frame with Destroying, Cause_to_fragment, and Render_nonfunctional.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The conscious entity, generally a person, that performs the intentional action that results in the damage to the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Patient is damaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that interacts with a Patient to accomplish the damage.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the action of damaging which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the damage.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the location of the scene where a Agent damages a Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent causes the damage to the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in performing the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Patient which is directly affected by the damaging.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent damages the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The entity which is affected by the Agent so that it is damaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Character_of_end_state: A description of the state of the Patient after the damaging has taken place, including descriptions of the severity and the permanence of the damaged condition.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of the Agent's action.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which leads to the damage of the Patient.

annotations.definition: An Agent performs some Action which is considered imprudent. This frame is distinct from Attempt in that the danger that the Agent puts themselves in by performing the Action is profiled. The danger is not spelled out, but generally the Action has a possibility or likelihood of causing social or physical harm to the Agent.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that performs the Action, resulting in danger to themself.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: This FE denotes the Action taken by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Action is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation the Agent takes the risk.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent dares to perform the Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent risks doing the Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the risking action which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: A Protagonist is in the dynamic, maintained state of being alive or has exited this state.

frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Explanation identifies any object or event that brings about the death of the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which a Protagonist is dead or alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: Protagonist identifies the being or entity that is dead or alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: This FE identifies the Figure or form being described by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time in which the Protagonist is dead or alive.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe the death of a Protagonist. A Cause of death may also be expressed obliquely.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The FE Protagonist is the being or entity that dies. With verbs in this frame, the Protagonist is the External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The FE Explanation expresses any object or eventuality that brings about the Protagonist's death.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Some measure of the event, generally frequency.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The FE Depictive denotes the state of the Protagonist as the death occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The FE Result indicates what occurs as the result of the death of the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The FE Place indicates where the death takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The FE Time denotes when the dying or killing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies the larger event in which the Protagonist dies, i.e. changes from living to dead.
frameElementDefinitions.Maleficiary: An individual negatively affected by the death of the Protagonist.

annotations.definition: A Deceiver succeeds or fails in causing a Victim to have a misunderstanding about a Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the target of the deception.
frameElementDefinitions.Deceiver: The Deceiver attempts to make the Victim unable to know about or detect the truth about a Phenomenon or Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon is presented to the Victim as a way to mislead about the Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The entity to which the misleading idea relates, what it is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the deception fails or succeeds.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the deception fails or succeeds.

annotations.definition: Deception is a scenario in which a Deceiver attempts to make it such that a Victim comes to an incorrect understanding about a Topic. This may be done by presenting a misleading Phenomenon to the Victim, or by concealing the existence of items which the Victim may be interested in. Note that the Victim is not necessarily aware of the (possible) existence of the Topicor items related to it.

frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the target of the deception.
frameElementDefinitions.Deceiver: The Deceiver attempts to make the Victim unable to know about or detect the truth about a Phenomenon or Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon is presented to the Victim as a way to mislead about the Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The entity to which the misleading idea relates, what it is about.

annotations.definition: A Deceiver successfully causes a Victim to end up with incorrect beliefs about a Topic. The Victim comes to these beliefs from perceiving a Deception.

frameElementDefinitions.Deceiver: The person who acts so as to give the Victim an incorrect idea about a Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The person who receives an incorrect idea because of the Deceiver's actions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The entity to which the misleading idea relates, what it is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Deceiver to fool the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the deception occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the deception occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon, as percieved by the Victim, causes the Victim to have an incorrect understanding about a Topic.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer makes a Decision, which may be about an entity or a course of action. How (and whether) this frame is distinct from Choosing is currently up for debate.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer decides on a course of action.
frameElementDefinitions.Decision: Decision identifies the entity or course of action upon which the Cognizer decides.
frameElementDefinitions.Possibilities: A Cognizer makes a Decision from among a set of Possibilities.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time or time period over which the Cognizer makes the decision.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Cognizer makes the decision.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Cognizer makes a Decision
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The Cognizer intends the Decision to fulfill an Inherent_purpose .
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstance: The conditions under which the Cognizer makes a Decision.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Cognizer makes the Decision.

annotations.definition: A Defender responds to an Assailant's attack on a Victim by directly, and usually violently, countering it.

frameElementDefinitions.Defender: This FE is the being or entity that responds forcefully to the Assailant's attack.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity used by the Defender fend off the Assailant .
frameElementDefinitions.Assailant: The person (or other self-directed entity) that is attempting physical harm to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Defender that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Defender that accomplishes their defense of the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Defender defends the Victim from the Assailant.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Defender defends the Victim from the Assailant.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Defender is responding to that leads them to protect the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Defender takes forceful action against the Assailant.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: The starting-point of the motion of the Defender or projectiles of the Defender as they move toward the the Place that the Assailant is occupying. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Defender or Victim during the Assailant's attack.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The individual or entity that is under attack from the Assailant .
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_assailant: The location of the Assailant or the weapons of the Assailant.

annotations.definition: LUs in this frame modify a gradable attribute and describe intensities at the extreme positions on a scale.
A Material has been processed (generally industrially) to some Degree in order to make it more useful for some (usually implicit) function. In some cases, an Alterant is used to process the Material.

frameElementDefinitions.Alterant: The Alterant causes a change in the Material.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The substance which is altered in some useful way.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount to which a Material has undergone processing.

annotations.definition: An Item which encompasses characteristics or types whose diversity is indicated by adducing a Limit1 and a Limit2 which differ considerably from each other. In some cases, the Limit2 and Limit1 are construed together as Limits. Alternatively, the values of a particular Variable for the Item and its Limits may be profiled.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity whose scalar property is specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Variable: The scalar property that the Item possesses.
frameElementDefinitions.Limit1: The first-mentioned possibility of the range of variation.
frameElementDefinitions.Limit2: The second item of the range of variation.
frameElementDefinitions.Limits: The range of positions on the scale that the Item occupies.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The deviation of the Upper_limit and the Lower_limit from each other (and thus from some single value).

annotations.definition: A State (often an ongoing process) is ongoing for some period of time.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: A quantity or other characterization of the State's absolute duration.
frameElementDefinitions.State: An ongoing state whose duration can be measured.

annotations.definition: A Deliverer hands off a Theme to a Recipient or (more indirectly) a Goal location, which is accessible to the Recipient.

frameElementDefinitions.Deliverer: This is the person who completes the movement of the Theme after having transported it.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The end of the path and intended goal of the sending.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The objects being delivered.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The vehicle employed by the Deliverer.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Deliverer or Theme
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the delivery event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Deliverer that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: This is the Recipient of the sent Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose of the delivery.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for the delivery.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the delivery takes place
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: An additional theme delivered along with the main Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: A container in which the Theme is delivered.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the delivery takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed at which the Deliverer delivers the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The conditions under which the delivery takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The location of the Theme before the delivery process begins.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means are intermediate actions performed by the Deliverer in order to move the package to the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The path along which the Theme moves while being delivered.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The person for whom the Delivery action occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE applies to appraisals of the Delivery event.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the Delivery event began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: In this frame, an Authority allows a Protagonist to know (in some way) that they may or may not engage in an Action. For lexical units of this frame marked with the semantic type "Negative", e.g., authorize.v, the Authority has the power to tell the Protagonist not to do something, but does not do so. It is assumed that if the Protagonist engages in the Action without permission, then the Authority will punish the Protagonist in some way, at least with disapproval; if the Authority grants permission, however, the Authority is agreeing not to impose a punishment on the Protagonist for performing the Action.

frameElementDefinitions.Authority: The Authority orders the Protagonist to not engage in a particular Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist wants to engage in an Action, but is ordered not to by the Authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action is what the Protagonist seeks to do, but is ordered not to.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the denying of permission occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the denial of permission occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is what the Authority hopes to accomplish by denying permission.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole. There are two somewhat distinct subgroups of sentence modifiers that cover this function. First, it applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness. The notion of appraisal requires a factive portrayal of the event. The second subgroup consists of any descriptions of the state of affairs denoted by the target as role fillers in other frames. The state of affairs reported by the target is understood to play a role in the state of affairs of the modifier. What that role is depends only on the predicate heading the modifier phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Allowed_category: A category that the Action fits into, which dictates the Action's being permitted (in accord with some implicit Principle.)
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the Authority or the Protagonist while they are involved in the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A holistic description of the event (the same way), or a description of the Authority that also affects the overall event (carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by an Authority that brings about the Denying or Granting Permission.

annotations.definition: An object (the Theme) moves away from a Source. The Source may be expressed or it may be understood from context, but its existence is always implied by the departing word itself.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: This is the object which moves. It may be an entity which moves under its own power, but it need not be.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: All the verbs in this frame express some change of location, away from one place and to another. Any constituent that expresses the initial position of the Theme, before the change of location, is tagged with Source. Often the Source is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of the trajectory of the departure which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a sense meaning 'via'.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE labels any expression which tells where the Theme ends up, or would end up, as a result of departing from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and many other things count as Manner expressions, though Speed and Means should be tagged using the more specific FE labels.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Theme which results in its departure.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This frame element labels words or phrases that describe how quickly the Theme is moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Theme is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Theme. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. In this frame, Mode_of_transportation is often expressed obliquely in a prepositional phrase headed by 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: The Cotheme is the second moving object, expressed as a prepositional phrase generally headed by 'with' or 'along with'.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance that the Theme has traveled.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the departure takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency is the rate at which each Theme departs.
frameElementDefinitions.Journey: The Journey is the trip or expedition on which the Theme embarks.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location in which the departing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a phrase which describes the Theme at the time of departing.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the motivation or explanation for why the Theme departs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Theme departs from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of time that the Theme stays away from the Source location.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes of the state of affairs denoted by the Departing event as role fillers in other frames; the state of affairs reported Departing event is understood to play a role in the state of affairs of the modifier.

annotations.definition: The existence of a State_of_affairs is sufficient reason for taking an Action . The agent who is justified in taking the suggested Action is not part of the immediate scene, however.

frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: An Eventuality or an Entity that is taken to constitute sufficient reason for taking another Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: An Action that is taken to be justifiable given the State_of_Affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: How sufficiently State_of_affairs can be considered a reason for the Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The general spectrum of reasoning along which the State_of_affairs is a justification.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the Deserving event and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns an Evaluee being judged for its quality, i.e. how much it would probably be liked. In many cases, the Evaluee is implicitly judged good or bad relative to other instances of its type. The Evaluee's desirability is determined by one or more Parameters, which are scalar properties of the Evaluee. The evaluation may also explicitly be relativized to a set of Circumstances, a Comparison_set of entities that belong to the same class as the Evaluee, or an Affected_party. The Degree of goodness or badness may also be expressed. Note: With some targets, desirability is conventionally aligned with quantity, i.e., GOOD is MORE.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: The Evaluee is being judged for its quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Evaluee is judged good or bad.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances marks expressions that indicate a set of conditions under which the quality of the Evaluee is being judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: This FE marks expressions that denote a scalar property of the Evaluee with respect to which the Evaluee is judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: This FE marks expressions that indicate what class of things or events the Evaluee is being judged against.
frameElementDefinitions.Affected_party: The Affected_party indicates entities that benefit or suffer from the good or bad quality of the Evaluee.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Frame Element Depictive encodes a state that the Evaluee is in and which does not represent a temporary or hypothetical set of Circumstances.

annotations.definition: A particular State_of_affairs is desirable. The State_of_affairs may be required to achieve a Purpose. A set of Circumstances may be specified under which the assessment of desirability holds.

frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The State_of_affairs that the speaker finds desirable.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The FE Circumstances encodes a setting, often temporal or spatial, within which the State_of_affairs has the specified assessment of desirability.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The event or state of affairs that is achieved by bringing about the desirable State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the specified desirability of the State_of_affairs deviates from the expected desirability.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer desires that an Event occur. (Note that commonly a resultant state of the Event will stand in for the Event.) In some cases, the Experiencer is an active participant in the Event, and in such cases the Event itself is often not mentioned, but rather some Focal_participant which is subordinately involved in the Event.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person (or sentient being) who wishes for the Event to occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The change that the Experiencer would like to see.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_participant: This is the entity that the Experiencer wishes to be affected by some Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is a the extent of the Experiencer's Desiring.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Experiencer desires something.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An aspect of the Experiencer that causes a desire.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose_of_event: The Purpose_of_event is the purpose pertaining to the desired Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_event: The time that the Event would ideally occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_event: The Location_of_event is the place involved in the desired Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Experiencer desires something.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place. Note that this FE is of extremely rare occurrence in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Experiencer has desired something.
frameElementDefinitions.Role_of_focal_participant: The Role is a role filled by a Focal_participant in the Event that the Experiencer desires.

annotations.definition: An State_of_affairs will come to hold with respect to a Protagonist at some future time. The Protagonist is not conceived of as having control over whether the State_of_affairs will hold: obscurely related previous actions or circumstances, or some hidden power may have already determined that it will occur.

frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The State_of_affairs that is destined to occur to the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist is the one for whom the future State_of_affairs is destined.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of the sort of State_of_affairs that will occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The Role filled by the Protagonist in his/her destiny.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time for which the Protagonist's future is determined.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which State_of_affairs is destined to occur to the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the State_of_affairs is destined to occur to the Protagonist.

annotations.definition: A Destroyer (a conscious entity) or Cause (an event, or an entity involved in such an event) affects the Patient negatively so that the Patient no longer exists.

frameElementDefinitions.Destroyer: The conscious entity, generally a person, that performs the intentional action that results in the Patient's destruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the destruction is completed.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Destroyer that interacts with a Patient to accomplish the Patient's destruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the process of destruction which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Destroyer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Destroyer that accomplishes the destruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Destroyer destroys the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which a Destroyer causes the destruction of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Destroyer is responding to in destroying the Undergoer.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event on the Patient. This FE is very rare in this frame, and since the Patient is destroyed by this process, the Result is generally constrained to indicate the Patient 's resulting inexistence.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Patient which is directly affected by the destruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Destroyer destroys the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The entity which is destroyed by the Destroyer.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The event or entity which is responsible for the destruction of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The category in which the Patient is destroyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Cause or Destroyer destroys the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium expresses the Medium in which the Patient is destroyed by the Cause or Destroyer. This FE is generally used in Metaphorical uses of this frame.

annotations.definition: The Detaching frame covers two situations: a scene in which somebody causes one thing to be physically detached from something else; or a scene in which somebody causes two things to be disconnected from each other. In the first, the frame includes an Agent who detaches an Item from a Source, creating an asymmetric relationship between the Item and the Source. In the second, the Agent attaches two Items to each other, where each serves as a Source relative to the other, creating a symmetric relationship between the two Items. Note in both cases, after the removal of the connector, the Items that were formerly attached re-appear as the two separate or potentially separate entities that they were throughout.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that causes the Item and Source to come apart by manipulating the connector.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: This FE identifies the Item that the Agent detaches from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of a detaching event.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source identifies the location to which an Item was attached before getting detached.
frameElementDefinitions.Items: This FE identifies the plural Items that are detached from each other, resulting in their separation.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent detaches an Item or Items.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the detaching event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent detaches an Item or Items.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent intentionally detaches an Item or Items.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent detaches the Item from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an Agent detaches an Item or Items. Typically, it transmits the force applied by the Agent, and is not left behind as a participant in the resultant state.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Item ends up detached.

annotations.definition: The Authorities hold a Suspect under suspicion of having committed a crime (Charges). The Holding_location and period of detention (Duration) may also be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities hold the Suspect in custody.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: This FE identifies the crime with which the Suspect is charged.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the detaining takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: This FE identifies the person who is taken into custody and held, under suspicion of having committed a crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the detaining occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Holding_location: This FE identifies the place where the Suspect is confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the length of time that the Suspect spends in confinement.
frameElementDefinitions.Legal_basis: This FE identifies the law, act, or precedent that allows the Authorities to detain the Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Authorities detain a Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_state: This FE identifies the state of the Suspect being held.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes an Explosive device to explode.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent detonates the Explosive.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent detonates the Explosive.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent detonates the Explosive.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent detonates the Explosive.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent detonates the Explosive.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action ( deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explosive: The Explosive causes damage to surrounding objects when operated by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of the Agent's detonating the Explosive his or her Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event within which the Agent detonates the Explosive. Often, the Containing_event will also give an indication of the Agent's Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question "how often."
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE modifies a clause with an iterative interpretation, which may be signified by the simple present tense on the main verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute of the Explosive or the detonation act and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An implement used by the Agent to detonate the Explosive.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim (typically an individual or structure) is targeted by the explosion or inadvertantly damaged by the explosion. May be indicated explicitly with on or against, but is often implied by place expressions, in which case the FE is indicated on the second layer.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame have to do with a Cognizer being aware (or not being aware) of the difference between two Phenomena, which may be expressed jointly or disjointly.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The cognizer is the person (or other sentient being) who potentially determines that two Phenomena are distinct from each other. It is normally expressed as an External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon_1: The phenomena that are potentially judged to be different from one another can be expressed as separate constituents. When they are, one is typically expressed as an NP Object, and the other as a PP Complement headed by from:
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon_2: See Phenomenon_1, above. The phenomenon expressed less prominently as a PP complement is called Phenomenon_2:
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomena: Both phenomena that are potentially judged to be different from one another can be expressed jointly by the same constituent:
frameElementDefinitions.Quality: This is a property that is potentially used by the Cognizer to distinguish one phenomenon from another. It is normally expressed as a PP headed by a preposition such as from, by, or on the basis of:
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: State of a participant while the action is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Cognizer that allows them to differentiate the Phenomena.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances describe the situation in which the Cognizer evaluates the Phenomena.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer has an easy or difficult time carrying out an Activity. The ease or difficulty associated with the Activity seems to often be associated with a prominent participant. However, we consider this impression to be due to the syntax and pragmatics of the relevant sentence constructions and employ no special FE for such participants. The Degree of ease or difficulty is often specified as well as a set of Circumstances. A Parameter may be mentioned that indicates in which respect the Activity is judged to be easy or difficult.

frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This Frame Element marks expressions indicating the Activity that the Experiencer finds difficult or easy. In many cases, the relevant Activity has to be inferred from the context.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate the Degree of difficulty that the Experiencer has in carrying out the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate a set of conditions in which the Activity is being experienced and which influence the Experiencer's perception of how easy or difficult the Activity is.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate which person judges carrying out the Activity to be easy or difficult.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: This FE marks expressions that indicate a respect in which the Activity is judged easy or difficult to carry out by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: The experience of the Activity by the Experiencer may be compared to a Standard situation, which may be evoked either as an event or state, or by reference to a salient participant.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Activity takes place.

lexicalUnits.name: like taking candy from a baby.adv

annotations.definition: This frame concerns words that express an Object's position on a scale, its Measurement, with respect to some attribute, its Dimension. Location, identifying the sub-region of the Object where the Dimension is measured, may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: This FE identifies the Dimension which is measured. Most LUs in this frame incorporate the Dimension.
frameElementDefinitions.Object: Object identifies the item which is measured along one of its Dimensions.
