frameElementDefinitions.Measurement: Measurement identifies the extent of the Object along the specified Dimension.
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: Locus identifies the sub-region of the Object where the Dimension is measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant dimension of development.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: FOR REFRAMING!

annotations.definition: This frame covers the basic uses of direction words like the adverbs east, up, and forward. These are used either to talk about a direction of facing (e.g., facing outward, pointing left, looking downward, turn east) or to talk about actual or metaphorical motion (went up, traveling due north). Lexical units of this frame typically have a semantic type that refers to the means of determining the direction (e.g. forward.adv has the semantic type Landmark_front, meaning that "forward" is defined with respect to the direction from the center of some entity to its front, however defined). Nevertheless, some words like way.n are used exactly when normal means of specifying direction are not available. This frame is most frequently used to describe the motion of something along the Path from the Landmark, which is normally implicitly identified as the location of the viewpoint:

frameElementDefinitions.Landmark: The point of reference from which the rest of the Path is reckoned.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The one-dimensional set of locations.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The kind of space in which the set of points of the Path is defined.
frameElementDefinitions.Independent_variable: The one-dimensional variable with respect to which the positions on the Path are reckoned. For normal motion, the positions on the Path are with respect to time, while for directions of facing, the positions are merely reckoned with respect to another spatial dimension, the distance from the Landmark in the specified direction. As these features are the default, literal interpretation of targets of this frame, this frame element is only to be used for metaphorical examples like the following:
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: How much movement in the stated direction along the Path from the Landmark there has been.

annotations.definition: A Figure is located relative to a (presumably more easily located) Ground, asserting that the Figure lies (approximately) on line from the Ground whose direction is established by semantic types of the particular LUs.

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as located relative to a certain Ground location in a certain direction specified by the particular LU. The Figure can be an entity or an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: An indication of how accessible the Figure is to some participant in the speech context (either the speaker or the addressee, or some other highly salient viewpoint in a narrative). This FE normally describes availability for manipulation and use, but may simply describe how easily seen or difficult to see the Figure is. There is a closed class of items that can fill this slot, including only 'just' and 'right' indicating ease of access, and 'way' and 'all the way' indicating distance and difficulty of access.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure. This FE is filled by one of a closed class of adverbs: up, down, out (meaning 'beyond the boundary around the viewpoint'), off (meaning 'disconnect between landmark and figure'), back (meaning 'along a line from front to back of landmark, and relatively inaccessible'), and, more rarely, away, along, and dialectally, back down (meaning 'down and behind').
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the locative relation between Figure and Ground obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance between the Figure and the Ground. This FE requires an image schema with a vector that extends from the Ground, or a subpart of the Ground, in some direction, and the Distance is measured along this vector from the Ground to the profiled region.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'.
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Ground interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class of adverbials back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Figure before) and on.adv (which indicates a continued path). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, in which case it either refers to fictive, imagined motion, or it backgrounds motion entirely (as in the example below).

annotations.definition: This frame covers the large scenario for characterizing disasters, including the occurrence of particular types of disasters and the range of responses to disasters.

frameElementDefinitions.Responder: The individual or group that is responding to a disaster with directed action.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim (typically a large group) which is affected by the disaster event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Undesirable_event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Response: Directed action leading to recovery, rescue, or general mitigation of distress due to an ensuing disaster.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the disaster scenario occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time between the instantiation of the Disaster and the conclusion of the Response.
frameElementDefinitions.Disaster: An event of great proportional magnitude with evidence leaving blame to which only the stars might answer. A disaster is an indisputably ill-fated occurrence.

annotations.definition: Two (or more) people (the Interlocutors, also encodable as Interlocutor_1 and Interlocutor_2) talk to one another. No person is construed as only a speaker or only an addressee. Rather, it is understood that both (or all) participants do some speaking and some listening--the process is understood to be symmetrical or reciprocal. This frame differs from the Chatting frame in that the Interlocutors have a particular question that they are attempting to decide or understand.

frameElementDefinitions.Interlocutor_1: In cases where the Interlocutors involved in a conversation are construed as two separate groups with different levels of volitionality or control, this FE identifies the conversational participant with greater semantic and syntactic prominence, as compared with Interlocutor_2. That is, Interlocutor_1 is generally encoded as Subject, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to Interlocutor_1. Thus a Purpose (or Means, Depictive, or Manner) is construed to be that of Interlocutor_1, not necessarily that of Interlocutor_2 or the group of Interlocutors as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Interlocutor_2: In cases where the Interlocutors involved in a conversation are construed as two separate groups with different levels of volitionality or control, Interlocutor_2 refers to the semantically and grammatically less prominent group. It is generally encoded within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by with). Note that it is possible for a single role to correspond to multiple referents. For example, if the prepositional object in the example below were plural, we would still assign it the single role Interlocutor_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Interlocutors: In some sentences both participants are expressed by the Subject, and there is no 'with'-phrase (cf example 1). In sentences like this, the Subject is assigned the FE role Interlocutors. In such sentences, the Subject always denotes more than one person, and it is understood that the reciprocal communication takes place between these two (or more) people. The Subject may denote two or more people either through conjunction, as in example 1, or through plurality, as in example 2, or by denoting a group, as in example 3. In each of these cases the FE Interlocutors is assigned. Notice that for nouns in the Discussion frame, it is possible for the Interlocutors role to be expressed by a prepositional phrase as in example 4.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic expresses the subject that the Interlocutors are trying to come to some agreement about. It may refer, for example, to an undesirable issue that the Interlocutors want to have the proper response to, or it may refer more directly to an unresolved question.
frameElementDefinitions.Language: The linguistic system(s) used by the Interlocutors to discuss something.
frameElementDefinitions.Amount_of_discussion: Amount_of_discussion applies to phrases expressing how thoroughly a Topic has been discussed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies a depictive phrase describing Interlocutor_1 or the pair of Interlocutors.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This frame element marks phrases that expresss the Manner in which one or both of the Interlocutors communicate.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This Frame Elememt marks constituents expressing the means the parties to the conversation use to talk to each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration of the conversation.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal that Interlocutor_1 or the Interlocutors jointly have, i.e. the state of affairs that they wish to bring about by the conversation.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the discussion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which a discussion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_communication: The device or system that facilitates a discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or a state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the discussion takes place and of which it is taken to be part.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times the discussion happens in a given period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The general field or area in which the discussion takes place, or which shapes the discussion or its topic.

annotations.definition: A Traveller leaves from or dismounts a Vehicle.

frameElementDefinitions.Traveller: The Traveller is the entity which disembarks from the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Vehicle is the means of conveyance that the Traveller disembarks from.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes a property of the motion not related to its trajectory.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path describes the trajectory of the Traveller's motion off or out of the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The amount of time the Traveller spends off or outside of the Vehicle after disembarking from it.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Traveller or Vehicle during the disembarking.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the disembarking of the Vehicle takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: A theme that leaves the Vehicle together with the main Traveller. This may be an entity the Traveller transports or a co-traveller moving under their own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Traveller disembarks from the vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Traveller disembarks from the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The location that the Traveller reaches by leaving the Vehicle.

annotations.definition: An implicit cognizer judges that a particular State_of_affairs for which a Protagonist is responsible is socially unacceptable.

frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The entity or event which brings disgrace upon the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The person or organization responsible for the occurrence of the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the State_of_affairs diverges from the social norm for things of its kind.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the judgment of disgrace logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: An Agent or a Cause disperses or scatters Individuals from the Source,a relatively confined space, to a the Goal_area, a broader space.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent disperses or scatters the Individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: The Individuals are scattered by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is the relatively confined space from which an Agent scatters the Individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal_area: Goal_area identifies the broader space to which the Agent scatters the Individuals
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event or force that causes that Individuals to disperse.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the dispersing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). With agentive uses, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Individuals are dispersed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the dispersal.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: A state of the Individuals that results from the dispersal.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The trajectory along which the Individuals move.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the dispersing event happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in dispersing the Individuals
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance that the Individuals move from their original location, the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate of motion of the Individuals, usually in terms of how long it takes for them to leave the Source and end up in the Goal_area.

annotations.definition: A improvised explosive device, the Bomb, is rigged to detonate when it receives a signal, typically by a wire that leads to a trigger. The trigger may be operated directly by the Detonator, in which case the weapon is known as a CWIED (command wire IED). Or, the triggering mechanism may be activated wirelessly, such as by a cell phone. In this case, the weapon is called an RCIED (remote control IED).

frameElementDefinitions.Bomb: The Bomb is set off by the Detonator at a distance, injuring or damaging a Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The type of activity that the Bomb is generally involved in. Often this can be considered a kind of Type specification also.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Bomb belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The substance that makes up the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Detonator: The person that wishes to attack the Target and who activates the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: This FE identifies a part of the Bomb. This includes parts that aid in the detonation or damaging, or mere attachments.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which a Bomb comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The individual or group responsible for the creation of the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description or characteristic of the Bomb not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Target: The intended or actual target of the Bomb.

annotations.definition: A Feature of an Entity serves to distinguish the Entity from other members of its type which have a different value for the Feature. Note that the Entity is often implicit.

frameElementDefinitions.Feature: The aspect of the Entity which makes it different from the other members of its class.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The item which differs from other members of its class in the Feature's value.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the values of the Feature of the Entity and the Comparison_set are similar to each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.

annotations.definition: There is a Location and an abundant or large Theme. The Location and Theme are largely coextensive, i.e. occupying the same area.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme covers or fills the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: Location is the object or area that the Theme fills or covers.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree of abundance with which the Theme covers or fills the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time during which the Theme and the Location are positioned relative to each other.

annotations.definition: This frame involves a static (primarily spatial) relationship between a Location and a Theme, in which the Theme is located in many or all subregions of the Location, and is thus construable as covering it. The Time when the covering configuration holds, and the Subregion of the Location that is involved may, optionally, be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme in this frame is the object which is described as standing in some spatial relation to a particular Location. Theme typically occurs as the External Argument or, with passives, as a PP Complement headed by "with" or "by".
frameElementDefinitions.Location: Location is the entity or area with respect to which the Theme's spatial arrangement is described. This frame element generally occurs as an NP Object.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Location which contains or is covered by the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Theme occupies the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Region_quantification: A description of how much of a potential profiled region actually has (or is expected to have) a Theme in it. It is normally filled by closed-class items like 'all', 'somewhere', 'nowhere', etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Location interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class adverbial back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Theme before). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can background motion entirely (as in the example below).

annotations.definition: A Group is described as having members which are similar or dissimilar to each other. The Degree of similarity may also be expressed. Some of the nouns in this frame are transparent (e.g. range.n) whereas others denote the scale of diversity (diversity); variety.n is usable both ways but is normally transparent.

frameElementDefinitions.Group: This FE marks constituents that express the set of objects or types whose diversity is at issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: This FE marks constituents which express a property in respect to which the similarity of the Group is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the members of the Group are similar to each other, in general or with respect to some Dimension(s)
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Group is diverse or homogenous.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the general area in which diversity occurs or exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which Dimension is similar or different
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An inanimate entity or process that causes the diversity or homogeneity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describing the state of the Group

annotations.definition: Words in the frame refer to any Document that has a legal status or conventional social significance. Some Documents empower the Bearer of the Document to execute the Right. Others indicate the Obligation of the Bearer. Still others show the identity or Status of the Bearer.

frameElementDefinitions.Issuer: The authority that issues the document.
frameElementDefinitions.Obligation: Obligation is the obligation that is imposed on the Bearer of the Document.
frameElementDefinitions.Right: Right is the right of the Bearer of the Document that is authorized by the Document.
frameElementDefinitions.Status: Status is the state of the Bearer of the Document indicated by the Document.
frameElementDefinitions.Specification: A Specification is a condition that applies to the Rights and/or Obligations described in the Document.
frameElementDefinitions.Document: Document is the piece of paper that has the legal status.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The language the Document is written in, or a specification of the kind of object that instantiates it.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Descriptor covers any characterization or description of the target that is not covered by another more specific FE.

annotations.definition: A Dodger acts to avoid an oncoming moving Bad_entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Dodger: The Dodger acts to avoid an oncoming Bad_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Bad_entity: The Bad_entity is the oncoming entity to avoid.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the dodging occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the dodging occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the dodging occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which the dodging occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the dodging occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the dodging occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the dodging occurs.

annotations.definition: A Predicate is said to apply in a particular Domain. The understanding of the relation between Domain and Predicate depends on the nature of the Predicate. When the Predicate is a complete proposition, then a Domain adverbial also functions as a topic restrictor. Sentences with topic restrictors often come in pairs. When the Predicate is a simple predicate, there is no notion of topic restriction on the sentence level.

frameElementDefinitions.Predicate: This Frame Element denotes the Predicate which is applied to the Domain.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: This Frame Element applies to the Domain in which the Predicate is said to apply.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.

annotations.definition: An Agent, by virtue of strength or forcefulness, controls, rules, or strongly influences some Patient in some Situation.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent controls the Patient by being forceful and possibly aggressive.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is trying to gain control of a situation, but ends up being subject to the control and influence of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation within which the Agent is able to gain control over the Patient (thus excluding them from controlling the Situation).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time over which the domination occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the event as a whole, or of any of the participants that does not fit any of the other more specific Frame Elements.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the domination occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the domination takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Agent dominates the Patient.

annotations.definition: An Agent acts so as to be the strongest force, or most important causal factor in some Situation, to the exclusion of other competing actors.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the most forceful and pivotal factor in the situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation is primarily affected by the Agent, in particular how it develops or concludes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent is dominant.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time over which the domination occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the domination takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the event as a whole, or of any of the participants that does not fit any of the other more specific Frame Elements.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Agent dominates the Situation.

annotations.definition: An Agent has a influential, controlling position in a Situation, and is the primary causal factor in the development of the Situation, leaving other Competitors unable to take controlling positions. Two perspectives are taken on this frame, one where the Agent is viewed as controlling primarily the Situation, and another where the Agent is viewed as primarily influencing the other Competitors in the Situation.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent has a controlling position in the Situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation is primarily affected by the Agent, in particular how it develops or concludes.
frameElementDefinitions.Competitor: The Competitors are trying to gain control of a situation, but end up being subject to the control and influence of the Agent .
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the domination takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the dominationw
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner of the domination.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which the domination takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Agent dominates.

annotations.definition: The LUs of this frame describe a lump of Dough expanding as gas develops inside it. This is part of a process where, in order to produce a lighter, more easily chewed bread, Dough is leavened with yeast, baking soda or in another fashion to add gas to it before baking.

frameElementDefinitions.Dough: The paste made out of any cereals (grains) or leguminous crops by grinding, with some water added, which expands in volume as a result of leavening before baking.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_activity: The length of the interval during which the Dough rises.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the expansion of the Dough takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed at which the Dough expands.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the rising of the Dough which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: A setting, often temporal or spatial, within which the event takes place. This FE also covers conditions that are causally connected to the event of the Dough rising.
frameElementDefinitions.Size_change: The amount by which the volume of the Dough increases.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The entity or state of affairs that leads to the event of the Dough's rising.

annotations.definition: An Agent disables a flying or floating Patient, causing it to be unable to support itself in its current medium.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent forces the Patient to come to the ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity that is being forced to the ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of causing the Patient to come to the ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by an Agent that enables them to force the Patient to the ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which causes the Patient to come to the ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An object used by the Agent to facilitate bringing down the Patient.

annotations.definition: A Wearer puts on an item of Clothing, which then occupies the Body_location.

frameElementDefinitions.Wearer: The individual that puts Clothing on their body.
frameElementDefinitions.Clothing: Clothing is used for the item of clothing that the Wearer ends up wearing.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_location: The body part that the Clothing ends up covering.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of the donning event which is not directly related to the trajectory of the Clothing's motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the Wearer or Clothing during the act.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an act whereby the Wearer puts on the Clothing
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the Placing.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of the Wearing that results from putting on the Clothing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place indicates where the Donning occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation indicates why the Wearer puts on the Clothing.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Wearer puts an item of Clothing on.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Wearer intends to accomplish by putting on Clothing.

annotations.definition: A Visitor arrives at the Host_location as a guest of the Host, for the purpose of achieving a Purpose. The Purpose is often social in nature, i.e., the visit itself constitutes a goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Visitor: The Visitor arrives at the Host_location to visit the Host for a Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: Normal_location is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion. While Normal_location expressions are possible in this frame, they are relatively infrequent. If present, they often express a general direction from which a Visitor moves, rather than a landmark away from which it moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Normal_location nor the Host or Host_location. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host is the person highly associated with the goal location of the Visitor, and who allows the Visitor onto his/her territory, and possibly to make use of available resources. The Host and Visitor and in a host-guest relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A Manner expression describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Visitor arrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Visitor is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Visitor. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when arriving occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Visitor arrives at a location associated with the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Host_location: The Host_location is a place primarily associated with the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The Containing_event denotes an event or state of affairs that occurs or holds at a time that includes the time during which the visiting takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frequency with whic hthe Visitor drops in on a Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Visitor remains with the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The larger area within which the visiting takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: This FE indicates that the visiting is a particular instance in a series of repeated visiting events.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that explains why the Visitor is making a visit.

annotations.definition: An Agent temporarily places a Theme into a Substance, often with the intention to remove it later. The Substance may be metonymically represented by its container. The Theme may be partially or completely submerged.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent places the Theme into a Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme temporarily goes partially or completely into the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Substance: The Substance is the fluid into which the Theme is placed. This may be metonymically represented by the container of the fluid.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Agent puts Theme in the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The location in time when the dunking occurs.

annotations.definition: This frame involves a Creator making a duplicate, the Copy, of some Original entity. A Source, the location of the Original, and Goal, the location of the Copy, may be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The Creator produces the Copy based on the Original. It is normally expressed as an External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Original: The Original is the entity which is copied. With verbs it is frequently expressed as an NP Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Copy: The Copy produced by the Creator is frequently not expressed. However, when Copy does occur, it is usually expressed as the External Argument of verbs:
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location from which material is copied, i.e. the location of the Original. Unlike the FE Source in other domains and frames, there is no actual movement of either Copy or Original from this location. Source is usually expressed by a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the location to which material is copied. Unlike the FE Goal in other frames, there is no actual movement of any entity to this location. Goal is usually expressed as a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Characterization: This FE designates the character of the medium in which a Copy is made. Usually, it is expressed as PP-in or AP.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Creator that lets them bring about the duplication.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase that describes a primary participant, either the Creator or the Copy/ Original.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes Place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Fidelity: The way in which the Copy matches or does not match the Original.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The Iterations is the number of times the Original is copied.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency is the frequency with which the Original is copied.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: An entity that participates in much the same way as another participant (Creator, Copy, or Original), although it is characterized as being less focal to the action.

annotations.definition: An event or state (here termed an Eventuality) or time-Period is described in terms of the Degree to which its duration diverges from a standard. As is usual for gradable attributes, the standard of comparison is normally based on the expected duration for the type of Eventuality or Period. (In conjunction with the comparative construction, the standard may also be based upon an explicit value.)

frameElementDefinitions.Eventuality: A situation, action or event whose duration is being described. The Eventuality occurs as the External Argument if the adjective is used predicatively, or as the modified noun if the adjective is used attributively: The lecture was short. We had a lengthy conversation.
frameElementDefinitions.Period: Sometimes these adjectives describe simply a period of time, rather than an event or state. The Period almost invariably occurs as the modified noun with an attributive adjective: We waited for only a short time. Use of the drug over a lengthy period can cause memory loss.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs

annotations.definition: A Period or Eventuality is related to its Duration. Alternatively, an Entity may stand in metonymically for an Eventuality that it is involved in.

frameElementDefinitions.Eventuality: A situation, action or event whose duration is being described. The Eventuality occurs as the External Argument if the adjective is used predicatively, or as the modified noun if the adjective is used attributively:
frameElementDefinitions.Period: Sometimes a period of time, rather than an event or state is described.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the Eventuality, Period, or Entity lasts.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: An item which is metonymically related to an Eventuality. Some common eventualities that are metonymically entailed are the effect of the entity in its capacity as an instrument and the existence of the entity.
frameElementDefinitions.User: An individual that deploys a resource to receive a benefit. The construction that introduces this FE is idiosyncratic, combining with last (in this frame), cost, and take--and more marginally see (in "saw me through the winter") and keep (in "It 'll keep me for a while .") In this frame, the resource maps onto the Entity, and the benefit is the Eventuality.

annotations.definition: An Event or Period has a particular Duration. The Duration may be characterized in several different ways: as a Durational direct measure, a Degree of deviation from an expected Duration, or as a Count of Units of standard, known duration.

frameElementDefinitions.Period: The Period whose the duration Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Period which is under discussion, namely the duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: A quantity or other characterization of the Period's absolute duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Duration from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: A standard measure, having a known duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of Units which are equivalent to the Duration of the Period .
frameElementDefinitions.Event: An process whose duration is measured.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist is near or approaching death.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The FE Protagonist is the being or entity that dies.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the dying event happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation why the dying event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the dying event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: How long the Protagonist is dying.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the dying event occurs.

annotations.definition: This frame denotes simple Events in which an Entity is in a dynamic situation, usually punctually or continually changing to conform to the situation encoded in the Event_endstate. This frame also covers situations in which there is no significant difference between the pre- and post-states, but the dynamic situation that occurs between them is notably temporally bounded.

frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Concrete or abstract entity
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: Name of the event which occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.

annotations.definition: A physical Exerter, Action, or Force exerts energy at a level specified by the target. This frame covers both individual-level/static uses ("an INTENSE person") as well as stage-level/dynamic uses ("an INTENSE look"), although the individual-level meaning seems more basic.

frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action that exerts an energy of the level specified by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Exerter: The Exerter which exerts an energy of the level specified by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes the energy exertion of the Exerter, Action, or Force.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Exerter, Action, or Force exerts an energy.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Exerter, Action, or Force exerts an energy.
frameElementDefinitions.Force: The Force that exerts an energy of the level specified by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Exerter, Action, or Force exerts an energy.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Exerter, Action, or Force exerts its energy.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain is the field in which the Exerter, Action, or Force exerts its energy.

annotations.definition: An Earner receives Earnings by providing Goods to a Buyer; providing the Goods also generally entails a cost, which may or may not be included in the consideration. This frame emphasizes the effect on the Earner and often generalizes across an indefinite number of transactions with various parties. Some of the LUs (e.g. earn.v) primarily refer to the to the Earnings without reference to cost, others to the amount the Earnings differ from the cost, emphasizing either a net loss (e.g. loss.n) or net gain (e.g. net.n).

frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The Buyer is the source of the Earnings of the Earner.
frameElementDefinitions.Earner: The Earner is involved in a transaction or set of transactions as the party that receives payment.
frameElementDefinitions.Earnings: Earnings are the money given to the Earner in a transaction, generally in exchange for Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: Goods is anything including labor or time, for example, which is exchanged for Earnings in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, earning is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Earner gains money.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the action of earning which reveals correllated details, especially characterizations of the Earner which influence the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: A state of affairs that the Earner intends to bring about as a result of participating in the Commercial Transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Earner makes their Earnings.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Earner makes their Earnings.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies an event during which the Earner receives Earnings in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the event of earning or losing logically follows.

annotations.definition: An Obstruction blocks an Eclipsed entity partially or completely from view. The positions of the Obstruction and the Eclipsed entity are often reported relative to a Vantage_point.

frameElementDefinitions.Obstruction: The entity which blocks the Eclipsed entity from view.
frameElementDefinitions.Eclipsed: The entity which is blocked from view.
frameElementDefinitions.Vantage_point: The position from which an observer, if an observer were present, would have their view of the Eclipsed entity blocked by the Obstruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Eclipsed entity is blocked from view.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Eclipsed entity which is blocked from view.

annotations.definition: A Political_region, often a nation, has an economic system, theEconomy, defined by the level of production and consumption of goods and services. The Market_type or Domain may be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Political_region: The Political_region is the nation, state, or other political body within which an economic system exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Economy: The Economy is the state of the Political_region as defined by the production, consumption, etc. of goods and services.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain of the state for which the Economy is being considered.
frameElementDefinitions.Market_type: The type of economic market in which the Economy exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A further depction of the Economy.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words referring to teaching and the participants in teaching. A Student comes to learn either about a Subject; a Skill; a Precept; or a Fact as a result of instruction by a Teacher. Some of the nouns (schoolmaster, -mistress) in this frame refer to administrative positions and do not take relevant frame elements; these will be moved.

frameElementDefinitions.Teacher: One who instructs a Student in some area of knowledge or skill.
frameElementDefinitions.Student: One who is instructed by a Teacher in skills or knowledge.
frameElementDefinitions.Institution: An educational establishment, such as a school or college.
frameElementDefinitions.Subject: The area of knowledge or skill which is taught by a Teacher or to a Student.
frameElementDefinitions.Level: This FE identifies the Level of a student in his/her education. This is distinct from the goal or qualification towards which a student is working (Qualification ).
frameElementDefinitions.Qualification: A formal Qualification such as an academic degree or a certificate for which a student is aiming.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the actor (Student or Teacher depending on the profiling of particular words) that lets them achieve the instruction or learning they are attempting.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the teaching occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location within which the teaching takes place. Place phrase is really where the training took place or is rather part of a description of the Institution. -->
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Skill: An action which the Student is able to perform as a result of instruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Precept: A guideline for correct behavior. In most cases, this concerns morally or socially desirable actions.
frameElementDefinitions.Fact: A piece of information that the Student is informed of by the Teacher.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Educational Material, such as books, tapes, or videos, used by a Teacher or a Student to acquire skills or knowledge.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: A role, typically professional or vocational, that the Student is meant to be able to fill as a result of their training.
frameElementDefinitions.Course: FN: a program of lectures or other matter dealing with a subject
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Teacher hopes to bring about by teaching.

annotations.definition: Lexical units in this frame refer to Electricity, in particular as a form of energy harnessed for particular uses (such as powering machines). The Source of the Electricity may also be expressed, or incorporated in the meaning of the LUs.

frameElementDefinitions.Electricity: Electricity is the physical force that is used as power.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source of the Electricity.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of some property of the Electricity.
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The intended Inherent_purpose of the Electricity.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer does not achieve a Desired_goal. Typically, the Experiencer is the object of the verb. Metaphorically, a pursued entity is not captured.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer does not achieve a Desired_goal, or capture a pursued entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Desired_goal: Desired_goal identifies the goal that is not achieved or the entity that is not captured.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Experiencerr fails to achieve a Desired_goal .
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Experiencer fails to achieve a Desired_goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the goal is not achieved.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Experiencer fails to reach a goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time during which the Experiencer does not acheive the Desired_goal.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Emission emanates from a Source along a Path or to a Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is used for expressions which describe a general area in which the emanating takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the emanation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the the state of the Emission.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is an expression characterizing the extent of motion of the Emission
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which emanation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Emission: The Emissions are discharged out of the Source .
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE is the point at which the Emission ends up as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the emanating occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: This FE is a description of a trajectory of emanation which is neither a Source nor Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate at which emanation occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the emanation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Carrier: This is the means of conveyance of the Emission .
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location from which the Emission emanates.
frameElementDefinitions.Form: The Form is the state in which the Emission is emitted.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the result of the emanating.

annotations.definition: This frame characterizes the situation or state of affairs in which someone should or must act to prevent a Undesirable_event from occurring. Often, an Entity affected by the Undesirable_event is more salient than the event itself and is mentioned in its place.

frameElementDefinitions.Undesirable_event: The potentially damaging event that should be prevented or ameliorated.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The individual or valued object that is threatened by a worsening condition.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The conditions in which the Undesirable_event occurs; or, the circumstances that explain the impending threat to the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the emergency situation arises.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The domain of the situation which the emergency is affecting.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: This FE identifies the experiencer within the emergency action chain, often relating the person or group who feels or is threatened by the emergency.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Emergency occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Timespan: The period within which an Emergency has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: How long the Emergency takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Emergency occurs.

annotations.definition: A fire which burns and requires an emergency-type response.

frameElementDefinitions.Fire: The total material currently involved in combustion, including both the parts of the Fuel currently undergoing chemical alteration and the partially ionized gas that results from this process.
frameElementDefinitions.Fuel: The entity that is undergoing rapid chemical alteration leading to damage or destruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Emergency_fire event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (loudly, urgently), and general descriptions comparing events.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: Name given to the burning Emergency_fire
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Fire is burning.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Fire is burning.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Source_emitter discharges its Emission along a Path or to a Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Source_emitter emits the Emission
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Source_emitter emits the Emission.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_emitter: The Source_emitter is the item from which the Emission are discharged.
frameElementDefinitions.Emission: The Emissions are discharged out of the Source_emitter .
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is used for expressions which describe a general area in which emission takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which emission occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the the state of the Emission or Source_emitter.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is an expression characterizing the extent of motion of the Emission
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE is the point at which the Emission ends up as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Source_emitter emits.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: This FE is a description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source_emitter or Sub_source nor Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Sub-source: The Sub-source is the subregion of the Source_emitter which emits the Emission.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate at which emission occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Source_emitter emits the Emission
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Source_emitter emits the Emission.
frameElementDefinitions.Carrier: This is the means of conveyance of the Emission.
frameElementDefinitions.Form: The Form is the state in which the Emission is emitted.

annotations.definition: This frame has similarities to Experiencer_subject, but here the verbs are more 'active' in meaning. (For example, they often occur in negative imperatives, e.g. "Don't worry!''). They also differ from Experiencer_subject verbs in that they occur with Topic expressions and can be used in the present progressive:

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The FE Experiencer indicates the one who is actively expressing emotion over the Topic. It usually occurs as the subject of the verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Experiencer's emotion generally is about something. This is indicated with a Topic which usually occurs in a PP-Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The FE Degree indicates the degree to which the Experiencer expresses the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The FE Manner indicates the manner in which the active emotion is expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The FE Result indicates what happens due to the Experiencer actively expressing emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Experiencer which brings about the active emotion.

annotations.definition: The adjectives and nouns in this frame describe an Experiencer who is feeling or experiencing a particular emotional response to a Stimulus or about a Topic. There can also be a Circumstances under which the response occurs or a Reason that the Stimulus evokes the particular response in the Experiencer.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the degree to which the Experiencer feels the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Frame Element Expressor marks expressions that indicate a body part, gesture or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occasion or happening that Experiencers in a certain emotional state participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.

annotations.definition: This frame contains verbs that describe emotional experiences and participate in the locative alternation, as in the following examples:

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the individual that experiences the Emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Emotion: The Emotion experienced (usually restricted to words such as anger, fury, rage, passion) can occur as the External Argument or in a PP Complement. Alternatively, there may be no expression of Emotion separate from the target verb:
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause of the Emotion can be expressed in a PP Complement headed by at:
frameElementDefinitions.Seat_of_emotion: The words in this frame can occur with an overtly expressed Seat of the Emotion. This frame element occurs as a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is the entity that the proposition or propositions conveyed relate to, that they are about. Had someone communicated to the capital about the flagrant disregard of the religious law ?
frameElementDefinitions.Expression: The Expression is the words or behavior through which the Emotion is expressed.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer has a particular emotional State, which may be described in terms of a specific Stimulus that provokes it, or a Topic which categorizes the kind of Stimulus. Rather than expressing the Experiencer directly, it may (metonymically) have in its place a particular Event (with participants who are Experiencers of the emotion) or an Expressor (a body-part of gesture which would give an indication of the Experiencer's state to an external observer).

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part, gesture, or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occasion or happening that Experiencers in a certain emotional state participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer, Expressor, Event, or State can be described as characterized by a particular emotion as evoked by a possibility in their future (Stimulus) or concerning a given Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part, gesture, or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occasion or happening that Experiencers in a certain emotional state participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer, Expressor, Event, or State can be described as having said emotion.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer, Expressor, Event, or State has an emotion as brought on by an Stimulus or Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part, gesture, or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occasion or happening that Experiencers in a certain emotional state participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer, Expressor, Event, or State can be described as having said emotion.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer can be described as having an emotion as induced by a Stimulus.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer has an emotion as caused by the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE sums up what is expressed in the main clause, often with a VPing.

annotations.definition: A Topic or Stimulus fails or succeeds at causing an emotion in an Experiencer, which is conveyed by an Expressor or manifested in a State or description of an Event.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part, gesture, or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occasion or happening that Experiencers in a certain emotional state participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer, Expressor, Event, or State can be described as having said emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times the Experiencer feels the emotion.

annotations.definition: An Agent who is engaged in an Undertaking accords a Degree of importance to a Consideration that influences the success of an Undertaking. As a consequence, the Agent acts in a way that reflects that judgment of importance.

frameElementDefinitions.Consideration: An event or state of affairs, an attribute, or an entity whose properties have the potential to influence the success of the Agent's Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE marks expressions that indicate the Degree to which the Consideration is important to the Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Undertaking: The Undertaking is a process or state or field of activity in which the Agent participates.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Consideration is important to the Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the individual who devotes time, concentration, or other resources to a Consideration that is important for the success of a larger Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by assigning the specified level of importance to the Consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that allows the Agent to respond to the importance of the Factor to the Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Competing_consideration: The Competing_consideration is another factor that is relevant to the Undertaking to which the Agent devotes less attention or effort.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Agent attaches a specified degree of importance to the Consideration to further the success of the Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Agent attaches the specified level of importance to the Consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any further description of the Agent's placing of importance on the Consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Artifact: The Artifact is a physical object designed with some Consideration in mind, such that it functions to facilitate the Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action is performed with importance placed on the Consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often. It is to be distinguished from Iteration, which pertains simply to the number of times an event occurs-Iteration expressions answer the question how many times. In the future, we will further distinguish Particular_iteration, which is intended to pick out a particular iteration from a series.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state or quality of an entity involved in the action that holds during the action.

annotations.definition: The sequence of events in which the Employee hires on with an Employer, holds a Position, and finally leaves the Position.

frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: Compensation is the payment that the Employee receives for performing a Task. It often varies over the lifetime of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person who is obligated to perform some Task in order to receive Compensation.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) that gives Compensation to an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: Task identifies the action that the Employee is obligated to do for the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: This FE identifies the Field in which one works.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the amount of time for which the Employee continues in employ.

annotations.definition: The sequence of events in which the Employer hires an Employee, employs them in a Position for some Duration, and finally lets them go from the Position.

frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: Compensation is the payment that the Employee receives for performing a Task.
frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person who is obligated to perform some Task in order to receive Compensation.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) that gives Compensation to an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: Task identifies the action that the Employee is obligated to do for the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: This FE identifies the Field in which one works.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the amount of time for which the Employee continues in employ.

annotations.definition: An Employer employs an Employee whose Position entails that the Employee perform certain Tasks in exchange for Compensation.

frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Employee receives for performing a Task.
frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The FE Employee denotes the person who is obligated to perform some Task in order to receive Compensation.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The Employer is the person (or institution) that gives Compensation to an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: The FE Field identifies the field in which the Employee is employed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The FE Manner identifies the way in which an Employer hires an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The FE Place identifies the location where the Employer hires the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The FE Position indicates a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The FE Purpose identifies what the Employer intends to accomplish through the hiring of the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: The Task indicates the action/duty that the Employee is obligated to do for the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The FE Time identifies the time at whcih the Employer hires the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The FE Duration identifies the amount of time for which the Employee continues in employ.
