frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis is the condition of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period or payment arrangements.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes a characteristic of the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Subgroup: The Subgroup is the department or subdivision of the institution that the Employee is a member of.

annotations.definition: This is a non-perspectivized frame representing the middle stage of the Employment_scenario, in which there is a stable employment relationship between the Employee and the Employer.

frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person who is obligated to perform some Task in order to receive Compensation.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) that gives Compensation to an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: Compensation is the payment that the Employee receives for performing a Task.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the amount of time for which the Employee continues the employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: This FE identifies the Field in which one works.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the relationship between an Employer and an Employee develops.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Employee works for the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: Task identifies the action that the Employee is obligated to do for the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when an Employer employs the Employee.

annotations.definition: This is a non-perspectivized frame representing the final stage of the Employment_scenario, in which the relationship between the Employer and the Employee comes to an end. There are two different ways in which the relation ends, represented in the Firing frame (in which the Employer is agentive) and the Quitting frame (in which the Employee is agentive).

frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person whose employment relationship with an Employer ends.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) whose employ the Employee leaves.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an employment relationship between an Employer and an Employee ends.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Employer/Employee relationship is ended.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Employer/Employee relationship ceases.

annotations.definition: An Employee and Employer enter into an employment relation, wherein the Employee remains employed for some Duration of time, and finally the relationship ends either by the Employee leaving the job or the Employer letting go (or firing) the Employee. To each of these events there are concomitants, such as agreeing to/signing a contract for entering employment, compensation and performance of a service for the employment period itself, and severance for the dissolution of the relationship. There are several other events involved, including preparatory actions on the part of the Employer (posting the Position), the prospective Employee's part (looking for a job), or both (job interviews). In addition, there is the possibility of change in the relationship of Employer and Employee during the employment period, such as a change in Position (e.g. promotion, demotion) and a change in Compensation (e.g. raise, paycut).

frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: Compensation is the payment that the Employee receives for performing a Task.
frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person who is obligated to perform some Task in order to receive Compensation.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) that gives Compensation to an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: Task identifies the action that the Employee is obligated to do for the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: This FE identifies the Field in which one works.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the amount of time for which the Employee continues in employ.

annotations.definition: This is a non-perspectivized frame representing the initial stage of the Employment_scenario: the formation of the employment relationship between the Employer and the Employee.

frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person who enters an employment relationship with an Employer, thereby obligating themself to perform assigned Tasks in order to receive Compensation .
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) that enters an employment relationship with an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: Compensation is the payment that the Employee receives for performing assigned Tasks in the course of their employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: This FE identifies the Field which the Employee enters as they take up employment with the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the employment relationship between an Employer and an Employee comes about.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Employer and the Employee enter into the employment relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: Task identifies the assignment that the Employee is obligated to do when she enters the employment relationship with the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when an Employer and an Employee establish an employment relationship.

annotations.definition: These are words relating to emptying containers and clearing areas of some substance or items. The area or container can appear as the direct object with all these verbs, and is designated Source because it is the source of motion of the Theme. Corresponding to its nuclear argument status, it is also affected in some crucial way, unlike Source in other frames. Some words in this frame, such as empty, may also occur with Path or Goal expressions, e.g.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the entity that does the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme, when overtly expressed, invariably occurs in a PP Complement headed by "of".
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the region or container which is emptied of something. In this frame, the Source occurs as an NP Object.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is any expression which tells where the Theme ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depicitive phrase describing either the Agent or Theme of the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the Agent emptying the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This is the object with which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-sentient force which instigates the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the emptying takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the emptying takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason why the Agent empties the Source of the Theme.

annotations.definition: A person (Speaker) expresses a Message or mental content, broadly understood, in a particular Manner. The content may be emotional, as in

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: Speaker, the person who encodes the Message, occurs as the External Argument of all the verbs:
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message is almost invariably expressed in an NP Object. (The only exceptions occur with the verb express, which is occasionally used with that-clause complements.)
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: For most of the words in this frame, some expression of Manner or Medium is obligatory. As in other frames, Manner expressions characterize the action (in this case, the speech act performed by the Speaker). However, a wide range of Manner expressions occurs in this frame, including the following types:
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium expresses the Medium of communication, such as the language used or the specific type of text in which the Message occurs:
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the communication is directed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act done by the Speaker whereby they achieve the phrasing of the Message.

annotations.definition: This frame describes Individuals who unintentionally come together to form a Group. Alternatively, a grammatically more prominent Party_1 meets by chance a Party_2, expressed as an oblique phrase.

frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group describes the resulting formation of the Individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: The Individuals are self-movers who unintentionally meet up.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: Party_1 is the grammatically more prominent participant in the encountering event. It can refer to either a single individual or a larger party separate from Party_2 previous to the by chance meeting.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: A grammatically less prominent participant in the encountering event. It can refer to either a single individual or a larger party separate from Party_1 previous to the by chance meeting.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the unintentional meeting which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects and general descriptions comparing events In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Individuals or Group that also affect the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means descibes the way in which the Individuals came together to form the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the encountering event happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the encountering event happens.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause is responsible for placing a Valued_entity at risk. Endangering_act, Place, Time, Reason, and Circumstance may also be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An (non-sentient) entity or state of affairs that leads to the peril to the Valued_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Valued_entity: The entity which may be negatively impacted as a result of the emperilment.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity which causes the risk to the Valued_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Endangering_act: The specific event or state of affairs brought about by the Agent or Cause which endangers the Valued_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes an aspect of the endangering act that is not directly related to the cause, intermediate action, or entity involved.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Valued_entity is put in danger.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Valued_entity is put in danger.

annotations.definition: Some individual or group ceases to be able to sustain an Endeavor (a long-term activity involving economic interaction with the public, often a business venture). Unlike Business_failure and Locale_closure, the Endeavor need not refer to an organization or business tied to a specific location or locations.

frameElementDefinitions.Endeavor: The Endeavor is rendered unable to continue its regular operations.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is named as one of the causes of failure.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time of the failure.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place of the failing Endeavor.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances that caused failure. Compared to Explanation, this describes situations or environments that lead to failure, not a specific reason for failure.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes the way in which the Endeavor failed.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The Containing_event is an event within which the failure occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A Depictive describes something about the Endeavor.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Endeavor fails.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Endeavor fails.

annotations.definition: An Agent acts to ensure that a Rule (often a constraint on behavior) is being followed. The Agent's action is according to a general plan for Rules (such that implicit in the Rule is the affordance to certain entities to ensure that the Rule is followed). The Rule may be specified for the Duration for which it is in effect.

frameElementDefinitions.Rule: The Rule is the set of constrants on behavior that the Agent implements (i.e., forces others to obey or face consequences).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Rule is or is not in effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Agent enforces the Rule.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place at which the Rule is or is not enforced.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state-of-affairs under which the Agent puts the Rule into effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A cause, reason, or other explanation for the enforcing of the Rule.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_participant: The individual or group who is obligated to follow the Rule.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent, often an institution, governmental organization, or arm thereof, puts into effect (i.e., enforces) a Rule.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes some state of the Agent, or provides additional characterization of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an intermediate action done by the Agent in order to enforce the Rule.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent enforces the Rule.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes how fully the Rule was enforced.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This FE describes the medium through which the rule was enforced. It may occur in the same syntactic position as the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of enforcing the rule.

annotations.definition: The Accused enters a Plea in answer to the Charges. In most jurisdictions, the possible pleas are innocent, guilty, or nolo contendere. Accused persons may stand mute, in which case, the court usually enters a plea on their behalf.

frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The judge of the court in which the accused enters a plea.
frameElementDefinitions.Accused: The person entering the plea in court.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: The crime with which the Accused is charged
frameElementDefinitions.Plea: The Plea that a defendant makes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Court: The Court is the institution before which the plea is entered.

annotations.definition: This frame is for words that denote highly schematic entities.

frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: Salient parts that make up the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Formational_cause indicates the action that brings about the features of an Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: This FE is used for the Name of an Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: A thing (either abstract or physical) that exists with some degree of permanence.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Entity belongs to.

annotations.definition: A group of Individuals form a group, the Entourage, which remains co-located with the Attended_person. The Entourage may have a specific Purpose for following the Attended_person, such as to protect or serve him or her, or the Purpose may be vague (e.g., to be near a person of power).

frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: This FE identifies the people or things that form the Entourage.
frameElementDefinitions.Entourage: This FE identifies the collection of entities that follow the Attended.
frameElementDefinitions.Aggregate_property: This FE describes a property of the Entourage rather than the Individuals and is used to tag the adjective that modifies the entourage noun when it is unambiguous.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to the Entourage.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location or political locale that the Entourage exists within.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant Domain as it pertains to the Entourage.
frameElementDefinitions.Attended_person: The Attended_person is followed by the Entourage.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause removes a piece of Information from a Document such that it cannot be retrieved.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who removes the Information.
frameElementDefinitions.Information: The thing that the Agent removes from the Document.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: A non-agentive entity that removes the Information.
frameElementDefinitions.Document: The collection of pieces of information from which the specific Information is removed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Agent or Cause removes the Information from the Document.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Agent or Cause removes the Information from the Document.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Agent or Cause removes the Information from the Document.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Agent or Cause removes the Information from the Document.

annotations.definition: A Self-moving Escapee departs from an Undesirable_location.

frameElementDefinitions.Escapee: The Escapee manages to leave the Undesirable_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal identifies any expression which tells where the Escapee ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: covers any act performed by the Escapee brings about the escape.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner is used for any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion, including descriptions of steadiness, grace, means of motion, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Undesirable_location: The Undesirable_location is where the Escapee begins.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: Speed idenitifies the rate at which the escaping occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: Vehicle identifies the means of conveyance by which the Escapee leaves the Undesirable_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the escaping occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the escaping takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Escaping occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a word or phrase describing a participant of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path describes the trajectory of motion of the Escapee.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the (abstract) nearness of the Escapee to being caught in the Undesirable_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance along a path away from the Undesirable_location that the Escapee travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The situation that explains why the (instance of) the event occurred.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer has carried out an approximate calculation or considered some Evidence and produced an approximation of the Value of a feature of an Item. There are two alternative construals in which the relationship between the feature and the Value is conceived. Either a holistic Estimation by the Cognizer is presented, or the Question that is addressed by the Cognizer's estimating is given.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The individual who has considered evidence or performed calculations to produce the approximation.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The position or area on the feature scale that the Cognizer has concluded the Item occupies.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity that possesses a feature whose Value the Cognizer has approximated.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: Some fact perceived by the Cognizer which has contributed to their estimation of the Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Cognizer formed the estimate.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Cognizer has formed their estimate.
frameElementDefinitions.Estimation: A proposition expressing the Cognizer's approximation of a feature's Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Question: An open proposition for which the Cognizer has approximated the Value of a variable.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer carries out an approximate calculation or considers some Evidence so as to arrive at an approximate Value for some Feature of an Item. There are two alternative construals. In one, the Feature is presented as a Question about the Item and the Value is conceived as an answer to the Question. In the second construal, a fully propositional Estimation by the Cognizer is presented which expresses the result of the Cognizer's approximation.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The individual who considers evidence or performs calculations to arrive at an approximation.
frameElementDefinitions.Feature: The feature is a scalar property of the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The position or area on the Feature scale that the Cognizer concludes the Item occupies.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity that possesses the Feature whose Value the Cognizer seeks to approximate.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: Some fact perceived by the Cognizer which contributes to their estimation of the Feature's Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Cognizer forms the estimate of the Feature's Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Cognizer forms their estimate.
frameElementDefinitions.Estimation: A proposition expressing the Cognizer's approximation of a Feature's Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Question: An open proposition for which the Cognizer seeks to approximate the Value of a variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Cognizer enabling estimating.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner is used for any expression which describes a property of the activity of Estimating
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the Circumstances under which the Cognizer estimates.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state-of-affairs enabling the estimating to occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE describes how the Estimation made by the Cognizer corresponds to reality. This FE can be represented by "correctly" or "wrong."
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: An Evader moves under its own power to thereby avoid Capture or contact with a Pursuer, also generally moving under its own power. This frame characterizes a more specific event than Avoiding in that both the Evader and the Pursuer are moving entities. In addition, this frame differs from Escaping by requiring a Pursuer and being neutral about the prior captivity of the Evader.

frameElementDefinitions.Area: This FE is used for phrases that describe a general Area in which motion occurs when it is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path. Locative setting adjuncts of motion expressions may also be assigned this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is used for any description of a trajectory of the motion of the Evader, including directional phrases.
frameElementDefinitions.Evader: The Evader moves under its own power to Capture or contact with the Pursuer. Normally the Evader frame element is expressed as an external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: This FE identifies the Mode_of_transportation that the Evader uses to move. It can either bea part of the Evader's own body or a vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Evader bringd about the evasion.
frameElementDefinitions.Pursuer: The Pursuer is going after the Evader unless they act.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE is used for any expression that describes the Degree to which the Evader avoids Capture or contact with the Pursuer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the evading occurs. It is used for any expression that does not relate directly to the trajectory of motion, including speed, steadiness, and grace.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Evading is done.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Evading is. done.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances under which the Evader evades the Pursuer, or avoids Capture.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which the Evading occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive is used to identify the state of any of the participants in the evading event.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This FE marks the Speed at which the Evader evades the Pursuer.
frameElementDefinitions.Capture: An actual or hypothetical event in which the Pursuer takes physical control of the Evader.

annotations.definition: This frame is about the static comparison of an Profiled_item to a Standard_item, often with respect to some Attribute. In this frame, there is an inherent asymmetry between Profiled_item and Standard_item in that the two cannot be expressed jointly as a subject. Degree expressions are also found that indicate how close the items are to each other on the scale evoked by the Attribute. Furthermore, the particular value of the standard or item on a certain scale may be given by the Standard_attribute of the Profiled_attribute respectively.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_item: In active sentences, the Profiled_item is the grammatically more prominent one of the items that are compared and realized as the subject. .
frameElementDefinitions.Standard_item: In active setnnces, the Standard_item is the grammatically less prominent element, i.e. the object or an oblique.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: This Frame Elements marks constituents that indicate in which respect Item-1 and Item-2 are equal to each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate how close the Profiled_item and the Standard_item are to each other on the scale evoked by the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard_attribute: The Standard_attribute is used when the standard is a specific value on a scale.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_attribute: The Profiled_attribute is a particular value on a scale which is being compared to a Standard_item or Profiled_attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: The Comparison_set includes the Standard_item and is what the Profiled_item is judged with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Profiled_item rivals the Standard_item.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Profiled_item rivals the Standard_item.

annotations.definition: An Event takes place at a Place and Time.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: Name of the event which occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: How long the Event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Timespan: The period within which an Event has occurred.

annotations.definition: The endstate of a simple event X-schema.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Concrete or abstract entity
frameElementDefinitions.State: State of an Entity.

annotations.definition: The initial state of a simple event X-schema.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Concrete or abstract entity
frameElementDefinitions.State: State of an Entity.

annotations.definition: This frame describes a particular instance of an event.

frameElementDefinitions.Instance: The particular Instance of a given event. All LUs in this frame incorporate this Frame Element.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The event that occurred in one or more instances.
frameElementDefinitions.Instance_prop: A property of the particular Instance.

annotations.definition: An Entity undergoes some change (the Event) that is (or is construed as) punctual. (This frame exists mainly to allow common FEs to be inherited.)

frameElementDefinitions.Event: This FE identifies the event which occurs and affects the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: This FE identifies the entity, concrete or abstract.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Event happens to the Entity.

annotations.definition: An Event causes the Cognizer to accept some Content. Although the Cognizer forms an intention, it is not entailed that he/she acts.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The person who perceives the Event and changes his or her mind.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The new idea that the Cognizer accepts as a result of experiencing the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event which motivates the Cognizer to change his/her mind.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Cognizer's mind is changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Cognizer is affected by an outside Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Cognizer accepts the Content.

annotations.definition: The Support, a phenomenon or fact, lends support to a claim or proposed course of action, the Proposition, where the Domain_of_relevance may also be expressed. Some of the words in this frame (e.g. argue) are communication words used in a non-communicative, epistemic sense.

frameElementDefinitions.Support: Support is a fact that lends epistemic support to a claim, or that provides a reason for a course of action. Typically it is expressed as an External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Proposition: This is a belief, claim, or proposed course of action to which the Support lends validity. Normally it is expressed as a Finite Clause Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the degree to which a Proposition is supported.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the manner in which a Proposition is supported.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intermediate logical step that justifies the relationship of the Support to the Proposition.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the status of the Support within its relationship to the Proposition. This proves my conclusions all by itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Domain_of_relevance: This FE identifies the domain (usually, time or space) for which a given Proposition is true.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The person for whom the Proposition is supported by the Support.

annotations.definition: Some Stimulus causes a Cognizer to think of a prior Phenomenon due to its perceived similarity.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person who remembers the Phenomenon .
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon is the mental content that a Cognizer remembers.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which Cognizer perceives the Stimulus to match the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including speed (quickly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Other expressions involving how the experience affects the Cognizer may also be expressed (painfully).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the amount of time for which the Stimulus continues to remind the Cognizer of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Stimulus evokes the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Cognizer remembers the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The entity or situation that causes the Cognizer to think of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Feature: A characteristic of the Stimulus that contributes to the perception of similarity to the Phenomenon.

annotations.definition: This frame deals with testing or examination of someone's Knowledge or skill in a particular area. An Examiner conducts an Examination to an Examinee to determine the Examinee's Knowledge and/or determine their Qualification for some privilege; this proceeds either by the Examinee demonstrating a skill or by writing responses to questions.

frameElementDefinitions.Examiner: One who administers a test of some kind.
frameElementDefinitions.Examinee: One who undergoes a test of some kind.
frameElementDefinitions.Knowledge: The ability, practice, or command of information on which an Examinee is tested.
frameElementDefinitions.Qualification: A credential or status that one acquires by passing an Examination.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Examiner assesses the Examinee.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Examiner wants to bring about by giving the Examinee the Examination.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the examination takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the examination occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Examiner that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: Two parties, the Exchangers, each give and receive from the other Themes. The parties are also construable separately as Exchanger_1 and Exchanger_2, in which case the Themes may be construed separately, too, as Theme_1 and Theme_2.

frameElementDefinitions.Exchanger_1: In cases where the Exchangers involved in a conversation are construed as two separate groups with different levels of volitionality or control, this FE identifies the conversational participant with greater semantic and syntactic prominence, as compared with Exchanger_2. That is, Exchanger_1 is generally encoded as Subject, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to Exchanger_1. Thus a Purpose (or Means, Depictive, or Manner) is construed to be that of Exchanger_1, not necessarily that of Exchanger_2 or the group of Exchangers as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the exchange occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which Exchanger_1 or the Exchangers make the trade.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the exchange takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which Exchanger_1 or the Exchangers effect the trade.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the exchange act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of Exchanger_1 or Exchangers that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Exchanger_2: In cases where the Exchangers involved in a conversation are construed as two separate groups with different levels of volitionality or control, Exchanger_2 refers to the semantically and grammatically less prominent group. It is generally encoded within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by with). Note that it is possible for a single role to correspond to multiple referents. For example, if the prepositional object in the example below were plural, we would still assign it the single role Exchanger_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Exchangers: In some sentences both participants are expressed by the Subject, and there is no 'with'-phrase (cf example 1). In sentences like this, the Subject is assigned the FE role Exchangers. In such sentences, the Subject always denotes more than one person, and it is understood that the reciprocal communication takes place between these two (or more) people. The Subject may denote two or more people either through conjunction, as in example 1, or through plurality, as in example 2, or by denoting a group, as in example 3. In each of these cases the FE Exchangers is assigned. Notice that for nouns in the Exchange frame, it is possible for the Exchangers role to be expressed by a prepositional phrase as in example 4.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of Exchanger_1 or Exchangers during the exchange.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme_1: The item that begins in the possession of Exchanger_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme_2: The item that begins in the possession of Exchanger_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Themes: The items which change possession between the Exchangers.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element denotes how often the Exchangers trade Themes.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: A Money_owner exchanges Sum_1 in the Source_currency for Sum_2 in the Target_currency at some Exchange_service.

frameElementDefinitions.Money_owner: The person who exchanges Sum_1 for Sum_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Sum_1: An amount of money in the Source_currency that the Money_owner exchanges for an amount in the Target_currency.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_currency: The kind of currency of the Money_owner has before the exchange.
frameElementDefinitions.Sum_2: The amount of money in the Target_currency that the Money_owner receives in exchange for Sum_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Exchange_service: The individual or institution which changes the Money_owner's money into the Target_currency.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_currency: The kind of currency that the Money_owner receives as part of the exchange.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the money is exchanged.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the currency is exchanged.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the exchange event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Money_owner that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Money_owner whereby the exchange is acheived.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The action that the Money_owner is attempting to accomplish with the exchange.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Currency that is being exchanged by the Money_owner.

annotations.definition: A former Member of a Group ceases to be a member as a result of the action of an Authority within the Group. (In directly democratic Groups, the Authority may consist of the Group itself as a voting whole.)

frameElementDefinitions.Member: The person who used to belong to a Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: A set of people standing in a semi-permanent relationship together.
frameElementDefinitions.Authority: An empowered subset of the Group which decides to expel its former Member.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Member is excluded.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A cause, reason, or other explanation for the exclusion of the Member.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Member is excluded.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Authority excludes the Member.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes a participant in the exclusion event.

annotations.definition: An Excreter excretes Excreta.

frameElementDefinitions.Excreta: The Excreta is the waste expelled by the body.
frameElementDefinitions.Excreter: The Excreter is the agent who excretes the Excreta.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the starting point of the Excreta's motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path identifies the trajectory of the Excreta.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is where the Excreta finishes its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Excreter engages in excretion.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Excreter engages in excretion.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifieis the Place where the excretion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Excreter engages in excretion.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is the motivation behind the Excreter's act.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: TheFrequency is the number of times per time period that the Excreter is engaged in exctretion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which all of the Excreta are removed from the body.

annotations.definition: An Agent acts according to a Plan, carrying it out (or taking steps to carry it out). In some cases, the Agent is a focal participant in the Plan, although a separate Focal_participant may also be mentioned. The Plan may be specified for the Duration for which it is in effect.

frameElementDefinitions.Plan: The Plan is a goal or purpose that the Agent carries out.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Plan is or is not in effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Agent puts the Plan into effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place at which the Plan is or is not operational. [CHANGE: or, place where agent puts into effect? 2 fes?]
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state-of-affairs under which the Agent puts the Plan into effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A cause, reason, or other explanation for the implementation of the Plan
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_participant: The individual or group who is obligated to follow the Plan. [CHANGE: may only be relevant for "implemented the policy for residents of florida only"]
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent, often an institution or governmental organization, puts a Plan into effect. The Agent is generally a primary participant in the Plan, but a separate Focal_participant may be specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes some state of the Agent, or provides additional characterization of the event of executing the plan.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an intermediate action done by the Agent in order to execute the Plan.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent executes the Plan.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes how fully the Plan was executed.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This FE describes the medium through which a plan was executed. It may occur in the same syntactic position as the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of executing the plan.

annotations.definition: An Executioner punishes an individual (Executed) with death as a consequence of some action of the Evaluee (the Reason).

frameElementDefinitions.Executioner: The Executioner is the individual who performs the execution.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which an Executioner performs an execution.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that is taken that accomplishes the execution
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the execution takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the execution is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Execution: The punishment of death given to the Executed by the Executioner.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the execution occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing a participant in the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Executed: The Executed is the person who is killed by the Executioner in retribution for the crime that the Executed was convicted of.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the execution event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Moreover, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Executioner that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Often, there is a constituent expressing the Explanation for the Judge's judgment which the Executioner is carrying out. It is usually a for-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event

annotations.definition: This frame concerns a specific subset of Instances of a Type which are evaluated in terms of their similarity to a conceptual prototype of the Type. The judgment of exemplariness may be made along a certain Domain.

frameElementDefinitions.Instance: A thing or episode that is considered exemplary of a certain Type.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The category of things or events from which an Instance is picked out.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The comparative terms along which the Instance is deemed an exemplar of the Type.
frameElementDefinitions.Instance_prop: A property of the particular Instance.

annotations.definition: An Entity, with regard to a particular Domain, is representative of a conceptual prototype of the Comparison_set of which it is a member.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The thing being judged by its similarity to an ideal.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: The group which the Entity is a member of.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The domain along which the Entity is judged to be exemplary.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Entity is exemplary.

annotations.definition: An Agent actively maintains or improves their level of phyiscal fitness. The action may target a specific Body_part, or happen through a particular Means.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who is trying to improve or maintain their physical fitness.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: An area of the Agent's body that the activity is direct at.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: Something that the Agent uses to exercise.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A general description of how the Agent acts while exercising.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time that the Agent exercises.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the Agent while exercising.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Agent works on their fitness.
frameElementDefinitions.Subtype: This frame element describes a subtype of the more general exercise category.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent exercises.

annotations.definition: An Entity is declared to exist, generally irrespective of its position or even the possibility of its position being specified. Time, Duration, Inherent_purpose, and State may also be mentioned. This frame is to be contrasted with Presence, which describes the existence of an Entity in a particular (and salient) spacio-temporal context, and which also entails the presence of an observer who can detect the existence of the Entity in that context.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Some entity, abstract or concrete, which is stated to exist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A time at which the entity is in existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period during which the Entity exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The reason why the Entity exists. Generally, this is more specifically the reason why the Entity was made.
frameElementDefinitions.State: The condition that the Entity exists in.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A situation, force, or entity which brings about the existence of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Entity exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances marks expressions that indicate a set of conditions under which the Entity is declared to exist
frameElementDefinitions.Viewpoint: The perspective of an individual who judges whether they consider the Entity (or further state of affairs) to exist or not.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.

annotations.definition: An Item changes its physical size. The Size_change of an Item may be explicitly indicated or characterized in terms of Initial_size and/or Result_size. In the default case, expansion is along all dimensions, but sometimes the Dimension of expansion is incorporated or made explicit.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: This FE identifies the Item whose size changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: Change of size may be limited to or described in terms of a single Dimension.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: This FE identifies the Group in which an Item changes size.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_size: Initial_size is used for the size of the Item before any change occurs. It is rarely expressed without Result_size also being explicit. Initial_size generally occurs in a PP-from Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Result_size: Result_size is the size that the Item has after the event. It is typically expressed in a PP Complement headed by to.
frameElementDefinitions.Size_change: This FE identifies the Size_change of an Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: This FE identifies the Rate of change of the value.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time that the expansion takes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the expansion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-variable: Co_variable is the quantity that varies commensurately with the size of the Item, usually construed to be causally linked to the size.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE is used for any description of the Manner of the expansion including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the expansion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is used for any expression that gives information about points on the scale of size that the Item traverses between the starting and ending point of its movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Where the Iteam ends up due to Expansion.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame have to do with a Cognizer believing that some Phenomenon will take place in the future. Some words in the frame (e.g. foresee.v) indicate that the Phenomenon is asserted also to be true, while others do not.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: This is the person who believes some Phenomenon will take place in the future. It is normally expressed as an External Argument: Pat expects that the Yankees will win the World Series.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: This is what the Cognizer believes will happen in the future. It can be expressed as a Finite Clause Complement, an NP Object, or a VP Complement with or without an NP Object (see General Grammatical Observations):
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: Words in this frame may occur with a PP headed by from which expresses the Evidence on which knowledge or belief is based: I have learned from experience that poison oak can be painful.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Cognizer foresees the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Cognizer makes the prediction.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: An entity that serves as the focus of a predicted Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_phenomenon: The time that the Cognizer expects the Phenomenon to occur. Note that it is distinct from simple Time, which is when the Cognizer is actually involved in thinking about the Phenomenon.

annotations.definition: A Person usually resides or works in a Location.

frameElementDefinitions.Location: The Location is the place of business or residence prototypically occupied by the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is located in or at a place that they customarily occupy.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Person occupies the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of the time interval during which the Person occupies the Location.

annotations.definition: An Agent uses a Resource which is consumed and unavailable for future use.

frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The Resource is exhausted over time by being used.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent uses the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Agent uses the Resource for a Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action ( deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent exhausts the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent exhausts the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent exhausts the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Resource is depleted by the Agent.

annotations.definition: A Payer gives up (or potentially gives up) the use of an Asset (generally money) in order to achieve an Intended_event. This event is often more specifically described as gaining possession of some Goods or receiving a Service. In the majority of cases, the Payer is described generically (INI), and the situation depicted answers the question of how much of an Asset would have to be given up to receive the Goods or bring about the Intended_event.

frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is the item(s) whose price is being assessed. Goods is usually an External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Payer: The person whose Asset is given up in order to achieve an Intended_event. This FE is often expressed as INI.
frameElementDefinitions.Asset: The price of or payment made for the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The place where a transaction would take place, generally where Goods and services are created or sold.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which Goods are cheap or expensive.
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_event: The event that the Payer brings about by deploying the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when a transaction takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The amount of an Asset relative to some measure of the Goods

annotations.definition: An Experiencer is involved in a bodily injury to a Body_part. (In some cases, no Body_part need be indicated.) Often an Injuring_entity on which the Experiencer injures themselves is mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The location on the body of the Experiencer where the bodily injury takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The event in which the bodily injury takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which the injury takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the harming event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The being or entity that is injured.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the harming event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Injuring_entity: The Experiencer injures him/herself on an Injuring_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Severity: The extent to which the Experiencer is affected by the injury.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that the injury happens to the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time during which the Experiencer repeatedly receives bodily injuries.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that the Experiencer has an experience of bodily harm.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe an Experiencer's emotions with respect to some Content. A Reason for the emotion may also be expressed. Although the Content may refer to an actual, current state of affairs, quite often it refers to a general situation which causes the emotion.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer experiences the emotion or other internal state.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: Content is what the Experiencer's feelings or experiences are directed towards or based upon. The Content differs from a stimulus because the Content is not construed as being direcly responsible for causing the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why an Experiencer experiences the particular emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the experience occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Frame Element Expressor marks expressions that indicate a body part, gesture or other expression of the Experiencer that reflect his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the area about which the Experiencer has the particular experience.
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occasion or happening that Experiencers in a certain emotional state participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances FE encodes the circumstances or conditions under which the Experiencer experiences the emotion. Unlike Content it does not give the specific content of the emotion, but the Content can often be inferred from the Circumstances.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Experiencer is in the specified emotional state.

annotations.definition: Some phenomenon (the Stimulus) provokes a particular emotion in an Experiencer.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer reacts emotionally or psychologically to the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the event or entity which brings about the emotional or psychological state of the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the degree to which the Stimulus brings about an emotion in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a phrase which describes a state of the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Stimulus affects the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Stimulus affects the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the Stimulus affecting the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the circumstances under which the Stimulus brings about the experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why the Stimulus causes the emotion in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer has an emotion as caused by the Stimulus.

annotations.definition: An Experimenter conducts an Experiment based on a Hypothesis. This Experiment contains a Control_group or Control_group_member which is compared with a Treatment_group or Treated_individual in order to determine the Result.

frameElementDefinitions.Experimenter: The Experimenter is the individual conducting the Experiment.
frameElementDefinitions.Control_group: The group of entities in the Experiment which have not been treated and are compared to the Treatment_group or Treated_individual.
frameElementDefinitions.Control_group_member: An individual entity of the Experiment which is left untreated and then compared with the Treatment_group or the Treated_individual.
frameElementDefinitions.Treatment_group: A group of subjects that receive Treatment and are compared to the Control_group or Controlled_individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Treated_individual: An individual subject that receives treatment and is then compared to the Control_group or Controlled_individual.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time the Experiment lasts.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place in which the Experiment is conducted.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Experimenter conducts the Experiment.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: A property that belongs to either the Control_group or the Treatment_group. Usually expressed by a PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result depicts the outcome of the Experiment.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A Descriptor identifies a characteristic or description of the Experiment or the Subjects not captured by any other frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiment: The test or Experiment conducted by the Experimenter using a Control_group and a Treatment_group.
frameElementDefinitions.Hypothesis: The Hypothesis is a conjecture as to the result of an Experiment. The Experiment takes place in order to prove the Hypothesis as correct or incorrect.
frameElementDefinitions.Subjects: In this frame Control_group and Treatment_group can be combined and expressed as Subjects. This FE can also be used for a generic individual on whom the Experiment is being conducted (see second example).
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: A tool used in conducting the Experiment. Usually expressed by a with-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject that the Experiment is studying.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns people's knowledge or skill in certain domains. It does not concern acquaintance or familiarity with people. With adjectives as targets in this frame, we annotate on two FE layers.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The person whose expertise is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Skill: Skill identifies the Protagonist's talent or ability to do things with one's hand or body.
frameElementDefinitions.Knowledge: The area of knowledge (as opposed to a practical skill) in which the Protagonist 's experience is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior_product: A product of behavior that shows the Protagonist's skill or lack of skill.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_participant: This frame element can only occur in place of a skill expression. It refers to the prototypical patient in an action involving the Protagonist. For instance, in the following sentence the children are experts at some (contextually given) action such as rolling the hoops. Some children were quite expert with wooden hoops.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: This frame element denotes a role in which the protagonist has developed his expertise.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner give further description of the way in which the Protagonist exhibits their skill or lack of skill.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Protagonist has the expertise.

annotations.definition: A Question about the reason or cause of a known State_of_affairs is presented as being answered by the existence of a particular Fact. The exact chain of reasoning, including the answer itself, of which the Fact is a part is not specified. Contrast this frame with the Evidence frame which casts a Support (a fact) as providing Evidence for, or conclusively proving, the Proposition (another proposed fact). Thus, there is no open question in the Evidence frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Fact: A Fact which can be connected by a chain of reasoning to the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: A Fact whose cause is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies how much of the State_of_affairs is explained by the Fact.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE indicates salient characteristics of the Fact considered as an explanation.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intermediate logical step that justifies the relationship between the Fact and the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Viewpoint: The perspective of an individual who judges whether they consider the Fact an explanation for the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Question: An open proposition concerning the cause of the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: The state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the Concessive.

annotations.definition: Due to chemical, nuclear, or other reactions, an Explosive explodes, potentially causing damage to the surrounding area. The Explosive is often, but need not be, a weapon.

frameElementDefinitions.Explosive: The Explosive releases a large amount of force, destroying itself and causing damage to the surrounding area.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the explosion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the explosion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is targeted by the explosion or inadvertantly damaged by the explosion. Typically expressed by an on-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the explosion occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Explosive explodes.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the explosion not specifically covered by other Frame Elements.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The strength of the explosion, or to what degree it damaged the affected areas or objects.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often an explosion occurs.

annotations.definition: An Exporter moves Goods across a border from an Exporting_area to an Importing_area.

frameElementDefinitions.Exporter: The conscious entity, generally a person, that moves the Goods across a border out of the Exporting_area. Very often, an Exporting_area will metonymically stand in for the person or persons directly responsible for the movement of the Goods. In such cases the constituent should be labeled Exporter on the first layer and with the appropriate locational FE on the second layer.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: A mobile entity directed by the Exporter that holds the Goods and moves them along from the Exporting_area to the Importing_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Exporter that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Exporter that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The items of value whose location is changing.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the state-of-affairs that the Exporter is attempting to bring about by exporting.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that explains why the Exporter moves the Goods across the border.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Exporter moves the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the state of a participant during the action
frameElementDefinitions.Importing_area: The FE Importing_area is the place that the Goods end up as a result of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path identifies the location of the Goods during transit, i.e. after leaving the Exporting_area and before reaching the Importing_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Exporting_area: The place where the Goods are initially, before the the Exporter moves them.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Exporting event belongs to.

annotations.definition: A Communicator publically communicates some difficult-to-express Content which they (or a social group of which they are a member) have had for some time. The Content may not have been previously communicated due to inability or social stricture. In cases where the individuals addressed are a smaller group than the public at large, a separate Addressee may be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The Communicator is the person who expresses the Content (whether spoken or written).
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the Content is communicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The attitude or feeling that the Communicator had not previously expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Communicator that also affect the action (eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Communicator communicates the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or physical state of the Communicator that leads to their communication.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the observable action or product of the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Communicator during the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Content is communicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The FE specifies the place where the communication takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE indicate that the particular Statement event referred to is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. Most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is the desired result of public expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the communication and any of its participants. Quite often, Circumstances secondarily provide an Explanation for the particular communication.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the particular public expression logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the expression, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: A Suspect in a Current_jurisdiction is forced by Authorities (of the Current_jurisdiction) to go to the Crime_jurisdiction, i.e. the jurisdiction which requested that the Suspect be handed over for trial for a crime which he or she is suspected to have committed in the Crime_jurisdiction. The purpose or reason for the extradition may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities act on behalf of the Current_jurisdiction, giving up the Suspect to the Crime_jurisdiction.
frameElementDefinitions.Current_jurisdiction: The jurisdiction (generally a nation) the Suspect starts off in.
frameElementDefinitions.Crime_jurisdiction: The jurisdiction (generally a nation) to which the Suspect is sent.
frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: The Suspect is forced to go to the jurisdiction where a crime he or she is suspected of took place or is being tried.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Authorities extradite the Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Authorities extradite the Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Legal_basis: This FE indicates the basis in law of an extradition.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Suspect is moved.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the manner of the extradition.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the Authorities or Suspect during the extradition.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The act of the Authorities which brings about the transfer from the Current_jurisdiction to the Crime_jurisdiction.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the extradition occurs.

annotations.definition: An Entity that has characteristics which change over time is at a point or in a phase of its development in which, compared to other actual or expected stages of development, it possesses extremely high or low powers or intensity. The high or low level of development correlates with the occurrence of a Marker, a product produced by the Entity or an event in which it participates, which is indicative of the Entity's abilities or intensity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: An concrete or abstract entity that possesses characteristics that change over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Marker: A point or phase in time when, viewed across the actual or expected development, the Entity's powers have reached an extreme high or low point. The point or phase is often identified metonymically by reference to a product created by the Entity or an event involving the Entity.

annotations.definition: An Entity possesses a Value for a certain Attribute which is extreme when compared to the values either of other entities or to values of the same Entity at other points of time.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity whose Value of a particular gradable Attribute is extreme.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: A quantity or other characterization of the Entity's state with respect to the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Period: The period for which values of a particular Attribute of one or more entities are compared.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain for which there is a Value of an Attribute of an Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of some property of the Extreme_value.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Extreme_value event occurs.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame refer to facial Expressions and the Possessors of those Expressions.

frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of Expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Expression occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: This is the person who exhibitis the Expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The Expression may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause - the Possessor's mental or emotional state. Internal_cause is expressed in a PP Complement as in:
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the face saliently exhibiting the Expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Conveyed_emotion: The emotion that is conveyed by a particular Expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_perceiver: An entity toward which the Expression is directed.
frameElementDefinitions.External_cause: The state of affairs perceived by the Possessor which leads to an Internal_cause (emotion, etc.) which then gives rise to the Expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time for which the facial Expression is held.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Action is evaluated with respect to how fairly, justly or equitably the Action affects the Affected_party. An Actor may also be identified, who performs the Action and may be characterized as fair, unfair, etc. based on how these actions affect the Affected_party. Grounds may also be identified.

frameElementDefinitions.Actor: The Actor is the individual who performs the Action and can thus be characterized with repsect to how that Action affects the Affected_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action is judged based on how just and appopriate it is to the Affected_party in a particular situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Grounds: The Grounds is the event or state that requires some decision or action in response to it for which the Actor is judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Affected_party: The Affected_party's treatment is what determines the evaluation of the Actor's Action.
