frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the extent to which the Action or Actor is evaluated as being fair or unfair.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is the area in which the Action is judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A holistic characterization of the description of the Actor, including depictions of the Actor's state in terms of how it affects the entire description.

annotations.definition: A Sleeper goes from wakefulness to an altered state of consciousness where they are largely unaware of their environment. The LUs of this frame are differentiated by how quickly and deeply the state is entered and what causes the change (especially whether it comes about naturally as a result of sleepiness or is brought about by injury).

frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Sleeper: The Sleeper is the entity that falls asleep.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time where the event occurs.

annotations.definition: A Victim comes to have incorrect beliefs as a result of exposure to a Deception. The Deception is necessarily orchestrated by one or more agents, though these are not expressible by the LUs in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim has false beliefs as a result of exposure to a Deception.
frameElementDefinitions.Deception: The Deception is perceived by the Victim, leading to false beliefs. The Deception, orchestrated by an agent or agents with the intention of deception, may be any misinformation, event, or situation perceived by the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Victim falls for the Deception.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Victim falls for the Deception.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the way Victim is deceived which is not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Victim believes the Deception.

annotations.definition: An Entity is well-known among a wide range of people (in general or in a particular domain) for a particular Reason, which may be a behavior, characteristic, or some other associated item. In the case of events, fame entails a large number of people being aware of the event's occurence; in the case of concrete entities, including humans, fame entails awareness of existence. In both cases, often fame is accompanied by awareness of the Entity's significance in historical, cultural, or social contexts.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity which is widely known and recognized.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the degree of fame of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity is considered famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that provides an explanation (via reasoning, causation, etc.) for the level of fame of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Reason: The behavior, attribute, or associated entities that provide the reason for the Entity being famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time during which the Entity is famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity is famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The Role underwhich the Entity become famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The way in which the Entity becomes famous.

annotations.definition: An Entity is presented as having been seen or experienced by a (typically generic and backgrounded) Cognizer on a certain number of occasions, causing the Entity to have a certain degree of recognizability for the Cognizer.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: A concrete thing or abstract phenomenon or fact that is assessed in terms of how well recognizable it is to a Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The (typically non-specific and backgrounded) sentient being for whom the Entity possesses a specified level of recognizability.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The deviation of the actual level of recognizability of the Entity from the level specified by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: This FE identifies the role filled by a particular participant of the clause headed by the target. In so doing, it relates the specification of a level of familiarity to another background state of affairs by indicating that one of the role fillers of the current frame also fills a second kind of role in the background, either generically or in some specific instance.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Cognizer as being in some state during the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: A situation or source from which the Entity is made more recognizanble to the Cognizer.

annotations.definition: The Fastener is an artifact created to close a Container (e.g. coat, jar). Sometimes, the Container_portal rather than the Container is expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Container: This FE identifies the item that is closed with the Fastener.
frameElementDefinitions.Fastener: The artifact created to close a Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_portal: This FE identifies the active zone of the container where the Fastener is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: This FE identifes the material from which the Fastener is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE identifies a characteristic or description of the Fastener.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Fastener into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which the Fastener comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to the Fastener.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of the Fastener.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Fastener is made.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer, Expressor, or State can be described as characterized as having an emotion of fear concerning a particular Topic or as evoked by a Stimulus.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part, gesture, or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer, Expressor, or State can be described as having said emotion.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Experiencer experiences an Emotion or is in an Emotional_state. There can also be an Evaluation of the internal experiential state.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer experiences the Emotion or is in the Emotional_state.
frameElementDefinitions.Emotion: The Emotion is the feeling that the Experiencer experiences.
frameElementDefinitions.Emotional_state: The Emotional_state is the state the Experiencer is in.
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluation: The Evaluation is a negative or positive assessment of the Experiencer regarding his or her Emotional_state.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The thing or state-of-affairs that leads to the Experiencer feeling the Emotion or Emotional_state.

annotations.definition: An Agent acts in such a way as to give the incorrect impression to observers that a particular State_of_affairs holds.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who acts in a misleading way to create the impression that a State_of_affairs holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Original: This is the entity which is copied. With verbs it is frequently expressed as an NP Object: Pat falsified the papers.
frameElementDefinitions.Copy: The Copy produced by the Creator most commonly occurs as the External Argument of a predicative use of an adjective or noun target: The painting is a fake.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent that accomplishes the imitation.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The State_of_affairs is a an event, a state, or a property that the Agent wants others to believe have occurred or hold.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action or state that the Agent intends to accomplish by performing the feigning of some State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the feigning event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Agent engages in the Feigning event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent feigns something.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the Agent feigns something.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs as being in some state during the feigning action.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place in which the Agent feigns something.

annotations.definition: An individual or group (Practitioner) or a part of their work life (Work) is defined professionally by the Activity that they typically engage in. Alternatively, the Activity may be indicated by a Salient_entity conventionally associated with it.

frameElementDefinitions.Activity: An activity that defines a group of people in their professional capacity.
frameElementDefinitions.Practitioner: The individual, group, or organization that engages in the Activity as part of their profession.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: A physical or abstract entity that is characteristically associated with the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Work: A phase of a Practitioner's career that is spent engaged in an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The subfield of the Activity that the Practitioner is engaged in.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Practitioner engages in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Practitioner engages in the Activity.

annotations.definition: A generally large group of people, the Combatants, are engaged in a chaotic, violent, and usually protracted Fight amongst themselves. There are no distinct sides, and generally no particular goal (compare Hostile_encounter). The Fight may be described as having a Manner or Degree of violence. Time and Place may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Combatants: The Combatants participate as fighters in the Fight.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Fight takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Fight takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner of the Fight.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This is the Degree of violence of the Fight.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_activity: An activity that engaged in by the Combatants while fighting.
frameElementDefinitions.Issue: The Issue over which Fight happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Fight takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Combatants engage in the Fight.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The weapon or other instrument used by the Combatants.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that (some of) he Combatants wish to acheive by fighting.

annotations.definition: These are words relating to filling containers and covering areas with some thing, things or substance, the Theme. The area or container can appear as the direct object with all these verbs, and is designated Goal because it is the goal of motion of the Theme. Corresponding to its nuclear argument status, it is also affected in some crucial way, unlike goals in other frames.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the actor who instigates the filling.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the physical object or substance which changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location where the Theme begins its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is the trajectory of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the area or container being filled. Goal is generally the NP Object in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Agent of the filling action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the resultant state after the filling has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Agent fills or covers the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the filling takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent fills the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion is the smaller area of the Goal where the filling or covering occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which brings about the filling of the Goal.

annotations.definition: The Payer is (legally) forced to pay a Fine by an official Speaker as a punishment for some action (the Reason). The Speaker represents an entity which receives the payment.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the person who imposes the Fine upon the Payer.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the fining is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that is taken that results in punishement/reward.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Payer: The Payer is the person or thing about whom/which a judgment is made. With verbs this FE is typically expressed as Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the event (or state) not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Speaker that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Reason: Typically, there is a constituent expressing the Reason for the Judge's judgment. It is usually a for-PP, e.g. I admire you for your intellect. For some words in this domain, the Reason frame element is obligatory. That is to say, the overt expression of this frame element may only be omitted when the reason for the judgment is understood from context. For example, the sentence 'I blame you!' can only be uttered when it is clear what the addressee is being blamed for.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Fine: The money given to expiate some debt for transgression.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any Unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The imposition of a debt due to culpable behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Fine is punitive to the Payer.

annotations.definition: A Competition comes to an end, with a Competitor tying, winning, or losing against an Opponent (or opponents) according to their Score. (In the symmetrical situation of a tie, the Competitor and Opponent may be jointly mentioned as Competitors.) According to the Score that the Competitor and Opponent have achieved, they may receive a Rank or a Prize. The Margin may also be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Competitor: The individual involved in the Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Opponent: The individual that the Competitor has been competing against.
frameElementDefinitions.Competitors: The individuals who compete against each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Score: A quantity whereby progress is measured during the game, and Rank and Prizes are awarded to the Competitors afterward.
frameElementDefinitions.Margin: The amount by which the Competitor 's and Opponent 's Scores differ.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: The rank (according to Score) that a Competitor acheives.
frameElementDefinitions.Prize: A socially and/or monetarily significant entity which is given to a Competitor according to Score or Rank.
frameElementDefinitions.Competition: The event in which the Competitors compete with each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Competitor that results in the outcome of the Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Competition which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of a Competitor that also affect their action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Competition takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Competition takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Venue: This FE is for the named venue of a competition, particularly when the place has been built for the purpose of a competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: A Player finishes a Game, possibly with an outcome of winning or losing. A Game is an activity that is considered recreational, and is valued for associated pleasure, as well as social interaction, material reward, or status. Not all games can be won or lost. Those that are specifically competitions are defined in the inheriting frame Finish_competition. In some LUs, the Game is DNI.

frameElementDefinitions.Game: An activity the Player participates in.
frameElementDefinitions.Player: The individual participating in a Game.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Game which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of a Player that also affect their action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Player that results in the outcome of the Game.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Game takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Player finishes the Game.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Player wins or loses the Game.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Player wins or loses the Game.

annotations.definition: This frame pertains to Fire_breaks, entities intended to prevent a fire from traveling in a direction. They are constructed by removing combustible/flammable material from the predicted path of the flames.

frameElementDefinitions.Fire_break: The constructed entity that is intended to prevent a fire from reaching a protected area.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Fire_break into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Fire_break, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to a Fire_break.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which a Fire_break comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Fire_break.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which a Fire_breakis intended.

annotations.definition: A Fire burns Fuel, resulting in damage and possibly complete destruction of the Fuel.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Fire is burning.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Fire is burning.
frameElementDefinitions.Fire: The total material currently involved in combustion, including both the parts of the Fuel currently undergoing chemical alteration and the partially ionized gas that results from this process.
frameElementDefinitions.Fuel: The entity that is undergoing rapid chemical alteration leading to damage or destruction.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Fire event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Name: Name given to the burning fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the Fire burns.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Descriptive phrase describing the fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state or category of the affected entity after the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the affected entity that undergoes the process evoked by the target.

annotations.definition: This frame characterizes any scenario in which the presence of a fire necessitates an emergency response in order to mitigate damage directly caused by the fire.

frameElementDefinitions.Fire: The fire which causes an emergency response.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time between the declaration of a Fire as in an emergency state and the conclusion of the emergency.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the fire emergency scenario occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Responder: The individual or group that is responding to a fire emergency with directed action.
frameElementDefinitions.Response: Directed action leading to recovery, rescue, or general mitigation of distress due to an ensuing fire emergency.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the fire emergency scenario occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim(s) affected negatively by the fire emergency event.

annotations.definition: This scenario begins with a Fire burning, transitions as the Fire goes out, and ends with the Fire dissipated.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Fire goes out.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Fire goes out.
frameElementDefinitions.Fire: The combustion process that is coming to an end.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the how near the Fire is to going out.

annotations.definition: A Fire goes out.

frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the Depictive phrase describing an undergoer of an action during their participation in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the state of the Fire that affects the nature of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Fire goes out.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Fire goes out.
frameElementDefinitions.Fire: The combustion process that comes to an end.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the how near the Fire is to going out.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.

annotations.definition: The Fire_stopping_scenario begins with a Fire in process, transitions with the action of an Agent to end the Fire, and ends with the Fire having stopped burning.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent puts out the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Fire goes out.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual or group that causes the Fire to no longer burn.
frameElementDefinitions.Fire: The combustion process that the Agent causes to end.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the how near the Agent is to putting out the Fire.

annotations.definition: This frame characterizes the situation or state of affairs in which someone acts to prevent a Fire_disaster from occurring, accelerating, or affecting any area not identified as a Controlled_area. The named Burn_area, targeted by the Fire_disaster, is more salient than "fire" itself and is often used for reference instead. The organizational methods for Firefighting are of utmost importance in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Firefighter: The individual or group who respond(s) to the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Firefighter fights the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the location of the Firefighters as they attempt to fight the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Firefighters are fighting the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The desired result of the firefight. It may either directly refer to the state of affairs that is desired, e.g.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An instrument with which an intentional fire-fighting act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs as being in some state during the firefighting action.

annotations.definition: An Employer ends an employment relationship with an Employee. There is often a Reason given for the action.

frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person who is let go by their Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) that end an employment relationship with an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Employer fires an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Employer fires the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Employer fires the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: The action that the Employee is relieved from doing for the Employer .
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when an Employer fires the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Employee is terminated.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means whereby the Employer fires the Employee

annotations.definition: A location from which a Detonator activates a distantly-located explosive device.

frameElementDefinitions.Firing_point: The location from which the Detonator activates an explosive device.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Firing_point is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Firing_point.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The name of the locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Detonator: The Detonator is located at the Firing_point when he or she activates the explosive device.

annotations.definition: An implicit experiencer has an Experience for the first time. Some LU's may specifically imply an agentive activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Experience: An event happens for the first time within the Context.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: A spatial, cultural, or personal delimitation within which the Experience occurs for the first time.

annotations.definition: An Item is located at the top end of a scale of importance or social rank (or some other Attribute) in comparison to other members of a Contrast_set (usually, implicit) that is otherwise similar in kind to the Item. Oftentimes, the limits of the Contrast_set are determined by an expression of the Limits_of_consideration.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: An Item is the foremost thing in the Contrast_set.
frameElementDefinitions.Limits_of_consideration: This FE identifies the domain (usually temporally or spatially defined) within which the Item is primary among its kind.
frameElementDefinitions.Contrast_set: The Contrast_set is a group of entities similar to the Item out of which the Item is the most preeminent.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The Attribute of the Item which is under consideration.

annotations.definition: A Self_mover responds to a (real or imagined) danger by moving away from it.

frameElementDefinitions.Self_mover: Self_mover is the living (or self-directed) being which moves under its own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is used for any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is used for any description of a trajectory of motion involving a landmark which is neither a Source nor a Goal. This includes "middle of path'' expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is used for any expression which tells where the Self_mover ends up as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: An expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory or rate of motion. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Any expression which characterizes the extent of motion expresses the frame element Distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: An entity whose motion is correlated with that of the Self_mover, following and tracking the Self_mover, being followed and tracked, or with both parties mutually matching Paths.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate at which the location of the Self_mover changes with respect to time.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Self_mover achieves movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the world which exists because of the motion of the Self_mover
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or mental state of the Self_mover which motivates them toward motion or the particular manner of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Self_mover moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which a Self_mover moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location within which a defined motion takes place. Any specified or implied Source, Path or Goal should designate a location contained with the Place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state of affairs that leads to the particular motion that the Self_mover adopts.
frameElementDefinitions.External_cause: External_cause is something in the external environment that causes the Self_mover to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction that the Self_mover heads in during the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_motion: The entity used by the Self_mover in getting away from the undesirable Source.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Fluid moves from a Source to a Goal along a Path or within an Area.

frameElementDefinitions.Area: Area is the setting in which the Fluid's movement takes place on an unspecified Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE describes the phsyical extent of the motion of the Fluid.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Fluid moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is the location the Fluid ends up.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is the trajectory along which the Fluid moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location the Fluid occupies initially.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This is the rate at which the Fluid flows.
frameElementDefinitions.Fluid: The Fluid is the entity that changes location and moves in a fluidic way.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Fluid flows.
frameElementDefinitions.Configuration: The Configuration encodes information about the amount, arrangement, and unitization of the flow.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Fluid flows.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the reason for the fluidic motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the Depictive phrase describing the Fluid throughout the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the result of the Fluid moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place describes the location of the motion which has a specified Source, Path or Goal.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words referring to items of food.

frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE indicates a characteristic or description of the food.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies a particular Type of the food item.

annotations.definition: A Gatherer removes Crop ripe and ready to an accepted degree. The Crop comes from a Source where it has been grown and matured.

frameElementDefinitions.Gatherer: The Gatherer is the agent gathering the Crop.
frameElementDefinitions.Crop: Crop is item being collected together by the Gatherer.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what length of time the Gatherer was gathering the Crop.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Gatherer gathers the Crop.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Crop is gathered.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The greater geographic location where the Gatherer gathers his Crop.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Crop gathering action occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Gatherer intends to accomplish by gathering his Crop.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Gatherer collects his Crop.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the ground where the Crop is gathered from.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the amount of effort put into the food gathering event.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The person on who's behalf the Gatherer gathers the Crop.

annotations.definition: A Current_country is identified as matching or not matching the instance of the Discourse_salient_country.

frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: The materials or Constituent_parts that make up the Current_country.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Current_country is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Current_country: The country or countries that the speaker is indicating in the current instance, which may match or be distinct from the Discourse_salient_country.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: ULTIMATELY TO BE REPLACED BY MULTIWORD TARGETS (in almost all cases). This FE is used for the Names of Locales.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that the Current_country is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description, characteristic, or property of the Current_country which is not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Population: The people living in the jurisdiction of the locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Discourse_salient_country: The country that has been featured in the discourse, or that the speaker/hearer identify with or are located in; it serves to identify the Current_country by specifying it as the same or different. This FE is incorporated in the targets of this frame.

annotations.definition: An Agent produces a Forgery, an entity that they intend to pass off as a particular (instance of) a Standard. An instance of the Standard may actually exist that served as an original that is copied by the Agent, or the Standard exists only as an idea.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person that creates a Forgery with the intent of passing it off as (an instance of) the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Forgery: The Forgery is the product that the Agent creates with the intent of passing it off as (an instance of) the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent that accomplishes the imitation.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the forging event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action or state that the Agent intends to accomplish by producing the Forgery.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: The Standard is the actually existing or imagined (kind of) thing that the Agent wants to pass the Forgery off as.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity manipulated by the Agent in order to produce the Forgery.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Agent makes a Forgery.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the forgery takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Fidelity: The way in which the Forgery does or does not match the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: An entity corresponding to the Standard which the Agent scrutinizes in order to copy.

annotations.definition: A Judge refrains from imposing, or demanding a punishment for an Evaluee who has committed an Offense.

frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The Judge holds the Evaluee responsible for the Offense.
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: The Evaluee is subject to the judgment of the Judge for having committed the Offense.
frameElementDefinitions.Offense: The Offense is the illicit or unpleasant act committed by the Evaluee.

annotations.definition: AForgoer decides not to partake in something ordinary that is otherwise Desirable.

frameElementDefinitions.Forgoer: The Forgoer decides not to partake in the desirable activity or event.
frameElementDefinitions.Desirable: Desirable identifies the event or activity in which is the Forgoer does not partake.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner the forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which the the forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances under which the Forgoer does not partake in the Desirable.

annotations.definition: Partner_1 interacts with Partner_2 (also collectively expressible as Partners) to change their social relationship.

frameElementDefinitions.Partner_1: The individual who comes to form a relationship with Partner_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Partner_2: The individual whose relationship with Partner_1 changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Partners: The individuals whose relationship is changing.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that Partner_1 or the Partners take in order to bring about the change in the relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the relationship changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Partner_1 or Partners that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A salient state of Partner_1, Partner_2, or Partners which affects the perception of the formation of relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of time that the changed relationship status lasts.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the relationship is formed.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Partners or Partner_1 hope to bring about by changing their status.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the Forming_relationship event operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Forming_relationship event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.

annotations.definition: A Whole fragments or breaks into Parts, or alternatively a part, Part_1, breaks off from the rest of the item, called Part_2.

frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole is a single entity which breaks into Parts.
frameElementDefinitions.Parts: This refers collectively to the Parts resulting from fragmentation of a Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_1: Part-1 is the first of two Parts mentioned. This is most often the external argument of a verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_2: Part_2 is the second of two Parts mentioned. This is usually a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Criterion: This frame element expresses a property of the Parts or components of the Whole which correlates with the locations (e.g.) of fragmentation on the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing any of the undergoers of the fragmentation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the fragmenting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the breaking occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Resistant_surface: The hard surface against which the Whole (or Part_1) breaks.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action or state that enables the fragmentation.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Theme which was held captive to be free from a Location_of_confinement, against the wishes of the captors.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent causes the confinement or captivity of the Theme to end, against the intentions of the captor.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the person or object which is released by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the release which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Agent which brings about the release.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation identifies the reason for which the Agent sets the Theme free.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose identifies the purpose for which the Agent releases the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Theme ceases to be confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time identifies when the Agent frees the Theme .
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_confinement: The location to which the Theme's motion was formerly inhibited.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the event of setting free occurs.

annotations.definition: This frame has to do with the repetition (and especially the frequency of repetition) of an event. Many of the adjectives and adverbs in this frame are based on nouns in the Calendric_unit frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: This FE is used for any (bounded) Event whose frequency or iteration is described.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the actually observed rate of occurrence of the Event or Salient_entity differs from the expected rate.
frameElementDefinitions.Interval: The Interval is a noun modified by the target word. The target word specifies the frequency of the Interval .
frameElementDefinitions.Time_span: The Time_span is the length of time of the Interval. This FE is incorporated by most of the lexical units in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The number of times per unit of real or scanning time that an Event or Salient_entity is encountered.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: An entity which the implicit cognizer encounters with the specified frequency.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The frequency feature of the Event or Salient_entity that is under discussion. All the lexical units in this frame incorporate this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Event has a particular frequency.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group in which the Event or Salient_entity is frequent.

annotations.definition: A Theme moves on or against the surface of a Static_object. (Alternatively, Themes moves against each other simultaneously). The Theme exerts pressure on the surface and experiences resistance to its movement.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The physical entity which moves over the suface of the Static_object
frameElementDefinitions.Static_object: A non-moving physical object over a surface of which the Theme moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Themes: Two moving entities whose surfaces move against each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Theme moves against the Static_object
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The setting in which the motion of the Theme happens
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of friction produced in the Theme's motion.

annotations.definition: Two parties or individuals are on the same side or on different sides of a (typically military) conflict. The two sides may be expressed jointly as the Sides, or separately as Side_1 and Side_2.

frameElementDefinitions.Side_1: Side_1 is the party or individual in a relationship with Side_2. Side_1 is the primary protagonist of the frame, and is denoted by noun and adjective targets.
frameElementDefinitions.Side_2: The party or individual with whom Side_1 bears an adversarial or friendly relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the relationship holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Sides have the indicated relationship with each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is a proposition denoting a state of affairs from which the Sides having the relationship indicated logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE indicates a property of Side_1 or the Sides.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the two Sides have an adversarial relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Sides: A joint expression of the two Sides with a friendly or hostile stance towards one another.

annotations.definition: An individual or organization, the Front, serves as a cover for an Illicit_activity or an Illicit_organization.

frameElementDefinitions.Front: The Front acts as a legitimate individual, organization, or enterprise, but is truly a cover for an illicit organization or activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Illicit_organization: The existence and activities of the Illicit_organization are concealed by the Front.
frameElementDefinitions.Illicit_activity: The Illicit_activity of the Illicit_organization is concealed by (the activities of) the Front .
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the deceptive activity occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Front is attempting to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the fronting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action taken by the Front to ensure successful deceit of authorities.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the similarity of the event to some other event, particularly descriptions of the Front that also affect the details of the action.

annotations.definition: The words of this frame describe how a Resource_controller spends money or other Resources for a particular purpose . They can be applied to the Resource_controller or to their Behavior in particular instances. These words indicate a judgment of how conservatively the Resource_controller guards their Resource.

frameElementDefinitions.Resource_controller: The Resource_controller is the individual whose frugality or generosity is being described. Person typically occurs as the External Argument or, in some cases when Behavior is also expressed, in an of-PP:
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: Some specific Behavior may be described as frugal or extravagant, rather than attributing the characteristic directly to the Resource_controller.
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: This FE is the resource which the Resource_controller expends.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which a Resource_controller is frugal or profligate.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: A Fugitive avoids recapture by or on the authority of Authorities in relation to a Crime which the Fugitive has committed or is suspected of having committed.

frameElementDefinitions.Fugitive: The Fugitive has escaped and/or is evading capture by law enforcement Authorities.
frameElementDefinitions.Prison: The facility where the Fugitive was being held.
frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities want to take (or retake) the Fugitive into custody on suspicion of having committed a Crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Crime: The crime of which the Fugitive is wanted. This FE also covers expressions that indicate suspicion of crimes, convictions, and sentences.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the general Place where the Fugitive is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Fugitive is evading law enforcement.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the state of being imprisoned which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Fugitive is avoiding capture.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Fugitive is on the run.

annotations.definition: A Container is in a state of fullness/emptiness with respect to some Contents. Note the connection between words in this frame and those in the Containers, Emptying, and Filling frames.

frameElementDefinitions.Container: Container is used for the entity whose fullness is being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Contents: Contents is used for the stuff that is present/absent in the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the amount of Contents in the Container diverges from the maximal expected amount for the Container.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Container is full.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The amount of times the Container is empty per unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of the Container's fullness to the end.

annotations.definition: An Entity is designed for or exists to perform an Activity or fulfill a Role.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity is an object or person that performs the Activity or fulfills the Role.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity is the action that the Entity performs.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The Role is the position or duty that is occupied by the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor provides additional information about the Entity .
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration that the Entity performs an Activity or fulfills a Role.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Entity functions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which an Entity functions.

annotations.definition: A Supplier gives funds to a Recipient to fulfill a need or purpose (Imposed_purpose) of the Recipient.

frameElementDefinitions.Supplier: Supplier is the person or entity that funds the Recipient or Imposed_purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: Recipient is the person, people, or organization funded by the Supplier.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: Imposed_purpose is the intended purpose of the funding.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the conditions under which the funding is supplied.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE is the Explanation for which the Supplier gives funding to the Recipient or Imposed_purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the funding event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Supplier that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE expresses the Means by which the Supplier funds the Recipient or Imposed_purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE designates the period over which the Supplier engages in (multiple) funding acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Supplier funds the Recipient or Imposed_purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source of the funding.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Supplier gives funds to the Recipient or Imposed_purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the specified currency or amount of funding.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Supplier funds the Recipient.

annotations.definition: This frame describes an Agent's gathering of Individuals (people or entities) into a group, the Aggregate, defined by relative proximity.

frameElementDefinitions.Aggregate: This FE identifies the aggregate that results from the gathering. It may be expressed either as an adjunct result phrase or as a direct object of a target verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: Agent is the person who gathers Individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the gathering event occurs, if specified. The crowd assembled at dawn.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent that accomplishes the gathering.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the gathering event occurs. He assembled the children in the hall.
frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: This FE identifies the entities that come together to form an Aggregate.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Individuals are gathered up.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An Instrument that the Agent uses to gather the Individuals together.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the Agent is attempting to bring about by gathering.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times the Gathering_up event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the Gathering_up event occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal designates the location where the Individuals might end up.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: The Co-participant participates in a coordinated way in the same event as the primary protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is the trajectory of motion that the Individuals take.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location where the Individuals are at the beginning of the gathering event.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame have to do with non-verbal communication. Topic is not normally expressed with these words. This seems to be related to the absence of a verbal code in cases of gestural communication. Topic can be viewed as a partial characterization of a Message, but such partial characterization is either difficult or irrelevant in the absence of a verbal code.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: This is the sentient entity that communicates a Message non-verbally. It is normally expressed as an External Argument: The teacher gestured us into the room.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: This is the person to whom a non-verbal Message is communicated. It may be expressed as an NP Object or as a PP Complement: The teacher gestured us into the room. The teacher gestured to us to enter the room.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: This is the content of non-verbal communication. It may be expressed as a that-clause Complement, a to-marked infinitive VP Complement, or a directional PP Complement: The teacher gestured that it was time to leave. The teacher gestured to us to enter the room. The teacher gestured us into the room.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Communicator that results in the successful transfer of a Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The Body_part is the specific body part of the Communicator which gestures.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the gesturing occus.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the gesturing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Indicated_entity: The Indicated_entity is the entity which the Communicator is pointing out to the Addressee as meaningful.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An Instrument that the Communicator uses to gesture to the Addressee together.

annotations.definition: A new Employee obtains a Position with an Employer, with which there are certain Tasks associated; in exchange for the performance of these Tasks, the Employee receives Compensation from the Employer.

frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Employee receives for performing a Task.
frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person who enters an employment relation with an Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) that the Employee enters an employment relationship with.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Employee enters an employment relationship with an Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Employee enteres an employment relationship with an Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Employee enters an employment relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: The action that the Employee is obligated to do for the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when an Employee signs on with an Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action by which the Employee enters an employment relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis is the condition of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period or payment arrangements.

annotations.definition: A Recipient starts off without the Theme in their possession, and then comes to possess it. Although the Source from which the Theme came is logically necessary, the Recipient and its changing relationship to the Theme is profiled.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The Recipient indicates the entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that changes possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location that the Theme formerly occupied. Persons that begin in possession of the Theme may metonymically stand in for the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place denotes where the Getting takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation refers to the existing eventuality that the Recipient is responding to.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is the action of the Recipient which results in their possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Agent intentionally acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state that either the Recipient or the Theme end up in after the acquisition.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The Beneficiary is an entity which the Recipient passes the Theme to, or shares the Theme with.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Any condition or situation that serves as a very general background, against which the further importance of the Getting is understood.

annotations.definition: In this scenario, the Recipient begins without the Theme, then acquires the Theme from a Source.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The Recipient indicates the entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that changes possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location that the Theme formerly occupied. Persons that begin in possession of the Theme may metonymically stand in for the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation refers to the existing eventuality that the Recipient is responding to.

annotations.definition: A System interacts with the environment (which may include a volitional agent) and goes into operation in accordance with its intended purpose.

frameElementDefinitions.System: The System interacts with the environment and activates.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any holistic description of the event, including overall depictions (the same way) and descriptions pertaining to the System (quietly).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the System goes off.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the System triggers.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times the triggerent event occurs in a particular time period.

annotations.definition: A Self_mover begins the motion away from a relatively deprofiled Source location. The focus is on the motion rather than on the location that is being departed. The label Self_mover here also includes vehicles that are steered by an intentional agent.

frameElementDefinitions.Self_mover: The Self_mover is the volitional agent that moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source refers to the starting-point of the Self_mover's motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is any description of the Self_mover's trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is any expression that tells where the Self_mover ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A Manner expression describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Self_mover begins the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Self_mover begins the motion away from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the state of the Self_mover when she begins the motion away from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of time of the Self_mover's absence from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation is the transportation device that a human Self_mover uses to travel.
frameElementDefinitions.Journey: The journey is the trip or expedition on which the Self_mover embarks.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The Direction in which the Self_mover begins to move.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist leaves the place where they have slept, their Bed, to begin or resume domestic, professional, or other activities. Getting up is distinct from Waking up, which is concerned only with the transition from the sleeping state to a wakeful state.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The person or animal that has slept in a particular place and now leaves it to engage in other activities.
frameElementDefinitions.Bed: The place where the Protagonist has slept.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Protagonist leaves their place of sleeping.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Protagonist gets out of Bed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Protagonist that affect the action (eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: The New_situation is the situation the Protagonist finds him or herself in upon getting up from Bed.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The FE Circumstances encodes a setting, often temporal or spatial, within which the getting up event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Protagonist is responding to in getting up.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of the interval for which the Protagonist is not in Bed after getting up.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Protagonist's Bed is situated.

annotations.definition: A Driver prepares a Vehicle for travel by placing it in the normal location for the operation of the vehicle or by detaching it from a place where it was held.

frameElementDefinitions.Driver: This is the being, typically human, that prepares the Vehicle for motion, and will control it once it is in motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The device--such as a car, boat, spaceship,. etc.--which is used for transport and is prepared for travel by the Driver.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which describes the location that the Vehicle has been stationed at.
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_goal: The location that the Vehicle is intended to travel to.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Driver that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Driver or Vehicle during the preparations.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Driver by which they prepare the Vehicle for travel.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of the Driver's action.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: An individual or vehicle that is also involved in the preparations.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE indicates the Circumstances under which the preparation of the Vehicle takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place describes the location where the Driver prepares the Vehicle for motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of the time that the Vehicle remains in the state of readiness.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Driver prepares the Vehicle for motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE marks a proposition from which the main clause logically follows.

annotations.definition: In this class of perception words, a Phenomenon, typically expressed as External Argument, and its perceptual characteristics are given some description. This may be a Characterization describing purely perceptual properties, an Appraisal (i.e. positive or negative judgment) or an Inference about some non-perceptual property based on the Perceiver_passive's perception.

frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon is typically the External Argument of the verbs in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Characterization: Characterization is the subjective description (of the Perceiver_passive ) of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Appraisal: Appraisal is the positive or negative judgement given to the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Inference: Inference expresses some property of the Phenomenon that is not a direct feature of the particular sensory modality (if any), but rather is based on perception within that modality.
frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver_passive: The Perceiver_passive experiences some perception without necessarily intending to, but makes a judgment about the Phenomenon. The Perceiver_passive generally occurs in a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: Body_part expressions are not common in this frame, but may occur with the verb "feel" (the only verb in this frame that does not lexically encode the Body_part involved in perception) or occasionally (and redundantly) with other verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: Location_of_protagonist identifies the position of the Perceiver_passive during the act of perception. Typically it is expressed in a PP-from.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: This frame element, usually expressed in a PP, is used for all path-like expressions, except those indicating the Location_of_perceiver (see above), that describe how the Perceiver_passive's attention is directed during the act of perception.
frameElementDefinitions.State: This FE identifies expressions that predicate something of the Phenomenon, providing some information about its State while the Perceiver_passive experiences it.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: Ground is the perceptual background against which the Phenomenon is experienced (or not experienced) by the Perceiver_passive.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which a Phenomenon gives the impression that a certain Characterization or Inference holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The physical or abstract Circumstances that determine the appearance of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Phenomenon has the Appraisal, Characterization, or Phenomenon.

annotations.definition: A Donor transfers a Theme from a Donor to a Recipient. This frame includes only actions that are initiated by the Donor (the one that starts out owning the Theme). Sentences (even metaphorical ones) must meet the following entailments: the Donor first has possession of the Theme. Following the transfer the Donor no longer has the Theme and the Recipient does.

frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person that begins in possession of the Theme and causes it to be in the possession of the Recipient .
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that changes ownership.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Donor gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Donor gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Donor gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Donor gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Donor gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the manner in which the Donor gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the conditions under which the Theme is given.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Recipient's intended purpose for the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the Donor, Recipient, or Theme given independently of the giving event per se.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: A Mother and Father produce a Child or an Egg.

frameElementDefinitions.Mother: Mother is the female creature that produces the Child.
frameElementDefinitions.Child: Child identifies the new self-motile creature produced from the Mother and Father.
frameElementDefinitions.Father: Father is the male creature that copulates with the Mother, thus leading to the birth of the Child.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies the phrase describing a participant of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances under which a Mother and Father produce produce a Child.
frameElementDefinitions.Egg: Egg is an immobile object containing an organism that may hatch as a mobile, infant organism.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Parents: The Mother and Father expressed together.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the birth.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Parents (or a Father or Mother) that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: A Capitulator who has been opposing a Force, which is typically exerted by a Compeller, ceases opposing that Force. As a result, the Force runs its course and an Issue is resolved against the interests of the Capitulator.

frameElementDefinitions.Capitulator: The individual that yields to the Force exerted by the Compeller.
frameElementDefinitions.Issue: The matter, usually concerning a course of action, in which the Capitulator has disagreed with the Compeller.
frameElementDefinitions.Compeller: An individual that intentionally exerts Force on the Capitulator.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Capitulator yields to the Compeller on the Issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is a proposition denoting a state of affairs from which the Capitulator's giving in on the Issue logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Force: A state of affairs, typically brought about by a Compeller, that impinges on the Capitulator and eventually causes them to no longer pursue their own view on the Issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Capitulator ceases to oppose the Force.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Capitulator intends to accomplish by ceasing their opposition.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Giving_in which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Capitulator that affect the action (reluctantly, meekly).
