frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of how the Issue is resolved from the desires of the Compeller.
frameElementDefinitions.Resultant_action: The action that the Capitulator engages in after giving in to the Force.

annotations.definition: The sequence of events in which the Donor begins in possession of the Theme, then gives it to the Recipient, relinquishing possession.

frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person that begins in possession of the Theme and causes it to be in the possession of the Recipient .
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that changes ownership.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Donor gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the manner in which the Donor gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Recipient's intended purpose for the Theme.

annotations.definition: This frame contains general words denoting equipment where the Use or purpose is not specified by the lexeme but (typically) by a separate expression. The words are described with respect to the Use for which the item is used or its typical User.

frameElementDefinitions.Use: This FE identifies the prototypical Use of a tool or device.
frameElementDefinitions.User: User identifies the person who (typically) uses a particular tool.
frameElementDefinitions.Power_source: The Frame Element Power_source marks expressions that indicate how a device is powered.
frameElementDefinitions.Gizmo: The complex artifact with a particular use.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Gizmo.

annotations.definition: A Theme, which may or may not have a default shape or configuration, goes into a particular shape without being made to do so by an agent. Depending on the type of shape, there may be a Locus about which the Theme changes shape.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the shape-changing event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the change into the shape occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the changing into a particular shape occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: How long the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the shape-changing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the shape changing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme goes into a particular shape.
frameElementDefinitions.Resultant_configuration: The Result is the shape the Theme becomes.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The conditions under which the Theme is shaped.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The state of the Theme with respect to the change brought about by making the adjustment.

annotations.definition: A Landmark (in combination with the image schema evoked by particular targets) serves to pick out the final location of a Trajector in a construed or actual motion event.

frameElementDefinitions.Landmark: The relatively locatable entity which serves as a basis for determining the location of the Trajector using the Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The location (located with respect to the Landmark) which is profiled by the particular image schema pertaining to the locative relation.
frameElementDefinitions.Trajector: The entity which is construed to be in motion, whose location at the end of the construed motion event is in question.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Trajector is located in the Profiled_region.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist has made a Commitment but does not honor it, thereby doing harm to an Affected_party.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist is the person who has committed themselves to do something and later does not follow through on it.
frameElementDefinitions.Affected_party: The individual or group to whom the Protagonist had made the Commitment and who is negatively affected by the Protagonist's not honoring the Commitment.
frameElementDefinitions.Commitment: An expression of the commitment that the Protagonist has taken on earlier and which they fail to follow through on.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, secondary effects (quietly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Protagonist that also affect the action (coldly).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Protagonist fails to honor their commitment.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Protagonist fails to honor the commitment they made.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the Protagonist's failure to honor the Commitment logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the Protagonist's act of going back on their Commitment as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: How much the Protagonist goes back on their Commitment.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re_encoding, but not necessarily.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times the Protagonist backed out on a commitment.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The outcome of the Protagonist going back on a commitment.

annotations.definition: The Institution is an organization which is part of a government, the Purpose of which is to provide a service or fulfill a role designated by the government. The Purpose may, but need not, entail direct interaction of the citizenry.

frameElementDefinitions.Institution: The Institution forms a part of a governmental body. It consists of individual Members and is dedicated to a Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Members: A person belonging to an Organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which an Organization is formed or the purpose to which it is dedicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_existence: The interval during which the Organization exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: A name given to the Organization for identification.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE describes the general location where the Organization operates, but does not indicate the possessor of the Organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description of the Organization not covered by a more specific frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The concern of the Institution -- broadly speaking, the aspect of society that they affect.

annotations.definition: This frame contains LUs that ascribe an artistic quality to a work. This frame does not contain "period" words such as "romantic" or "classical".

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which a particular gradable Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Entity or Attribute from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity or its Attribute is in the state of having the specified style.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity or its Attribute has a particular style.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity has the Attribute to a given Degree.

annotations.definition: An Entity has an implicit Value for an Attribute. Degree expresses either explicit comparison or the deviation of the Value when compared with other entities of similar kind. The Value may hold only relative to a particular Time or in particular Circumstances.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which a particular gradable Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Value from the norm or from the value for another Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular Value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity has a particular Value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The generally implicit Value of the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity is has the Attribute to a given Degree.

annotations.definition: A Figure is (stably) located with respect to a Ground in terms of how the distance between them compares to some general or contextually relevant scale. The Degree of divergence from the expected distance may also be expressed. In addition to the asymmetrical consideration of Figure and Ground, these may be construed collectively as Figures, in which case each entity is assessed for how expected its distance is from others in the group of Figures.

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as located relative to a certain Ground location. The figure can be an entity or an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: This FE identifies the Accessibility between the Figure and some reference location (generally, the deictic center or the Ground).
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the locative relation between Figure and Ground obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Figures: The Figures are items which mutually serve to identify the location of the other items.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: An assessment of how the distance between the Figure and the Ground diverges from expectation.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'.
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Ground interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class of adverbials back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Figure before) and on.adv (which indicates a continued path). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, in which case it either refers to fictive, imagined motion (as in the first example below), or it backgrounds motion entirely (as in the second example).
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance between the Figure and the Ground. This FE requires an image schema with a vector that extends from the Ground, or a subpart of the Ground, in some direction, and the Distance is measured along this vector from the Ground to the profiled region.

annotations.definition: This frame descibes a Shape that Data makes on a Graph.

frameElementDefinitions.Shape: A pattern that can be recognized when the Data is represented on a Graph.
frameElementDefinitions.Data: A (usually quantitative) abstraction of information being represented in some Shape on a Graph.
frameElementDefinitions.Graph: The visual representation of the information extracted from the Data.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: An sequenced based naming system for individual members of a group.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Shape.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: A specific region within the Graph which reveals a Shape or pattern.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer possesses knowledge about the workings, significance, or meaning of an idea or object, which we call Phenomenon, and is able to make predictions about the behavior or occurrence of the Phenomenon. The Phenomenon may be incorporated into the wider knowledge structure via categorization, which can be indicated by the mention of a Category. The Cognizer may possess knowledge only in part and this may be expressed in a Completeness expression. The Cognizer may also be realized as a cognitive-emotional Faculty that is conceived of as having understanding. Note that the knowledge may have been acquired either from instruction or from the Cognizer's own experimentation, observation, or mental operations. Words in this frame are frequently used metonymically to denote the transition into the state described above.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The sentient animate being who acquires new knowledge.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: A state of affairs or dynamic system whose internal make-up and working the Cognizer comes to assimilate into their knowledge structure.
frameElementDefinitions.Completeness: The extent to which the Cognizer has incorporated the workings and significance of a Phenomenon into their knowledge structure.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate the relative ease of the Cognizer in acquiring new knowledge (readily, easily).
frameElementDefinitions.Reference_point: A mental concept or domain to which the Cognizer relates their new knowledge.
frameElementDefinitions.Category: This frame element expresses a general type or class of which the Phenomenon is considered an instance by the Cognizer, allowing them to make predictions about the qualities, occurrence, or behavior of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the when the Cognizer asssimilates the new knowledge.
frameElementDefinitions.Faculty: A part of a person's cognitive-emotional faculties that is said to acquire knowledge.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: Evidence marks expressions that indicate the source of the Cognizer's knowledge.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Grinder or Grinding_cause causes a Patient to be broken into smaller pieces. A Result or Goal can be present.

frameElementDefinitions.Grinding_cause: An animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that grinds. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic.
frameElementDefinitions.Grinder: The Grinder breaks the Patient into smaller pieces.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which the Grinder grinds the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: The Locus is a description of the active zone of the Grinder, where the grinding most saliently takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Grinder grinds the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Grinder grinds the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Grinder grinds the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Grinder grinds the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the shape the Patient becomes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Grinder grinds the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity which undergoes the change brought about by the Grinder or Grinding_cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Grinder grinds the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This is where the Patient might end up, after undergoing the grinding process.

annotations.definition: In this frame, an Agent engages in personal body care by grooming either a Patient or a Body_part. An Instrument can be used in this process as well as a Medium.

frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of the grooming event.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The agent is the person who does the grooming.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The patient is the person who gets washed.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This is an object that aids the washing process.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: This is the region of the body that gets groomed.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: This is a region of the object, body or the body part that gets groomed.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the substance that is the grooming agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frequency with which the Agent groom himself/other.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Agent intentionally acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: How long the grooming takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Agent is trying to bring about by grooming.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Agent to accomplish the grooming.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Patient is in a state of having been broken into smaller pieces.

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity acted on and that undergoes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the shape the Patient becomes.

annotations.definition: A Grower endeavors to tend to and influence the environment of Food so as to foster its growth.

frameElementDefinitions.Grower: The Grower is the agent growing Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Food: Food is the item grown by the Grower, which can include things not traditionally eaten, like flowers, etc., or items for other consumption-related uses, like medicines, smokables, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is used by the Grower as a tool grow Food. (This tool is not limited to typical artifacts and can be hands, arms, and other atypical hunting and fishing instruments.)
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what length of time the Grower is growing Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Grower grows Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Grower grows Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The greater geographic location where the Grower grows Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the growing event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Grower intends to accomplish by growing Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Grower grows Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: This is the the physical background or context for the Food to which the Grower pays attention in his growing efforts.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The starting state of the Food, e.g., a seed.

annotations.definition: Two individuals, the Guest and the Host are in a temporary social relationship within which the Host provides use of a location or resources for the Guest at a location highly associated with the Host (in many cases, where the Host lives or works). The Guest is not expected to reciprocate the hospitality of the Host while the relationship holds, though may be expected to act as Host at a later time.

frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host provides a location and/or resources for the Guest at the Host_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Guest: The Guest receives the hospitality of the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Guest or Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby a focal participant achieves an action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of the relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Host_location: The location associated with the Host, where the Guest is staying, working, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the guest-and-host relationship holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A trait or characteristic of the Guest or Host.

annotations.definition: An Accused individual is deemed by a (usually implicit) Judge to be involved (or not) in a Misdeed.

frameElementDefinitions.Accused: This is the person who is accused of a Misdeed.
frameElementDefinitions.Misdeed: The wrongful act which the Accused was purportedly involved in.
frameElementDefinitions.Viewpoint: The individual whose point of view is taken in connecting the Accused to the Misdeed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Accused is involved in the Misdeed.

annotations.definition: A Person has a particular mental state and the ability to engage in an activity with enthusiasm, energy, and positivity.

frameElementDefinitions.Person: The person who is evaluated for a level of enthusiasm.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Person's level of enthusiasm and participation.

annotations.definition: Temporary or permanent Styles and Configurations of Hair, as well as its inherent Nature. Note that Attachment and Body Part will generally be incorporated in the lexical meanings. Subregions (e.g. roots, ends) are often not specified, and sometimes incorporated (e.g. in 'bangs')

frameElementDefinitions.Hair: This FE is the hair itself
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: This FE is the Possessor of the body part.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: A subregion of the body part referred to by the target, e.g.
frameElementDefinitions.Configuration: Temporary Configurations of hair (eg braid, bun, ponytail). Something done to the hair merely by movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Hair_property: A property of the hair (eg curly, fine, brown). This acts much like "Descriptor" in the Observable_bodyparts Frame. Colors, styles, natural properties are described by this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: Where the salient part of the hair is.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessory: Items that are put in the hair as part of a Configuration or Style

annotations.definition: This frame consists of LUs which denote a Theme ceasing motion.

frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which a Theme stops the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Theme's motion stops.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Theme stops.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for a Depictive phrase describing the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Theme stops motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: This FE identifies Theme whose motion ceases.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the length of Time during which the motion is stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Theme stops motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: This FE identifies the last Co-timed_event of the stopped motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the halting event began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: Containing event is an event that is occuring at a time during which the Theme ceases motion.

annotations.definition: The obtaining of a Requirement state of affairs or the presence of a Required_entity is profiled as a prerequisite for the obtaining or occurring of a Dependent state-of-affairs.

frameElementDefinitions.Dependent: The state-of-affairs which cannot hold without the state of affairs of the Requirement or the presence of the Required_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Requirement: The state-of-affairs that must obtain in order for the Dependent to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Dependent depends on the presence of the Required_entity or the obtaining of the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state-of-affairs which militates that the Requirement is necessary for the Dependent to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Requirement is necessary for the Dependent.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: The conditions under which the the Requirement is necessary.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The field of endeavor whose nature imposes the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Required_entity: An entity that has to be present for the Dependent state of affairs to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Required_individual: An individual that has to provide the Required_entity or to see to it that the Requirement state of affairs holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place denotes a location in which the specified relation between Requirement and Dependent holds.

annotations.definition: A Source_symbol in a Source_representation system is presented to be matched by a Target_symbol in a Target_representation in the ability to express a particular Content.

frameElementDefinitions.Source_symbol: A symbol or set of symbols representing the Content in the Source_representation format.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_representation: The representation system in which the Content pre-exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the translation equivalence holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the set of conditions in which the translation is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_symbol: A symbol or set of symbols representing the Content in the Target_representation format.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_representation: The representation system within which the Target_symbol is equivalent in its signification to the Source_symbol within the Source_representation system .
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The information that is meant to be represented by the Source_symbol and Target_symbol.
frameElementDefinitions.Equivalence: An assessment of how equivalent the Source_representation and the Target_representation are.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: A text in which the Source_symbol is rendered by a Target_symbol to express a particular Content.

annotations.definition: A Topical_entity has properties which are affected by the existence and association of an Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Some entity, abstract or concrete, which is stated to exist and be associated with the Topical_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Entity exists and is associated with the Topical_entity
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period during which the Entity exists in association with the Topical_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A situation, force, or entity which brings about the existence of the Entity or its association with the Topical_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Entity is associated with the Topical_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances marks expressions that indicate a set of conditions under which the Entity is declared to exist in association with the Topical_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Viewpoint: The perspective of an individual who judges whether they consider the Entity to be associated with the Topical_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Topical_entity: The entity under discussion, whose states and properties are impacted by its association with the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: A Host, at a location strongly associated with him/herself (such as a place of employment or residence), temporarily hosts a Visitor so that the Visitor can achieve a goal. The purpose of the visit is often social in nature.

frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host allows the Visitor to enter his/her location (house, office, etc.) for a temporary period so that the Visitor can achieve a goal. The goal is often socially-motivated (e.g., socialization between the Visitor and the Host).
frameElementDefinitions.Visitor: The Visitor is a guest of the Host at a location strongly associated with the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose of the Host in hosting the Visitor.

annotations.definition: Party_1 and Party_2 (collectively refered to as Parties) make a commercial agreement, an Obligation, which both Parties are expected to keep or fulfill.

frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: The individual or individuals that form the more prominent (i.e. agentive) party of the commercial agreement, as compared with Party_2. That is, Party_1 is generally encoded as the External Argument of verbs, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to Party_1. Thus manner expressions are construed to apply to Party_1, not necessarily to Party_2 or the group of Parties as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: The semantically (and grammatically) less prominent participant (or group of participants) in the agreement. It is generally encoded within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by with).
frameElementDefinitions.Obligation: An expression of the commitment which the Parties have to engage in a commercial transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is a description of the domain covered by the commercial agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: As with other frames in the Communication domain, the Medium of communication may be expressed. Medium is the physical entity or channel used to record or negotiate the Obligation.
frameElementDefinitions.Parties: The group of individuals portrayed as equally involved in having a commercial agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where an agreement was made.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the agreement was made.

annotations.definition: A Theme has access to a Useful_location or is blocked from it by a Barrier.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme whose motion is blocked or free.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Theme exceeds or falls short of the ability to move to the Useful_location
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Theme meets or fails to meet the pre-conditions for getting to the Useful_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An Explanation for why the Barrier exists, or for why the Theme meets or fails to meet pre-conditions for entering the Useful_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Useful_location: The place or thing that the Theme is headed towards, despite a potential or actual Barrier.
frameElementDefinitions.Barrier: An entity, event, or state that (at least potentially) prevents the Theme from getting to the Useful_location.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist is sensitive to a Trigger, which has the potential to cause some kind of reaction in the Protagonist.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist occurs as the External Argument of the targets in this frame. The Protagonist represents the one exhibiting the response to the Trigger.
frameElementDefinitions.Trigger: The Trigger most commonly occurs in a PP Complement headed by to. The Trigger is that substance or element which causes a health response in the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner describes the way (or style) in which the Protagonist Health_response manifests itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree describes the extent to which the health response occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The Body_part identifies the part of the body that is responding to the Trigger.

annotations.definition: The Hearer receives a Message or communication on a particular Topic. Words in this frame denote events of verbal communication, but express the Hearer rather than the Speaker as an External Argument.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: This is the person who produces a Message. Typically, it is expressed in this frame as a PP-complement headed by from:
frameElementDefinitions.Hearer: This FE is the person who receives a Message. It is expressed as an Exteranal Argument in this frame:
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message is the content that is communicated from one person to another. It is normally expressed as an NP Object or a finite clause Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is the subject matter about which the Message is communicated. It is normally expressed as a PP-complement headed by about, with or without a quantificational noun:
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used to transmit the Message. It is normally expressed as a PP-complement headed by on or in:
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time the Hearer receives the Message.

annotations.definition: An Insulator is judged for its potential to increase the body temperature of an Experiencer. The Activity that the Experiencer engages in to experience the heat and the Circumstances in which the Insulator is judged may also be provided.

frameElementDefinitions.Insulator: The object which provides warm. It does not have to radiate heat; it may provide warmth with heavy insulation.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity the Experiencer engages in to feel heat.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the Experiencer feels warmth
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Experiencer feels a level of heat.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The sentient entity that utilizes the Insulator.

annotations.definition: When words of this frame are used as hedge markers, they signal that the speaker of the sentence wishes to be careful about asserting some Hedged_content. Use of a hedge marker may mean that the speaker is uncertain of how factual of the Hedged_content is, or uncertain of whether they're expressing the idea behind Hedged_content well, or it may mean that the Hedged_content is socially undesirable or awkward in the conversational context.

frameElementDefinitions.Hedged_content: The idea or ideas that the speaker wishes to be careful about asserting.

annotations.definition: A Communicator announces (linguistically or non-linguistically) the imminent arrival of an Individual or the occurrence of an Event. Generally, the Communicator is attempting to inform the public.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The Communicator greets the arrival of an Individual or occurrence of an Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Communicator heralds.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which a Communicator heralds.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Communicator heralds.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Communicator heralds.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Communicator that also affect the heralding ( eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Individual: The person who is greeted at their arrival by the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The occurrence which is greeted by the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The symbol-containing artifact whereby a Communicator heralds.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Hidden_object to become perceptually inaccessible to potential perceivers by placing it in a Hiding_place or putting in place an Obstruction that screens the object.

frameElementDefinitions.Obstruction: The entity that the Agent uses to block the Hidden_object from being perceptible.
frameElementDefinitions.Hidden_object: The Hidden_object is the entity which is blocked from view.
frameElementDefinitions.Potential_observer: The Potential_observer denotes a person who might perceive the Hidden_object if it was not hidden.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Hidden_object becomes perceptually inaccessible.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who causes the Hidden_entity to be not perceptually accessible to potential perceivers.
frameElementDefinitions.Hiding_place: The location in which the Hidden_object is placed so as not to be perceptually accessible.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the spatial setting in which the hiding event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Time denotes the interval of time during which the Agent causes the Hidden_object to become hidden from perceptual access.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the occurrence of the hiding event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Agent that brings about the hiding of the Hidden_object.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Agent hopes to bring about by hiding the Hidden_object.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: In this frame a Hindrance makes it more difficult for a Protagonist to complete their intended Action or a Hindrance directly affects an Action where the Action can be a natural act or the actions of many individuals in a collective endeavor. Crucially, these lexical units do not entail that the Protagonist was unable to complete the Action or that the Action did not occur. The Hindrance may indicate a lack of enablement or an obstacle on the natural progression of the Protagonist or Action

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist is the entity that tends towards or plans some Action which the Hindrance causes to be more difficult.
frameElementDefinitions.Hindrance: The Hindrance is the force which makes the Action more difficult or less likely to be successfully completed. The Hindrance may be a lack of enabling force:
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action is what the Protagonist desires to do, which is made more difficult by the Hindrance. It can also be a natural act or collective endeavor, not linked to an overt Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Hindrance affects the Protagonist or Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location where he Hindrance affects the Protagonist or Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the extent to which the Hindrance affects the Protagonist or Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE describes the means by which hindering occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE label is used for the cause of the Hindrance
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which hindering occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which the hindering takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause. In other words, given only the facts of the *Concessive, one would expect them to cause the world to be the opposite of what is protrayed in the main clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Employer hires an Employee, promising the Employee a certain Compensation in exchange for the performance of a job. The job may be described either in terms of a Task or a Position. In some cases, the Employee FE will also indicate the Position (see fourth example below).

frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person whom the Employer takes on as an Employee, obligating them to perform some Task in order to receive Compensation.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) that takes on an Employee, giving them Compensation in return for the performance of an assigned Task.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Employee is set to receive for performing an assigned Task.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Employer hires an Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Employer hires the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when an Employer hires the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Employer hires the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: The action that the Employee is taken on by the Employer to do.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of employment.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: The Field that the Employee is going to work in for their Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity that the Employer uses to hire the Employee.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action by the Employer whereby the hire is brought about.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis is the condition of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period or payment arrangements.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the hiring event began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: In the course of history, an Event or Entity is taken to have importance or significance.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: This FE identifies the event which occurs to create history.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: This FE identifies the entity, concrete or abstract.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain is (a characterization of) the type of event that is part of the Event's history.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: A recordable sequence of events is associated with a given Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject about which the history is given.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain is (a characterization of) the type of event that is to be included in consideration of the Topic's history.
frameElementDefinitions.End_time: The end of the span of time during which the Topic occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Start_time: The beginning of the time span over which the Topic occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_span: The span of time over which the events related to the Topic take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration, often expressed in calendric units, of the history of the Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the events concerning the Topic occured.

annotations.definition: This frame describes how Events are related to each other and Place and Time.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: This FE identifies the event which occurs to create history.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the event occurs.

annotations.definition: An Agent succeeds or fails to interact perceptually or physically with the Target or Target_location. The Instrument which is used to interact with the Target may stand in metonymically for the Agent.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual who tries to interact with the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Target: The Target is the entity located in an indirectly accessible location relative to the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt at interaction, incorporated in the LUs of the frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent achieves a Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the interaction takes place at the Target_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when something hits or misses.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the Agent during the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Agent manages or fails to interact with the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of how the details of the event compare to others, either generally (the same way), or in terms of a state of the Agent which influences the event (carelessly).
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Agent attempts to interact with the Target
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which contextualizes the hitting or missing of the Agent but is not conceived of as caused by it.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The object manipulated by the Agent in order to interact with the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_location: The location that the Agent is attempting to access using the Instrument.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that the Agent hits or misses the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Deviation: The distance of the actual location from the Target_location.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes the ballistic motion of a projectile and its subsequent impact on a Target.

frameElementDefinitions.Target: The entity which is hit by a Projectile directed by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that directs the Projectile such that it impacts the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Agent hopes to bring about by hitting the Target
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent hits the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the event happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Target hit by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Agent that accomplishes hitting the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The entity, most often ammunition or a firearm, which the Agent manipulates to hit the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: An Agent decides temporarily not to perform a Desirable_action or not to interact with a Salient_entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent decides not to engage in a Desirable_action for a while or not to interact with a Salient_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Desirable_action: Desirable_action identifies the event or activity in which the Agent does not engage for a while.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the temporary forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the temporary forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the temporary forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the temporary forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the interval of Time during which the the forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the temporary forgoing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the Agent's decision to temporarily forgo a Desirable_action or to not interact with a Salient_entity logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances under which the Agent does not engage in the Desirable_action.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: An entity that evokes a contextually inferrable Desirable_action that the Agent is temporarily forgoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of the Agent's decision to forgo a Desirable_action to the point in time when the Agent engages in the action.
frameElementDefinitions.End_point: The point in time, defined either as a clock time or in terms of an event that takes place, at which the Agent engages in the Desirable_action or interacts with the Salient_entity.

annotations.definition: A Host is judged regarding their attitude as a potential host in a guest-host relationship. The Host exhibits a Behavior that is judged as demonstrating a particular degree hospitality towards a Guest. Alternatively, the Host may reveal an Expressor that indicates a particular sort of attitude.

frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host is the person whose Behavior towards others as a (potential) host is evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Guest: The Guest is the entity which is positively or negatively treated by the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The Behavior is the action of the Host on which the evaluation of the Host's character is based.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the extent to which the Host exhibits the particular Behavior towards others.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the situation or person that the Host's Behavior responds to or is related to.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Expressor is the body part or action by a body part which indicates the character of the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the Host embodies the characters of a good or poor host.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the evaluation.

annotations.definition: This frame consists of words that describe a hostile encounter between opposing forces (Side_1 and Side_2, collectively conceptualizable as Sides) over a disputed Issue and/or in order to reach a specific Purpose.

frameElementDefinitions.Side_1: One of two participants in a hostile encounter.
frameElementDefinitions.Side_2: One of two participants in a hostile encounter, usually the second mentioned.
frameElementDefinitions.Sides: The jointly expressed sides in a hostile encounter.
frameElementDefinitions.Issue: An unresolved question over which the two sides of a hostile encounter are in disagreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The desired result of the outcome of the hostile encounter for the Side_1 or for all the Sides collectively. It may either directly refer to the state of affairs that is desired, e.g.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: Means describes the way in which a side engages in a hostile encounter.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of the hostile encounter. Descriptions of the intensity count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the hostile encounter takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the hostile encounter occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The body movement may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal Cause, the Agent's mental or emotional state. Internal Cause is expressed in a PP complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: A Hunter tries to gain Food by way of outsmarting or tracking a living. The Hunter hopes the attempt will result in the catching and killing of the living entity that is intended for human consumption. The act of seeking Food inherently involves the assistance of some tool, which can be an extension of the Hunter's body or an artifact fashioned or exploited for the use of food seeking, catching, and/or killing.

frameElementDefinitions.Hunter: The Hunter is the agent hoping to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Food: Food is the sought-out item by the Hunter.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is used by the Hunter as a tool to help obtain Food. (This tool is not limited to typical artifacts and can be hands, arms, and other atypical hunting and fishing instruments.)
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what length of time the Hunter attempted to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Hunter makes the attempt for Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an attempt at achieving a Food obtainment.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The greater geographic location where the Hunter attempts to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Time seeking action occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Hunter intends to accomplish by seeking Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Hunter attempts to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the amount of effort the Hunter puts into his attempt to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: This is the entity to which the Hunter pays attention. It is referred to as Ground because it serves as the background or context for the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co_participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the same event as the primary protagonist, regardless of whether the protagonist, and hence the Co-participant, is more agent- or more undergoer-like.

annotations.definition: A Hunter endeavors to- and either fails or succeeds at finding and obtaining Food. This scenario occurs within the context of some Ground within which the preparation, hunt, and success or failure of it plays out.

frameElementDefinitions.Hunter: The Hunter is the agent hoping to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Food: Food is the sought-out item by the Hunter.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is used by the Hunter as a tool to help obtain Food. (This tool is not limited to typical artifacts and can be hands, arms, and other atypical hunting and fishing instruments.)
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what length of time the Hunter attempted to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Hunter makes the attempt for Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an attempt at achieving a Food obtainment.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The greater geographic location where the Hunter attempts to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Food seeking action occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Hunter intends to accomplish by seeking Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Hunter attempts to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the amount of effort the Hunter puts into an attempt to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: This is the entity to which the Hunter pays attention. It is referred to as Ground because it serves as the background or context for the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: A Hunter succeeds or fails at capturing his sought-out or tracked Food.

frameElementDefinitions.Hunter: The Hunter is the agent that has successfully obtained Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Food: Food is the item obtained by the Hunter.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is used by the Hunter as a tool to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Hunter obtains the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Food is obtained.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The greater geographic location where the Hunter obtains Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Food seeking action occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Hunter intends to accomplish obtaining Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Hunter obtains Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the amount of effort the Hunter puts into an attempt to obtain Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: A Current_instance of a certain Type is under discussion. This instance is evaluated as being the same instance or a different instance from a Previous_instance encountered in a Previous_context.

frameElementDefinitions.Type: The kind of thing of which instances are under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Current_instance: The instance of the Type that is currently under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Previous_instance: An instance of the Type that was encountered previously and which is now compared for identity with the Current_instance.
frameElementDefinitions.Previous_context: The situation in which another instance of the Type was encountered earlier.
frameElementDefinitions.Set_of_instances: The complete set of instances of the Type involved in the mentioned contexts.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Current_instance is the same or different.

annotations.definition: An Instance of some type is conceived of as having an Identity, defined as the unique feature that distinguishes each Instance from all other instances of its type. Identity persists across time and remains constant for an Instance despite arbitrary changes in other attributes of the Instance. Establishing Identity answers the question "which".

frameElementDefinitions.Instance: The instance of a type or out of a set that is currently under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Identity: The feature of each Instance that distinguishes it from all others. Notionally, each Instance has a separate Identity, although in practice many Instances cannot be distinguished from each other.

annotations.definition: An Instance of some Type is conceived of as having a distinct Identity. Its Identity persists across time and remains constant for an Instance despite arbitrary changes in other attributes of the Instance. Establishing Identity answers the question "which", and is often answered by saying that an Instance is the same or different from a previously mentioned instance (see Identicality), or by deictic reference ("that one"), etc. Identity is not the same as appearance or name (if the Instance even has such), although these attributes of the Instance may be useful in establishing Identity. Identity can be established knowing the totality of the history of an Instance; however, this is impractical and usually proxies like name and appearance are used. For the Identity of humans, history sufficient to establish Identity can usually be satisfactorily established by documents associated with the person, often with information like appearance and name, though use of such documents leads to the problem of multiple identities, only one of which is considered legitimate or "true" in a given context.

frameElementDefinitions.Type: The kind of thing of which instances are under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Instance: The instance of the Type that is currently under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Identity: The property of an Instance that is defined as unique for each separate Instance of a Type.

annotations.definition: A certain Idiosyncrasy belongs to an Entity distinguishing it from other entities.

frameElementDefinitions.Idiosyncrasy: An entity or characteristic belonging to the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity that has a certain distinguishing Idiosyncrasy.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Idiosyncrasy differs from the norm.

annotations.definition: A Profiled_region is picked out relative to a Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: A region of space located relative to a Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the Profiled_region.

annotations.definition: An Agent displays a Characteristic of a Standard in his or her own way of behaving.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person that borrows a Characteristic behavior from a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Characteristic: The Characteristic is a behavior or property that the Standard possesses and which the Agent adopts in his or her own behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent that accomplishes the imitation.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: The Standard is the entity that exhibits a certain Characteristic that the Agent models their own behavior on.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by performing the imitation.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the imitation event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the imitating event as being in some state. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the imitating event.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the imitation event occurs and of which the imitating event is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Agent's way of behaving is similar to that of the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the imitation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent imitates some entity which acts as a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end.

annotations.definition: The Agent immobilizes a Patient optionally, to an Anchor.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent immobilizes the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is immobilized by the Agent, optionally to an Anchor.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent that accomplishes the immobilization.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent immobilizes a Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Anchor: The Agent attaches the Patient to an Anchor, generally, an immobile object. Lisa handcuffed Mike to the refridgerator.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Patient is immobilized as a result.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the immobilizing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). This FE also covers expressions that indicate salient characteristics of the Agent which also affect the action (carefully, eagerly).

annotations.definition: While in motion, an Impactor makes sudden, forcible contact with the Impactee, or two Impactors both move, mutually making forcible contact.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which an Impact occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Impactor: The entity that hits the Impactee.
frameElementDefinitions.Impactee: The entity which is hit by the Impactor.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The speed at impact.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: Subregion identifies the affected part of the larger whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Force: The amount of force in the course of the impact.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The Time throughout which the impact repeatedly takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Impactors or the Impactor during the impact.

annotations.definition: An Agent moves Goods (or some other useful item) from an Exporting_area, across a boundary or border to a different area, the Importing_area. The Path along which the Goods pass may also be indicated. Depending on the lexical unit, the Agent 's point of view may be centered on the Exporting_area at the beginning of the process (e.g. export) or on the Importing_area and the end of the process (e.g. import) or on the Path (e.g. transship). In some sentences, a constituent which encodes the Agent FE also encodes information about the Exporting_area, Path, or Importing_area.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The conscious entity, generally a person, that moves the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: An mobile entity directed by the Agent that holds the Goods and moves them along from the Exporting_area to the Importing_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The items of value whose location is changing.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Agent moves the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in performing the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent moves the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the state of a participant during the action
frameElementDefinitions.Importing_area: The FE Importing_area is the place that the Goods end up as a result of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path identifies the location of the Goods during transit, i.e. after leaving the Exporting_area and before reaching the Importing_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Exporting_area: The place where the Goods are initially, before the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This Frame Element is used for a characteristic or description of the Goods. Note that Descriptor is not an FE of the same character as other FEs. Specifically, rather than filling a slot, it identifies a modifier of its head noun.

annotations.definition: A Factor affects the outcome of an Undertaking, which can be a goal-oriented activity or the maintenance of a desirable state, the work in a Field, or something portrayed as affecting an Interested_party. A Reason may be given for the importance of the Factor. The Degree of importance may also be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Factor: Factor is used for the concrete or abstract entity whose importance is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE marks expressions that indicate the Degree to which the Factor is important to the Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Undertaking: The Undertaking is a process or state or field of activity in which the Interested_party has a stake.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for which the Factor is important to the Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Interested_party: Interested_party identifies the people who are affected by a particular Factor.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: A Factor may be generally important to the people or the activities in a field of endeavour.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Factor is important to the Undertaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Factor is important.

annotations.definition: An Importer receives Goods from an Exporting_area, across a boundary or border to a different area, the Importing_area. The Path along which the Goods pass may also be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Importer: The conscious entity, generally a person, that brings the Goods into the Importing_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: A mobile entity directed by the Importer that holds the Goods and moves them along from the Exporting_area to the Importing_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Importer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Importer that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The items of value whose location is changing.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Importer moves the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that explains the Importer's importation of the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Importer moves the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the state of a participant during the action
frameElementDefinitions.Importing_area: The FE Importing_area is the place that the Goods end up as a result of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path identifies the location of the Goods during transit, i.e. after leaving the Exporting_area and before reaching the Importing_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Exporting_area: The place where the Goods are initially, before the being moved.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative instance of the importing event , and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar importing events.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the final state of the Goods when brought into the Importing_area.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This FE applies to participants who derive a benefit from the occurrence of the act of importation.

annotations.definition: A Duty is imposed on a Responsible_party according to a Principle which regulates how the Responsible_party should respond to a Situation. The Situation may be expressed metonymically by reference to an Obligator, whose action invokes the Principle. It is only rarely the case that the Principle and the Situation/Obligator are both expressed overtly.

frameElementDefinitions.Responsible_party: The person who must perform the Duty.
frameElementDefinitions.Duty: The action that the Responsible_party is obligated to perform.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: This FE indicates the Conditions under which the Responsible_party is obligated to perform the Duty .
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Duty is imposed on the Responsible_party by the Obligator or the Situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A location in which the Responsible_party has a specified Duty .
frameElementDefinitions.Obligator: The Obligator is the person who imposes the Duty on the Responsible_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: A state of affairs that results in the Responsible_party being obligated to carry out the Duty in accordance with the Principle.
frameElementDefinitions.Principle: A regulating idea (which may be instantiated as a document) that the Responsible_party is subject to.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Obligator hopes to bring about by imposing the obligation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any holistic description of the details of the action, either comparing to another event or describing the action in terms of characteristics of the Obligator that affect the action holistically.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event which brings about the obligation.

annotations.definition: This frame describes the Impression which a Figure, which can be a person, an organization, or an object, leaves with an Audience. The Audience is often left unstated when it represents anyone who is aware of the subject. The Impression may be describe with a Descriptor; the effectiveness of the Impression may be provided by Degree. The Context indicates the domain in which the Impression is understood.

frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The entity (a person, organization, product, or event) for which the Audience has an Impression.
frameElementDefinitions.Audience: A person or group of people who have an Impression of a Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The effect of the Impression left on the Audience.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The effectiveness of the Impression on the Audience.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: A spatial, cultural, or personal delimitation within which a Figure leaves an Impression on an Audience.
frameElementDefinitions.Impression: A generalized conception of a Figure presented to or shared by an Audience.

annotations.definition: The Authorities put a Prisoner in Prison as punishment for an Offense.

frameElementDefinitions.Prisoner: The person who is confined in a penal institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Prison: The Prison within which the Prisoner comes to be confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities are the institution or actors carrying out the imprisonment.
frameElementDefinitions.Offense: The action that the Prisoner is being punished for.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Prisoner is kept.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the imprisonment happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose of the imprisonment.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Authorities that brings about the imprisonment.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the imprisoning event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Authorities that affect the action (ruthlessly).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The act or state of affairs that the Authorities respond to by imprisoning the Prisoner.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Authorities maintains the confinement of the Prisoner.
frameElementDefinitions.Conviction: The legal classification which characterizes the Offense.
