frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: Iterations marks expressions that indicate the number of times the Prisoner was imprisoned.

annotations.definition: An Entity improves from a less desirable Prior_state to a more desirable Post_state. (Note that members of this frame marked with the semantic type "Negative" indicate deterioration.) In most cases the Prior_state and the Post_state are implicit, since the most important fact is simply that the Post_state is better than the Prior_state.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The object that undergoes change.
frameElementDefinitions.Prior_state: The former state of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Post_state: The state of the Entity following the improvement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the improvement occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The rate at which the change happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the improvement, generally a description comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period over which the improvement occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of improvement that the Entity has attained.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant dimension of improvement.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An event or state of affairs that brings about the improvement of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Improvement occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes the state of affairs denoted by the target as role fillers in other frames
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Improvement event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An improvised explosive device is a Bomb or similar device used in non-conventional warfare. It consists of explosive material connected to a detonator, possibly including other materials meant to damage nearby people and property.

frameElementDefinitions.Bomb: The Bomb explodes, injuring or damaging a Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The type of activity that the Bomb is generally involved in. Often this can be considered a kind of Type specification also.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Bomb belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The substance that makes up the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Wielder: The person that wishes to attack the Target and who controls (directly or indirectly) the detonation of the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: This FE identifies a part of the Bomb. This includes parts that aid in the detonation or damaging, or mere attachments.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which a Bomb comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The individual or group responsible for the creation of the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description or characteristic of the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Target: The intended or actual target of the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The location of the Bomb.

annotations.definition: Due to internal forces/properties, an Entity is inclined towards a particular Behavior, which may be to perform an action or to maintain a state. The underlying force is seen as being beyond the Entity's control. The Degree to which the Entity is inclined towards the Behavior may also be specified as well as the Basis for the inclination.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: TheFrame Element Entity identifies the person or thing that has a tendency towards some Behaviour.
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The FE Behavior marks expressions that indicate which state or action the Entity tends towards.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate how great the inclination of an Entity towards a Behavior is.
frameElementDefinitions.Basis: This FE marks expressions that indicate why the Entity has the propensity for some Behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.

annotations.definition: A Total has a Part, either as a member of an aggregate or as a constituent part of a simple entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Total: A complex entity, either an aggregate or a unit, of which a Part, a constituent or a component, is specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: A component or constituent that is specified to form part of the Total.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE indicates the purpose for which some implicit (non-expressed) agent causes the inclusion of the Part within the Total.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE identifies the frequency with which the Part is included int he Total.
frameElementDefinitions.Contrast_set: This FE indicates other elements that are included within the Total.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: This FE indicates the sub-location of the Total in which the Part is included.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the inclusion event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Total includes the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Total includes the Part.

annotations.definition: A Part is either a subset of a collection of items that make up a Whole, or is a subpart of a singular Whole. This frame covers both super/sub-set relations and part-whole relations.

frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole is the larger entity that includes the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: The Part is either a subset or a constituent part of the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the inclusion event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the inclusion relation holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the inclusion relation holds.

annotations.definition: A member or members of a Class have just been mentioned or are present in the discourse setting and now a different instance (or group of instances) of the same class is referred to.

frameElementDefinitions.Class: The general category of entity which has been referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Added_set: The instance of the Class that is added to the Initial_set of entities in the Class.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_set: The previously mentioned group of entities of the Class.
frameElementDefinitions.Increment: The number of entities within the Added_set.

annotations.definition: A Communicator or Medium assigns linguistic meaning to an Unresolved_referent thereby identifying the specific actor or entity which fulfills the answer to this question.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey the Unresolved_referent to another person.
frameElementDefinitions.Unresolved_referent: The thing to which the Communicator or Medium refers for identification purposes.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physical or abstract setting in which the Unresolved_referent is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Amount_of_information: The amount of information exchanged when communication occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes the state of the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Communicator makes the indication.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Communicator makes the indication.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the the Communicator is makes the indication.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration is the length of time during which the indicating takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Communicator or Medium indicates the Unresolved_referent.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why the Communicator is indicating the Unresolved_referent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Communicator or Medium indicates the Unresolved_referent.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: To what end the Communicator or Medium makes the indication.

annotations.definition: An Entity began its existence in the context of a location of Origin, which is a discourse-salient place, or culture.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The item whose Origin is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The discourse-salient place or culture where the Entity was produced.

annotations.definition: This scenario describes a series of Events, associated with a single Participant, i.e., the history of the Participant. The Events do not necessarily form a coherent whole (e.g., are not necessarily causally related), but merely occupy a period of time. In many cases the history is limited to what are considered the important or significant events. Words in this frame can also denote the time period over which the sequence of Events took place. Note: uses of words in this frame to describe a record of the series of events are not in this frame (e.g., we compiled a history of Rome).

frameElementDefinitions.Participant: The Participant is either an active participant in all of the Events (e.g., Agent or Patient), or is more peripherally associated with the Events (e.g., Location or Topic).
frameElementDefinitions.Events: The Events which the Participant is a part of. This FE is used to mark the frame-evoking element.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain is (a characterization of) the type of event that is to be included in consideration of the Participant's history.
frameElementDefinitions.End_time: The end of the span of time during which the Events occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Start_time: The beginning of the time span over which the Events occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_span: The span of time over which the Events take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration, often expressed in calendric units, of the history of the Participant.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A characterization of the Participant or Events.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Events concerning the Participant occured.

annotations.definition: A Stimulus inspires a feeling of awe and interest in a (generally implicit or generic) Experiencer due to desirable qualities which are difficult to explain. Alternatively, the Attribute of the Stimulus may be described instead.

frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The entity which inspires a feeling of amazement at its exceptionality.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer experiences the emotion brought about by the Stimulus
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: An expression of how unusual the ineffability of the Stimulus is.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE marks expressions that indicate a set of conditions under which the Stimulus is able to bring about the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is the area in which the Stimulus brings about an effect on the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: The Comparison_set is the set of individuals the Stimulus is compared to when determining the Degree of the emotional reaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Property: A feature of the Stimulus which causes a particular experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: An expression of the attribute of ineffability.

annotations.definition: The action of spreading some Infection to an Infected_entity, intentionally or otherwise. Pathology indicates that the Infection_cause can be airborne, carried on the skin/hair, or transmitted via various other forms of contact.

frameElementDefinitions.Infection: An invasion, typically undetected, of an entity's system. The infection most often compromises the entity's stability. Infections are a common pathology for diseases.
frameElementDefinitions.Infected_entity: This FE labels the recipient of the Infection.
frameElementDefinitions.Infection_cause: This FE labels the cause of the Infection which affects the Infected_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The medium through which the infection is transmitted.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Infecting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification, force, secondary effects, and general descriptions comparing events.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place at which the Infecting event occurs; or, the location of the Infection_cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the infection occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing a participant in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act by an Infection_cause that enables them to act upon the Infected_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of an Infection.

annotations.definition: A Force has the potential to influence an Entity or State_of_affairs within a certain Domain due to some Source.

frameElementDefinitions.Force: Something which has the capability of affecting a Entity in some way.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Something which is dependent on the influencing Force.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: A description of the areas in which the Force can affect the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: A principle factor which gives the Force the capability to affect the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: A situation which can be influenced or brought about by the Force.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer knows or comes to know some piece of Information about a Topic. In this frame, many LUs encode a specific Means_of_Gathering and/or Source, but these may also be expressed separately.

frameElementDefinitions.Information: The Information that the Cognizer possesses or comes to posess.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is aware of the Information.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is what the Information is about in general.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Cognizer obtains the Information from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_gathering: An action or method used by the Cognizer that results in the acquisition of the Information.

annotations.definition: A man-made artifact, digital or physical, which is used to display information presented via the object.

frameElementDefinitions.Display: This FE identifies the entity or substance that is designed to have a particular Use.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which a display is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Display into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which an Display comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Display.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Display.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Display, including components, code, etc.

annotations.definition: A group of entities exist (as an infrastructure) such that a particular type of Activity is either enabled or facilitated. The entities may or may not have been organized specifically for the Activity. The infrastructure is constituted by Resources that can be made use of by Users, and the infrastructure may be specified for its Place or Possessor.

frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity which is enabled or facilitated by the infrastructure.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the infrastructure.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The organization, institution, or individual who owns the infrastructure.
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The infrastructure may be conceived of as consisting of Resources that can be made use of by Users in performing an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.User: The people for whom the infrastructure is designed to be used by. In the cases where the constituent denoting the user also evokes the Activity, we annotate this on the second FE layer.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the infrastructure.
frameElementDefinitions.Infrastructure: The collection of entities and/or institutions that help to promote an Activity of a public character.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Ingestor takes a Substance into his or her body. The Delivery_device can appear as a direct object.

frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Ingestor ingests the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Ingestor ingests the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Ingestor ingests the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Ingestor ingests the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Ingestor ingests the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Delivery_device: The Delivery_device is the means by which the Substance is ingested.
frameElementDefinitions.Substance: The Substance is the drug or other substance that is ingested by the Ingestor.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the Ingestor ingests.
frameElementDefinitions.Entry_path: The Entry_path is the path along which the Substance enters the Ingestor's body.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the length of time for which the Ingestor ingests.

annotations.definition: An Ingestor consumes food or drink (Ingestibles), which entails putting the Ingestibles in the mouth for delivery to the digestive system. This may include the use of an Instrument. Sentences that describe the provision of food to others are NOT included in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Ingestor: The Ingestor is the person eating or drinking.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Ingestibles: The Ingestibles are the entities that are being consumed by the Ingestor.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Place from which the Ingestor takes the Ingestibles
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Ingestor that enables them to accomplishes the whole act of ingestion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Ingestibles are consumed by the Ingestor.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The action that the Ingestor hopes to bring about by ingesting.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time spent on the ingestion activity.

annotations.definition: A Material is used to make a Product.

frameElementDefinitions.Material: The entity which is used to make a Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Material belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The location that the Material comes from, including both extraction sites and emission/production sources.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Material.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The purpose for which the Material is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Product: The entity or substance that is created out of the Material.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: An sequenced based naming system for individual members of a group.

annotations.definition: The universe is considered through a teleological perspective and natural phenomena are seen to exist for the purpose of fulfilling some ultimate aim. A natural phenomenon is an Apparatus and an ultimate aim is a Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Apparatus: An entity that was not created by a living thing that is conceptualized to exist to fulfill some Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The ultimate aim of the Apparatus.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The sphere of activity in which the Apparatus operates.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: A description of the Goal.

annotations.definition: This scenario concerns an Agent inhibiting the motion of a Theme against the desire, plan, or tendency to be free. After a period of time, the Agent may choose to release the Theme, or the Theme may escape or be broken free by an intervening cause.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent prevents the Theme from moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme has a tendency or desire to move, but is prevented from doing so.
frameElementDefinitions.Holding_location: The location where the Agent keeps the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: A non-volitional cause that inhibits the motion of the Theme.

annotations.definition: An Agent restricts the movement of a Theme to within the vicinity of the Holding_location, despite the Theme's desire, plan, or tendency towards motion; the Agent may also use an Instrument. Alternatively, there may be a non-sentient Cause instead of an Agent restricting the Theme. For many of these targets, the means is incorporated. For some LUs, the Theme is required to be a sentient entity. Several of these LUs may be used to describe punishment situations when the Theme is a sentient entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity which is confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Holding_location: The area within which the Theme comes to be confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person intentionally restricting the movement of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the confinement is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose of the confinement.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in performing the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Agent maintains the confinement of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-sentient entity which is responsible for restricting the movement or location of the Theme.

annotations.definition: An Inspector directs his/her perceptual attention to a Ground to ascertain whether the Ground is intact or whether an Unwanted_entity is present. Alternatively, the desired outcome of the inspection may be presented as a Purpose.

frameElementDefinitions.Inspector: The person who inspects the Ground to see if it is intact or without blemish.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The entity that the Inspector inspects with his senses to ascertain whether it is intact.
frameElementDefinitions.Unwanted_entity: An entity whose presence would impair the acceptability or usefulness of the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE is used for the Means by which the Inspector examines the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Some of the words in this frame occur with a constituent that expresses the desired outcome of the inspection. Normally this constituent is a PP Complement headed by for, or a to-marked VP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Inspector scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the extent to which the Inspector examines the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: This FE is the position of the Perceiver during the act of perception. Typically, it is expressed in a from-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Desired_state_of_affairs: The desirable state of affairs which the Inspector hopes to find to obtain when inspecting the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Inspector examines the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The circumstances under which the Inspector examines the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity used by the Inspector to scrutinize the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: An Agent places a Component in a Fixed_location so that the Component is attached and interconnected and thereby functional.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person (or other force) that installs the Component.
frameElementDefinitions.Component: The Component is the object that is placed in a Fixed_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: This frame element specifies the location of the Agent (or of parts of their body) in cases where the Agent cannot manipulate the Component in straightforward fashion during the installation.
frameElementDefinitions.Fixed_location: The Fixed_location is the location where the Component is placed and to which it is attached so as to be useful.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the installation event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree indicates the degree to which the installation is completed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the Agent or Component that is installed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an act whereby the Agent achieves the installation of the Component.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The Duration_of_final_state is the amount of time for which the Component is to remain part of the System.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place indicates where the installation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation indicates why the installation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period in which the Component is installed.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is the setting into which multiple Components or a complex Component is installed.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: This is a second component that is installed alongside the primary Component or an individual performing the installation together with the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by perform the installation.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns transparent nouns that denote Instances of Types of entities or events.

frameElementDefinitions.Instance: A thing or episode of a certain Type.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The category of thing or event of which an Instance is picked out.

annotations.definition: A Patient is committed to the care of a medical Facility by a proper Authority. Regardless of whether the Patient agrees or does not agree with their placement in the Facility, the Authority judges that it is in the best interest of the Patient or his environment that the Patient receive treatment in the Facility.

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The person who is committed to a facility with a view towards helping them mentally or physically.
frameElementDefinitions.Facility: The facility where the Patient is taken for treatment.
frameElementDefinitions.Authority: The Authority is the actor carrying out the institutionalization with proper legal authorization, either invested into them in general by the government or granted due to power of attorney over the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Affliction: The condition exhibited by the Patient, which is deemed to merit institutionalization by the Authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the institutionalization happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose of the institutionalization.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Authority that brings about the institutionalization of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the institutionalization event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Authority that affect the action (ruthlessly).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the occurrence of the institutionalization event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: This FE identifies the length of the time period for which the Authority has placed the Patient in the Facility.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Facility is situated.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the institutionalization as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE marks the amount of times the Patient is committed to the Facility per unit of time.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns permanent organizations (the Institutions) with a public character, meaning that they are intended to affect the lives of the public at large in a particular Domain. In some cases, most or all members of the public have a direct relationship with the Institution, but in many cases, the effect on the populace is indirect.

frameElementDefinitions.Institution: A permanent organization with a socially important purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The concern of the Institution-- broadly speaking, the aspect of society that they affect.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal of the Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Members: The individuals that make up an Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Institution is located.

annotations.definition: A Deceiver performs some action so that the Victim ends up with an incorrect understanding about some Topic. The Means may be via communication, perceptual obscuration, or merely performing an action that the Victim will draw incorrect conclusions from.

frameElementDefinitions.Deceiver: The person who acts so as to give the Victim an incorrect idea about a Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The person who receives an incorrect idea because of the Deceiver's actions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The entity to which the misleading idea relates, what it is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Deceiver to fool the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the deception occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the deception occurs.

annotations.definition: A Self_mover intentionally moves in a Directionon a Path relative to landmark that is neither the Source nor the Goal of the overall motion. The shape or configuration of the Path may be expressed with a Path_shape

frameElementDefinitions.Self_mover: This is a sentient being or a vehicle steered by a sentient being which moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path, excluding directional expressions:
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Any expression which indicates where the Self_mover ends up as a result of the motion expresses the frame element Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of a Self_mover that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Any expression which characterizes the extent of motion expresses the frame element Distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event involving the Self_mover that results in their moving in the specified way.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the Self_mover moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: This is a second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique, relative to which the Self_mover moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the motion (which is specified by a Source, Path or Goal) is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The configuration formed by the entire Path of the Self_mover, taken simultaneously.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction in which the Self_mover goes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Self_mover wishes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_motion: The device or bodypart that serves to move the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Self_mover spends in motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A factor which explains the particular motion or manner of motion of the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at the time the Intentional_traversing occurs and which the Intentional_traversing is taken to be a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that an action occurs in a given time.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: A larger conceptualization that includes the Intentional_traversing as an integral part. The Intentional_traversing expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action in this larger conceptualization.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: An unexpected event that affects some agent (usually the Self_mover), which is enabled by, and occurs after, the Intentional_traversing.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the Traversing event itself and any of its participants, and is merely peripherally related to the event as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Endpoints: The Source and Goal of either a single or a repeated motion taken together as Endpoints of the Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co-participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the same event as the primary protagonist, regardless of whether the protagonist, and hence the Co-participant, is more agent- or more undergoer-like.

annotations.definition: This is an abstract frame for acts performed by sentient beings. It exists mostly for FE inheritance.

frameElementDefinitions.Act: This FE identifies the Act that the Agent performs intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent performs the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the intentional act occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent performs an intentional act. John flattered her to receive a raise.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent acts intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent acts intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain within which the Agent acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the Agent does the Act in a given period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of an act.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the state of affairs expressed by the target, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE gives a description of the Intentionally_act event.
frameElementDefinitions.Apparent_conclusion: This FE describes an additional situation or modification that would seem to hold given the rest of the situation. In some cases, it marks a completely separate conclusion than one would draw from the manner of the event in the main clause. As far as we know, this idea is only explicitly marked in English with "as if".

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Patient to be affected, sometimes by a particular Means or by use of an Instrument.

frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: This FE identifies the act that the Agent performs intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The conscious entity, generally a person, that performs the intentional act that affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that interacts with an Patient to accomplish the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent performs the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent intentionally affects a Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: Patient is the entity acted on and that may, but need not, undergo a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in performing the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Patient is affected by the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes the event.

annotations.definition: The Creator creates a new entity, the Created_entity, possibly out of Components.

frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The Creator creates a created entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place ehere the Agent intentionally creates the new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifes the Time when an Agent intentionally creates a new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent intentionally creates a new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Components: This FE identifies the Components that are attached together to form a Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies a depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Created_entity: This FE identifies the entity that the Agent intentionally creates.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the creation event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Creator that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Creator intentionally creates a new entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the instrument with which an Agent intentionally creates a Created_entitiy.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The role of Created_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: A secondary agent with whom the Creator intentionally creates the Created_entity.

annotations.definition: A volitionally acting Interceptor takes control, without being authorized to do so by the sender, of a Theme that is underway from a sender to a recipient. The Interceptor (or somebody on whose behalf they act) believes that the Theme reaching its goal would be against their interest. The Theme may be a physical thing that is being conveyed, a self-moving entity, or information that is broadcast.

frameElementDefinitions.Interceptor: The volitional agent who without authorization of sender or recipient takes control of a Theme that is en route to a destination.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity, physical or abstract, construed as traveling from a sender to a goal location, which is taken hold of by the Interceptor without permission.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Interceptor takes control of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Interceptor takes control of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the intercepting event as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Intercepting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate also salient characteristics of the Interceptor that also affect the action (carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The circumstances under which the interception takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the Interceptor hopes to bring about by intercepting.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Interceptor that results in success.

annotations.definition: A Figure is located in the interior (the Profiled_region--see Locative_relation frame) of the Ground. Some members of this frame indicate the plexity of the Ground, as either single-entity gestalt (inside), two-entity or bi-partite gestalt (between.prep), and multiple-entity or multipart gestalt (among.prep, amid.prep), while others are maximally general (in.prep).

frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is the entity located with respect to the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground is the area that the Figure is located in.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time during which the Figure was located in the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: This FE identifies the Accessibility between the Figure and some reference location (generally, the deictic center or the Ground).
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The area or zone interior to the Ground. This FE excludes Figure and is only used in particular constructions such as the following:
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'.
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Ground interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class of adverbials back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Figure before) and on.adv (which indicates a continued path). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, in which case it either refers to fictive, imagined motion (as in the first example below), or it backgrounds motion entirely (as in the second example).
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Region_quantification: A description of how much of a potential profiled region actually has (or is expected to have) a Figure in it. It is normally filled by closed-class items like 'all', 'somewhere', 'nowhere', etc.

annotations.definition: A Cause (or associated Actor) causes an ongoing Process to temporarily stop.

frameElementDefinitions.Cause: A force, process, or event that produces the interruption.
frameElementDefinitions.Process: The Process goes into a paused state due to the Cause or Actor.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the interruption takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the interruption occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Actor: An entity which participates in a Cause, perhaps volitionally and perhaps not, but effectively causing the interruption.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any holistic description of the event, including overall depictions (the same way) and descriptions pertaining to the Actor or Cause's influence on the character of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action or sub-event through which the Actor or Cause interrupts the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the interruption occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.

annotations.definition: An Intoxicant is ingested in order to achieve an altered state of consciousness. A Type or Descriptor may be indicated as well.

frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type identifies the kind or sort of Intoxicant.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Descriptor describes a quality of the Intoxicant.
frameElementDefinitions.Country_of_origin: Country_of_origin identifies where the Intoxicant is grown or originates.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns people being in an altered mental state that is induced by an Intoxicant.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The mental state of the Cognizer is changed by an Intoxicant .
frameElementDefinitions.Intoxicant: The Intoxicant alters the Cognizer's mental experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This Fe identifies the Degree to which the Cognizer is altered by the Intoxicant.
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluation: Evaluation indicates the Cognizer's reaction to the intoxicatoin.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the intoxication holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Cognizer is intoxicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Cognizer becomes intoxicated.

annotations.definition: The Invader enters a Land in an aggressive attempt to cripple or dominate its people and its government.

frameElementDefinitions.Land: The political area that is being crippled by the Invader's entry.
frameElementDefinitions.Weapon: An entity used by the Invader to assist in the invasion of the Land.
frameElementDefinitions.Invader: The army or other self-directed entity that is entering the Land in a hostile manner.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Invader that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Invader that accomplishes the assault.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the location where the Invader invades the Land.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Invader attacks the Land.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Invader is responding to that leads them to make an assault on the Land.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Invader invades Land.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The starting-point of the motion of the Invader or projectiles of the Invader as they move toward the the Place that the Land is occupying in the attack. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describing the state of the Invader or Land while the Invasion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the invasion on the Land by the Invader.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held. Form: once, twice, Cardinal expression + /it times, /it lots/a lot
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole and describes and describes the event more generally.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the Invader travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Invader invades the Land.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates the subtype of invasion into the Land.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes how intense the invation of the Land is.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For how long the Land is invaded by the Invader.

annotations.definition: An Invader enters a land in an attempt to dominate the Attacked and they either fail or succeed.

frameElementDefinitions.Invader: The Invader is the entity that overtakes the Attacked.
frameElementDefinitions.Attacked: The Attacked is the land, government, and its people that is seized by the Invader.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the intensity and extent of the invasion event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Invader takes over the Attacked.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action by which the conquering event is made possible through the efforts of the Invader.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Invader takes over the Attacked.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Invader seizes the Attacked.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason why the Invader seized the Attacked.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The tool used by the Invader to seize the Attacked.

annotations.definition: A Combatant is declared or loyal to a Side in a military Conflict. The Combatant is not part of any regular or (highly) organized fighting organization (such as an army), and is not necessarily a fighter by vocation.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Combatant was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Combatant: The Combatant is the human being involved in combat.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Combatant which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Combatant.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Combatant belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Combatant is associated.
frameElementDefinitions.Side_1: The side of the conflict for which the Combatant fights.
frameElementDefinitions.Side_2: A (or the) side of the conflict to which the Combatant is opposed.
frameElementDefinitions.Conflict: The Conflict in which the Combatants are fighting.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The desired result of the outcome of the hostile encounter for Side_1. It may either directly refer to the state of affairs that is desired, or the state of affairs may be implicit in an (abstract or concrete) entity that they desire.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The larger military group of which the Combatant is a part.

annotations.definition: A Place is located far off from centers of population, culture, and/or trade.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: The town or area that is in a remote location.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place with respect to which the Place is located.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer makes a judgment about an Evaluee. The judgment may be positive (e.g. respect) or negative (e.g. condemn), and this information is recorded in the semantic types Positive and Negative on the Lexical Units of this frame. There may be a specific Reason for the Cognizer's judgment, or there may be a capacity or Role in which the Evaluee is judged.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer makes the judgment. This role is typically expressed as the External Argument (or in a by-PP in passives).
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: Evaluee is the person or thing about whom/which a judgment is made. With verbs this FE is typically expressed as Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Reason: Typically, there is a constituent expressing the Reason for the Judge's judgment. It is usually a for-PP, e.g.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is used for the capacity in which the Evaluee is judged, and is expressed in as-PPs.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: Expressor is the body part or action by a body part that conveys the judgment made by the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the judging occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies any expression describing the state of a participant in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner of judging.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Cognizer judges the Evaluee.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic identifies the entity to which the proposition or propositions conveyed relate.
frameElementDefinitions.Extent_of_acclaim: The Extent_of_acclaim is a description of the number or locales of the Cognizers.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the judgement event.

annotations.definition: A Communicator communicates a judgment of an Evaluee to an Addressee. The judgment may be positive (e.g. praise) or negative (e.g. criticize), and this is indicated by the semantic types Positive and Negative attached to the lexical units. This frame does not contain words where Evaluee and the Addressee are necessarily the same .

frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee hears the judgement from the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The Communicator uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey his or her evaluation of the Evaluee to another person.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium identifies the physical or abstract setting in which the evaluation is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic identifies the entity to which the proposition or propositions conveyed relate.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Communicator judges the Evaluee to be good or bad.
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: A judgment is made about the Evaluee With verbs this FE is typically expressed as Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: Expressor is the body part or action by a body part that conveys the judgment made by the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the judgmental communication occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a judgmental communication is accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Reason: Typically, there is a constituent expressing the Reason for the Communicator's judgment. It is usually a for-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is used for the capacity in which the Evaluee is judged, and is expressed in as-PPs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies any expression describing the state of a participant in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Grounds: The Grounds is the standard by which the Evaluee is judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Extent_of_acclaim: The Extent_of_acclaim provides a description of the number or whereabouts of the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the communication of the judgment takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the communication of the judgment takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or physical state of the Communicator that leads to their communication of a judgment.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE describes the frequency with which the specified communication occurs

annotations.definition: In this frame, a Communicator judges the Addressee and then communicates that appraisal directly to the Addressee. The judgment is given for a particular Reason or about a particular Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The Communicator communicates a judgment to the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is jduged by the Communicator and receives the message of approval or disapproval.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject on which the Addressee is being judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Reason: The Reason is why the Communicator forms the type of judgment they form.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium identifies the physical or abstract entity in which the judgment is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the severity of the judgment.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes the state of the Communicator or Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Grounds: The Grounds describes the basis for the judgment.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the judgment is communicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is the method employed to communicate the judgment.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Communicator admonishes the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point in time at which the Communicator berates or compliments the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The laudable or reprimandable status held by the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: An action or entity under the control of the Communicator that expresses the criticism or praise directed towards the Addressee.

annotations.definition: A speaker characterizes the intensity of an Attribute of an Entity by a Degree.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which a particular gradable Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the intensity of the Attribute from the norm or from the value for another Entity. This FE is incorporated for each LU in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular Value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity has a particular Value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity is has the Attribute to a given Degree.

annotations.definition: A Source_of_authority, typically a government, relies upon a set of institutions specialized for the administration of justice. These Judicial_body s hear cases that originate within their Jurisdiction and relate to the Task given to them by the Source_of_authority. Often, a Judicial_body is part of a larger system within which its Rank can be specified. The Judicial_body is typically located in a specific location, the Seat.

frameElementDefinitions.Judicial_body: A permanent organization with the socially important purpose of administering justice.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The concern of the Judicial_body-- broadly speaking, the aspect of society that they affect.
frameElementDefinitions.Task: The responsibility that a Judicial_body has, either defined in terms of the type of cases that they can hear, or more specifically by reference to an action that the authority constituting them wishes the Judicial_body to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: The degree of authority invested in a Judicial_body relative to other courts in the same system.
frameElementDefinitions.Seat: The location (typically a city) in which the Judicial_body hears cases.,
frameElementDefinitions.Jurisdiction: The geographical area within which the Judicial_body has authority and responsibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_of_authority: The political entity under whose laws the Judicial_body is constituted and whose laws are applied.
frameElementDefinitions.Judges: The individuals that make up the Judicial_body.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Judicial_body is located.

annotations.definition: The Jury discuss the Case and its Charges in order to evaluate the Possible_sentence of the accused.

frameElementDefinitions.Jury: The group that debates the case.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the deliberation is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: The legal label for the crime that the defendant is accused of.
frameElementDefinitions.Case: The Case which a trial is convened to decide.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the deliberation takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the deliberation takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Possible_sentence: A sentence that the jury considers during deliberation.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer, Expressor, or State can be described as having a surprised emotion as evoked by a Stimulus or concerning a Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part, gesture, or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer, Expressor, or State can be described as having said emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type describes the kind of response the Stimulus or Topic invoke in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used to transmit the experience invoked by the Stimulus.

annotations.definition: An Agent has done or omitted to do an Act or is responsible for the obtaining of a State_of_affairs, which a Judge deems to constitute a violation of an obligation or a moral guideline. The Agent gives a Reason why the Act or omission or State_of_affairs was licit despite appearances. NOTE: There is a consistent metaphorical conception of justifying as defending. Words in the defending frame can also be used in the justifying frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person or organization that is held responsible for a prior Act or for a State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The set of facts or circumstances that the Agent offers in explanation of their earlier Act or of the State_of_affairs
frameElementDefinitions.Act: An action for which the Agent is held responsible, frequently one that the Agent has taken themself.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: A State_of_affairs that the Agent is held responsible for.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The person or institution that holds the Agent responsible for an Act or State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action, typically an act of speaking, which the Agent performs in order to justify an Act or State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE denotes the number of times the Act or State_of_affairs are justified.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The general spectrum of reasoning that the Agent uses to explain the Act or State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time of the justifying event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the justification was adequate.

annotations.definition: This frame is for objects, physical or otherwise, which are used to grant access to some location or function.

frameElementDefinitions.Key: This FE identifies the object that is designed to grant access to a location or function. This is the target LU, and this frame element is incorporated into each of this frame's LUs.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: The function that the Key grants access to.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Key into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which an Key comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Key.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Key, often denoting the kind of device that the key operates on.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Key, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The location that the Key grants access to.
frameElementDefinitions.Object: The artifact that the key operates on.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe situations in which a Perpetrator carries off and holds the Victim against his or her will by force.

frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: The Perpetrator is the person (or other agent) who carries off and holds the Victim against his or her will.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the person who is carried off and held against his/her will.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the kidnapping takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the kidnapping occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is the initial location of the Victim before it changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is the place in which the Victim ends up.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which the kidnapping takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Perpetrator that helps them accomplish the kidnapping.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is what the Perpetrator hopes to accomplish by kidnapping.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation expresses why the Perpetrator commits the kidnapping.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The abduction of a Victim by a Perpetrator.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: An additional abductee taken along with the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time for which the Victim is held by the Perpetrator
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that the Victim is kidnapped.

annotations.definition: A Killer or Cause causes the death of the Victim.

frameElementDefinitions.Killer: The person or sentient entity that causes the death of the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The living entity that dies as a result of the killing.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An inanimate entity or process that causes the death of the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the Killer is trying to bring about by killing.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The method or action that the Killer or Cause performs resulting in the death of the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The preexisting state of affairs that the Killer is responding to.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the death took place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point or period of time within which the Victim is killed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Killer or Victim during the killing.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Victim that results from being killed. (Normally this is somewhat redundant.)
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The device used by the Killer to bring about the death of the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of a Killer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree appears with adjectives to describe the killing potential of an object or with nouns to describe how widespread the killing is.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This extra-thematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that denote Kinship relations. In annotating the directional uses of these words, we use the standard terms from anthropology, Ego and Alter.

frameElementDefinitions.Alter: The person who fills the role named by the Kinship term with respect to the Ego.
frameElementDefinitions.Ego: The person from whose perspective the Kinship relationship is defined.
frameElementDefinitions.Relatives: The combination of Alter and Ego together.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: An expression of the closeness or distance of the relation. We do not go into full anthropological detail in measuring the distance of the relation, but simply annotate all such expressions as Degree
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describe some characteristic of the Alter or the Ego.

annotations.definition: An Agent creates a Knot (possibly in a Place and/or with an Instrument).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which a Knot is produced..
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed. John flattered her to receive a raise.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent by which the creation of the Knot is accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Knot: The stable configuration of a String which results from tying.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies a depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action. We wandered around naked. Cicadas screech unseen from within.

annotations.definition: An Agent manipulates a long thin object (the Rope and creates a Knot.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the sentient being who manipulates the Rope.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the Rope is manipulated.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Rope: The basically linear object which is manipulated by the Agent to produce a Knot.
frameElementDefinitions.Knot: The stable configuration of a Rope which results from tying.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: Where the manipulation of the Rope takes place.

annotations.definition: A Speaker uses as a Label to refer to an Entity. The Label is not a conventional name for the Entity but a description categorizing the Entity and thereby expressing a judgment.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the person who conventionally uses a linguistic sign to refer to an Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The concrete or abstract thing with which the Speaker has associated a particular linguistic sign.
frameElementDefinitions.Label: The linguistic sign that the Speaker uses to designate the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A time period in which a Speaker uses the Label.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A physical space whose occupants use the Label to refer to the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: A text whose author applies a particular Label to an Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: Duration_of_final_state denotes the length of time from the beginning of a state resulting from the activity denoted by the target until the state no longer holds.

annotations.definition: An Agent or a Cause mentally or physically expends energy rendering an Input, whether implicit or explicit, into a Output.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: A sentient entity whose physical and mental faculties are directly expended on the Input to create the Output.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: A non-sentient entity whose physical faculties are directly expended on the Input to create the Output.
frameElementDefinitions.Input: The mental or physical faculties onto which the Agent or Cause expends energy.
frameElementDefinitions.Output: The end product, either a concept or physical object onto which the Agent or Cause has exerted its mental or physical faculties from the rudimentary state of Input.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A word or phrase that characterizes the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Output was committed into reality.
