frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Output was birthed.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The entity for whom the Output is created.
frameElementDefinitions.Presentation: The place where the target or Output is presented.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A Descriptor is a chracterization or description of the Output that is not covered by any more-specific FE.

annotations.definition: An Agent acts to begin the life of a Process.

frameElementDefinitions.Process: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate the process that the Agent initiates.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Process is initiated.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is the way in which the Agent initiates the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE is for the Time when the Agent initiates the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical or abstract setting in which the Agent initiates the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the actor responsible for starting the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: To what end the Agent initiates the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: This FE identifies the first Co-timed_event of the started Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Agent initiates the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the target operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why or the way in which the Agent initiates the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world at the time when the Agent produces the Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: A consecutive event to the starting of the Process of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the Depictive phrase describing the actor or undergoer of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The party for who's benefit the Process is launched.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Process is launched.

annotations.definition: A Law regulates activities or states of affairs within a Jurisdiction, dictating what Required states should be the case and what Forbidden states should not. Often it also indicates negative consequences for individuals that violate it, and these negative consequences are generally enforced by some official authority. They may or may not be created by some official legislative body.

frameElementDefinitions.Law: This FE identifies the rule designed to guide activity, deprecating some types of activity and/or requiring other types of activity. This FE is incorporated into each LU in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which a law is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Law into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which a Law comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to a Law.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Law.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: Any indication of what makes up the Law.
frameElementDefinitions.Forbidden: The state-of-affairs which is disallowed by the Law.
frameElementDefinitions.Required: A state-of-affairs which the Law declares must be maintained.
frameElementDefinitions.Jurisdiction: The location where a given Law is in force.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description of the Law not covered by a more specific frame element.

annotations.definition: A law enforcement Agency is tasked by a government with guaranteeing compliance with the laws in a particular Jurisdiction.

frameElementDefinitions.Agency: The Agency is a part of the government dedicated to enforcing the laws of the government's jurisdiction.
frameElementDefinitions.Members: A person belonging to the Agency.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Agency is formed or the purpose to which it is dedicated. This FE is for expressions that indicate more specific purposes than the general one(s) indicated by the target itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_existence: The interval during which the Agency exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: A name given to the Agency for identification.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE describes the general location where the Agency operates, but does not indicate the jurisdiction of the Agency.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description of the Agency not covered by a more specific frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Jurisdiction: The Agency is charged with enforcing the laws of and within a particular Jurisdiction.

annotations.definition: These are words referring to control by a Leader over a particular entity or group (the Governed) or an Activity. The frame contains both nouns referring to a title or position (e.g. director, king, president), and verbs describing the action of leadership (e.g. rule, reign). With verbs, it is possible to mention the Role played by the Leader (often a name of a leading position, e.g., king)

frameElementDefinitions.Leader: The Leader is the one who has the role of leading. With verbs, and with predicate nominals linking an individual to a Role, the Leader is usually the External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The role played by the Leader in a given situation, often an official title; incorporated in most noun LUsin this frame. When expressed separately from the target, Role usually occurs in a PP Complement headed by as.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Leadership by any particular individual is frequently limited in time, which is noted here as Duration. Duration usually occurs in a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive refers to that which describes one of the participants in the leadership relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner refers to the style/way in which the leadership is asserted.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: Means identifies act(s) performed by the Leader through which leadership is maintained.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which leadership is asserted.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Time refers to the point in time that the leadership took place.
frameElementDefinitions.Governed: The entity whose actions or beliefs the Leader directs in the context of a joint activity or movement. This entity can be a political body, a person, or a group of people.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Leader.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Leader belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant Domain within which the Leader rules. This includes a specification of a country that the Leader serves (Iranian officials) or any other organizations that the Leader acts as part of (congressional representative; several directors from UNHCR).
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Leader directs the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The locale from which the Leader leads the Governed or the Activity.

annotations.definition: A piece of Evidence indicates that an Event occured, or metonymically, Entity had formerly existed, in a Location. The Evidence consists of physical changes to the Location that resulted from the Event. Some LUs of this frame are general (trace.n) while others specifically involve evidence of motion (trail.n, track.n, wake.n, contrail.n) or existence (vestige.n).

frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The thing that indicates an Entity was in a Location. He found traces of the burglar in the hallway.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The thing whose former existence in a Location can be deduced from the Evidence. Traces of the former inhabitants can still be found in the abandoned manor.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This frame element describes characteristics and properties of the Evidence not covered by other frame elements.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The place or context in which the Evidence of an is discovered, showing that an Event occured there or an Entity existed there.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: An event whose details are revealed through the discovery of Evidence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A description of the time that an Event occurs or an Entity exists at a place.
frameElementDefinitions.Reliability: Any description of how clearly the Evidence indicates the former Event.

annotations.definition: A Process of using or modifying is scheduled to be applied to a group or mass. At a certain Time, the Remainder that is still left to be processed can be identified. The Agent who is in line to work on the Remainder is usually deprofiled but may sometimes be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Process: The activity that is being applied to the mass or the set of entities.
frameElementDefinitions.Remainder: The mass or group that has not yet been processed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Remainder is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individuals who are scheduled to apply the Process to the Remainder. The Agent may be the same or a different individual who applied the process to the part that has already undergone the Process.

annotations.definition: An Authority with the power to make decisions hands down a Finding over a question presented in a formal or informal Case.

frameElementDefinitions.Authority: The Authority (which may be the jury, judge, magistrate or court) decides on the Finding.
frameElementDefinitions.Finding: This FE identifies the outcome of the Authority's deliberation or consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Case: This FE describes the question on which the Authority gives a Finding.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Authority gives his Finding.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Finding takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Legal_basis: This FE indicates the basis in law of a Finding.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the ruling was reached.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Authority acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose why the Authority returns a particular Finding.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies an action that the Authority performs in order to return the verdict.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for the Finding.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the ruling event operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that a Finding holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Defendant: The person about whom the Authority passes a judgment or Finding.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame describe the status of an Action with respect to a Code of laws or rules. An Object may also be in violation or compliance of the Code by virtue of its existence, location or possession.

frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action is the behavior which complies with or violates the Code.
frameElementDefinitions.Code: The Code is the set of rules, laws or regulations that determine whether the Action is allowed or prohibited.
frameElementDefinitions.Object: The Object complies with or violates the Code by virtue of its existence, location or possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.

annotations.definition: The Lender gives the Theme to the Borrower with the expectation that the Borrower will return the Theme to the Lender after a Duration of time. This frame differs from the Borrowing frame in that this frame profiles the Lender in active sentences, whereas the Borrowing frame profiles the Borrower.

frameElementDefinitions.Borrower: The person or institution who receives the Theme from the Lender for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amout of time in which the Borrower has possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Lender: The person or institution who gives the Theme to the Borrower for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that is transferred from the Lender to the Borrower for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The aim of the Lender which they believe will be accomplished by lending the Theme to the Borrower.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the lending event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Lender lends the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Lender lends the Theme to the Borrower.

annotations.definition: An Exerter, Action, or Force is capable of exerting or does exert a physical force at a level specified by the target. That physical force may cause deformation or breakage of, or simply motion in the receiving entity. The receiving entity is conceptually present (even if remotely), but is usually not lexically explicit. This frame covers both individual-level/static uses ("a STRONG person") as well as stage-level/dynamic uses ("a STRONG push"). However, the durative, individual-level meaning appears more frequently in the data and seems more basic.

frameElementDefinitions.Exerter: The Exerter that can or does exert a force of the level specified by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Force: The Force that can or does exert a force of the level specified by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Exerter, Action, or Force can or does exert its force.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Exerter, Action, or Force can or does exert a force.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action that can or does exert a force of the level specified by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Exerter, Action, or Force can or does exert a force.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Exerter, Action, or Force can or does exert its force.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes the force exertion of the Exerter, Action, or Force.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain is the field in which the Exerter, Action, or Force can or does exert its force.

annotations.definition: A Resisting_entity is capable of resisting or does resist a physical Opposing_force to a level specified by the target. With certain LUs (e.g. impregnable.a), the Opposing_force FE is incorporated, and with many LUs, it is mostly null instantiated. This frame covers both individual-level/static uses ("the DURABILITY of jade") as well as stage-level/dynamic uses ("the shoulder is PLIABLE after stretching"), although the individual-level meaning is the more basic and is much more prominent in the data.

frameElementDefinitions.Resisting_entity: The Resisting_entity is the item that resists the Opposing_force to the level specified by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Resisting_entity resists.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Resisting_entity resists an external force.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Resisting_entity resists an external force.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Resisting_entity resists an external force.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes the force resistance of the Resisting_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain is the field in which the Resisting_entity resists an external force.
frameElementDefinitions.Opposing_force: The Opposing_force is the item placed in opposition to the Resisting_entity. Opposing_force hinders, blocks, helps, or allows (lets) the Resisting_entity to resist at the level specified by the target. Sometimes this Core FE is incorporated in the target; otherwise, it is often null instantiated in example sentences.

annotations.definition: There is a gradable amount of light in a particular Location. In some cases the Source of light may be indicated instead of the Location.

frameElementDefinitions.Location: The place whose amout of light is being described. The Location can also be a location in time, as seen in the second example below:
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The entity from which the light eminates.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree of the amount of light.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the level of light not covered by a more specific frame element.

annotations.definition: An Emitter emits a Beam of light from a Source, along a Path, and/or towards a Goal. Essentially, this frame denotes the (semi-)fictive motion of the the Beam.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the light movement occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Emitter: This FE identifies the entity at the Source from which the Beam originates.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location the Beam occupies initially before its change of location.
frameElementDefinitions.Beam: The light that travels from a Source along a Path to a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path refers to where the Beam travels over or to a landmark the Beam travels by.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is the location the Beam ends up in.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with the likelihood of a Hypothetical_event occurring. The Hypothetical_event is its only core frame element.

frameElementDefinitions.Hypothetical_event: The state of affairs or occurrence whose likelihood is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE expresses a reason the speaker has for making judgment about the likelihood of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Hypothetical_event is likely.

annotations.definition: An Entity that would be expected to have a characteristic to a certain degree (typically based on comparison to other entities of the same type) instead has a more limited degree of that characteristic.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity would normally be expected to have a set of possibilites, but instead exhibits only certain possibilites, or exhibits them on fewer situations than expected.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Entity is limited.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Cause limits a Range_of_options to having a certain Characteristic. The Characteristic may be that only certain types of the Range_of_options may be acceptable, or (in the case of a plural or mass Range_of_options) that only certain cardinalities or quanties are acceptable. Alternatively, the Degree to which the Range_of_options is limited may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent consciously places limits upon the Range_of_options.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is a natural occurrence or state-of-affairs that limits the Range_of_options.
frameElementDefinitions.Range_of_options: The Range_of_options would normally be expected to have a set of possibilites, but is limited by an Agent or Cause to particular subtypes, situations or characteristics.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Range_of_options has been limited.
frameElementDefinitions.Characteristic: The Characteristic indicates the subtypes or situations to which the Range_of_options is limited.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the limiting takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which the Range_of_options is limited.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Agent of Cause limits the Range_of_options.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Agent or Cause limits the Range_of_options.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Agent or Cause that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent or Cause limits the Range_of_options.

annotations.definition: A linguistic Form has a particular Meaning, possibly restricted to a particular Context. Some linguistic Forms, nouns and nominal expressions, also can refer to an object in the real or an imagined world, a Referent.

frameElementDefinitions.Form: Form is the formal pole of a linguistic sign.
frameElementDefinitions.Meaning: Meaning is the sense of a linguistic Form.
frameElementDefinitions.Textual_location: The FE Textual_location is used for expressions that indicate under which semantic, collocational, pragmatic or other circumstances a Form has a particular Meaning.
frameElementDefinitions.Referent: Referent identifies an object in the real (or a hypothetical) world that a linguistic expression is used to talk about.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute of the Form and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: A Location is characterized by a high amount of (often goal-oriented) Activity, or metonymically, with Individuals who are engaged in the activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Location: The Location where a lot of activity is taking place, or prototypically takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation in amount of activity from the norm or from the amount for another Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Location is characterized by a lot of activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Location has some degree of activity or liveliness.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The larger or general area where the Location is lively.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity that is conceptually filling the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: The Individuals are engaged in the Activity, creating a lively Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Individuals or Location is engaging in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the state of affairs expressed by the target, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer must cope with some Condition for a prolonged period, usually against his or her will.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: A person who experiences the effects of some situation or Condition for a long period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: This FE describes the situation that the Experiencer must cope with over a long period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Experiencer is located when he or she faces the Condition.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A general description of the temporal location of the Experiencer while coping with the Condition.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Experiencer must cope with the Condition.

annotations.definition: This frame contains general locale words, and is mostly a place-holder in the hierarchy. Words in this frame designate relatively stable bounded ares of the world which have permanent Relative_locations with respect to other locales, and thus are useful as permanent landmarks. It is inherited by Locale_by_use, which has to do with places that are named according to their intended use, or some habitual use, such as factory.n, dance floor.n, village.n, field.n. It is also inherited by Locale_by_event, which covers places that are names according to some event which took place there, such as concerts, battles, and meteor impacts.

frameElementDefinitions.Locale: A stable bounded area. It is typically the designation of the nouns of Locale-derived frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: Salient parts that make up a Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Locale is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Indicates the action (or causer) which brings the features of the Locale about. This FE should also include words that indicate that there was no salient formation.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Locale is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: ULTIMATELY TO BE REPLACED BY MULTIWORD TARGETS (in almost all cases). This FE is used for the Names of Locales.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Related_event: An event related to the locale in some way other than (1) Formational_cause (which is a separate FE) or an intended use (which would place the instance in Locale_by_use), or (3) a defining event (which would place the instance in Locale_by_event).
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The activity that is carried out in the Locale.

annotations.definition: A Locale is characterized by the type of entity associated with the Locale. Some words specifically denote regions characterized by the individuals who typically live or work there. Typcial such Characteristics include ethnicity, national origin, language, and other shared cultural traits.

frameElementDefinitions.Locale: A stable bounded area characterized by properties of the entities who are typically located there (e.g., work and/or live there).
frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: Salient parts that make up a Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Locale is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Indicates the action (or causer) which brings the features of the Locale about. This FE should also include words that indicate that there was no salient formation.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The larger area within which the Locale is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Related_event: An event related to the locale in some way other than (1) Formational_cause (which is a separate FE) or an intended use (which would place the instance in Locale_by_use), or (3) a defining event (which would place the instance in Locale_by_event).
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The activity that is carried out in the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Characteristic: A property or characteristic of the entities in the Locale.

annotations.definition: A Profiled_region is identified as being the region where an Entity is located.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The region where the Entity is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: Salient parts that make up a Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Profiled_region is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: This FE indicates the action (or causer) which brings the features of the Profiled_region about. This FE should also include words that indicate that there was no salient formation.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Profiled_region is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Related_event: An event related to the Profiled_region in some way other than (1) Formational_cause (which is a separate FE) or an intended use (which would place the instance in Locale_by_use), or (3) a defining event (which would place the instance in Locale_by_event).
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The activity that is carried out in the Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The entity is located at the Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Entity is at the Profiled_region.

annotations.definition: A place defined in terms of an event which occurred (or will occur) there.

frameElementDefinitions.Locale: A stable bounded area. It is typically the denotation of the nouns in Locale_by_event, which will be annotated on the target word.
frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: Salient parts that make up a Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Locale is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Defining_event: The event for which the locale is known or is named
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The name of the locale.

annotations.definition: A place defined by an Owner.

frameElementDefinitions.Locale: A stable bounded area. It is typically the designation of the nouns of Locale-derived frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Locale is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Owner: The individual or group of individuals who hold posses the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: Salient parts that make up a Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any feature or characteristic of the Locale not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Locale is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The name of the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The activity that is carried out in the Locale.

annotations.definition: Geography as defined by its use.

frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: FN: Salient parts that make up a Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Locale is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Indicates the action (or causer) which brings the features of the Locale about.
frameElementDefinitions.Locale: A stable bounded area. It is typically the designation of the nouns of Locale-derived frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: ULTIMATELY TO BE REPLACED BY MULTIWORD TARGETS (in almost all cases). This FE is used for the Names of Locales.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Locale is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The activity that is carried out in the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any feature or characteristic of the Locale not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent Locale itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: A building or location that can be sealed to prevent entrance, the Locale, ceases to be open for use.

frameElementDefinitions.Locale: The Locale is the entity that ceases to be available for use.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for the closure, if any.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The Containing_event describes some event in which the closure occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A Depictive describes something about the Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time of the closure.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes the way in which the Locale closed.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances that caused closure. Compared to Explanation, this describes situations or environments that lead to failure, not a specific reason for failure.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place of the failing locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Locale remains closed.

annotations.definition: A Perceiver determines the Location of a Sought_entity within a Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The individual that determines the Location of the Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Sought_entity: The entity that the Perceiver succeeds in finding.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The position of the Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The general set of entities that the Perceiver examined in order to find the Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Sought_entity is located.

annotations.definition: The word(s) in this frame detail a particular moment in time.These often occur with demonstratives such as this and that.

frameElementDefinitions.Relative_time: Relative_time denotes words that locate the time with respect to an identifiable reference point.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: Name denotes the name of the particular point in time.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole describes the larger unit of which the target word describes one part. If there is an appositive that is necessarily larger, it's the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_time: The particular, salient point in time. If a specified time is present, it is the focal time. Otherwise, the unit is the focal time. If there is an appositive that specifies an entity that it not necessarily larger than the target, both the unit and the specification are the FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_event: Salient_event is an event that is associated with the given point in time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A Descriptor is any characterization or description of the point in time that is not covered by a more specific FE.

annotations.definition: A perceptible Figure shows up against a Ground location due to Light shining on/from it, its salience possibly dependent on a Cause_of_shine.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Light is emitted, usually indicating the brightness.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of a focal participant during the emission of Light .
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of Light emission.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The percept which is perceived at a certain Ground location.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The location of the phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the emission of the Light .
frameElementDefinitions.Cause_of_shine: This is the cause of the shininess of the phenomenon, generally introduced by 'with'.
frameElementDefinitions.Light: The Light which is emitted. In this frame, it is usually incorporated in verbs and adjectives, and is the denotation of many of the nouns.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period for which the Figure shows up against the Ground due to Light falling on it.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The spatial setting in which the Figure shows up against a Ground location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Figure is observed against the Ground location due to Light shining on it.

annotations.definition: A Figure (fictively or literally) occupies a position within a Profiled_region related to a Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure somewhere in the midst of their motion or during the entirety of the motion event.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as in motion relative to a certain Ground location.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance traveled by the Figure after the point where they were in the Profiled_region of the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction traveled by the Figure. This FE is filled by one of a closed class of adverbs: up, down, out (meaning 'beyond the boundary around the viewpoint'), off (meaning 'disconnect between landmark and figure'), back (meaning 'along a line from front to back of landmark, and relatively inaccessible'), and, more rarely, away, along, and dialectally, back down (meaning 'down and behind').
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the locative relation between Figure and Ground obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Figures: The Figures are items conceived of as moving with respect to each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: An indication of how accessible the Figure is to some participant in the speech context (either the speaker or the addressee, or some other highly salient viewpoint in a narrative). This FE normally describes availability for manipulation and use, but may simply describe how easily seen or difficult to see the Figure is. There is a closed class of items that can fill this slot, including only 'just' and 'right' indicating ease of access, and 'way', 'all the way', or (dialectally) 'clear' indicating distance and difficulty of access.
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'. Some general adverbs that indicate exact and non-exact match to an idea can also express this FE:
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Ground interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class of adverbials back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Figure before) and on.adv (which indicates a continued path). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, in which case it either refers to fictive, imagined motion (as in the first example below), or it backgrounds motion entirely (as in the second example).
frameElementDefinitions.Region_quantification: A description of how much of a potential profiled region actually has (or is expected to have) a Figure in it. It is normally filled by closed-class items like 'all', 'somewhere', 'nowhere', etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The region within which a Figure might be found. This FE is exclusive of Figure, and is a reconstrual that is not possible with verb lexical units.

annotations.definition: A Figure is located relative to a Ground location.

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as located relative to a certain Ground location. The figure can be an entity or an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance between the Figure and the Ground. This FE requires an image schema with a vector that extends from the Ground, or a subpart of the Ground, in some direction, and the Distance is measured along this vector from the Ground to the profiled region.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure. This FE is filled by one of a closed class of adverbs: up, down, out (meaning 'beyond the boundary around the viewpoint'), off (meaning 'disconnect between landmark and figure'), back (meaning 'along a line from front to back of landmark, and relatively inaccessible'), and, more rarely, away, along, and dialectally, back down (meaning 'down and behind').
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the locative relation between Figure and Ground obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Figures: The Figures are items which mutually serve to identify the location of the other items.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: An indication of how accessible the Figure is to some participant in the speech context (either the speaker or the addressee, or some other highly salient viewpoint in a narrative). This FE normally describes availability for manipulation and use, but may simply describe how easily seen or difficult to see the Figure is. There is a closed class of items that can fill this slot, including only 'just' and 'right' indicating ease of access, and 'way', 'all the way', or (dialectally) 'clear' indicating distance and difficulty of access.
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'.
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Ground interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class of adverbials back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Figure before) and on.adv (which indicates a continued path). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, in which case it either refers to fictive, imagined motion (as in the first example below), or it backgrounds motion entirely (as in the second example).
frameElementDefinitions.Region_quantification: A description of how much of a potential profiled region actually has (or is expected to have) a Figure in it. It is normally filled by closed-class items like 'all', 'somewhere', 'nowhere', etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The region within which a Figure might be found. This FE is exclusive of Figure, and is a reconstrual that is not possible with verb lexical units.

annotations.definition: A Figure is located with respect to a Ground, whose location is (generally) better known. This is accomplished by identifying the Figure as within a Profiled_region--a shape defined by an Image Schema, located at, on, or in the Ground, and scaled by context. Alternatively, a group of Figures is described within a symmetrical Profiled_region located simultaneously with respect to each of the Figures.

frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The entity that is located with respect to the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The entity with respect to which the Figure is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Figures: The entities that are located with respect to each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The area with respect to the Ground or Figures in which the Figure or Figures reside.

annotations.definition: A Host provides a Residence for a Lodger to temporarily stay.

frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host is the individual (or group of individuals) that own or control the Residence and allow the Lodger to temporarily stay there.
frameElementDefinitions.Lodger: The Lodger is the individual who is given permission to lodge in the Residence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Host provides a Residence to the Lodger
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the Host provides a temporary Residence.
frameElementDefinitions.Residence: The location controlled by the Host which the Lodger stays at temporarily.

annotations.definition: Subframe of Transfer in which the Donor begins by possessing a Theme and finishes by not possessing the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the Relinquishing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation indicates what the Donor is trying to accomplish by the Relinquishing.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time indicates when the Relinquishing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person that begins in possession of the Theme and causes it to be in the possession of the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that changes ownership due to the Relinquishing.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose indicates the purpose for which the intentional Relinquishing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The Recipient is the entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.

annotations.definition: This is the Frame which binds together the initial state of the Donor possessing the Theme, the transfer, and the final state of the Donor not possessing the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The entity that began by owning the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The thing which was originally owned by the Donor. (This does not include bodyparts or medical conditions, but does include intellectual property, etc.)

annotations.definition: This frame describes a situation in which an Owner loses his or her Possession. The Owner may have failed to keep ownership of their Possession (i.e. robbery or gambling) or may have unintentionally misplaced their Possession.

frameElementDefinitions.Owner: The original Owner of the Possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Possession: The object that is possessed by the Owner.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Possession is lost.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Possession is lost.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE describes how the Owner loses the Possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the event of losing a Possession logically follows.

annotations.definition: An Protagonist goes from a default state of mental and emotional equilibrium into a temporary state of disequilibrium, often a state of anger. A Reason for the change of state may be given. Likewise, Actions that the Experiencer takes as a result of their new emotional state may be expressed. Both Reasons and Actions may be introduced just as coordinated clauses. The lexical units also are frequently accompanied by expressions of an Affected_party. This frame is populated by many lexical units that are based on the emotion-heat/pressure metaphor. Note also that many phrases with equivalent meaning to targets of this frame are compositional, e.g. 'go insane', which we treat as the LU insane.a (of the Mental_property frame) plus the inchoative support go.v.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: This is the person (or people) whose mental state changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: Something done by the Protagonist as a result of losing mental equilibrium.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree marks the extent to which the Protagonist's mental state changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, especially general descriptions comparing events.
frameElementDefinitions.Affected_party: A person (or persons) who is saliently affected by the Protagonist 's Actions.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The cause for the Protagonist to go crazy.

annotations.definition: This frame describes a Sufferer who experiences the loss of someone through death. The LUs in this frame imply a strong emotional connection between the Sufferer and the Deceased.

frameElementDefinitions.Sufferer: The being or entity that emotionally experiences the death of the Deceased.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Deceased passed away.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Deceased passed away.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE describes how the Deceased passed away.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Timespan: The period over which a loss has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: How long the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the loss occurs.

annotations.definition: This non-lexical frame describes a scenario in which a Perceiver fails to keep sight of a Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The Perceiver is the entity who fails to keep the Theme in sight.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that becomes unperceivable.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Perceiver loses perception of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Perceiver loses perception of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the loss of perception logically follows.

annotations.definition: This frames describes a Theme who evades the sight of a Perceiver. The focus of this frame is the Theme, which appears in subject position. This frame contrasts with the opposing perspective in Losing_track_of_theme, where the Perceiver is the subject and the Theme is the object.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that becomes unperceivable.
frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The Perceiver is the entity who fails to keep the Theme in sight.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Perceiver loses perception of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Perceiver loses perception of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the loss of perception logically follows.

annotations.definition: This frames describes a Perceiver who fails to keep sight of a Theme. The focus of this frame is the Perceiver, which appears in subject position. This frame contrasts with the opposing perspective in Losing_track_of_perceiver, where the Perceiver is the object and the Theme is the subject.

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The Perceiver is the entity who fails to keep the Theme in sight.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that becomes unperceivable.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Perceiver loses perception of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Perceiver loses perception of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the loss of perception logically follows.

annotations.definition: A State_of_affairs is evaluated as good (or bad), against a background assumption that previously, the State_of_affairs was thought to be unlikely to occur. The State_of_affairs holds for a particular Protagonist.

frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The State of affairs that is evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist is the one for whom the destiny is evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of the sort of State_of_affairs. This occurs with nominal LUs which are both the target and the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The Role filled by the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time for which the Protagonist's destiny is good.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the State_of_affairs is lucky (either a degree of goodness or a degree of unexpectedness, or both).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Protagonist has luck.

annotations.definition: Individuals first encounter each other and make introductions at a particular Time, Place, or Context. The Individuals may have known of each other before making introductions. The Individuals may be separately construed as Individual_1 and Individual_2. In some cases, there is a Explanation of the circumstances which lead to the Individuals' meeting.

frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Individuals make each other's acquaintance.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Individuals make each others acquaintance.
frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: The persons (or in rare cases animals) that make each other's acquaintance.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: The Event, frequently a social occasion, during which the Individuals make each other's acquaintance.
frameElementDefinitions.Individual_1: The semantically more prominent one of the two parties when the Individuals are realized separately.
frameElementDefinitions.Individual_2: The semantically less prominent of the two parties when the Individuals are expressed separately.
frameElementDefinitions.Intermediary: A person or social group that connects both of the Individuals, with a group member typically introducing the Individuals to each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state of affairs that leads to the Individuals' making each other's acquaintance.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: Two (or more) people (the Parties, also encodable as Party_1 and Party_2) negotiate an agreement. Both sides are construed as making a commitment to assume an Obligation--the process is understood to be symmetrical or reciprocal. Instead of a specific mention of the Obligation, a Topic expression may be used to indicate the domain covered.

frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: The individual or individuals that form the more prominent (i.e. agentive) party of the agreement, as compared with Party_2. That is, Party_1 is generally encoded as the External Argument of verbs, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to Party_1. Thus Manner is construed to be that of Party_1, not necessarily that of Party_2 or the group of Parties as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: The semantically (and grammatically) less prominent participant (or group of participants) in the agreement. It is generally encoded within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by with).
frameElementDefinitions.Obligation: An expression of the commitment made by the Parties .
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is a description of the domain covered.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: As with other frames in the Communication domain, the Medium of communication may be expressed. Medium is the physical entity or channel used to record or negotiate the Obligation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the act of making an agreement which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of either Party_1 or Parties that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Parties: The group of individuals portrayed as equally involved in making an agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agreement occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time where the Agreement occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes a characteristic of the Obligation.

annotations.definition: Using Evidence, which may or may not be expressed, a Cognizer recognizes or proves that Concept1 is associated causally or collocationally with another concept, Concept2. These may be expressed collectively as associated Concepts.

frameElementDefinitions.Concept_1: Concept1 is the phenomenon which is believed to be related to Concept2.
frameElementDefinitions.Concept_2: The phenomenon which is believed to be linked to Concept1.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The phenomenon which proves the causal connection.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The sentient entity that recognizes the causal connection.
frameElementDefinitions.Concepts: The phenomena which are believed to be related.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE describes how often the connection is made
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Cognizer make the connection.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Cognizer makes the connection.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: Two (or more) people (the Parties, also encodable as Party_1 and Party_2) come to an agreement committing them to carrying out an Obligation. The agreement is construed as non-ideal for both parties but necessary to end a dispute. The process is understood to be symmetrical or reciprocal. Instead of a specific mention of the Obligation, a Topic expression may be used to indicate the domain covered.

frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: The individual or individuals that form the more prominent (i.e., agentive) party to the compromise, as compared with Party_2. That is, Party_1 is generally encoded as the External Argument of verbs, and peripheral FEs that do not make reference to the event itself generally make reference to Party_1. Thus Manner is construed to be that of Party_1, not necessarily that of Party_2 or the group of Parties as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: The semantically (and grammatically) less prominent participant (or group of participants) in the compromise. It is generally encoded within a prepositional phrase (e.g. headed by with).
frameElementDefinitions.Obligation: An expression of the commitment made by the Parties .
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is a description of the domain covered by the compromise.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: As with other frames in the Communication domain, the Medium of communication may be expressed. Medium is the physical entity or channel used to record or negotiate the Obligation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the act of making an agreement which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of either Party_1 or Parties that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Parties: The group of individuals portrayed as equally involved in reaching a compromise.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the compromise occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Place where the compromise occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes a characteristic of the Obligation, Party_1, or Parties.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Parties make the compromise.\

annotations.definition: A physical entity, construed as a point-Sound_source, emits a Sound. This includes animals and people making noise with their vocal tracts. Sometimes the sound itself is referred to with a nominal expression, in which case it is called the Sound. Manner expressions may also be relevant in this frame, if they describe properties of the sound as such. A path-schema can be overlaid on the simple noise-making scene, adding a Location_of_source and/or a Path. This frame does not cover scenarios where objects create sound by coming into forceful contact with one another or rubbing against each other. Such scenarios are covered by the Impact and Friction frames. The current frame also does not cover cases where an animate Agent or a natural Force or Cause causes a single Source or several Sources (moving or rubbing against each other) to make noise. Such scenarios are covered by the Cause_to_make_noise, Cause_impact and Cause_friction frames (the latter of which is not yet existent). Cases where the major emphasis of the portrayed scene is on the motion of a Theme, with sound emission only an accompaniment, are covered by the Motion_noise frame. Likewise, cases where the Sound itself is portrayed as moving through space are not covered in this frame but rather in Sound_movement. Finally, all nouns used to evoke Sounds have been placed in the Sounds frame. We have done this because these nouns do not denote sound emission/production events but only the Sound itself (*The siren's blare occurred right on time).

frameElementDefinitions.Sound_source: The frame element Sound_source is assigned to a constituent referring to a physical entity which emits a sound. Such an entity must have an appropriately complex internal structure such that it can create a sound. For example, a machine or some other thing with moving parts can create sound by itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for the Sound_source producing the sound. Generally, this is some external stimulus that prompts the Sound_source to make noise.
frameElementDefinitions.Sound: The frame element Sound is incorporated in the meaning of the verbs of this frame. Otherwise, this FE is assigned to NPs which actually refer to a sound in the presence of the target. This typically occurs when the target is a verb used as a modifier in its -ing form, e.g.:
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner expressions may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of a sounds as such: loudly, shrilly, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: The location of the Sound_source by which the Sound is emitted.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: State of the Sound_source during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The noise-making may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause, the mental or emotional state of an animate Sound_source. Internal_cause is expressed in a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion is the sulocation of the Sound_source from which the sound is emitting.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: Addressee identifies the intended audience of an event of making a noise as a communicative signal.
frameElementDefinitions.Noisy_event: An event in which one or more Sound_sources participate and which is accompanied by Sound.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path along which the Sound travels away from the Sound_source.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the Sound_source. This FE differs from Location_of_source only in terms of point of view; the latter FE implies a point of view that is not on the Sound_source.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: A state or location of the Sound_source which contributes to the Sound_source's making noise.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Noisy_event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times the noise-making event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: This FE indicates that the noise-making event is embedded within a series of similar events.

annotations.definition: An Agent performs a series of unspecified tasks to plan for an Event to take place at a Scheduled_time and Place.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who makes arrangements for the Event to take place at a Time and Place.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The incident for which the Agent has made arrangements.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The person for whom the Event was arranged.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frequency with which the Agent organizes the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Agent makes the arrangements for the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means used by the Agent to make arrangements for the Event to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Agent has made arrangements for the Event to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Agent's motivation for arranging the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The cause for arranging the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Scheduled_time: The time for which the Agent has arranged the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE describes the time at which the arrangements are made.

annotations.definition: An Agent makes a particular facial expression, generally in response to some Internal_cause (generally an emotional state). Sometimes the Body_part involved in the expression is mentioned in lieu of the Agent.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual that assumes a particular facial expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotion or other mental state that leads the Agent to assume a facial expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The particular part of the face that the Agent moves into a facial expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount to which the expression on the face of the Agent matches the prototypical image for the indicated facial expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_perceiver: The individual that the Agent intends to see the facial expression; often the Intended_perceiver is also the focus of the emotional Internal_cause of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point in time at which the change in facial_expression takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: The location relative to some viewer (often the Intended_perceiver) at which the Agent makes an expression.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_of_gaze: A path expression that describes a line from the Agent to the Intended_perceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognate_event: The facial expression of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Agent during the event.
frameElementDefinitions.External_cause: An event experienced by the Agent that leads them to make an expression.

annotations.definition: A Manipulator gets his Victim to perform a Resulting_action against what their better judgment would have been at the time. The means by which the Manipulator overcomes or bypasses the Victim's resistance to act need not be linguistic expression. In some cases, the Goods may stand in for a Resulting_action of the Victim giving the Manipulator the Goods.

frameElementDefinitions.Manipulator: The person who gets the Victim to act against their interest.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The person who the Manipulator gets to do something they would not have done had no pressure or deception been applied.
frameElementDefinitions.Resulting_action: What the Manipulator intends to make the Victim do and the Victim actually does.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Manipulator that enables them to alter the action of the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE goods is anything (including labor, time, or legal rights) that can be taken away.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity that the Manipulator uses to get the Victim to perform the Resulting_action.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: A state-of-affairs that the Manipulator is hoping to bring about by manipulating the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the manipulation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Manipulator manipulates the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including metaphorical force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of a Manipulator that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).

annotations.definition: An Agent manipulates a Theme so that it ends up in a particular shape or configuration denoted by a Resultant_configuration.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the volitional actor who manipulates the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the Theme is manipulated.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the manipulation of the Theme takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the manipulation of the Theme takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the manipulation of the Theme takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the manipulation of the Theme takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the manipulation of the Theme takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object which is manipulated by the Agent into a particular shape.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the manipulation of the Theme takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: Where the manipulation of the Theme takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Resultant_configuration: The shape or configuration of the Theme after being manipulated by the Agent.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe the manipulation of an Entity by an Agent. Generally, this implies that the Entity is not deeply or permanently physically affected, nor is it overall moved from one place to another.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent manipulates an object. Agent typically refers to animate beings, but is also extended to mechanical and other inanimate objects. Agent is most frequently expressed as the external argument of verbs:
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity being manipulated often occurs as the direct object of verbs:
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: The Agent's manipulation of an Entity may be further specified as being localized to some part of the Entity, a Locus. The Locus is usually expressed as a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Bodypart_of_agent: The part of the Agent's body being used to manipulate the Entity may also be expressed. While the default is for an Agent to use his/her hands, other body parts may be specified. This frame element usually occurs as a PP-complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of the manipulation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of manipulation.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the manipulation takes place
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Agent's purpose in performing the manipulation.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that interacts with the Entity to accomplish the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action by the Agent to accomplish the desired manipulation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The spatial setting in which the manipulation takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies a depictive phrase describing the Agent or Bodypart_of_agent that holds throughout the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs leading to the manipulation.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: A Manner describes general or specific characteristics of an event in one of three ways: First, it can refer to how a Comparison_event transpires, absent the influence of an entity (e.g. "the way you talk"). Second, it can refer to how a Salient_entity conducts, performs, or otherwise participates in a Comparison_activity (e.g. "her manner of speaking"). Thirdly, the Manner can describe a Foreground_event by relating the Foreground_event to a Manner_descriptor (e.g. "written (in) a silly way", "written the way I do").

frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_event: The Comparison_event is the principal event being characteriszed by the Manner. The Manner of this Comparison_event is often used in comparison with the Foreground_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: The Salient_entity that is associated with or directly responsible for the Comparison_activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The description of the Comparison_event or Comparison_activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Foreground_event: The Manner of Foreground_event is compared with the Manner of the Comparison_event (i.e. the principal event of the clause).
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_activity: The Salient_entity conducts or engages in the Comparison_activity that is being described.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer actualizes a certain pattern of behavior, a Lifestyle, which persists for a significant period of time in the Experiencer's life and is recognized as part of his character or normal routine. The Lifestyle is not necessarily realized intentionally, and may just be the result of circumstance.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The person who actualizes a pattern of behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Lifestyle: A pattern of behavior which the actions of the Experiencer matches for a significant period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Lifestyle may be characterized by a Manner, which is often expressed adverbially.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A temporal description of when the Experiencer adopts a Lifestyle.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Experiencer is carrying out the expected patterns of behavior of the Lifestyle.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The spatial location where the Experiencer carries out a Lifestyle.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The general category under which the Lifestyle can be categorized.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE covers characterizations of the Lifestyle that are not covered by other frame elements.

annotations.definition: A Producer produces a Product from a Resource for commercial purposes.

frameElementDefinitions.Producer: The person or company that produces the Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Product: The Product is produced by the Producer.
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: Resource is the material that the product is made from.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Product is manufactured.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This Fe identifies the Time when the product was produced.
frameElementDefinitions.Factory: Factory identifies the particular plant where the Product is manufactured.
frameElementDefinitions.Consumer: The Producer produces the Product for the Consumer.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A phrase that describes the state of the Producer or the Product during the manufacturing process.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which a Producer produces a Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which a Producer creates a Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Producer creates a Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Producer produces a Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE identifies the Circumstances under which the the product was produced.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of manufacturing events in a given time period.
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: The Locus is a description of the active zone of the Producer, where the manufacturing most saliently takes place.

annotations.definition: The Outcome of a contest is described in terms of the Margin between what would be required for victory and the actual Outcome.

frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome of a contest whose Margin is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Margin: The difference between the minimum required for victory and the actual Outcome.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Value from what is expected given the Outcome and the Value indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Outcome has a particular Value for the Margin.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The generally implicit Value of the Margin.

annotations.definition: A Mass_theme, generally made up of many individuals, moves from a Source to a Goal with some Path.

frameElementDefinitions.Mass_theme: The mass of entities which moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree of abundance.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The place where the overall motion event takes place, thus including Source, Path, and Goal locations.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location the Theme occupies initially before its change of location.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path refers to (a part of the) ground the Mass_theme travels over or to a landmark the Mass_theme travels by.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is the location the Mass_theme ends up in.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
