frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion. This frame element occurs throughout the motion domain but is very infrequent in Motion.Noise. The engine spluttered forward a short way and stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Mass_theme is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Mass_theme. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the motion event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Mass_theme that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully)
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The spatial setting in which the Mass_motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that is neither the Goal of the posture change nor a landmark along the way of the moving part of the body. Often Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the Mass_theme, or the orientation imposed by an implicit observer.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: In cases wherein the Mass_theme is construed as a sentient entities, this FE denotes the state of affairs that the entities wish to bring about by mass movement.

annotations.definition: A mathematical expression Expression_1 has a relationship with another mathematical expression Expression_2. This relationship may be of equality, inequality, or near-equality. The Type of the equation may be specified. Senses of LUs in this frame that are not overtly mathematical should be placed in Contigency.

frameElementDefinitions.Expression_1: In an equation that has more terms on one side than the other, Expression_1 is the side of the equation with fewer terms.
frameElementDefinitions.Expression_2: In an equation that has more terms on one side than the other, Expression_2 is the side of the equation that has more terms.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The value or type of object that is computed by the equation.

annotations.definition: An Agent makes use of a Means (either an action or a (system of) entities standing in for the action) in order to achieve a Purpose. (This frame may be broadened to include non-agentive intermediate actions.)

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent uses the Means to acheive a Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal or purpose of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Means.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent uses the Means.

annotations.definition: An Entity has a particular scalar Attribute with a value exceeding a contextually-specified or generally-understood standard. Except where there is a difference in FEs, we place all adjectives related to measurable quantities in this frame. Note that when an adjective appears with a quantity expression (e.g., 6 feet long), it belongs to the Dimension frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which a particular gradable Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: A quantity or other characterization of the Entity's state with respect to the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Value from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular Value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity has a particular Value for the Attribute.

annotations.definition: This transparent noun frame is concerned with Units for measuring the Area of regions.

frameElementDefinitions.Unit: The Unit of measurement.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The region whose surface is being measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Occupant: Things that occupy the Area that is being measured.

annotations.definition: This frame involves words that provide a measure for some undelimited (mass) Entity by referring to an Event that a prototypical quantity (or duration) of the Entity stereotypically participates in. The Entity is not necessarily a physical entity, but may instead be an undelimited type of event (such as a noise, or an activity).

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: An abstract or concrete undelimited entity that is measured by the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The event that a delimitable quantity or duration of an Entity participates in.
frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of iterations of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: This frame contains (transparent ) nouns that are Units of measurement for time. The frame elements are Count, Unit and Process.

frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of Units of the measured entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: The standardized quantity in terms of which other quantities are measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Process: The activity or state that takes place during the period of time under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent either brings about or is the most involved in the Process.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with nouns that denote Units of measurement for linear extent. The Frame Elements are Unit, Count, and Stuff.

frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of Units of the linear extent.
frameElementDefinitions.Stuff: The Stuff whose linear extent is being measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: The standardized quantity in terms of which other quantities of the same kind are measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: This FE marks constituents which express what property is being measured.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with nouns that denote Units of measurement for masses. The Frame Elements are Unit, Count, and Stuff.

frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of Units of the measured mass.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: The standardized quantity in terms of which other quantities of the same kind are measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Stuff: The Stuff whose weight is being measured.

annotations.definition: An Entity has a particular Value for an Attribute which may be described in several different ways, including:

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which a particular gradable Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: A quantity or other characterization of the Entity's state with respect to the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Value from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular Value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity has a particular Value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: A standard measure, having a known value for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of Units which are equivalent to the Value of the Entity .

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with nouns that denote Units of measurement for volumes. The Frame Elements are Unit, Count, Stuff, and Size. The frame will be split soon into container-derived volumes (box, bottle etc.) and pure volumes (gallon, quart). For the moment we tag things as follows:

frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of Units of the measured volume.
frameElementDefinitions.Stuff: The Stuff whose volume is being measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: The standardized quantity in terms of which other quantities of the same kind are measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Size: A specification of the volume of the container.

annotations.definition: An Entity is measured in some (implicit) Attribute by quantifying the Count of a particular Unit whose value for the Attribute is known. LUs and constructions whose meanings inherit from Measures are used in a relatively small, delimitable set of constructions. They may be complements of particular lexical constructions (a lengthof 20 ft., measures 20 ft.) or parts of non-lexical constructions (including Transparent Noun constructions and Measurement_plus_adjective and Measurement_plus_PP).

frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of Units of the measured Entity .
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: The known quantity with respect to an Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The item which is quantified in terms of the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The aspect of the Entity which is being described and quantified.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame name medical conditions or diseases that a patient suffers from, is being treated for, may be cured of, or die of. The condition or disease may be described in a variety of ways, including the part or area of the body (Body_Part) affected by the condition (e.g. liver cancer, cardiovascular disease), the Cause of the condition (e.g. bacterial meningitis, viral pneumonia), a prominent Symptom of the condition (e.g. asymptomatic stenosis, blue ear disease), the Patient or population (originally) affected by the condition (e.g. bovine tuberculosis, juvenile diabetes), or the (proper) Name used to identify the condition (e.g. Munchausen Syndrome, Lou Gehrig's Disease). Annotation in this frame is done in respect to the name of the condition or disease.

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: This FE is used for the patient, entity, or population (originally) affected by the condition or illness.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: Body_Part is used for the part or area of the body affected by the condition or disease.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: This FE is used for the cause of a condition or disease.
frameElementDefinitions.Symptom: This FE is used for a prominent symptom of the condition or disease.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: This FE is used for the Name that identifies the condition or disease.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Ailment diverges from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Ailment is effecting the Patients.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which a medical condition persists.

annotations.definition: This frame includes words for medical instruments--i.e. Instruments designed for an institutional function or Purpose.

frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Instrument is used.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the medical Instrument.

annotations.definition: A Patient interacts with one or more Medics (doctors, nurses, and/or EMTs with a responsibility for the Patient's health) so that the Medics can determine the health status of the Patient and maintain or restore the Patient's health as necessary.

frameElementDefinitions.Medic: The medical professional who has responsibility for the care of the Patient
frameElementDefinitions.Affliction: The particular disorder that the Patient is suffering from and the the Medic treats.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_system: The part of the Patient's body that is being treated.
frameElementDefinitions.Medical_center: The place visited by the Patient and where the Medic works.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The person who is under the care of a Medic or other form of medical professional.

annotations.definition: Procedural or Medicine based Interventions are used on a Patient to attempt to alleviate a Medical_condition. These interventions can have a Frequency_of_success as well as Side_effects. This frame differs from Cure in that this frame deals only with attempts to alleviate a Medical_condition, whereas Cure deals with situations in which the Affliction or Medical_condition has been cured.

frameElementDefinitions.Intervention: A drug or procedure is administered or performed in order to treat a Medical_condition.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The consequence of the Intervention.
frameElementDefinitions.Medical_condition: A holistic description of the medical state of the patient (or a part of the state of the patient), which may or may not indicate the cause of a deviation from normal.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: Individuals that receive medical Intervention because of their Medical_condition.
frameElementDefinitions.Side_effects: Effects of the Intervention that are unintended or undesirable.
frameElementDefinitions.Extent: The degree to which an Intervention has affected the Medical_condition or Symptoms.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency_of_success: The regularity with which an Intervention is effective for treating a Medical_condition or Symptom(s).
frameElementDefinitions.Medical_professional: The individual or team that attempts to improve the medical condition of the Patient.

annotations.definition: This frame includes words that name medical professionals and is closely related to the Medical_specialties frame. The FE Type characterizing a Professional's sub-specialty may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies the Type of medical professional.
frameElementDefinitions.Professional: This FE identifies the medical Professional.
frameElementDefinitions.Affliction: The particular disorder that the medical Professional treats. Most words in the frame inherently indicate this information.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_system: The part of the body that the medical Professional treats. This is usually indicated by the Lexical Unit itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The length of time the Professional has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The payment that the Professional recieves for working.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis describes the conditions of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period, or payment basis.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The Employer gives compensation to the Professional for working.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Professional belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place_of_employment: The place where the Professional works.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: Rank is the position in a hierarchy that the Professional occupies within an organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Professional was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.

annotations.definition: This frame includes words that name medical specialties and is closely related to the Medical_professionals frame. The FE Type characterizing a sub-are in a Specialty may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Specialty: This FE identifies the medical Specialty that someone practices.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies the Type of medical Specialty .
frameElementDefinitions.Body_system: The sub-part or sub-system of the body which is studied. This is normally incorporated in the LUs of the frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Affliction: The disease or disorder that a specialist attempts to treat. This concept is normally incorporated in the name of the Specialty.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame describe a relationship in which an Item is expressed through a Text.

frameElementDefinitions.Text: The written source, body of work, or linguistic utterance in which the Item is found/expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The piece of information or expression that is contained within the Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Item appears in the Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Item appears in the Text.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Entity becomes sufficient enough to meet a Standard. Alternatively, an action or occurrence can be thought of as Event that fulfills a Standard. In this frame, the Agent, Entity, or Event can be conceptualized as being on a scale where a change of value or circumstances brings it in line with the position of the Standard on the scale.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The intentional Agent acts to meet to a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The non-intentional Entity becomes sufficient enough to meet a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: An act or occurence that changes circumstances sufficiently so as to meet a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: A condition or requirement that the Agent, Entity, or Event attempt to meet.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent or Entity become sufficient in regards to the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Agent or Entity become sufficient in regards in to the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent, Entity, or Event becomes sufficient in regards to the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Standard is exceeded or not by Agent, Entity or Event.

annotations.definition: In this frame Party_1 meets with Party_2 at a prearranged Time and Place. If expressed, Purpose only applies to Party_1. The Purpose of the meeting is usually discussion over a certain topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: Party_1 is the grammatically more prominent participant in the meeting event. It can refer to either a single individual or a larger party separate from Party_2 before the meeting.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: A grammatically less prominent participant in the meeting event. It can refer to either a single individual or a larger party separate from Party_1 before the meeting.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that Party_1 and Party_2 plan to engage in upon meeting.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the meeting happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the meeting happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means descibes the way in which Party_1 and Party_2 convene.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the meeting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of Party_1 or Party_2.

annotations.definition: A Trigger elicits a Response. The Response may either be some kind of action meant to address the Trigger event or an expressed judgment of the Trigger event.

frameElementDefinitions.Trigger: The event that elicits a Response.
frameElementDefinitions.Response: The means action or judgement in response to the Trigger.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A characterization of the Response action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Response action takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point in time when Response is made to the Trigger.

annotations.definition: A Person is a member of an organized military organization belonging to a nation-state or similar body. The military membership may be part-time, but is considered a job. This is in opposition to combatants who may be loyal to a particular cause but are not part of the military arm of a government.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is the member of the military.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Person is associated.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: Rank is the position in the military hierarchy that the Person occupies.
frameElementDefinitions.Place_of_employment: This FE identifies the place where the Person is stationed.
frameElementDefinitions.Loyal_side: The Person belongs to the military of the Loyal_side.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies a more specific Type of military function or specialty that the Person has.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Person receives for performing military service.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis describes the conditions of military membership with respect to permanency, time or duration of duty, payment basis, and so on.
frameElementDefinitions.Military_organization: The military organization to which the Person belongs.

annotations.definition: A Member is semi-permanently part of a socially constructed Group.

frameElementDefinitions.Member: The person who belongs to a Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: A set of people standing in a semi-permanent relationship together.
frameElementDefinitions.Standing: An indication of the Member's status within the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the inclusion event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Member is a member of the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the membership relationship holds.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer applies oneself to commit a Pattern to memory, so that the Cognizer would recognize future examples of the Pattern or be able to reproduce it.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The sentient being that focuses on a Pattern so that it will remain in the memory.
frameElementDefinitions.Pattern: The phenomenon that the Cognizer observes and commits to memory.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Cognizer memorizes the Pattern.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location wherein the Cognizer memorizes the Pattern.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the memorization event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of a Cognizer that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Completeness: The exactness of the match between the Cognizer's memory and the Pattern.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Cognizer by which they achieve memorization.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal that the Cognizer has in mind, which they believe will be accomplished by memorization.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with Cognizers remembering and forgetting mental Content.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person whose mental Content is at issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The Content is the mental content that a Cognizer retains or forgets.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject the Cognizer mental Content is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Cognizer manages to remember or forget the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result represents the consequences of the remembering or forgetting of the mental Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the amount of time for which the mental Content is remembered or forgotten.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the remembering or forgetting takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the remembering or forgetting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the remembering or forgetting of mental Content occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Experience: An event (or period of time standing in for events) that the Cognizer has experienced that leads to the Cognizer remembering the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The situation which surrounds a Cognizer remembering a Content or Topic.

annotations.definition: In this frame, a Sentient_entity has some activity of the mind operating on a particular Content or about a particular Topic. The particular activity may be perceptual, emotional, or more generally cognitive. This non-lexical frame is intended primarily for inheritance.

frameElementDefinitions.Sentient_entity: The individual whose mind has a particular piece of Content in it, or, more generically portrayed, whose mind is focussed on a particular Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The situation or state-of-affairs that the Sentient_entity's attention is focussed on.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The source of awareness or knowledge of the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: A description of the kind of Content in the Sentient_entity's attention in terms of an idea which is salient throughout the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the cognitive act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Sentient_entity that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: Expressor is the body part that reveals the Sentient_entity's state to the observer.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The category that an element of the Content is considered to belong to.
frameElementDefinitions.Paradigm: The system of knowledge which serves as the basis for the Sentient_entity's awareness.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Sentient_entity has the Content in their attention.

annotations.definition: The adjectives and nouns in this frame are all based on the idea that mental properties may be attributed to a person (Protagonist) by a (usually implicit) Judge on the basis of that person's Behavior, as broadly understood. Though on a conceptual level these words always attribute mental properties to people, they may be applied to Protagonist's Behaviors as well, with the understanding that the Behavior is revealing a (usually temporary) property of the Protagonist responsible for it. For example, while we may speak of a stupid person, we may also speak of a stupid thing to say/do/think. In addition, we may mention both the Protagonist and the Behavior, as in

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: This is the person (or people) to whom a mental property is attributed. The FE may be expressed as External Argument of a predicative use of the adjective, as a PP Complement, or as the head noun modified by an attributive use of the adjective:
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The Behavior refers to any action, utterance, belief, or artifact thereof on the basis of which a mental property is attributed to the Protagonist. The meaning of the English word behavior should not be taken to constrain the identity of this frame element too much---conceptually, this frame element includes things that would not normally be described using the word behavior. Note the following examples:
frameElementDefinitions.Practice: Some of the words in this frame have a slightly different use in that they evaluate someone only with respect to their mental performance in some particular domain or capacity. We call this domain or capacity Practice. E.g., She is astute at math. Note that this does not mean she is considered to be astute in general for doing math. Rather, she is astute with respect to math, but may be dumb with respect to other things. These uses probably belong in a different frame with words such as skilled, but for the time being they are marked with the FE Practice. General Grammatical Observations: The adjectives in this frame may exhibit Extraposition of a to-marked infinitive VP, with an optional Protagonist expressed in an of-PP: It was brilliant (of you) to invite the clowns to the party.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree marks the extent to which the Behavior or Practice is exhibited or attributed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner refers to the method or way in which the Mental_property is attributed to the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The domain in which the Behavior or Protagonist has a given mental property.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer has an emotion as caused by a Stimulus or concerning a Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the general area in which the emotion occurs. It indicates a range of possible Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer is has a feeling about the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part, gesture, or other expression of the Experiencer that reflects his or her emotional state. They describe a presentation of the experience or emotion denoted by the adjective or noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the amount of time for which the Experiencer is affected by the Stimulus, Topic, or State.

annotations.definition: A Stimulus serves to bring about an emotion of mental stimulation in an Experiencer.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the person, event, or state of affairs that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Empathy_target: The Empathy_target is the individual or individuals with which the Experiencer identifies emotionally and thus shares their emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Stimulus evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Stimulus evokes its response. In some cases it may appear without an explicit Stimulus. Quite often in such cases, the Stimulus can be inferred from the Circumstances
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the Stimulus which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer can be described as having said emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Stimulus Stimulus affects the Experiencer.

annotations.definition: A Communicator or Medium briefly references something in a Message, in which only a subpart of the full content is profiled.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a Message to another person.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee receives a Message from the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: A proposition or set of propositions that the Communicator wants the Addressee to believe or take for granted.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes the state of the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Communicator communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Communicator communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the the Message is mentioned.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration is the length of time during which the communication takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times the Message is mentioned by the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Communicator mentions the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Specified_content: The relevant entity or state-of-affairs that the Communicator mentions.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the Communicator mentions the Message or Specified_content.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Communicator communicates the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Communicator mentions the Specified_content.

annotations.definition: Some Possessor, either a nation, institution, or private individual, controls a Force whose primary functions to defend the Possessor (or, in the case of an institution or individual, some area of land significant to them) and to carry out offensive actions for the Possessor. The Domain of operation of the Force may be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Force: The Force is the body of soldiers that acts in military capacity for the Possessor. This FE is incorporated in each word in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The Possessor is the nation, institution, or individual who possesses and/or controls the Force.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Force.
frameElementDefinitions.Members: The consituent members of the Force.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain in which the Force operates.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: A (permanent or temporary) Goal of the Force.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_existence: The length of time that the particular military organization has existed.

annotations.definition: The Military of a Possessor (either a nation, institution, or private individual) conducts large-scale activities in a Area to accomplish military or combined military/non-military goals.

frameElementDefinitions.Force: The Force carries out the operation. Depending on the size of the operation, this may be as few as one individual or as large as the armed forces of a nation.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The Possessor controls the army and ultimately directs its goals and methods.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area in which the Military is conducting operations.
frameElementDefinitions.Opponent: The nation, group, or situation which the operation opposes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the operations are carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which the operation is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the operation or participants that are not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Military that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Military carries out its operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain of the operation, e.g., military, intelligence.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal of the operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the operation.

annotations.definition: A Miner attempts to obtain a desirable Resource, rocks and minerals, located in a Place being mined, by digging or tunneling in the ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Miner: The Miner searches for mineral Resources.
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The Resource is usually buried in the ground, and is searched for by the Miner.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Miner searches for the Resource
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Miner uses to obtain the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The success or failure of the attempt to obtain the Resource by the Miner.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Miner obtains the Resource.

annotations.definition: A Wrongdoer engages in a Misdeed.

frameElementDefinitions.Wrongdoer: This is the person whose Misdeed is being judged with respect to its morality.
frameElementDefinitions.Misdeed: The assessment of the Wrongdoer is based on his or her Misdeed.
frameElementDefinitions.Severity: The Severity is the degree to which the Misdeed offends the moral sense of the Viewpoint.
frameElementDefinitions.Viewpoint: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which a Misdeed is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where a Misdeed occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Wrongdoer is hoping to accomplish through their Misdeed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action taken by the Wrongdoer which accomplishes the Misdeed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Misdeed which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Wrongdoer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Injured_party: An individual who is harmed by the Misdeed of the Wrongdoer.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Wrongdoer commits the Misdeed.

annotations.definition: Money is a medium used for exchanging goods and services. It is in most cases issued by a government (its Creator) in the form of coins and banknotes. Particular amounts of it may have a designated or planned Use, or may have come from some Origin.

frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is used as a medium of exchange.
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The use for which the Money is intended or designed.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The government (or possibly other organization) that mints, issues and guarantees the legal use of the Money.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which an Money comes into existence or begins to be circulated.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to the Money.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Money.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Money, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The process or means by which the Money comes to be possessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The Possessor has control over the use the Money.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Evaluee is described by a (usually implicit) Judge with respect to the morality or rightness of his or her Behavior.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: This is the person whose Behavior is being judged with respect to its morality.
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The assessment of the Evaluee is based on his or her Behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the evaluation holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Expressor is the body part or action by a body part which is the basis of the evaluation of morality.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: Some entity (Theme) starts out in one place (Source) and ends up in some other place (Goal), having covered some space between the two (Path). Alternatively, the Area or Directionin which the Theme moves or the Distance of the movement may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity that changes location. Note that it is not necessarily a self-mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location the Theme occupies initially before its change of location.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the Theme travels or to a landmark by which the Theme travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the location the Theme ends up in.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: Area identifies the setting in which the Theme's movement takes place without a specified Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of the Motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This FE is used for the Speed at which the Theme moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Carrier: The Carrier is the means of conveyance of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result identifies the ultimate effect of the Motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describing the state of the Theme while the motion is occuring.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the general area in which a specific motion (with Source, Path, or Goal) occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Theme cross a boundary as it moves away from a Source region or into a Goal region.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that is neither the Goal of the posture change nor a landmark along the way of the moving part of the body. Often Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the Protagonist, or the orientation imposed by an implicit observer.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: In cases wherein the Theme is construed as a sentient entity, this FE denotes the state of affairs that the entity wishes to bring about by moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The shape of the overall Path travelled by the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that the described motion forms an integral part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Theme moves in a certain Direction which is often determined by gravity or other natural, physical forces. The Theme is not necessarily a self-mover.

frameElementDefinitions.Area: This FE identifies the general Area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the the state of the Theme during the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Distance identifies a phrase that characterizes the extent of motion of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration is used for the length of time for which the Motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the location the Theme ends up in (often unintentionally).
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the Theme travels or to a landmark past which the Theme travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This FE identifies the Speed at which the motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Carrier: The Carrier is the means of conveyance of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity that changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location the Theme occupies before the Motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where motion specified by Source, Path or Goal occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Theme crosses boundary as it enters a Goal region or exits a Source region.
frameElementDefinitions.Point_of_contact: This FE is used for expressing some part of the Theme participant that makes contact with a horizontal surface.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The shape formed by the path followed by the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event whereby the motion of the Theme comes about.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction of motion of the Theme. Direction characterizes the Path of motion according to some system of orientation, most often that imposed by gravity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Any state of affairs or event from which the particular direction motion logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the agentive Theme hopes to bring about by moving in a particular direction.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world as the action occurs.

annotations.definition: This frame pertains to noise verbs used to characterize motion. Motion_noise verbs take largely the same Source, Path and Goal expressions as other types of Motion verbs.

frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause describes the reason for the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity that changes location. In sentences containing a Cause, the Theme is typically the NP Object. In other sentences, the Theme is normally the External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of Motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is any expression which tells where the Theme ends up as a result of the Motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: This FE identifies the general Area in which motion takes place, used particularly if the motion is understood as following a complex or non-linear path.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of Motion. This frame element occurs very infrequently with Motion_noise verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive indicates the state of the Theme during Motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the Motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Theme moves in the specified way.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the ultimate effect of the Motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place describes the location of a more specific motion (which has Source, Path, Goal, or Area).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time for which the Theme moves under accompaniment of noise.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the motion event takes place.

annotations.definition: This frame binds together the various parts of movement, from setting out, to traveling, to arriving.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for the movement is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is the setting in which the Theme's movement takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: The Cotheme is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the location the Theme ends up in.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path refers to (a part of the) ground that the Theme travels over or to a landmark the Theme travels past.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location the Theme initially occupies before its change of location.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity that changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Theme is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Theme. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.

annotations.definition: A Theme moves with respect to a Fixed_location, generally with a certain Periodicity, without undergoing unbounded translational motion or significant alteration of configuration/shape.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: A physical entity that is participating in non-translational motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Fixed_location: The point or set of points that define the limits of motion for the Theme. For spinning motions, it is the axis, for vibrating it is a boundary, for swinging it is a point.
frameElementDefinitions.Periodicity: The number of times the Theme returns to a state in a given duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Angle: The amount of rotation that the Theme undergoes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Theme is in bounded motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the bounded motion happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction of rotation of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes the way in which the Theme moves in place.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result describes the end state brought about by the motion in place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Cause_external describes the force which sets into motion the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the Theme during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the agentive Theme hopes to bring about by moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The shape formed by the path that the Theme follows.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that talk about how Speakers name Entities.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The entity that Speakers refer to by using the Name.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The linguistic expression by which Speakers refer to the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person who uses the linguistic expression (Name) to refer to the Referent.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation of the intentional act. We did it for the hell of it.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed. John flattered her to receive a raise.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Name_source: This frame element is the person or thing whose name was chosen by the Speaker to give to the Entity. They named their daughter Carol after Paul's grandmother.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action the Speaker takes in order to accomplish the name conferral.

annotations.definition: An Entity shares a name with its Namesake. Note that the shared name is not a frame element as it cannot be directly expressed as an argument of the target lexical units in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity about whom it is asserted that they share a name with a Namesake.
frameElementDefinitions.Namesake: The person or thing that has the same name as the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Anchor: A domain, often a place or time, in which the Namesake of the Entity is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any temporary or permanent property of the Namesake.

annotations.definition: The Locale is a geographical location as defined by shape. This frame includes natural geographic features, including land/ice forms and bodies of water.

frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Locale is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Indicates the action (or causer) which brings the features of the Locale about.
frameElementDefinitions.Locale: A stable bounded area. It is typically the designation of the nouns of Locale-derived frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: This FE is used for the Names of Locales.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Locale is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The Type is the subtype of natural feature.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description, characteristic, or property of the Locale which is not covered by more specific FEs.

annotations.definition: The speaker believes that some state of affairs or entity (the Requirement) must be present in order to cause some other dependent state of affairs to occur (the Dependent). In the typical case, the Cognizer desires the occurrence of the Dependent and so also desires the obtainment or occurrence of the Requirement.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer requires or believes he/she requires the Requirement to hold so that the Dependent will occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Requirement: The entity or state-of-affairs that must be present or obtain in order for the Dependent to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent: The state-of-affairs which cannot hold without the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the conditions under which the Requirement is likely to be needed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Dependent depends on the presence of the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Cognizer requires the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the Cognizer when he/she requires the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Consequences: An undesirable state-of-affairs that results if the Requirement is not met.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times the Cognizer has a need per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why the Cognizer needs the Requirement.

annotations.definition: This frame models the semantic contribution of negation, in which the profiled content of the Negated_proposition is asserted to contrast incompatibly with a corresponding Factual_situation, which is normally implicit. The Negated_proposition and the Factual_situation share non-focal content, on the basis of which they correspond, and contrast in a subset of their content, the focal content, established with reference to a set of focus constructions not described here. (The focus of negation is often conveyed intonationally in spoken English; in text, however, negative focus must be derived inferentially from contextual reference. A subset of focus constructions will be annotated in the constructicon.)

frameElementDefinitions.Negated_proposition: The situation which is presented as false overall. The particular focus of negation which contrasts with the Factual_situation is not separately marked.
frameElementDefinitions.Factual_situation: The situation or part of situation that actually pertains, in contradistinction to the profiled situation in the Negated_proposition that is asserted not to pertain. In spoken language and casual written language, but not in normatively edited written texts, the Factual_situation may be expressed as an unmarked clause immediately preceding or following the Negated_proposition.

annotations.definition: An Anti_consequence is presented as not occurring if the Profiled_possibility occurs.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_possibility: The situation presented explicitly as a possibility, contrasting with the implicit Negated_possibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Anti_consequence: The situation that follows as a result of the Negated_possibility, and is not expected to occur if the Profiled_possibility holds.

annotations.definition: A set of entities of the same or similar types (Nodes) are linked to each other by Connections to form a Network allowing for the flow of information, resources, etc.

frameElementDefinitions.Network: The system functioning as a unit that is constituted by the Nodes and Connections.
frameElementDefinitions.Nodes: The entities that are connected into a Network.
frameElementDefinitions.Connections: The relationships or physical entities that connect the Nodes.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterization or evaluation of the Network.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: A person or organization that possesses or owns the Network or a larger entity of which the Network itself is a part.

annotations.definition: The Noise_maker is an artifact used to produce sound, especially for musical effect.

frameElementDefinitions.Noise_maker: This FE identifies the entity or substance that is designed to produce sound.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which an Noise_maker is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Noise_maker into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which a Noise_maker comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Noise_maker.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Noise_maker.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Noise_maker, including components, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The location with which the Noise_maker is associated.

annotations.definition: LUs in this frame describe a non-commutative process (as opposed to a generic statement) of arithmetic, e.g. subtraction and division, involving two numbers, labeled Term_1 and Term_2. Because of the non-commutative nature of these processes, Term_1 and Term_2 are not separated on a grammatical basis but rather on a mathematical basis (see FE definitions). When referencing the process, the numbers can be vague and are often omitted through indefinite null instantiation.

frameElementDefinitions.Term_1: Term_1 is the first operand in the non-commutative arithmetic that is modified by Term_2 (e.g. subtracted by or divided by Term_2).
frameElementDefinitions.Term_2: Term_2 is the second operand of the non-commutative arithmetic which modifies Term_1 (e.g. subtracts from or divides Term_1).
frameElementDefinitions.Calculator: The Calculator is the agentive entity that combines Term_1 and Term_2.

annotations.definition: LUs in this frame describe a non-commutative statement of arithmetic, e.g. subtraction and division, involving two numbers, labeled Term_1 and Term_2. This frame contrasts with Arithmetic_non-commutative_process in that the statement frame emphasizes the relationship between the terms in combining to form some Result.

frameElementDefinitions.Term_1: Term_1 is the first operand in the non-commutative arithmetic that is modified by Term_2 (e.g. subtracted by or divided by Term_2).
frameElementDefinitions.Term_2: Term_2 is the second operand of the non-commutative arithmetic which modifies Term_1 (e.g. subtracts from or divides Term_1).
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the non-commutative process combining Term_1 and Term_2.

annotations.definition: A Figure is characterized as near the Ground, according to some contextually given scale. Many lexical units of this frame additionally specify in which direction relative to the Ground one can find the Figure (under.prep, north.adv), or directions from the Ground from which the Figure is excluded (e.g. beside.prep excludes the vertical and front/back axes); for specifications of direction, see the semantic types attached to the lexcial units. Members of this frame that make reference to an absolute, earth-based grid (e.g. north, south, etc.) imply a geographic scale of proximity, and so are frequently not paraphrasable as "near".

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as located relative to a certain Ground location. The figure can be an entity or an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: An indication of how accessible the Figure is to some participant in the speech context (either the speaker or the addressee, or some other highly salient viewpoint in a narrative). This FE normally describes availability for manipulation and use, but may simply describe how easily seen or difficult to see the Figure is. There is a closed class of items that can fill this slot, including only 'just' and 'right' indicating ease of access, and 'way' and 'all the way' indicating distance and difficulty of access.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure. This FE is filled by one of a closed class of adverbs: up, down, out (meaning 'beyond the boundary around the viewpoint'), off (meaning 'disconnect between landmark and figure'), back (meaning 'along a line from front to back of landmark, and relatively inaccessible'), and, more rarely, away, along, and dialectally, back down (meaning 'down and behind').
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the locative relation between Figure and Ground obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance between the Figure and the Ground. This FE requires an image schema with a vector that extends from the Ground, or a subpart of the Ground, in some direction, and the Distance is measured along this vector from the Ground to the profiled region.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'.
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Ground interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class of adverbials back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Figure before) and on.adv (which indicates a continued path). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, in which case it either refers to fictive, imagined motion, or it backgrounds motion entirely (as in the example below).

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that denote people who are not participating in a military Conflict.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is a non-participant in a Conflict.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Person is associated.
frameElementDefinitions.Conflict: The Conflict in which the Person is not fighting.

annotations.definition: An entity is considered worthy of mention for their ability or influence among some Type of similar entities. The entity need not be well-known or famous, though if it is, it may be stated by its Name only. Their worth may be restricted to a Time or Place.

frameElementDefinitions.Type: The kind of thing which is notable. This may be an event, place, item, or role and is plural.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The name of the notable entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time during which the entity is considered notable.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The geographic area in which the entity is considered notable.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the individual of a certain Type from the norm of that Type.

annotations.definition: The judge or other court officer (the Authority) informs the Accused of the Charges against him/her, i.e. the alleged actions and the relevant laws.

frameElementDefinitions.Arraign_authority: The judge or other authority of the court in which the accused person is arraigned.
frameElementDefinitions.Accused: The person being charged with an offense against the law.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: The crime with which the Accused is charged
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The larger event which contains the Notification of Charges event.

annotations.definition: An Initial_element is converted into a Final_element via a process of decay, fusion, or fission which changes the number or type of hadrons (protons or neutrons) in the nucleus of the atoms that make up the Element.

frameElementDefinitions.Element: A substance definable in terms of the kind(s) of atoms that make it up.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_element: The type of Element that results from the process.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_element: The type of Element that begins the process.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Element undergoes the transformation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Change event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Element's substance is converted to the Final_element.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The physical or abstract Circumstances under which the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event or state that causes the change to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the nuclear process occurs.

annotations.definition: An Influencing_entity, an Influencing_situation, or an Influencing_variable has an influence on a Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or a Dependent_variable.

frameElementDefinitions.Influencing_entity: A thing or person whose behavior has an effect on an Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_entity: An entity, usually a thing, that is influenced in its behavior by an Influencing_entity or an Influencing_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Influencing_variable: A scalar variable (with unspecified value) which has an effect on the Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_situation: A situation that whose temporal or other characteristics are influenced by an Influencing_entity, Influencing_situation, or Influencing_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_variable: A scalar variable, whose value depends on the behavior of an Influencing_entity, a Influencing_situation, or an Influencing_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Influencing_situation: A situation whose temporal or other characteristics have an effect on a Dependent_entity, Dependent_situation, or Dependent_variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Influencing_entity effects the Dependent_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the influence occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the influencing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Influencing_entity that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A Descriptor gives additional information about the influence. It may be affecting, as with 'positive impact on GDP'; or not, as in 'potential (or possible) influence of SSRIs on metabolism'. Descriptors are typically adjective phrases.

annotations.definition: Under some, usually implicit, Condition a Duty needs to be fulfilled by a Responsible_party. If the Duty is not performed, there may be some undesirable social Consequence for the Responsible_party. This Consequence may or may not be stated overtly.

frameElementDefinitions.Responsible_party: The person who must perform the Duty.
frameElementDefinitions.Duty: The action that the Responsible_party is obligated to perform.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: This FE indicates the Conditions under which the Responsible_party is obligated to perform the Duty .
frameElementDefinitions.Consequence: The undesirable Consequence that will result if the Duty is not performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Responsible_party is obligated to fulfill the Duty.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A location in which the Responsible_party has a specified Duty .
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The deviation of the requirement from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The desirable situation which can be brought about by fulfilling the Duty.

annotations.definition: An Entity is obscure, unnotable, and in general little-known. In the case of an eventive Entity, lack of fame entails a large number of people not being aware of the event's occurence; in the case of a concrete Entity, including humans, lack of fame entails non-awareness of existence.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity which is not widely known and rather obscure.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the degree of fame of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is not well known.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity is considered not to be famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason that the Entity is not famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time during which the Entity is not famous.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity is not famous.

annotations.definition: A Phenomenon is portrayed with respect to the Degree of likelihood that it will be perceived and known, given the (usually implicit) Evidence, Perceiver, and the Circumstances in which it is considered. For several of the LUs, such as visible, audible, etc., the type of the Evidence is fixed by the targets themselves being a perceptual experience.

frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The entity or facts that are undeniable given the Evidence.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The gradable aspect of a Phenomenon which concerns how likely it is that the Phenomenon be known. This frame element is incorporated by the lexical units in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Phenomenon from the norm or from the value for another Phenomenon in terms of how likely the Phenomenon would be known.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Phenomenon is obvious
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the conditions under which the Phenomenon is likely to be known.
frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The sentient entity for whom perception or cognition of the Phenomenon is judged to be more or less likely.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: A state-of-affairs that has a bearing on whether the Phenomenon will be perceived or apprehended cognitively. Evidence is often metonymically indicated by the mention of an entity whose existence or whose properties are relevant.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The likelihood of a Phenomenon to be observed perceptually or cognitively in a particular Group of comparable background items may be contrasted, implicitly or explicitly, with different groups.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: The physical location of a Perceiver of the Phenomenon or a metaphorical point-of-view location of a cognizer relative to the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This frame element is used for all path-like expressions, except those indicating the location of the perceiver (see above), that describe how the perceiver's attention is directed during the act of perception.

annotations.definition: This frame is about Items in the state of occupying a certain Rank within a hierarchy.

frameElementDefinitions.Rank: The Rank which the Item occupies.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The Item which occupies the Rank in the hierarchy.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: The dimension along which the ranking is defined.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which/period over which the Item occupies the Rank
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: The Comparison_set is the set of entities ranked in a hierarchy. The Item is a member of the set.

annotations.definition: Words in this frame describe acts in violation of a legal code.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE broadly picks out the degree of offense.
frameElementDefinitions.Specification: The Specification is an additional aspect of the main crime being commited.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: This FE denotes the person or entity that is badly affected by the Offense.
frameElementDefinitions.Punishment: This FE denotes the Punishment that is specified by the statute in which the offense is defined.
frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: The individual who commits the Offense

annotations.definition: An Offerer indicates that he or she is able and willing to give a Theme to a Potential_recipient. In the absence of further qualifications, it is often understood that the Potential_recipient accepts the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Offerer: The person that begins in possession of the Theme and offers it to the Potential_recipient .
frameElementDefinitions.Potential_recipient: The entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object which is offered for transfer.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Offerer offers the Theme to the Potential_recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Offerer offers the Theme to the Potential_recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Offerer offers the Theme to the Potential_recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Offerer offers the Theme to the Potential_recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Offerer offers the Theme to the Potential_recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the manner in which the Offerer offers the Theme to the Potential_recipient.
