frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the conditions under which the Theme is offered.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Potential_recipient's intended purpose for the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the Offerer, Potential_recipient, or Theme given independently of the giving event per se.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the offer began to be repeated to when it stopped.

annotations.definition: This frame refers to a part of an organization that becomes separate from the main body. The part is usually partially or even wholly autonomous, and may eventually become larger than the body from which it originated.

frameElementDefinitions.Original_body: The body from which the Offshoot is derived.
frameElementDefinitions.Branch: The offshoot or branch of the original body which differs from it in some important respect.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place in which the Branch is locate or where it first forms.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Branch forms or thrives.

annotations.definition: One phenomenon, the Predictive_phenomenon is an indication of the future or current (but still uncertain) existence of another phenomenon, the Future_phenomenon. There is no Cognizer present on the scene, in distinction to the Expectation frame where a Cognizer takes (the equivalent of) Predictive_phenomenon as evidence for (the equivalent of) Future_phenomenon. Many words in this frame also allow a slightly different understanding, whereby Predictive_phenomenon provides clues as to the good or bad Outcome of an expected Future_phenomenon. Instead of Future_phenomenon an Interested_party that will be involved in the Future_phenomenon may be mentioned. In both construals, the two phenomena are asymmetrical and consequently they have no joint syntactic realization.

frameElementDefinitions.Predictive_phenomenon: Predictive_phenomenon provides evidence for the existence or the nature of the Outcome of Future_phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Future_phenomenon: Future_phenomenon is that phenomenon that is predicted to occur or have a particular quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The FE Outcome identifies those expressions that indicate how the Future_phenomenon is expected to turn out based on the evidence of the Predictive_phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Interested_party: Interested_party marks expressions that indicate people who are interested in the Outcome of the Future_phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Strength: The degree to which the Predictive_phenomenon predicts the occurrence of the Future_phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the omen is occuring.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Predictive_phenomenon is an omen for the Future_phenomenon or the Outcome.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Predictive_phenomenon indicates a particular Future_phenomenon or Outcome.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where something good or bad might occur.

annotations.definition: An Entity develops from a Prior_state to a Post_state. The Entity can also be represented by an entity-denoting adjective which is labeled Domain as shown in the first example below. NOTE: This frame is not intended for the evolution of an entire species, but rather the development of a single organism.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The part of the Organism which is undergoing biological development.
frameElementDefinitions.Organism: The living thing whose Entity is undergoing development.
frameElementDefinitions.Prior_state: The stage from which the Entity develops.
frameElementDefinitions.Post_state: The state into which the Entity develops.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Entity develops.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Entity develops.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant dimension of development. Usually expressed by an entity-denoting adjective.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the development occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity develops.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The Rate at which the Entity develops.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period over which the Entity develops.

annotations.definition: A Useful_location is accessible to some (possibly generic) Theme despite a potential Barrier, or is not accessible because of the Barrier.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme whose motion is blocked or free.
frameElementDefinitions.Useful_location: The place or thing that the Theme is headed towards, despite a potential or actual Barrier.
frameElementDefinitions.Barrier: An entity that (at least potentially) prevents the Theme from getting to the Useful_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which a Theme can easily access the Useful_location
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Useful_location is or is not accessible.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe motion involving a Vehicle and someone who controls it, the Driver. Some words normally allow the Vehicle to be expressed as a separate constituent.

frameElementDefinitions.Driver: This is the being, typically human, that controls the Vehicle as it moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: This is the means of conveyance controlled by the Driver. It may move in any way or medium. With verbs, Vehicle is usually expressed as Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element Source. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path. This includes directional expressions and "middle of path'' expressions, e.g.:
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Any expression which tells where the moving object(s) ends up as a result of the motion expresses the frame element Goal. Some particles imply the existence of a Goal which is understood in the context of utterance.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Any expression which characterizes the extent of motion expresses the frame element Distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: This frame element is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path. Locative setting adjuncts of motion expressions may also be assigned this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Driver or Vehicle during the Transportation.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: An event that the Driver is also participating in while directing the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by the Driver by which the Driver achieves directed Transportation.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of the Driver's action.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: This is a second moving object, expressed as a PP headed by with.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the Vehicle moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Route: The Route is the road or path that the Vehicle regularly travels on.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE indicates the Circumstances under which the operation of the Vehicle takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place describes the location of a more specific motion (which has Source, Path or Goal).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of the time interval during which the Driver operates the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Driver operates the Vehicle
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Vehicle crosses a boundary or the extent to which the Vehicle completes a course.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Driver tries to bring about by the way that they direct the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Operate_vehicle event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: "How often" the Operate_vehicle event occurs.

annotations.definition: This frame describes an Operator interacting with a Vehicle to transport him/herself (and possibly others) from a Source to a Goal. The three primary stages are preparation for canonical operation, operation of the vehicle proper, and bringing the vehicle back to a state of rest, where it may continue to move but not in its typical fashion.

frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Vehicle is directed to move by the Operator
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The starting-point of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path. This includes directional expressions and "middle of path'' expressions, e.g.:
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Where the Vehicle ends up as a result of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: This frame element is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path. Locative setting adjuncts of motion expressions may also be assigned this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the operation takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the operation takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the vehicle operation which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Operator that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action which enables the Operator to operate the Vehicle, or which more directly causes the Vehicle to operate.

annotations.definition: An Operator manipulates the substructure of a System such that the System performs the function it was created for. This frame differs from the Using frame in that this frame does not profile the purpose of an agent but rather their manipulation of an entity (System/Instrument). In the case of using, the Agent need not manipulate the Instrument at all, as in: Mrs. Adams used the room as a place to hang the family laundry to dry.

frameElementDefinitions.System: The entity that the Operator gets to perform its intended function.
frameElementDefinitions.Operator: The invididual who manipulates the substructure of the System.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Operator runs the System.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Operator runs the System.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Operator runs the System.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Operator runs the System.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the running of the System which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Operator that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of the time interval in which the Operator runs the System.

annotations.definition: A Tester uses a concrete or conceptual Product in order to determine either whether it works or to find out how well or in which manner it works. The Tester may have particular Unwanted_characteristics in mind that they are specifically looking for. The operational test may also be carried out under a particular set of Circumstances that are typically relevant to the functioning of the Product.

frameElementDefinitions.Tester: The person who uses the Product to see if it is intact or to ascertain its characteristics during operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Product: The entity that the Tester uses to ascertain whether it is intact.
frameElementDefinitions.Unwanted_characteristics: An entity or property whose presence would impair the acceptability or usefulness of the Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE is used for a Means action which the Tester carries out in testing the Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Tester intends to accomplish subjecting the Product to a test of its functioning.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Tester uses the Product in order to evaluate its functioning.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the extent to which the Tester examines the Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: This FE is the position of the Tester during the act of testing. Typically, it is expressed in a from-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Desired_state_of_affairs: The desirable state of affairs which the Tester hopes to find to obtain when using the Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Tester uses the Product to evaluate its functioning.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The FE Circumstances is used for circumstances that obtain during the test procedure. These are typically relevant to how the Product functions.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of the time period during which the Tester continuously or repeatedly operates the Product to determine its operational characteristics.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the test is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: A use to which the Product is put .
frameElementDefinitions.Tested_property: A variable of behavior of the Product which is specifically evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the testing event began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the Operational_testing.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer holds a particular Opinion, which may be portrayed as being about a particular Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person whose Opinion is in question.
frameElementDefinitions.Opinion: The Cognizer's way of thinking, which is not necessarily generally accepted, and which is generally dependent on the Cognizer's point of view.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The events or states-of-affairs that form the basis for the Cognizer's Opinion.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: A phenomenon that the Cognizer is considering with respect to their Opinion. Note that this extra-thematic frame element occurs freely with nouns, but only in very restricted contexts with verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Constancy: The level of firmness with which the Cognizer holds an opinion. It refers to the amount of convincing that they would need to change their mind.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of how the Cognizer holds an opinion which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quiet(ly)), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Cognizer that also affect their thinking (presumptuous(ly), cold(ly)).
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role describes the presuppositional terms of the Opinion by placing the Topic or the Cognizer of the Opinion into a category.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The aspects of the Opinion (and its Topic, if any) which are under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Cognizer has the Opinion.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Agent has a choice of whether or not to participate in some Desirable_event because of an Opportunity, a situation not completely under the Agent's control and usually of a limited duration.

frameElementDefinitions.Opportunity: The situation during which the Desirable_situation is possible. For nominal LUs, this is generally in the second layer and contains the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient agent that may or may not participate in the Desirable_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Desirable_situation: A situation or event that the Agent can participating in during the period of the Opportunity.

annotations.definition: This frame describes non-artifact Images resulting from an interation between light and some kind of matter, usually a Source entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Image: The optical representation of the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The entity from which the Image's form is based on.

annotations.definition: An Item is picked out either by the order in which the members of a set would be encountered by an implicit cognizer, or by the order in which the members of a set participate in an event or state which serves as the Basis_of_order. The former case usually involves a Starting_point for going through the set, while the latter involves a Basis_of_order, which is normally an event that the Item along with other members of its Comparison_set has participated in. Note that Item should be annotated on a second layer, except in cases when the noun structure the ordinal participates in is predicative.

frameElementDefinitions.Type: The kind of entity that the Item belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Starting_point: A usually spatial reference point from which the implicit cognizer scans across the members of the Comparison_set.
frameElementDefinitions.Basis_of_order: A kind of event in which the members of the Comparison_set, including the Item, participate in a certain order.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity picked out by the implicit cognizer as occupying a certain place in the ordered Comparison_set.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: A set of entites of which the Item is a member and which are ordered either by the Basis_of_order or by the order in which the implicit cognizer focuses attention on them.

annotations.definition: This frame describes intentionally formed human social groups (here termed Organizations) with some definite structure and Members. They may be relatively informal, consisting only of a group of individuals who are aware of who is and who is not a fellow member, or they may be complex and long-lasting, such as the Roman Catholic Church or the Conservative Party.

frameElementDefinitions.Organization: The entity of which the individuals are Members.
frameElementDefinitions.Members: A person belonging to an Organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which an Organization is formed.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_existence: The interval during which the Organization exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: A name given to the Organization for identification.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location or political locale that the Organization is contained within.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE describes the general location where the Organization operates, but does not indicate the possessor of the Organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description of the Organization not covered by a more specific frame element.

annotations.definition: An Entity began its existence in the context of a Origin, which can be a place, culture, time period, text, etc.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The item whose Origin is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The place or culture where the Entity was produced.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer feels an emotion as evoked as an Other.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person or sentient entity that experiences or feels the emotions.
frameElementDefinitions.Other: The Other is the person that evokes the emotional response in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Experiencer's emotion deviates from the norm for the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why the Other evokes a certain emotional response.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances is the condition(s) under which the Other evokes its response.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer experiences the feelings of the Other.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the way in which the Experiencer experiences the feelings of the Other which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). Manner may also describe a state of the Experiencer that affects the details of the emotional experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Experiencer feels for the Other.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation is the circumstances or topic about which the Experiencer feels an emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Other gets sympathy from the Experiencer.

annotations.definition: An Entity has ceased to exist.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Concrete or abstract entity that does not exist after the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This is the Place where the Entity (saliently) does not exist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A point at which the Entity (saliently) does not exist.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE labels the Explanation of the destruction or disappearance of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the length of time before the Entity comes into existence again.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The aspect of the Entity which no longer exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Entity disappears.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Authority excuses an Offense that an Offender has committed, with the effect of ending or preventing any punishment.

frameElementDefinitions.Offender: This FE denotes the offender that seeks or gets the pardon.
frameElementDefinitions.Authority: Authority is the organization or person taht grants the pardon.
frameElementDefinitions.Offense: This FE denotes the the illegal act that the Offender is pardoned for.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Explanation is the cause for pardoning an Offender.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE denotes the time when the pardon is given
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Authority that accomplishes the pardon.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the act of pardon which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of an Authority that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the pardoning takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Authority intends to accomplish by pardoning

annotations.definition: This describes a part_whole relationship where the Part provides the boundary between the Whole and what is not that object. The Part can exist along a continuum of width. It can be modified by an Orientation that specifies the particular portion of the edge of a Part_prop which describes a property of the Part.

frameElementDefinitions.Orientation: This is a property of the Part that determines the portion of the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: An undivided entity having all its Parts.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_prop: A property relating to the Part of a Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: Part is that which forms a boundary between the Whole and what is not the object.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns Parts of objects that are defined relative to the center or edge of the object. The Part is a part of the Whole and the Part_prop describes a property of the Part.

frameElementDefinitions.Part: This element describes the Part of the larger Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: An undivided entity having all its Parts.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_prop: A property relating to the Part of a Whole.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Part is part of a larger Whole comprised of parts that are ordered. The Parts can be ordered temporally (first to last and vice versa) or vertically (down to up and vice versa). The Part can be modified by the Part_prop or the number of the part can be given by Ordinality.

frameElementDefinitions.Ordinality: This is the number of the sequence of Parts.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_prop: A property relating to the Part of a Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: A part of a larger Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: An undivided entity having all its Parts.

annotations.definition: In this frame, there is a Part of a Whole in which the Part is defined with respect to the canonical or inherent orientation of the Whole. In cases where the Target is an adjective, it has a special relationship with the noun that it modifies such that a phrase which is a syntactic argument of the noun is semantically available to fill the argument structure of the adjective, as in

frameElementDefinitions.Part: A piece of a larger Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_prop: A property relating to the Part of a Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: An undivided entity having all its Parts.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with transparent nouns denoting a Piece of a Substance. Also present is Piece_prop which is a property of the Piece, e.g. relating to its size.

frameElementDefinitions.Piece: A Piece made of a substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Piece_prop: A property relating to the Piece.
frameElementDefinitions.Substance: The Substance is what the Piece is made of and may describe the type of larger whole from which it came.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with nouns denoting a part or parts of a Whole entity . The Part is not defined relative to the Whole's orientation, center, or edge and is not ordered. We also annotate properties of the Part, e.g. relating to its size.

frameElementDefinitions.Part: A Part of a larger Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: An undivided entity having all its Parts.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_prop: A property relating to the Part of a Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Orientation: This is a property of the Part that determines the portion of the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.System: A system of organization of the Parts within the Whole, of which a Part is identified with a Name .
frameElementDefinitions.Name: A label applied to a Part of a Whole in order to identify the Part within some System.

annotations.definition: This frame has to do with the bias or lack of bias of a Judge making an assessment concerning two or more Sides. The Sides may stand opposed to each other in a dispute or be alternatives in a situation of choice. The Domain in which the Judge is partial or impartial may be expressed as well as the Degree of bias or lack thereof. In sentences in this frame one may also find expressions of Manifestation_of_bias, i.e. expressions that tell us how the Judge's bias is realized.

frameElementDefinitions.Decision_maker: This Frame Element marks the person whose objectivity in making assesment is being evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Side_1: This Frame Element marks one of the Sides in a dispute or one of the alternatives in a situation of choice.
frameElementDefinitions.Sides: This Frame Element marks phrases jointly expressing both Sides in a dispute or all of the alternatives in a situation of choice.
frameElementDefinitions.Side_2: The second-mentioned of the Sides to a dispute or alternatives in a situation of choice.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: This Frame Element marks expressions of a Domain or parameter with respect to which the Judge exhibits bias or lack thereof.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This Frame Element marks expressions that tell to what extent the Judge exhibited or lacked bias.
frameElementDefinitions.Manifestation_of_bias: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate how the bias of the Judge expresses itself in their behavior or actions.
frameElementDefinitions.Dispute: The Dispute is the situation in which there is some conflict between two sides.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for the partiality.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE denotes the time when the partiality occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Expressor is the body part or action by a body part which indicates the character of the Decision_maker.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the Decision_maker embodies the bias associated with him or her.

annotations.definition: An Event with multiple Participants takes place. It can be presented either symmetrically with Participants or asymmetrically, giving Participant_1 greater prominence over Participant_2. If the Event is engaged in intentionally, then there is typically a shared Purpose between the Participants. It is, however, possible that an expressed Purpose only applies to Participant_1.

frameElementDefinitions.Participants: The individuals or entities that have been participants in an Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Participant_1: Participant_1 is the grammatically more prominent participant in an Event with multiple Participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Participant_2: A grammatically less prominent participant of an Event with multiple Participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: An Event with multiple Participants, who may or may not be involved intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the multiple-participant Event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Event involving multiple Participants occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). When the Participants are understood agentively frames, it may indicate salient characteristics of them that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Participants or Participant_1 intend to accomplish by performing the action indicated by the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by Participant_1 or the Participants that contributes to bringing off the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree_of_involvement: The extent to which Participant_1 contributes to the Event's coming off.
frameElementDefinitions.Institution: A group, location, or institution conventionally associated with an Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE describes the length of time for which participation occurs (usually appears as a for-PP)

annotations.definition: A Subset is profiled against the Group it belongs to. The Subset may consist of a single member. Frequently, the Group is actually a generic sample of a full population, and the Subset is representative of the relative proportion.

frameElementDefinitions.Subset: A Subset belonging to the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group of entities which includes the Subset.

annotations.definition: An Agent physically transfers a Theme to a Recipient. The Agent is not under control of the Theme's motion after the initial input of energy. Compare with Bringing and Sending. In the former, the Carrier is in constant control (even physical contact) with the Theme. In the latter, the Sender plans the motion of the Theme and places it in circumstances such that it travels along the specified path (or to the specified goal), but under the power of a separate individual.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the one who passes the Theme. The Agent is generally realized as the External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The FE Theme is generally an NP Object that is passed to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The FE Source is the starting point of the passing action
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The FE Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The FE Recipient is the entity which possesses the Theme as a result of the passing.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The FE Distance is any expression characterizing the extent of motion of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The FE Area is used for expressions which describe a general area in which passing takes place when the passing is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path. Locative setting adjuncts may also be assigned this frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of a participant during the passing action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The FE Means indicates the action that is taken that results in the motion of the Theme
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The FE Manner is any expression which describes a property of the motion of the Theme which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity saliently involved in the transmission of force from an Agent to a Theme. It may be the Agent's bodypart, or some kind of device.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the general area that provides a setting for a specific motion (with Source, Path, or Goal).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the passing of the Theme occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: A resultant state that the Theme is in at the end of its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: A previous state that the Theme was in before the beginning of its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the main clause logically follows.

annotations.definition: An Agent acts in such a way as to cause others to think that an Item is an instance of a particular kind of Simulated_entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who acts in a misleading way to create the impression that the Item is a particular (kind of) Simulated_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the passing off event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that contributes to the Item being perceived as a (particular kind of) Simulated_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: The Item is the concrete or abstract entity that the Agent misleadingly represents as a particular (kind of) Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by passing the Item off as a (particular kind of) Simulated_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the passing-off event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Simulated_entity: The Simulated_entity is a particular individual or a token of a particular type that the Item is represented as.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location that is the setting for the passing off event.
frameElementDefinitions.Audience: The Audience denotes the people that the Agent intends to mislead into thinking that the Item is a particular (kind of) Simulated_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent gives the impression that the Item is the Simulated_entity.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe the "fictive" motion of a stationary Road. Some of the targets cast the scene primarily in terms of the Path_shape:

frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of the Road. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. In particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The trajectory that would be followed by one moving on the Road.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Any expression which tells where the fictive mover travelling along a Road would end up.
frameElementDefinitions.Road: A physical path that a virtual moving entity can be imagined to travel along. The bike trail zigzagged through the woods.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of the kind of motion a hypothetical mover would have (including steadiness, grace, and general comparisons) count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Any expression which characterizes the extent of motion expresses the frame element Distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: This frame element is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Extent to which the described configuration deviates from the norm for a Road.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An state of affairs involving the Road that allows the Road to be in the specified configuration.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which an imaginary traveler on the Road would move.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the character of the Road.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the motion (which is specified by a Source, Path or Goal) is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The configuration formed by the entire Path along the Road.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction in which a "fictive" mover would travel along a Road.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the fictive motion takes place. This may describe the time when the Road or a particular piece of the Road existed, or it may implicitly describe the time when an actual motion event occurred on the Road.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Road brings about by being in a particular configuration.

annotations.definition: A Path, a series of connected locations, is traversed by a Theme, moving under its own power or under the influence of a physical force. The Path may be described in various terms depending on whether it is bounded or not. If it is bounded, the Path may be identified by its Endpoints, which may be presented separately as Source and Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Path: The series of interconnected locations that the Theme traverses.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is one of the Endpoints treated as a source.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the endpoint of the Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Endpoints: The Endpoints are two (or more) named endpoints of the Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Characteristic: The Characteristic is a characteristic(s) of the Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark: An entity relative to which the Theme moves on its Path .
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The Direction is the direction or directions the Path runs.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: A setting location through which the Path goes.
frameElementDefinitions.Length: The Length is the distance the Path extends.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The moving object whose motion describes the Path.

annotations.definition: An individual or group, the Patrol moves through and examines a Ground in order to ensure that it is in a generally Desired_state_of_affairs, particularly that it is safe and contains no dangerous Unwanted_entity. Rather than moving through the Ground, the Patrol may simply move such that all the (relevant portions of) Ground can be observed and checked.

frameElementDefinitions.Patrol: The person (or group) who inspects the Ground to see that it is safe.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The area that the Patrol inspects with to ensure its safety.
frameElementDefinitions.Unwanted_entity: An entity whose presence would impair the desirability or safety of the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE is used for the Means by which the Patrol examines the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The desired outcome of the patrolling. Normally this constituent is a PP Complement headed by for, or a to-marked VP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Patrol inspects the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the extent to which the Patrol examines the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: This FE is the position of the Patrol during the act of perception. Typically, it is expressed in a from-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Desired_state_of_affairs: The desirable state of affairs which the Patrol hopes to ensure by visiting Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: >The Time when the Patrol examines the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The circumstances under which the Patrol examines the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity used by the Patrol to scrutinize the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of one of the participants in the patrolling event.
frameElementDefinitions.Members: An indication of the members of the patrol.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time of the patrol.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: The Co-participant patrols along with the Patrol.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Patrol may move around in a Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The Possessor is the organization (usually a nation or group of nations) that orchestrates the patrol.

annotations.definition: This frame describes the interrelation of a collection of Entities; they may be physical entities or shapes in a recognizable configuration, a pattern of events, or a relation among abstract entities. The pattern is not the individual Entities nor the set of Entities, but an abstraction of their interrelations, as a gestalt.

frameElementDefinitions.Entities: The entities whose interrelations form the pattern.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The name of the pattern, such as "wing-T".
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A descriptor of the pattern, such as "regular".

annotations.definition: This frame contains general words for Individuals, i.e. humans. The Person is conceived of as independent of other specific individuals with whom they have relationships and independent of their participation in any particular activity. They may have an Age, Descriptor, Origin, Persistent_characteristic, or Ethnicity.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is the human being.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Person is associated.

annotations.definition: An Individual belongs to a group that is categorized in terms of the political views on particular issues that the members of the group tend to share, in opposition to other groups that hold different beliefs on the issues. The views on particular issues that the Individual shares with the other members are assumed to result from general principles or beliefs rather than being accidental or temporary.

frameElementDefinitions.Individual: The Individual who is referred to in terms of their place along the political spectrum.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Individual resides.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: A set of issues that is used to identify a sub-set of the group members.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Individual was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Individual.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Individual which is conceived of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Individual has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Individual belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Position_qualifier: A phrase that indicates more precisely the location in political space of the Individual relative to the center of the region designated by the target.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for individuals as viewed in terms of their age. The Person is conceived of as independent of other specific individuals with whom they have relationships and independent of their participation in any particular activity. They may have a Descriptor, Origin, Persistent_characteristic, or Ethnicity. A specific Age may sometimes be specified as well.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The human being of a certain age.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Person is associated.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for individuals, i.e. humans, who are governed by virtue of being registered in a certain Jurisdiction. Alternatively, they may be conceptualized with respect to their governance by a political or religious Authority They may have an Age, Descriptor, Persistent_characteristic, or Ethnicity.

frameElementDefinitions.Jurisdiction: The Jurisdiction is the place where the Person typically resides and by whose Authority he is governed.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is the human being.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Authority: The politically or religiously legitimated persons who govern the Jurisdiction.

annotations.definition: The LUs in this frame refer to Fighter in Military_organizations according to their specialty in general or their function at a particular place and time. The Fighter may be part of a nation-state's armed forces or may be a member of an irregular military organization.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Fighter was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Fighter: The Fighter has a military specialty.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Fighter which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Fighter.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Fighter has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Fighter belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Fighter is associated.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: The Fighter may have a Rank in the formal or informal military organization to which s/he belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place_of_operation: This FE identifies the place where the Fighter is stationed, or primarily operates from.
frameElementDefinitions.Loyal_side: The Fighter belongs to the military of the Loyal_side.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies a more specific Type of specialty that the Fighter has.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Fighter receives for performing military service.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis describes the conditions of military membership with respect to permanency, time or duration of duty, payment basis, and so on.
frameElementDefinitions.Military_organization: The military organization to which the Fighter belongs.

annotations.definition: This frame contains general words for Individuals, i.e. humans, whose specific acts or general behavior is evaluated against standards of morality or rightness. The Person may have an Age, Descriptor, Origin, Persistent_characteristic, or Ethnicity.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is the human being.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Person is associated.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: A domain of activity in which the Person exhibits behavior on which they are judged in terms of its morality.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for individuals, i.e. humans, with respect to their Origin. The Origin is generally incorporated, but may occasionally be specified separately. The Person is conceived of as independent of other specific individuals with whom they have relationships and independent of their participation in any particular activity. They may have an Age, Descriptor, Persistent_characteristic, or Ethnicity.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life. In this frame, it is generally incorporated in the lexical item, but may be separately specified as in the following:
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is the human being whose Origin is specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Person is associated.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for individuals, i.e. humans, with respect to their Religion. The Religion is generally incorporated, but with some lexical units it is specified separately.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is the human being.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is conceived of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Religion: The religious belief system that the Person follows.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for individuals, i.e. humans, with respect to their proximity of residence. This relationship may be defined either symmetrically (as Residents) or asymmetrically (in terms of a Known_resident and a Indicated_resident). The Residents or the Indicated_resident may have an Age, Descriptor, Persistent_characteristic, or Ethnicity specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Known_resident: The individual(s) that serve(s) as a reference point for the definition of the Indicated_resident due to the proximity of residences.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The place in which the Residents or the Indicated_resident reside.
frameElementDefinitions.Indicated_resident: A person or group of people defined in terms of the fact that they reside in the proximity of the Known_resident.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Indicated_resident has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Indicated_resident.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Indicated_resident which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Residents: The individuals who reside in proximity to each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Indicated_resident belongs.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for Individuals as viewed in terms of their vocation. The Person is conceived of as independent of other specific individuals with whom they have relationships and independent of their participation in any particular activity. They may have a Descriptor, Origin, Persistent_characteristic, or Ethnicity. A specific Age may sometimes be specified as well.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is the human being.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Person is associated.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: Rank is the position in a hierarchy that a Person occupies within an organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Place_of_employment: This FE identifies the Place where the Person works.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The Employer gives compensation to an Person for their work.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies the Type of the vocation that the Person practices.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Person receives for performing a task.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis describes the conditions of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period, or payment basis.

annotations.definition: A Perceiver perceives a Phenomenon. The general Perception frame is an inherited background to all frames that have to do with some sentient being responding to changes in the environment, independently of the sensory modalities. The inheriting frames may specify the modalities (see, hear, taste, smell), or may emphasize the experiences or acts of the perceiver (peek, eavesdrop), or the properties of the perceived phenomena (clank, rattle, thump).

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The FE Perceiver refers to someone whose cognitive state or experience (e.g. knowledge, beliefs, ideas judgments or classifications) is being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Sought_phenomenon: The Perceiver is the entity or event which causes the perceptual experience of the Perceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: Phenomenon is experienced by the Perceiver.

annotations.definition: This frame contains perception words whose perceivers intentionally direct their attention to some entity or phenomenon in order to have a perceptual experience. For this reason we call the perceiver role in this frame Perceiver_agentive.

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver_agentive: The Perceiver_agentive performs some action in order to have a perceptual experience. It is expressed as an External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: Phenomenon indicates the entity or phenomenon to which the Perceiver_agentive directs his or her attention in order to have a perceptual experience. Typically, it is expressed as an Object with verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: Body_part identifies the part of the body used as a sensory organ of the Perceiver_agentive, typically expressed in a with-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: This FE is the position of the Perceiver during the act of perception. Typically, it is expressed in a from-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: Direction is used for all path-like expressions that describe how the perceiver's attention is directed during the act of perception, with the exception of those indicating the location of the Perceiver_agentive (which would be marked Location_of_perceiver)
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: State is used for predicate expressions that apply to the Phenomenon, providing some information about the state it is in while the perceiver's attention is directed to it:
frameElementDefinitions.State: State is the location, activity, or other characteristic of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner expressions may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of active perception as such: closely, carefully, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means indicates the tool or method the Perceiver_agentive is using to actively perceive the Phenomenon. May be confused with Direction in cases with through-PP, but is referring more to the instrument used than the path used.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the activity of attempting to perceive occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Some action that the Perceiver_agentive is trying to accomplish by directing their own attention.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The general location within which the act of perception takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Perception takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Expected_entity: An entity or state-of-affairs that the Perceiver_agentive hopes, fears, or expects to find within the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: Ground is the perceptual background against which the Phenomenon is experienced by the Perceiver_agentive.
frameElementDefinitions.Obscuring_medium: The Obscuring_medium is the medium through which the Phenomenon makes itself perceivable to the Perceiver_agentive. This is similar to the Direction, but occurs in the opposite direction and implies a movement of the Phenomenon.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words describing physical experiences that can affect virtually any part of the body. The body part affected is almost always mentioned with these words. It is typically expressed by the noun heading the external argument, and this noun is typically accompanied by a possessive determiner that refers to the possessor of the body part, as in the first example below:

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the being who has a physical experience on some part of his or her body, or internally. Often information about this frame element is incorporated as a possessive determiner into the constituent expressing the Body Part, in which case it is not tagged separately (from Body Part), as shown below: My legs hurt! Sometimes, however, this frame element is expressed as an External Argument in its own right: I hurt all over! Here, the phrase all over can be considered a kind of generalized Body Part expression, and the pronoun I expresses the Experiencer independently. With nouns and adjectives in this frame, the separate expression of the Experiencer is more common: I have a pain in my leg.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: This FE is the location on the body where the physical experience takes place, typically expressed as External Argument, often as PP complement: My legs are a little sore. I'm a little sore in my legs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation describes the cause of the perception the body is experiencing.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion is the specific area of the Body_part which experiences the sensation.

annotations.definition: This frame contains perception words whose Perceivers have perceptual experiences that they do not necessarily intend to. For this reason we call the Perceiver role Perceiver_passive. Comparing the Perception_experience frame to the Perception_active frame, we note that for some modalities there are different lexical items in each frame. For instance, whereas Perception_experience has see, Perception_active has look at. For other sense modalities, we find the same lexical items in both frames. To illustrate, consider the verb smell where I smell something rotten exemplifies its Perception_experience use and Smell this to see if it's fresh exemplifies its Perception_active sense. This frame also includes words which are not specific to any sense modality, including detect, perceive, perception, sense.

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver_passive: This FE is the being who has a perceptual experience, not necessarily on purpose. It is typically expressed as an External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: This FE is the entity or phenomenon that the perceiver experiences with his or her senses. It is typically expressed as Object with verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The Body_part is the location on the Perceiver_passive's body where the perceptual experience takes place. It is typically expressed in a PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: This FE identifies the Location_of_protagonist during the perceptual experience. It is typically expressed in a from-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This frame element is used for all path-like expressions, except those indicating the location of the perceiver (see above), that describe how the perceiver's attention is directed during the act of perception.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The State indicates predicate expressions that apply to the Phenomenon, providing some information about the state it is in while the Perceiver_passive experiences it.
frameElementDefinitions.State: State is the location, activity, or other characteristic of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner expressions may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of perception as such: clearly, loudly, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree indicates the degree to which the Perception is experienced.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action whereby the Perceiver perceives.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Perceiver_passive or the Body_part perceives the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end.
frameElementDefinitions.Contrastive_context: An element of the discourse that serves to contextualize the Phenomenon, with which it is directly related (i.e. filling a frame-element role of the Phenomenon or vice-versa). It contrasts with other contextually relevant elements that potentially (but do not actually) fill the same role.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state-of-affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state-of-affairs would be expected given the state-of-affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: A situation which pertains during the event. This frame element is primarily used to highlight the event denoted by the target against a (potentially partially) causal or concessive background state-of-affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: An expression of how often the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The general location within which the act of perception takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground is the perceptual background against which the Phenomenon is experienced (or not experienced) by the Perceiver_passive. It can be distinguished from locative postnominal modifiers of the Phenomenon in negative contexts.
frameElementDefinitions.Obscuring_medium: The Obscuring_medium is the medium through which the Phenomenon makes itself perceivable to the Perceiver_passive. This is similar to the Direction, but occurs in the opposite direction and implies a movement of the Phenomenon.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words for Individuals as viewed in terms of their participation in artistic performances. The Person is conceived of as independent of other specific individuals with whom they have relationships. They may have a Descriptor, Origin, Persistent_characteristic, or Ethnicity. A specific Age may sometimes be specified as well.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Person was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person is the human being.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Person which is conceived of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Person has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Person belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Person is associated.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: Rank is the position in a hierarchy that a Person occupies within a performance.
frameElementDefinitions.Place_of_employment: This FE identifies the Place where the Person works.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The Employer gives compensation to an Person for their work.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies the Type of performer the Person is.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Person receives for performing a task.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis describes the conditions of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period, or payment basis.

annotations.definition: A Performer has a certain Role in a Performance and plays their part by following the directions in the Script for that particular Role.

frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Performer to transmit the Performance to the Audience.
frameElementDefinitions.Performance: The Performers generates the Performance which the Audience perceives.
frameElementDefinitions.Performer: The Performer provides an experience for the Audience.
frameElementDefinitions.Score: The music which may be performed in the Performance, either in isolation or accompanying actions and speech.
frameElementDefinitions.Script: The (usually written) directions which instruct the Performers on what actions to perform, when, and how. It has many different names depending on the Type of Performance including script, screenplay, libretto, book, and choreography.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates the subtype of Performance, including dance, theater, film, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role identifies one of the possible relations that the Performer can have to the Performance. The Role (s) a Performer has in the Performance will determine which parts of the Script should be performed by the Performer, as the Script is written in terms of which Role (s) each set of instructions applies to. In the fields of theater, movies, ballet, and opera, a Role generally corresponds to a character in the story of the Script, while other types of performance may use more general Roles like lead violinist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing.
frameElementDefinitions.Performer1: The person that performs alongside Performer2.
frameElementDefinitions.Performer2: The person that is also in the Performance, alongside Performer1.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: How long the Performer is in the Performance.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This is an appraisal or description of the performance event.

annotations.definition: The Performers, together with behind the scenes Personnel, execute a Performance according to a Script and/or Score. The purpose of the Performance is to create an experience for an Audience, who then judge its merits. Performances may be in many different Mediums and be of various Types. This frame is very elaborate in modern society and there are a number of traditions and practices typically (but not necessarily) related to Performances: The Performance is preceded by many practice sessions in which the Performers and other Personnel attempt to improve the quality and/or fidelity of their individual and collective performances to the Script and/or Score. The Audience members pay a fee to experience the Performance. The Performers (and Personnel) receive payment for contributing to the Performance. Often there are other individuals involved in the specifically financial aspects of the Performance and Performers. Critics act as part of the Audience and then write reviews that evaluate the merits of the Performance, describing the Performance to other potential Audience members. Due to the high quality of previous Performances, Performers can become famous, i.e. well known and liked. Those who like them particularly well become fans of the Performer. (Shared with writers...)

frameElementDefinitions.Performer: The Performer provides an experience for the Audience.
frameElementDefinitions.Personnel: The Personnel, including writers, producers, stage hands, key grips, etc., contribute to the Performance without actually being directly perceived by the Audience.
frameElementDefinitions.Performance: The experience generated by the Performers and perceived by the Audience .
frameElementDefinitions.Script: The (usually written) directions which instruct the Performers on what actions to perform, when, and how. It has many different names depending on the Type of Performance including script, screenplay, libretto, book, and choreography.
frameElementDefinitions.Score: The Score may be performed in the Performance, either in isolation or accompanying actions and speech.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates the Type of Performance, including dance, theater, film, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Performer to transmit the Performance to the Audience.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame have to do with people and the personal Relationships they are or can be a part of. Some of the words denote people engaged in a particular kind of Relationship, others denote the Relationship, yet others the events bringing about or ending the Relationships. Many of the words presuppose an understanding of states and events that must have occurred before another event takes place or before a person can be classified in a certain way.

frameElementDefinitions.Partner_1: That partner in the Relationship who is realized as the subject of verbs in active form sentences, or of adjectives in predicative uses.
frameElementDefinitions.Partner_2: That partner in the Relationship who is not expressed as the external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Partners: The joint construal of both Partners in the Relationship.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Partners.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby a focal participant achieves an action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of the relationship.

annotations.definition: A Person is in a state of prosperity and/or renown after some period of development or hardship in an Endeavor. LUs in this frame invoke a developmental path that is related to the goal one should have in a Person, such that the Person, after traversing a path of development (including hardship, effort, and time), completes or does not complete these goals. Note that this distinguishes an event's success in this frame from Successful_action.

frameElementDefinitions.Person: A sentient being or group of beings that is prosperous or well known after being involved in some Endeavor.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Entity is determined to be successful.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A qualification of the level of success that the Entity achieves.
frameElementDefinitions.Endeavor: An event that the Person is involved in which leads to their state of prosperity or renown.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An explanation of why the Person or Endeavor achieves its goal of prosperity or renown.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: The area in which the Person achieves success.

annotations.definition: A physical object, the Artifact, is produced by a Creator to stimulate the perceptions, emotions, or cognition of an audience. Though it need not, in many cases, the created Artifact is intended to represent an actual or imagined entity or event, the Represented. The Represented may be specifically shown in a particular state (State_of_represented).

frameElementDefinitions.Artifact: This FE identifies the entity or substance that is designed to have a particular Use.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which the Artifact is intended.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: An individual or individuals that bring the Artifact into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which an Artifact comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of the subtype of Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: Any indication of what makes up the Artifact, including components, ingredients, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Represented: The entity--which may be a thing, an action or a state--that is represented by the Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_represented: A characterization of the state in which the Represented entity is portrayed.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE identifies a characteristic, description, or evaluation of the Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Artifact is presently located.

annotations.definition: An Entity exists in some Place at some time.

frameElementDefinitions.Constituents: Salient parts or substances that make up an Entity. At this level of abstraction, not every Entity is conceived of as having parts, though any normal entity has them.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: A thing (either abstract or physical) that exists with some degree of permanence.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Indicates the action (or causer) which made or makes the Entity what it is.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: A label applied to a specific Entity. Not usual for general words inheriting from this frame, but common for Locales.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Entity belongs to. When a subtype is signified by some more specific FE, like Formational_cause, we use that more specific FE instead of Type, so this FE is most often needed when there is a vague or unknown relation between a name that indicates the Type and the Type of Entity in question. This FE is normally found as a pre-nominal modifier.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A place that a physical Entity is located with respect to.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe situations in which a Perpetrator forcibly seizes control over a Victim's Vehicle to gain some end.

frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: This is the person (or other agent) who forcibly seizes control over a vehicle to gain some end.
