frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: This is the means of transportation which gets under the control of the perpetrator. Example: The terrorists hijacked the plane.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: This FE describes the people that are suffering as a result of the Perpetrator's action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The unlawful seizure of a Vehicle by a Perpetrator.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Perpetrator by which the seizure of the Vehicle is accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This is the place to which the vehicle is directed.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: This is the place from which the vehicle is taken away.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The action that the Perpetrator is trying to accomplish by piracy.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The situation that the Perpetrator had in mind which lead to the decision to perpetrate the particular act of piracy.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An object that the Perpetrator uses in taking control of the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that the Vehicle is seized in acts of piracy.

annotations.definition: Generally without overall (translational) motion, an Agent places a Theme at a location, the Goal, which is profiled. In this frame, the Theme is under the control of the Agent/Cause at the time of its arrival at the Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person (or other force) that causes the Theme to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that changes location during the Placing.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the initial location of the Theme, before it changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The FE Goal is the location where the Theme ends up. This FE is profiled by words in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of the placing action which is not directly related to the trajectory of the Theme's motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree indicates the degree to which the placing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the Agent or Theme of the placing.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an act whereby the Agent achieves the placing of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the Placing.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the amount of time for which the Theme is to stay in the Goal location.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place indicates where the Placing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation indicates why the Placing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Time is when the Placing occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is the setting into which the Theme is placed.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: This is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which Placing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by perform the placing action.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This extrathematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate. Further, the target predicate should involve some sort of Agent that intends that the benefit go to the Beneficiary.

annotations.definition: This frame represents the abstract scene in which an Agent manipulates a Theme's location, thus affecting a Goal which ends up co-located with the Theme. This frame is primarily for Inheritance.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the actor who instigates the filling.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the physical object or substance which changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location where the Theme begins its motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is the trajectory of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the area or container being filled. Goal is generally the NP Object in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Agent of the filling action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the resultant state after the filling has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Agent fills or covers the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the filling takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the filling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion is the smaller area of the Goal where the filling or covering occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which brings about the filling of the Goal.

annotations.definition: A Theme traverses a trajectory. This trajectory consists of a Source, Path, and Goal. If the Theme is sentient, the anticipated trajectory will usually be equivalent to the planned trajectory. If the the Theme is non-sentient the anticipated trajectory will usually be the result of various forces acting upon the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity that traverses the trajectory.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source profiles the region where the Theme has travelled from (i.e. the region where the Theme's movement began).
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is the region upon which the Theme travels. It is any region in between the Source (where the Theme departed from) and the Goal (where the theme will eventually arrive).
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal profiles the region at the end of the Theme's Path. This is the region where the Theme movement stops.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor provides additional information that characterizes one or more of the aspects of the trajectory: the Source, the Path, or the Goal.

annotations.definition: This frame describes a situation in which the Agent puts the Theme into the Ground for the purpose of growing.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent plants the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: Theme is the item planted by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is used by the Agent as a tool to help obtain the Theme. (This tool is not limited to typical artifacts and can be hands, arms, and other atypical hunting and fishing instruments.)
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what length of time the Agent plants the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent plants the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent plants the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The greater geographic location where the Agent plants the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the planting event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by planting the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Agent cultivates the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground is the background or context to which the Agent pays attention in his agricultural efforts.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes a participant in the Planting action.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: The Co-participant is an entity that participates in the planting event in a coordinated way with another participant.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the planting event logically follows.

annotations.definition: The LUs in this frame refer to biological entities other than human beings and animals, which are labeled by the FE Plant.

frameElementDefinitions.Plant: The living organism belonging to the kingdom Plantae.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Plant not covered by another frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The location from which the Plant originated.

annotations.definition: The answer to a Question is under discussion in a Group, which still has a difference of opinion among its members. The prominence of the Question relative to others can be indicated by a Status expression.

frameElementDefinitions.Question: The Question is an open proposition the answer to which is under dispute.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group are the persons or organizations who have different points of view on the Question.
frameElementDefinitions.Status: The Frame Element Status is used for expressions that indicate the relative importance of a Question with respect to the other topics that are disputed.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Question, often with regard to its complexity or whether it has been resolved or not.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The area of human experience which the Question concerns.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: A state or event within which the Question arises as a problem for the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Question exists as a problem for the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Viewpoint: An individual or individuals from whose point of view the Question is a point of dispute within the Group.

annotations.definition: A less-powerful person or group of people (the Activists) engages in an activity aimed to inconvenience or weaken a more powerful person or group (the Institution) to seek redress, in the form of a series of Demands, for the wrondgoings committed by the Instituion.

frameElementDefinitions.Activists: The person or group of people that act out against the Institution in an attempt to have their Demands fullfilled.
frameElementDefinitions.Institution: The Institution is the company, factory, political party / institution, or entity that yields authority over the Activists.
frameElementDefinitions.Demands: The Demands are the guarantee(s) given by the Institution to the Activists that act to correct or address the issues or state of affairs that were grievous to the Activists.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the length of time that the political action takes to be resolved.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the political action is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Activists carry out the political action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location where the political action occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE is the subtype of the general category of action taken by the Activists against the Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The result of the event in which the Activists act against the Institution.

annotations.definition: This frame covers words that name Locations as defined politically, or administratively.

frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: The materials or Constituent_parts that make up a Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Locale is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Locale: A stable bounded area. It is typically the designation of the nouns of Locale-derived frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: ULTIMATELY TO BE REPLACED BY MULTIWORD TARGETS (in almost all cases). This FE is used for the Names of Locales.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Locale is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description, characteristic, or property of the Locale which is not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Population: The people living in the jurisdiction of the locale.

annotations.definition: An Evaluee, which can be a person or an object, has an amount of acceptability or utilization based on general (often non-aesthetic) desirability. The Judge who determines the Evaluee's desirability may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: The person or object that is being evaluated for its desirability.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A description of how desirable the Evaluee is.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The person who decides how desirable the Evaluee is.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Evaluee is judged to be desirable.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The geographic area where an Evaluee is considered desirable.

annotations.definition: An Agent behaves as if they were a particular individual or type of Simulated_entity with the intent of misleading observers of their behavior. This is generally accomplished by altering their normal appearance (especially dress), mannerisms, voice, and other observable characteristics.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who acts in a misleading way to create the impression that they are a particular individual or type of Simulated_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the posing event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that contributes to the his or her being perceived as a particular (kind of) Simulated_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Simulated_entity: The type or individual that the Agent gives the impression of being.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by posing as a particular (kind of) Simulated_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the posing event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent gives the impression that they are the Simulated_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent impersonates the Simulated_entity.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words that describe an Item's static position on a scale with respect to some property Variable.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: Item identifies the entity whose scalar property is specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Variable: Variable is the scalar property that the Item possesses. Soda is high in sugar.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The Value is the position or range of positions on the scale that the Item occupies. This is normally implicit in the LU.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the scalar property of an Item holds with respect to some Variable.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: Domain describes the set of Items for which the Variable 's position is determined.

annotations.definition: An Owner has (or lacks) a Possession.

frameElementDefinitions.Owner: The entity that owns a posession.
frameElementDefinitions.Possession: The thing which is owned by the Owner. Ex.: I have twenty bucks. (This does not include bodyparts or medical conditions, but does include intellectual property, etc.)
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: State of the Owner or Possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Owner possesses the Possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time interval during which the Owner has a possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Owner has the Possession.

annotations.definition: A Possible_event is deemed to have all pre-conditions satisfied; in other words, it has some probability of occurrence, if some (generally implicit) further Condition pertains. The Condition, whether overt or implicit, often concerns the decision of an agent to attempt to bring the Possible_event about. The primary implication is that certain other aspects of the situation that might have been expected to prevent the Possible_event, usually lack of capability or permission, do not pertain.

frameElementDefinitions.Possible_event: The Possible_event has met its preconditions, and will occur if some further Condition holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the preconditions of the Possible_event are met.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An entity, event, or state that brings about the possibility of the Possible_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: The state-of-affairs that does not hold, but if it did, the Possible_event would occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Possible_event can occur.

annotations.definition: The stage after which the Recipient has acquired the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual that has just come into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity which has just come into the possession of the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Recipient has the Theme
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time interval during which the Post_getting event occurs.

annotations.definition: The stage immediately after the Donor gives the Recipient the Theme, i.e. when the Recipient has possession of the Theme and the Donor no longer does.

frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The individual that has just given the Theme to the Recipient and no longer has the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity that the Donor has just given to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Donor lacks the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual that has just come into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Post_giving event occurs.

annotations.definition: The subframe of Relinquish in which the Owner (= Donor) no longer has the Possession (=Theme).

frameElementDefinitions.Owner: The entity that no longer owns the posession.
frameElementDefinitions.Possession: The thing which was owned by the Owner. (This does not include bodyparts or medical conditions, but does include intellectual property, etc.)
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the possession state which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Post_lose_possession event occurs.

annotations.definition: The state-of-affairs immediately after the Recipient has come into possession of the Theme offered by the Donor.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The person who has just acted to come into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person who has just given the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity that changes possession, beginning in the possession of the Donor and ending up with the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Recipient has the Theme
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Post_giving event occurs.

annotations.definition: The stage after the Theme is in the possession of the Recipient.

frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The individual that has just given the Theme away.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity which has ended up in the possession of the Recipient
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Donor possesses the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual that has ended up with the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Post_transfer event occurs.

annotations.definition: An Agent supports their body in a particular Location. The LUs of the frame convey which body part is the Point_of_contact where the Agent is supported, what orientation the body is in, and some overall arrangement of the limbs (especially the legs) and the torso.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the individual whose body is in a particular posture. The Agent FE is generally expressed as the external argument of verbs:
frameElementDefinitions.Location: A description of the position of the Agent. The Location of the Agent is frequently expressed and generally occurs as a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Agent's state throughout the time they are in a particular posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_state: A state of the Agent which crucially depends on their posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time interval during which the Agent held a certain posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Agent holds the posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Agent holds the posture.
frameElementDefinitions.Point_of_contact: The part of the body that provides support to the Agent's body in the posture.

annotations.definition: An Agent enacts an Action that is intended to be performed again at one or more later Occasions. The goal of practicing is to ensure that the action at the later Occasion(s) comes off flawlessly.

frameElementDefinitions.Action: This FE identifies the Action that the Agent performs intentionally in order to prepare for a later Occasion.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent performs the Action in preparation for later performances of the same type.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the practicing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent performs an intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent practices.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent practices.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the practicing which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the Action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Occasion: The later enactment or enactments that the Agent expects to perform. Note that the Agent does not have to have specific Occasions in mind.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time during which an Action is repeatedly practiced.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that the Action is practiced.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: A person who participates in a practice event along with the Agent.

annotations.definition: The Behavior of an Evaluee is described in terms of the reaction it deserves from somebody, such as praise, blame or admiration.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: The Behavior of the Evaluee is being judged as worthy of a particular reaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: This FE indicates the Behavior on which the assessment of praiseworthiness is based.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the judgement or reaction is deserved.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: A Joker, by means of a Prank, arranges to deceive a Victim, with the result that some harm or embarrassment comes to the Victim.

frameElementDefinitions.Prank: The Prank is a device, strategy, or system designed to mislead and cause harm and amusement to a Victim (and possibly others observing the prank).
frameElementDefinitions.Joker: The Prank is orchestrated by a Joker.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Joker intends to mislead and cause harm to the Victim by means of the Prank.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of the Prank.

annotations.definition: The state of the Recipient prior to the acquisition, i.e. before the Recipient gains possession of the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual that is about to gain a possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity which is about to be acquired by the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the way that the Recipient has the Theme
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Post_getting event occurs.

annotations.definition: The stage of giving just before the Donor discontinues possession of the Theme, yielding it to the Recipient.

frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The individual that has possession of the Theme and is about to transfer possession to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity which is in the possession of the Donor, which later ends up in the possession of the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Donor possesses the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual who is about to come into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Pre_giving event occurs.

annotations.definition: Subframe of Relinquish where the Owner (=Donor) has the Possession (=Theme).

frameElementDefinitions.Owner: The Owner is the entity that owns the Possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Possession: The Possession is the thing which is owned by the Owner.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the possession state which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Post_lose_posession event occurs.

annotations.definition: The state-of-affairs immediately before the Recipient gets the Theme which describes the Recipient's possession of the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The person who acts to come into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person who has the Theme and is about to give it to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity that changes possession, beginning in the possession of the Donor and ending up with the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the way that the Recipient has the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Pre_receiving event occurs.

annotations.definition: The stage before the transfer has taken place, with the Theme in the possession of the Donor.

frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The individual that is about to transfer the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity which is in the possession of the Donor.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Recipient or Donor possesses the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual who is about to receive the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Pre_transfer event occurs.

annotations.definition: A Theme has the potential of falling from its current Location, which would likely result in damage.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: A valuable entity which might fall and be damaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the actual level of precariousness from the expected value given the Theme and the state indicated by the target itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Falling_event: The event of falling, incorporated in the LUs of this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Dangerous_entity: An entity which may cause the Theme to fall.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A particular location in which the Theme is at risk of falling. Frequently, it is to be inferred that the characteristics of the location put the Theme in danger.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Theme is in danger of falling.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the state of precariousness of the Theme logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation under which the Theme is safe or unsafe from falling. Most expressions of Time and Place also express the Situation and should be labeled as such on the 2nd FE layer.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain in which the Theme has a degree of precariousness. Usable only in metaphorical sentences.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Theme as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the holistic properties of the balancing situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The location where the Theme is supported.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: An event or state that results from the instability of the Theme.

annotations.definition: Water in some solid or liquid form (the Precipitation) falls from the sky at a particular Place and Time, lasting for a particular Duration. The Rate or Quantity of precipitation may also be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Precipitation: The water in liquid or solid form that falls from the sky is the Precipitation. This FE is incorporated in many LUs in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The Quantity of Precipitation that falls within a certain Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Quantity: The Quantity is the amount of Precipitation that has fallen in a precipitation event or events. It is usually measured as the height that the Precipitation attains if prevented from further movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The area which experiences precipitation.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the precipitation event lasts.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the reason for the precipitation event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the precipitation falls.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature: This FE identifies the temperature of the precipitation.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer is in an undesirable Situation, whose Explanation may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is in an undesirable Situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: This FE identifies the undesirable Situation the Experiencer is in.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation of the Situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Thiis FE identifies the Time when the Situation is in effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Situation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain in which the Experiencer has the problem.

annotations.definition: A Speaker states or makes known a future Eventuality on the basis of some Evidence. Instead of (or in addition to) a Speaker, a Medium may also be mentioned. Likewise, a more general Topic may be stated instead of an Eventuality.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker makes a prediction.
frameElementDefinitions.Eventuality: This FE marks expressions that indicate an event or state that is predicted to occur or hold at a future time. A single Eventuality may be expressed discontinuously in a raising construction. This FE is also used for expressions that denote entities with variable attributes about which the Speaker makes predictions.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: Evidence marks expressions that denote a set of facts on the basis of which the Speaker makes the prediction.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the prediction is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Speaker makes the prediction.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner marks any description of the Speaker's prediction making that is not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_eventuality: This FE marks expressions that indicate for what future time an Eventuality is predicted.
frameElementDefinitions.Accuracy: This FE indicates the Accuracy of the prediction.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Descriptor describes a characteristic of the Eventuality.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Speaker to transmit the prediction.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject matter to which the prediction pertains. It is normally expressed as a PP Complement headed by about.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Speaker makes a prediction.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer has a greater desire to participate in some Event, as against another (contextually recoverable) event which exhibits a specific Contrast with the Event. Alternatively, the Experiencer may have a greater desire that some Focal_participant participate in the Event. The Location_of_event may also be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer is the person (or sentient being) who wishes for the Event to occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The change that the Experiencer would like to see.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_participant: This is the entity that the Experiencer wishes to be affected by some Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the extent of the Experiencer's Desiring.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Experiencer desires something.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation shows why the Experiencer wants the Event to occur. This FE occurs frequently as a PP complement headed by for.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose_of_event: The Purpose_of_event is the purpose pertaining to the desired Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_event: The time that the Event would ideally occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_event: The Location_of_event is the place involved in the desired Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Experiencer desires something.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place. Note that this FE is of extremely rare occurrence in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Experiencer has desired something.
frameElementDefinitions.Contrast: An alternative Event or Focal_participant that is less desirable for the Experiencer.

annotations.definition: In this scenario, there are two mutually incompatible scenes, the Profiled_possibility and the Opposite_possibility, leading respectively to a preferable Positive_consequence and a dispreferable Undesirable_consequence.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_possibility: The situation presented explicitly as a possible for agents to influence that leads to a better situation, contrasting with the implicit Opposite_possibility that leads to a worse situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Opposite_possibility: The implicit situation that is mutually exclusive with the Profiled_possibility. It is presented as leading to a less desirable situation, the Undesirable_consequence.
frameElementDefinitions.Positive_consequence: The preferable situation that follows from the Profiled_possibility.
frameElementDefinitions.Undesirable_consequence: The less desirable situation that follows as a result of the Opposite_possibility.

annotations.definition: This frame describes means actions (Preparatory_acts) that are taken by an Agent for the purpose of preparing for a later act. Contrast this frame with a scenario involving earlier versions of some entity being superseded by later versions (e.g., preliminary results, advance copy).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent uses the Preparatory_act to acheive a Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Preparatory_act: The action performed by the Agent that is a preliminary to the achievement of the Purpose. The Preparatory_act may also be expressed by a result of such an act.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The ultimate goal or purpose of the Agent, which the Preparatory_act enables.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Preparatory_act, Agent, or other participant.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Preparatory_act is distant from the achievement of the Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain_of_relevance: The area, field, or domain for which the preliminary action is relevant.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Preparatory_act takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Preparatory_act takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Preparatory_phase: The part of the larger process that is preliminary to the achievement of the Purpose.

annotations.definition: An Entity exists at a particular Location, at a particular Time, as observed by an implicit observer. A Depictive and/or Duration phrase is often found. This frame differs from Existence in that the Location is profiled as a ground where an observer is conceived of as confirming the Entity's existence.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity whose existence at the Location is confirmed.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The Location of the Entity. The Location is conceived as a ground for an event of inspection to determine the presense of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Entity is present at the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A separate description of the state of the Entity when it is present.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time period during which the Entity is present. In this frame, Duration may be expressed with for preceding an event which stands for the duration of the event. In cases where the Entity is sentient, there may be an inference that the Entity's intent is to be present so as to participate in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the presence or absence of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Entity is present.

annotations.definition: A Mitigating_situation is presented as occurring against the backdrop of a more preferable situation and a less preferable situation that, while expected, feared, or hoped for, did not or will not occur; the Mitigating_situation is thus, so to speak, sandwiched between a better and a worse situation, both of which are relevant in the discourse. Understanding the Mitigating_situation is therefore dependent on understanding the better and the worse situations, one or the other or both of which normally have already been mentioned or strongly evoked in the discourse. The relation of the Mitigating_situation to these situations may be filled in by discourse-level inference, as in the first example below, or implied by contrastive intonation, as in the second example.

frameElementDefinitions.Mitigating_situation: The Mitigating_situation is the situation that actually occurs. It is presented in a discourse to mitigate the fact that a better situation did not occur - since the FE is still preferable to an even worse situation.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Agent preserves a Patient in order to prevent it from decaying. A Medium may be used.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the intentional act that leads to the preservation.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the substance in which the Patient might be submerged to be preserved.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is organic matter that undergoes the preservation.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which an an Agent performs the Preserving.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Agent preserves the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent preserves the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the preserving occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent preserves the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Agent preserves the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of Time for which the process of Preserving is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive phrase describing one of the participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Patient is preserved from decaying.

annotations.definition: A Speaker communicates about a Topic in such a way as to mislead an Addressee, either by telling outright lies or by presenting a message in a misleading form.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a deceptive Message to the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The person that receives a Message from the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is the entity to which the proposition or propositions relate, that they are about.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the deception occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the deception occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The (communicative) action performed by the Speaker in order to deceive the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of Time that the Speaker spends on the Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal of the Speaker's deceit of the Addressee
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Speaker deceives the Addressee about the Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE describes how often the Speaker discusses the Topic with the Addressee.

annotations.definition: An Agent or Potential_hindrance keeps a Protagonist from having a State_of_affairs that they need or desire, or, despite the possibility of preventing the possession, they allow the Protagonist to have the State_of_affairs.

frameElementDefinitions.Potential_hindrance: The Potential_hindrance is the force which keeps the Event from happening, or does not prevent it.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The is the situation, state-of-affairs, or state of having a possession that the Protagonist is kept from having.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Potential_hindrance keeps the Event from taking place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the Potential_hindrance keeps the Event from taking place.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who keeps the Event from happening.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist is the person who is kept from having the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason why the Agent keeps the Protagonist from having the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time for which the Protagonist is denied the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: How it is possible that the deprivation event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE marks the frequency with which the Protagonist is prevented from having the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at the time of prevention and which the prevention is taken to be a part of.

annotations.definition: A Potential_hindrance or an Agent (via their actions) keeps an Event from taking place, or despite the possibility of prevent the Event, do not do so. Lexical units which indicate that the event is not prevented, e.g. allow.v, are marked with the semantic type "Negative". Some lexical units, like avert and avoid indicate that the Potential_hindrance or Agent would have been affected if the Event occurred.

frameElementDefinitions.Potential_hindrance: The Potential_hindrance is the force which keeps the Event from happening, or does not.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is prevented from occurring by the Preventing_cause or the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Potential_hindrance or Agent keeps the Event from taking place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the Potential_hindrance or Agent keeps the Event from taking place.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the sentient being which keeps the Event from happening.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: A situation that accompanies the preventing or allowing.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of details of the event that influence the overall understanding and impression, such as the attitude of an Agent (casually) or the overall nature of the event (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action performed by an Agent that brings about the prevention or allowing.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole. There are two somewhat distinct subgroups of sentence modifiers that cover this function. First, it applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness. The notion of appraisal requires a factive portrayal of the event. The second subgroup consists of any descriptions of the state of affairs denoted by the target as role fillers in other frames. The state of affairs reported by the target is understood to play a role in the state of affairs of the modifier. What that role is depends only on the predicate heading the modifier phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the Agent or an entity of the Event while they are involved in the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Allowed_category: A category that the Event fits into, which dictates the Event's being permitted or prevented (in accord with some implicit Principle.)
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The situation that the Agent hopes to bring about with their action.

annotations.definition: A Product is offered by a Seller at a particular Price such that some total cost for the Buyer in the transaction is a multiple of the Price.

frameElementDefinitions.Product: A commodity, item, or service being sold by the Seller at a certain Price.
frameElementDefinitions.Price: The amount of money that the Seller charges the Buyer for a Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Seller: The entity that offers a Product at a certain Price.
frameElementDefinitions.Buyer: The person who purchases the Product at a particular Price.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of the Price, usually characterizing its position on a scale of expensiveness.

annotations.definition: This Frame deals with Penal_institutions. They are run by an Operator and may be intended to hold certain kinds of Inmates. Penal_institutions may have a special Name and their Location may also be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Operator: The Frame Element Operator marks expressions that indicate which level of government operates the Penal Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Inmates: Inmates marks expressions that indicate the group of people a Penal Institution is intended to hold.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: This FE marks expressions that are the Name of a Penal Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: This FE marks expressions that indicate the Location of the Penal Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Security: The level of security maintained at the prison.
frameElementDefinitions.Penal_institution: The locale which serves to confine individuals, generally for the purpose of punishment. This FE is incorporated in each LU in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: A subpart of the Penal_institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE is used to describe a salent characteristic of the Penal_institution not otherwise specified.

annotations.definition: This frame characterizes the likelihood that a Hypothetical_event will happen as a position on a scale of impossible to inevitable. The likelihood can expressed as numerical Odds or a metaphorical representation of the Position on a scale.

frameElementDefinitions.Odds: A numerical representation of the probability that a Hypothetical_event will occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: A metaphorical representation of the position on the scale of likelihood that the Hypothetical_event will occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Hypothetical_event: The event that is expected to happen with a certain likelihood.

annotations.definition: This frame describes a complex event which lasts some amount of time, consisting of a beginning stage, a stage where the process is ongoing, and a finish or end. In some cases the process may pause, and then possibly resume. In this frame and its subframes, the distinguished argument is a Process, and there is no reference to an Entity which undergoes this Process or an Agent which initiates this Process. In formal terms, this frame refers to an overall controller x-schema (Narayanan 1997) with subframes corresponding to parts of the x-schema.

frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Reason for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Process: Name of the Process which occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.

annotations.definition: A Process is finished and can no longer proceed.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Process: This FE identifies the name of the Process that occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.

annotations.definition: An Event continues at a certain Place through Time. (Note that often when 'continue.v' occurs with definite time points, it denotes Resumption, which is out of frame here.) NB: Refer to Process frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase about the state of a focal participant.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which the Event is continuing.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time over which the Event is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: Name of the Event which is continuing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Next_subevent: The Next_subevent of the overall Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.

annotations.definition: A Process comes to an end.

frameElementDefinitions.Process: Name of the event which occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the event ends.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event ends.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result for the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that the event is repeated.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time it takes for the Process to end.

annotations.definition: The initial State of an Entity in an event; part of the Event X-schema. NB: Refer to Event frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Process: The overall process which is about to begin.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point at which the Process begins.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Process remains ready to begin.

annotations.definition: The Process stops making progress towards completion, but should resume and continue.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the pause takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the pause in the Process occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the pause.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation of the pause.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that a Process remains stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that the Process stops.,

annotations.definition: An event resumes at a certain Place and Time. NB: Refer to Event frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of Time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Process: The Process which is being resumed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that the Process resumes.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).

annotations.definition: An Event begins at a certain Time and Place. Reason may also be indicated. NB: Refer to Event frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: Name of the Event which occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the Event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an Event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which an action is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time for which the Process is ongoing after it has started.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that the specified type of Event begins.

annotations.definition: A Process stops at a certain Time and Place. Reason may also be specified. A Duration may be included in order to indicate the length of the persistence of the Paused-state before action resumes. NB: Refer to Process frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation of an event occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that a process remains stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that the Process stops.,

annotations.definition: The State of an Entity after an Event has stopped, i.e. part of an X-schema. NB: Refer to Event frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Process: The overall event which is stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point at which the Process is not continuing.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Process does not resume.

annotations.definition: A Process is in a state where it can no longer be completed.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the state takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Process: This FE identifies the name of the Process that occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.

annotations.definition: An Agent alters some Material in some useful way by means of some chemical or physical Alterant. Typically, this involves placing a reagent in contact with the Material, or applying heat, pressure, etc. Often a specific Agent and/or Result is mentioned. The Alterant is often incorporated in the target, as in tin.v, galvanize. (The Preserving frame is a special case of Processing_materials, in which the Material is immersed in the Alterant for the Purpose of preventing decay.) Processing_materials is distinguished from Filling in that the Material in Processing_materials is fundamentally altered (often chemically), rather than simply being covered with a different substance.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is a person who applies the Process to the Material.
frameElementDefinitions.Alterant: The Alterant causes a change in the Material.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The Material is altered by the Agent in some useful way.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent processes the Material.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The result of the processing action, whether intended or not.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time during which the application of the Alterant is applied to the Material
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Materials are processed, often a purpose-built facility such as a tannery or water treatment plant.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point in time or period during which the treatment of the Material takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Material which is affected by the process.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in processing the Material.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: The dimension or aspect of the Material that is processed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A holistic description encompassing all the details of an act of processing, often indicated by giving a characteristic of the Agent that affects the action.

annotations.definition: This frame exists to define a type of Sex where the intended result is procreation.

frameElementDefinitions.Participants: The set of entities engaged in or subjected to procreative sexual contact.
frameElementDefinitions.Participant_1: One party present for procreative sex.
frameElementDefinitions.Participant_2: Another party present for procreative sex.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE describes the length of time that an entire sexual encounter can last, including but not necessarily implying the length of all stages of the encounter.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the sex act which is not covered by more specific FEs. In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of one or more Participants that also affect the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which Participants accomplish a sexual act.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: To be clear: the place FE describes the overall location of the Participants of the sexual activity and nothing else.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE relates the relative time of occurrence of the sexual activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: For a sexual act, the Domain describes the specific realm of the contact between Participants; for example, phone sex.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the justification of a sexual act, for at least one of the Participants.

annotations.definition: In order to facilitate the purchase of Goods which start off in a location distant from a Purchaser, a Deliverer transports and hands off the Goods to a Purchaser or (more indirectly) a Goal location which is accessible to the Purchaser.

frameElementDefinitions.Deliverer: This is the person who completes the movement of the Goods after having transported it.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The end of the path and intended goal of the sending.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The objects being delivered.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The vehicle employed by the Deliverer.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Goods or the Deliverer during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the delivery event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Deliverer that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Purchaser: The individual who purchases and receives the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Deliverer hopes to bring about by delivering to the Purchaser.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state-of-affairs that explains why the Goods were delivered.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the delivery takes place
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: An additional theme delivered along with the main Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: A container in which the Goods is delivered.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the delivery takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed at which the Deliverer delivers the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The conditions under which the delivery takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The location of the Goods before the delivery process begins.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The intermediate actions performed by the Deliverer in order to move the Goods to the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The path along which the Goods move while being delivered.

annotations.definition: An Developer develops a Product, in some cases for a Company.

frameElementDefinitions.Developer: The person who develops the Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Product: The entity which is developed by the Developer.
frameElementDefinitions.Company: The organization for which the Developer develops the Product.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Product is developed by the Developer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Product is developed by the Developer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Product is developed.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which the Product is intended.

annotations.definition: An Developer works to create progress on an Entity. The Entity typically goes through three stages of development: the conceptual process (Achieving_first); the development process (Product_development); and the manufactuing process (Manufacturing). The Entity can also undergo additional processes such as designing, engineering, prototyping, and testing.

frameElementDefinitions.Developer: The Developer is the person who develops the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity is the item developed by the Developer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Entity is developed by the Developer.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity is developed.
frameElementDefinitions.Company: The organization for which the Entity is developed by the Developer.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Developer develops the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for why the Developer developed the Entity.

annotations.definition: A specified Designer or a Brand creates and distributes a Collection of related Products. The individual Products may all be part of a set or may just be related by season or other arbitrary reason.

frameElementDefinitions.Brand: The company that produces the Collection of Products.
frameElementDefinitions.Collection: The set of Products produced by the Brand.
frameElementDefinitions.Products: The individual items produced by the Brand.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Collection or individual Products.
frameElementDefinitions.Collection_name: The name of the Collection of Products produced by the Brand.
frameElementDefinitions.Designer: The person who designs the Collection.

annotations.definition: An Entity changes from a Prior_state to a Post_state in a sequence that follows a pre-defined plan, expectation, or model of change. The change is usually assumed to be an improvement for the Entity. Because this frame is describing change according to some known model of the Entity, the Prior_state and Post_state are backgrounded, and often implicit.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The object that undergoes change.
frameElementDefinitions.Prior_state: The former state of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Post_state: The state of the Entity following an advance.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the progress occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The rate at which the change happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the progress, generally a description comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period over which the progress occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of advancement that the Entity has attained.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant dimension of development.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An event or state of affairs that brings about the development of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Progress occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes the state of affairs denoted by the target as role fillers in other frames
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Progress event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: In this frame a State_of_affairs is prohibited (or allowed) by a Principle. Raising constructions are common in this frame. Because laws and principles usually focus on what is not allowed, the words of this frame that are synonyms of prohibit.v are considered more basic, and the words that are synonyms of permit.v are marked with the semantic type "Negative".

frameElementDefinitions.Principle: A moral, legal, or social norm which rules a State_of_affairs to be inadmissible behavior or an outcome of behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the prohibiting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the prohibiting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the conditions under which the State_of_affairs is prohibited.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the State_of_affairs is prohibited.

annotations.definition: An Agent, which may be an individual or group, is engaged in a complex Activity. A larger Goal which motivates the Agent carrying out the Activity may be indicated. Instead of a particular Activity, a Field of endeavor may be indicated. The project may also have a Name and the Time when as well as the Place where it is ongoing may be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual or organization that carries out the project.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: An area of human endeavor, typically scientifc in nature, in which the Agent's Activity falls.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: What the Agent (an individual or the members of an organization) are dedicated to doing.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: A name given to the project for identification.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The larger aim that the Agent is pursuing by carrying out certain coordinated measures.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: An entity (abstract or concrete) which is involved in an implicit Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the manner in which the Agent performs the Activity associated with the program.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which the project is lasts or is slated to last.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co-participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the Activity of the project as the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Project: The ongoing plan of action which allows the Agent to pursue the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Project is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where a Project is pursued.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE is used to describe a characteristic of the Project.

annotations.definition: The number of entities that make up the Set changes from an Initial_number to a Final_number. Alternatively, the situation can be described in terms of an Initial_state and a Final_state. In some cases the Attribute (i.e., number) or Difference in number may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The Attribute is the number of entities that make up the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Set: The group of entities, possibly construed as a collective, whose number changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Difference: The number of entities by which the Set increases or decreases.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any expression that gives information about points on the number scale that the Set traverses between the starting and ending point of its movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_number: The number of entities in the Set afterwards.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_number: The initial number of entities in the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate of change of the value.
frameElementDefinitions.Correlated_variable: The Correlated_variable is an ordered progression of positions or values (most often a sequence of spatial locations) that the (dependent) Attribute is measured against. The Correlated_variable is metaphorically mapped onto the progression of time, and in the absence of this FE, changes in the number in the Setare simply measured against (forward-directional) time.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the proliferation takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group within which the number of entities in the Set changes in the specified way.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is the time-frame in which the change in number occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_correlate: The value, location, or state-of-affairs that corresponds to the Initial_number of entities in the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_correlate: The value, location, or state-of-affairs that corresponds to the Final_number in the Set.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A location where the Set changes in number.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: A description that presents the Set's state before the change in the number of entities making it up as an independent predication.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_state: A description that presents the Set's state after the change in the Attribute's value as an independent predication.

annotations.definition: An Entity stands out perceptually in comparison to other members of its type.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which the saliency Attribute is appropriate and under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of the Entity which is under discussion, namely perceptual salience.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the prominence from the norm or from the value for another Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular salience.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity has a particular degree of prominence.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE refers to the location where the Entity has a prominence of some Attribute.

annotations.definition: A speaker marks explicitly that he intends to describe an Entity in a more restricted sense than it was used in the immediately preceding discourse, and that therefore his Intended_referent is different from the one that the hearer might have picked out based on the way the Entity label was previously used.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The name of a particular entity that otherwise might be interpreted as having a broader reference.
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_referent: The entity that the speaker intends to refer to using the Entity in a more restricted sense. The Intended_referent is typically a part of the referent that the hearer might have identified based on the broader use of the Entity.

annotations.definition: Criteria exist that identify a particular Subset of a Group of things into members and non-members. In this frame, a comparative relationship is determined which indicates the size of the Subset relative to the larger Group, expressed as the Ratio.

frameElementDefinitions.Criteria: A representation of the conditions upon which membership to a Subset of a Group is determined.
frameElementDefinitions.Subset: The members of a Group that fulfill particular Criteria to belong to a Subset of the Group.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: A general collection of entities which are judged against the Criteria to determine membership in a Subset.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of how the Ratio compares to expectations.
frameElementDefinitions.Ratio: The numerical representation of the size of the Subset relative to the size of the Group.

annotations.definition: The Denoted_quantity is (generally) an imprecise characterization of the quantity of Individuals or a Mass, made with reference to a relatively easily and precisely imaginable Reference_quantity.

frameElementDefinitions.Denoted_quantity: The quantity denoted by phrases headed by the Target.
frameElementDefinitions.Mass: A substance that is being quantified.
frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: A collection of entities, either quantifiable physical beings (e.g. toads) or quantifiable units whether physical or not (e.g. dollars).
frameElementDefinitions.Reference_quantity: A relatively precise quantity in some oft-used form that is used as the basis for defining the Denoted_quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: Some Protection prevents a Danger from harming an Asset.

frameElementDefinitions.Protection: The person, entity, or action that prevents harm to an Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Danger threatened the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Danger threatens.
frameElementDefinitions.Danger: A situation that could damage the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Asset: Something desirable possessed by or directly associated with the Protection which might be lost or damaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event involving the Protection that removes the Asset from potential harm through the Danger.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by a volitional agent acting as a Protection which interacts with an undergoer to accomplish the security of the Asset from harm by the Danger.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the protecting event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In cases of agentive Protection, it may indicate salient characteristics of an agent that also affect the action (carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: Tthis FE is used for phrases that describe the protecting event as a whole. It applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness. The notion of appraisal requires a factive portrayal of the event. It also applies to descriptions of the protecting event as a role filler in another frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description or characteristic of the Protection.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the act of protecting logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: FN: This FE denotes the length of time in which the Asset is protected.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: FN: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: FN: This extra-thematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the protecting event.

annotations.definition: A Protester (or Protesters) expresses a strong opinion either in support of or against an Issue, or the Action(s) of a Side which relate to the Issue. The public expression of support or opposition often involves multiple Protesters participating in an orginized assembly in a public area.

frameElementDefinitions.Protester: The individual (or individuals) who holds an opinion on an Issue or Side.
frameElementDefinitions.Issue: The matter, usually concerning a course of action, which the Protester has an positive or negative opinion about.
frameElementDefinitions.Side: The party or individual (generally associated with a particular position on an Issue) that the Protester sides with.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Protester supports or opposes something, or expresses views on the Issue.
