frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Protesters express their opinions.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is a proposition denoting a state of affairs from which the Protester's expressing his or her views on the Issue logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: An action taken by a Side or a view that the Side takes on an issue which the Protester either supports or opposes.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes the person or element that is protesting.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the a Protest which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Protester that affect the action (reluctantly, meekly).
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Protester takes Sides on an Issue .
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time of the protest.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frequency with which the protest occurs.

annotations.definition: A Host provides a temporary Residence for a Lodger.

frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host is the individual (or group of individuals) that own or control the Residence and allow the Lodger to temporarily stay there.
frameElementDefinitions.Lodger: The Lodger is the individual who is given permission to lodge in the Residence.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the conditions under which the Host provides lodging.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Host provides a Residence to the Lodger
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the Host provides a temporary Residence.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is the goal of the Host in allowing the Lodger to stay.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the cognitive cause of the Host's granting of permission.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the provision of lodging which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Host that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Host whereby they communicate or enact the granting of permission to stay in the Residence.
frameElementDefinitions.Residence: The location controlled by the Host which the Lodger stays at temporarily.

annotations.definition: A Profiled_region is picked out which is near to (or far from) the Ground to a certain Degree. The Profiled_region cannot contact or overlap the location of the Ground, as this is categorically different--see Contact_image_schema and Collocation_image_schema.

frameElementDefinitions.Near_profiled_region: A region of space located relative to a Ground that is maximally relevant near the Ground, with decreasing applicability away from the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of deviation from an idealized expected distance of the Profiled_region from the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Distant_profiled_region: A region of space located relative to a Ground that is not relevant near the Ground, with increasing applicability away from the Ground.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns permanent organizations (the Institutions) that are intended to help members of the Target_group in a particular Domain with a Service_action. Most or all relevant members of the Target_group have a direct relationship with the Institution.

frameElementDefinitions.Institution: A permanent organization with a socially important purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The concern of the Institution-- broadly speaking, the aspect of society that they affect.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_group: The members of the public whom the Institution is intended to serve.
frameElementDefinitions.Service_action: The action performed or enabled by the Institution for the Target_group.

annotations.definition: A Publisher makes a Work of an Author (or authors) available to some public Audience for general enjoyment, examination, and reference. The Medium of a Work can vary; examples include books, TV programs, movies, and radio shows. Publication normally involves a number of other events, such as editing and advertising of the Work, and mass production and sales of a represenation of the Work; thus in the most elaborated version of publication, there are a number of other potential roles, such as editor, publicist/advertizer, distributor, printer, etc., but none of these show up as separate, syntactically distinguished roles for the LUs in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Publisher: The entity that acts on behalf of the Author to release the Work to the public.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The format utilized by the Author to create the Work.
frameElementDefinitions.Work: The creation of the Author that is made public by the Publisher.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The date when the Work is available or is expected to be available.
frameElementDefinitions.Audience: The subset of the population that the Work is targeted towards or released to.
frameElementDefinitions.Author: The person or entity that is responsible for the detailed construction of the content of the Work. It is normally expressed in place of the Publisher FE, but the Publisher may cooccur with Author, appearing as an adjunct.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The situation that the Author or Publisher hopes to bring about by publishing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Publisher releases the work.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the action that clarifies, in a holistic way, the details of how the publication event unfolded, including comparisons to other events ('the same way') and states of performers of the action that influence the act itself ('peevishly').
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action taken by the Author or Publisher that makes the Work publicly available.

annotations.definition: A Perceiver briefly perceives a Phenomenon, often resulting in partial or uncertain perception. A Body_part that enables the perception may be mentioned, either metonymically replacing the Perceiver or in addition to the Perceiver.

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver: The being who has a perceptual experience, generally construed to be not on purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The entity or phenomenon that the perceiver experiences with his or her senses. It is typically expressed as Object with verbs.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The Body_part is the location on the body where the perceptual experience takes place. It is typically expressed in a PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: The Location_of_protagonist during the perceptual experience. It is typically expressed in a from-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This frame element is used for all path-like expressions, except those indicating the location of the perceiver (see above), that describe how the perceiver's attention is directed during the act of perception.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Perceiver during the event of perception.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The perceptual background against which the Phenomenon is experienced (or not experienced) by the Perceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of properties of perception, such as: clearly, loudly, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree indicates the degree to which the Perception is experienced.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action whereby the Perceiver perceives.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time, generally construed as punctual, at which the Perceiver or the Body_part perceives the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end.

annotations.definition: An Agent wants to achieve a Goal, or an object. A Means has been created or is used to allow the creator or a user to achieve a Goal. The Goal is a state of the world that does not currently hold but which the Agent wants to realize and is planning and/or working towards. In some cases, it is difficult to decide whether a noun phrase metonymically stands for the Agent or the Means (e.g. The goal [of this paper/seminar/project] is to determine ...). We mark these phrases as Agent on the first layer but also annotate them as Means on a second annotation layer. In some cases a Restrictor on the Goal state of affairs can be given prominence as a separate constituent. Talking about Goals frequently includes the mention of a Domain to which the Agent's Goal is relevant.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The volitional actor who intends to achieve a goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The action or state of affairs that the Agent wants to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: A Means is an object or action that is used or designed to achieve a particular Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: This frame element expresses a sphere of activity within which an Agent has a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This Frame Element expresses a time period during which an Agent had a particular Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Restrictor: This FE marks expressions that indicate a spatial or temporal or individual framework relative to which the Goal is said to hold. More than one Restrictor can be mentioned.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: This FE marks expressions that indicate an Attribute for which it is the Agent's goal that it have or reach a particular Value .
frameElementDefinitions.Value: This FE marks expressions that indicate which Value the Agent wants the Attribute to reach.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Fire to no longer burn.

frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies the Depictive phrase describing an actor or an undergoer of an action during their participation in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the state of an Agent that affects the nature of the event as the Agent brings about the end of the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Agent puts out the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Fire goes out.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual or group that causes the Fire to no longer burn.
frameElementDefinitions.Fire: The combustion process that the Agent causes to end.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent puts out a Fire
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which an Agent puts out the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the how near the Agent is to puting out the Fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.

annotations.definition: This frame contains transparent nouns (and some adjectives) denoting quantities of a Mass or of Individuals. As opposed to aggregate words such as group, set, these Quantity do not have a status as Wholes on their own.

frameElementDefinitions.Mass: A substance that is being quantified.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: This frame contains nouns denoting Quantities of a specified Entity and their numerical Values. Note that the nouns in this frame DO NOT convey a gradable amount on thier own.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Entity identifies the concept or substance being quantified by a Quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: Value identifies the numerical amount of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Quantity.

annotations.definition: A group of Arguers (also expressible as Arguer1 and Arguer2) express incompatible opinions or beliefs about an Issue.

frameElementDefinitions.Arguers: A group of people in an argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Arguer1: A person who is arguing with Arguer2.
frameElementDefinitions.Arguer2: The person who is being argued with by Arguer1.
frameElementDefinitions.Issue: The the thing the the Arguers are arguing over or about.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frequency with which the Arguers have a disagreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A state of the Arguers, Arguer1, or Arguer2 which saliently affects the perception of the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the Arguers spend in the altercation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the dispute which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Arguers that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that the Arguers perform in order to quarrel with each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The equipment, language, or other entity that the Arguers use to pursue their argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Amount_of_discussion: The degree to which the argument has been finished.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Arguers or Arguer1 hope to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Arguers quarrel
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the argument takes place.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame have to do with a Speaker asking an Addressee a question which calls for a reply (as opposed to making a request which calls for an action on the part of the Addressee).

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person asking a question generally occurs as the External Argument of verb and noun targets:
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is most frequently the NP Object of a target verb. However, with the verb inquire, Addressee can only occur as an of-PP and with a noun target, Addressee occurs in a PP Complement or as a possessive :
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message, or content of the question, usually takes the form of a direct quote or an embedded question with a verb target:
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic occurs very frequently in this frame with both noun and verb targets. It generally occurs as a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Speaker to transmit the question:
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing the request.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The body movement may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal Cause, the Agent's mental or emotional state. Internal Cause is expressed in a PP Complement:
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the questioning occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.

annotations.definition: An Employee voluntarily leaves the service of an Employer.

frameElementDefinitions.Employee: The person who is obligated to perform some task in order to receive compensation.
frameElementDefinitions.Employer: The person (or institution) in whose employ the Employee stood.
frameElementDefinitions.Field: The Field that one works in.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which an Employee leaves the job.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Employee quits the employment of the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Position: The label given to a particular type of service that the employer quits.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Employee quits the employment of the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when an Employee quits the employment of the Employer.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Employee quits.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means whereby the act of quitting is accomplished

annotations.definition: A Self_mover leaves an initial Source location. The Path by which the Self_mover departs, and the Intended_goal of the Direction may also be mentioned. In many words which evoke this frame, there is an additional implication that the Self_mover is discontented with the Source location.

frameElementDefinitions.Self_mover: This is the entity which moves under its own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element Source. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. In particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: A relation to a landmark that describes the trajectory in the midst of the motion. (It is thus neither a Source nor the Intended_goal.)
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_goal: Any expression which tells where the Self_mover intends to end up as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event involving the Self_mover that results in the Self_mover moving in the specified way.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the Self_mover moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: A second moving object that moves in coordination with the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Self_mover during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the motion (which is specified by a Source, Path or Goal) is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The configuration formed by the entire Path of the Self_mover, taken simultaneously.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction in which the Self_mover goes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Self_mover wishes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The device or bodypart that serves to move the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of time that the Self_mover spends away from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Self_mover at the end of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A factor which explains the particular motion or manner of motion of the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Reciprocation: An event paired with the motion, which explains why the motion is expected. In particular, Reciprocation is used to indicate an action that is being rewarded or punished by the motion, and/or is paired with the motion as the other side of an agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at the time of departure and which the departure is taken to be a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that an action occurs in a given time.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: A larger conceptualization that includes the departing as an integral part. The departure expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action in this larger conceptualization.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: An unexpected event that affects some agent (usually the Self_mover), which is enabled by, and occurs after, the departing.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the departing event itself and any of its participants, and is merely peripherally related to the event as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Coordinated_event: The label Coordinated_event is to be used for phrases denoting an event (it does not allow states) that the departing is rhythmically aligned with. (This would then further require that the departing be iterated.) The Coordinated_event is conceived of as independent: it would occur regardless of the event expressed by the target, which is not even an incidental or optional sub-part of the Coordinated_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance from the Source that the Self_mover ends up.

annotations.definition: This frame covers LUs that describe the race of a Person.

frameElementDefinitions.Person: The Person whose race is being described.

annotations.definition: There is a certain physical Distance within which some other entity can participate in an Event (typically perception or manipulation) with the Participant. Alternatively, the Event may be indicated metonymically by a Instrument . [Note the connection with *Distance, Sufficiency, and Capability. Words in this frame can generally be paraphrased "close enough to be able to".]

frameElementDefinitions.Event: A type of occurrence whose effects extend a certain distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Participant: An entity that is conceived as driving the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity which is deployable by the Participant to interact with things within a certain distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The spatial extent over which the Participant can interact in an Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The conditions under which the description of a Participant's range holds.

annotations.definition: This frame contains nouns denoting a rank or level within an established hierarchy.

frameElementDefinitions.Rank: The Rank itself, usually denoted by the noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Item: An entity which holds a particular Rank.

annotations.definition: An Entity is rated as unexpected for its context in terms of its magnitude. Either some different type of Entity with a different scope would be expected, or a different amount of the Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The item which is described in terms of its expectedness in the context.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe situations in which the Perpetrator has sexual intercourse with the Victim forcibly (or by threatening the use of force) and without his or her consent.

frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: This is the person who forces another person to have sexual intercourse with him or her against the other person's will.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: This is the person who is forced to engage in sexual intercourse against his or her will.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The rape of a Victim by the Perpetrator.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Perpetrator by which the sexual crime is accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE is any object used to commit the crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that a rape event takes place.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist performs some Action without fully considering the circumstances under which he or she acts, or the possibly (negative) consequences of the Action. Alternatively, the Action itself may be portrayed as having been performed under rushed circumstances.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist acts or is apt to act with some degree of consideration regarding the circumstances and possible consequences of an Action he or she may perform.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action is carried out by the Protagonist with or without regard for the circumstances under which it is performed or consideration of possible consequences.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time when the Protagonist acts with (or without) consideration regarding an Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Action of the Protagonist deviates from the expected in its rashness.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: The LUs in this frame describe the level that a Rate, or the variation in some quantity over a period of time, deviates from the norm. The focal Event for which the Rate is relevant may be profiled as metonymically referring to the Rate of the Event. This frame differs from Rate_quantification in that it does not describe the Rate with a specific numerical designation.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event whose Rate is being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The Rate at which something happens during an Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: How much the Rate deviates from a neutral or expected standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: A participant in an Event that is responsible for its unexpected Rate.

annotations.definition: An Event occurs that can be characterized by a given Rate. The Rate is a ratio of how much a particular quantity changes over time. Degree compares the the given Rate with a standard or expected rate for a similar Event. The Agent is a participant in the event who is responsible for the Rate.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event that occurs at a certain Rate.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: How much a particular salient quantity involved in an Event changes over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterization of the Rate that is not covered by another more specific FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: A participant in the Event who is responsible for the indicated Rate.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: A name of the Rate of or description of the particular category the Rate belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree compares the given rate for the standard or familiar rate for a given Event.

annotations.definition: A Ratifier responds to a Proposal constructed by another party to make it binding over the Ratifier's jurisdiction or constituency.

frameElementDefinitions.Ratifier: The individual or body that confirms a Proposal negotiated or created by another party, causing it to become binding in a jurisdiction represented by the Ratifier. In the case of direct democratic ratification, the jurisdiction and the Ratifier are the same.
frameElementDefinitions.Proposal: The idea or course of action proposed by another individual or body which the Ratifier endorses and makes binding.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Ratifier makes the Proposal binding.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the Ratifier ratifies the Proposal.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is the goal of the Ratifier when they ratify the Proposal.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the cognitive cause of the Ratifier's ratification.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the allowing which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Ratifier that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Ratifier whereby they make binding the Proposal.

annotations.definition: A Reader visually examines a Text, typically in a linear order, to understand its content. In place of the Text, a Topic may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Reader: The one who examines a Text to understand it.
frameElementDefinitions.Text: The entity that contains linguistic symbols.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Sought_phenomenon: A particular characteristic or thing that the Reader intends to process. Read Chapter 7 only for the major concepts..
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the reading event that implicates details of how the event unfolds or is carried out (e.g. "the same way"); additionally used for descriptions of the Reader that also affect the event (e.g. "eagerly").
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act that allows the Reader to read in the specified manner.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is the reason for which the Reader reads the Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the reading event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the reading event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Textual_location: The context wherein the Reader reads.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The idea that an implicit Text or texts focuses on.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: An assessment of how many iterations of (the specified kind of) reading occur per time.

annotations.definition: A Speaker interprets a written Text and speaks the contents, usually to an Addressee. The contents may be the Text verbatim or a representation of the text.

frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The person or people to whom the Speaker is directing the reading of the Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person who reads the Text aloud.
frameElementDefinitions.Text: The source from which the Speaker reads.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Speaker reads the Text aloud.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The medium from which the Text is read.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the reading takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The result that the Speaker is trying to achieve by reading aloud.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the reading takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The method by which the Text is read aloud by the Speaker.

annotations.definition: The Reader attends to a Text to process its information. Sometimes a particular kind of Sought_phenomenon is sought in the Text.

frameElementDefinitions.Reader: The one who examines a Text to understand it.
frameElementDefinitions.Text: The entity that contains linguistic symbols.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Sought_phenomenon: A particular characteristic or thing that the Reader intends to process. Read Chapter 7 only for the major concepts..
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act that allows the Reader to read in the specified manner.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is the reason for which the Reader reads the Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the reading event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the reading event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: The context wherein the Reader reads a particular Text.

annotations.definition: A volitional Agent is responding to a State_of_affairs by performing some Action.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who responds to a State_of_Affairs by performing some Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The action that the Agent performs in response to a State_of_Affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The eventuality that motivates the Agent"s performing a particular Action in response to it.

annotations.definition: An Arguer presents a Content, along with Support, to an Addressee. The Content may refer elliptically to a course of action or it may refer to a proposition that the Addressee is to believe. Some lexical units (e.g. "prove") indicate the speaker's belief about the Content.

frameElementDefinitions.Content: The Arguer wants the Addressee to entertain the Content, which can be a proposition to be believed or a course of action to be taken, based on the Support presented.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This FE identifies the physical or abstract setting in which the Content is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Arguer: The Arguer presents the Content before the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: Addressee is the entity before whom the Arguer presents a Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Support: Support lends epistemic support to a claim, or provides a reason for a course of action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifes the Manner in which an Arguer argues.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Arguer presents Content to an Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a phrase describing a participant in an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an arguing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an arguing event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which an Arguer makes an argument for the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the location where an argument is made by an Arguer.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The period of time for which the Arguer presents his or her reasoning to the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: An entity related to the Content.

annotations.definition: A Speaker conveys a Message to an Experiencer in order to reassure them about a Situation; Medium and the Means of the communication also may be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The sentient entity that uses language in the written or spoken modality to reassure another person.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer receives reassurance from the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message is a proposition or set of propositions that the Speaker wants the Experiencer to believe in order to feel reassured.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation is the problem plaguing the Experiencer, about which the Speaker reassures them.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes the state of the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Speaker reassures the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Speaker reassures the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the reassurance takes place.

annotations.definition: An individual or group, the Rebel, rejects the authority of the Current_leadership, and attempts to bring them out of power. The rebellion may, but need not be violent. Rather than expressing rebellion against the Current_leadership, the lexical units in this frame may appear with expressions describing the Current_order, the system of rule which the Rebels reject.

frameElementDefinitions.Rebel: The individual or group within a society that attempts to overturn the Current_leadership, and possibly also the Current_order of governance.
frameElementDefinitions.Current_leadership: The people currently in power, whom the Rebels attempt to overthrow.
frameElementDefinitions.Current_order: The currently-existing system of authority or government.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the rebellion.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the rebellion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Rebel rebels against the authorities.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Rebel opposes and fights against the Current_leadership.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the Rebel or the rebellion not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.New_order: The rebellion aims to install a New_order as their leader. This FE is more frequent with noun targets.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: A result of the rebellion.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time of the rebellion.

annotations.definition: A (potential) Visitor arrives at a location associated with a (potential) Host. The Host permits the Visitor to enter the location, and allows the Visitor to avail him/herself of available resources, in order to pursue some goal. The visit is often for social or entertainment reasons. After a temporary stay at the Host's location, the Visitor departs.

frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host allows the Visitor to remain with him/her to acheive a goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Visitor: The Visitor temporarily remains with the Host in order to achieve his/her purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal of the Host in allowing the Visitor to enter the Host's location.

annotations.definition: A Recipient comes into possession of the Theme as a result of the joint action of the Donor and the Recipient. The Receiving frame like the Giving frame requires that the Donor first has possession of the Theme and that following the transfer, the Donorno longer has the Theme and the Recipient does.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The person who acts to come into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person who gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity that changes possession, beginning in the possession of the Donor and ending up with the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose_of_donor: The aim of the Donor, which they believe will be accomplished by giving the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The act that the Recipient performs which brings about their reception of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Recipient comes into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Recipient comes into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transfer: The system whereby the Theme travels (literally or metaphorically) so that the Recipient comes into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Recipient or Theme during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The character of the Recipient which saliently affects the action, or a general description of the action itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Countertransfer: A good given or a service rendered by the Recipient, in return for wich the Theme is given to the Recipient by the Donor.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: When the Theme is received physically by the Recipient from the Donor, the Path of motion of the Theme may be specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose_of_theme: What the Recipient intends to do with the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: A role that the Theme is intended to fill by the Donor.

annotations.definition: The sequence beginning with the Recipient not having the Theme and ending with the Recipient in possession of the Theme.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The person who acts to come into possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person who gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity that changes possession, beginning in the possession of the Donor and ending up with the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The aim of the Recipient, which they believe will be accomplished by accepting the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The act that the Recipient performs which brings about their reception of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transfer: The system whereby the Theme travels (literally or metaphorically) so that the Recipient comes into possession of the Theme.

annotations.definition: This frame characterizes states-of-affairs with Protagonists in relations with each other that may be viewed symmetrically. When the Protagonists are equally prominent, each equally serving to identify the others, they are expressed together as Protagonists. When one of the Protagonists serves to define the other (more or less as a Ground), it is called Protagonist_2, and the other is called Protagonist_1 (i.e. the Figure).

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist_1: The particpant in a reciprocal event that is encoded as the subject of an active-form sentence or as a by-PP in a passive. `
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist_2: The participant in a reciprocal event that is coded in a with-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonists: The jointly expressed participants in a reciprocal activity.

annotations.definition: An Agent sets down in permanent form information about the occurrence of a Phenomenon. Alternatively, the Agent records that an Entity had a particular Value for one of its Attributes.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the sentient entity that creates a permanent record of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon is the entity or situation in the world of which a Agent becomes aware.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifes the action whereby the Agent creates a record of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent creates the record of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The goal that the Agent has in mind in creating a record of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Agent uses an Instrument in order to create a record of the Phenomenon or to measure an Entity's Value for a specified Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: That feature of the Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The Value of the Entity that is recorded for the specified Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: An Entity for which the Agent records the Value of an Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity in which the Agent creates a record of their impression of the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Language: The symbolic system used by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent sets down the facts.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Phenomenon has been documented.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The amount of times the Agent records something per unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE denotes a duration in which the recording event occurred iteratively.

annotations.definition: A Record that contains a permanent account of Data. This Record may be maintained by a Recorder for a certain Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Record: The Record is the entity in which events or accounts are permanently recorded.
frameElementDefinitions.Data: The Data is the information which is recorded in the Record. It is often expressed as a prepositional phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Recorder: The Recorder is the person who records the Data in the Record.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity is the person or institution for which the Record is maintained.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The Type refers to the kind of Record being kept.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Record.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: What a Record is made of (if applicable).
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is the language in which a Record is composed, or the physical medium (radio, ink) in which it is distributed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The region where the Record is sold, circulated, or otherwise popular
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The Time at which the Record was created.
frameElementDefinitions.Title: The Title of a Record.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is what the Record is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which the Record was produced; its purpose.

annotations.definition: These words describe the recovery or healing of a Patient from an Affliction without reference to the influence of any Treatment or Healer (see the Cure frame).

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient may either be expressed as the External Argument or, as in Health.Cure, may occur as the possessor of the Affliction (an example of FE conflation):
frameElementDefinitions.Affliction: This FE is the injury, pain or disease experienced by the Patient. Affliction occurs either as an External Argument of verbs or as a PP Complement of nouns or verbs:
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: Where Body Part is expressed as a separate constituent (rather than in phrases such as the wound on Pat's arm), it occurs as an External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Patient's state diverges from their former illness.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Patient that results in their recovery.
frameElementDefinitions.Company: An individual or group of individuals that the Patient is generally in the presence of during convalescence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the recovery takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the recovery takes place.

annotations.definition: An intentional Agent or a causing event (Cause) event causes a Theme which is already in motion to take a different Path or Direction, or reach a different Goal than the Original_path.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity that is in motion, and changes path due to the action of an Agent or due to a Cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Original_path: The route that the Theme was originally headed for. In cases when the Theme is an intentional actor or is controlled by an intentional actor, the Original_path correlates with the (former) plan of the actor. In other cases, the Original_path corresponds to what would be expected for the path of the Theme from general principles or physics. (In some cases, the Original_path may indicate the intended destination, and not just the path, of the Theme.)
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the Theme ends up travelling after being diverted.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The location the Theme ends up in after being redirected.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the redirection of motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the redirection which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Theme or Agent during the redirection.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location at which the redirection occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the current deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that does not necessarily correspond to the Goal. Often Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the Theme, the orientation imposed by an implicit observer, or the inherent orientation of the scene.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Agent intends to bring about by redirecting the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: An individual that determines the change in course of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event that brings about the change in the Path of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The method by which the Agent redirects the Theme.

annotations.definition: In a text, a Source_of_information is given that provides a reader of the text with further Information relevant to the text. In this frame the author and reader are completely deprofiled, with the Source_of_information made salient.

frameElementDefinitions.Source_of_information: The Source_of_information is referred to as a source of information related to the current text.
frameElementDefinitions.Information: The Source_of_information contains Information that the reader is directed to.

annotations.definition: A Speaker uses a particular linguistic form, a Name to refer to an Entity. The Entity often is an interlocutor but may be somebody that the Speaker is talking about, or even just a physical object. Note that we include titles such as "Sir" in the Name category.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person who uses the Name to refer to the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The entity that is designated by the Speaker with the Name used.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The linguistic form used by the Speaker to refer to the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point of time or interval when the Speaker uses the Name.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Describes the Name.

annotations.definition: Because it no longer functions adequately under the given circumstances, an Agent (or non-Agentive Cause) undertakes steps to change the structural makeup of a complex Entity with interdependent parts, for instance an institution or a system of laws. The Degree of change is typically gradual and partial rather than revolutionary and total. Note that with some LUs the Entity can be unexpressed when it is identical to the Agent.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity that effects a change in the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the change of the Entity is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Entity differs from its former state at the end of the reform.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event causing the change of the Entity which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly), general descriptions comparing events (the same way), and salient characteristics of the Agent that also affect the action (carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which reform event logically follows. In many cases, the state of affairs denoted is specifically one that the Agent takes as a reason for reforming the entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: This FE denotes a complex, abstract, human-created entity with interdependent parts which has either deteriorated or is no longer adequate for its purpose given a change in circumstances and which the Agent therefore wants to modify.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by effecting a change in the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_category: The category of which the Entity is a member before the Agent causes a change in its makeup.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_category: The category that the Entity belongs to after it has undergone the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the steps or actions directly enabling reform.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: An alternative description of expressed action
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE is used for phrases that describe the event of reforming as a whole .
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE describes the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the reform process.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time over which reforming takes place
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: This FE is for non-human causes, such as a processes or institutions, responsible for reform.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the reformation occurs.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer has a Judgment of an Evaluee, expressing how high or low their regard for the Evaluee is.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the one that makes a Judgment. This role is typically expressed as the External Argument (or in a by-PP in passives).
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: Evaluee is the person or thing that a judgment is made with respect to. With verbs this FE is typically expressed as Object:
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: There may be a constituent expressing the Explanation for the Cognizer's judgment. It is usually a for-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is used for the capacity in which the Evaluee is judged, and is expressed in as-PPs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies any expression describing the state of a participant in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Cognizer's judgment which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions of events (the same way, presumptuously, secretly).
frameElementDefinitions.Extent_of_acclaim: The Extent_of_acclaim is a description of the number or locales of the Cognizers.
frameElementDefinitions.Judgment: A description (from the point of view of the Cognizer) of the position of the Evaluee on a scale of approval.

annotations.definition: A Portal, Exterior, Surface, Boundary, and Interior are picked out relative to a Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Interior: A region of space located inside a Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the Interior, Surface, or Boundary.
frameElementDefinitions.Surface: The region located outside of, but in contact with, the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Boundary: The region associated with the boundary of the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Exterior: The region which is not located inside or in contact with the Ground.

annotations.definition: The action of an Agent or the occurrence of a Cause event returns an Entity to an earlier state of vigor and strength.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The concrete or abstract thing that is brought into an earlier state in which it was more effective and powerful.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent puts the Entity into a state of renewed vigor.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent brings about the change in the Entity's state.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent brings about the revitalization of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent causes the renewal of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the action that the Agent performs in order so as to bring about the reinvigoration of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain within which the Entity is affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that an Entity is caused to return to a more effective state.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event or state whose occurrence brings about the Entity's return to a vigorous state.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole, either appraisals of the event, or descriptions of the rejuvenation event as role fillers in other frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The device used by the Agent to help accomplish the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is used for the capacity in which the Entity is Rejuvinated, and is expressed in as-PPs.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The end state of the Rejuvination action.

annotations.definition: A Concept_1 is related to Concept_2 by some Evidence.

frameElementDefinitions.Concept_1: The concept which is linked to Concept_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Concept_2: The concept which is tied to Concept_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Point_of_view: The point of view from which the connection exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Evidence: The phenomenon that points to or proves the connection between Concept_1 and Concept_2. Generally expressed as an external.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the connection exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree of connection between the Concepts.
frameElementDefinitions.Specification: A further specification on the linkage.

annotations.definition: A relation holds between Entity_1 and Entity_2.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity_1: An entity with a relationship to Entity_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity_2: The entity with a relationship to Entity_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Entities: The things that are related to each other. Collective expression of the FEs Entity_1 and Entity_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Relation_type: A characterization of the relation that determines what model is active for further reasoning.

annotations.definition: Two sentient beings have a permanent or semi-permanent Relation to each other. This relation may be symmetrically construed as between the Individuals or it may be construed assymetrically, such that Individual_1 is identified by their relation to the (implicitly better-known) Individual_2.

frameElementDefinitions.Individual_1: The individual being described in terms of Individual_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Individual_2: The individual whose identity serves to identify Individual_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Relation: The type of connection between the Individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: The individuals in a (symmetrically construed) Relation to each other.

annotations.definition: This is a general frame that identifies a Profiled_region whose configuration is specified by an image schema (specified in more specific child frames) and located relative to a Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The region which is located relative to the Ground and which is shaped according to an image schema.
frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: Salient parts that make up a Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Profiled_region is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Indicates the action (or causer) which brings the features of the Profiled_region about. This FE should also include words that indicate that there was no salient formation.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Profiled_region is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Related_event: An event related to the Profiled_region in some way other than (1) Formational_cause (which is a separate FE) or an intended use (which would place the instance in Locale_by_use), or (3) a defining event (which would place the instance in Locale_by_event).
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The activity that is carried out in the Profiled_region.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the Profiled_region.

annotations.definition: The Focal_feature is defined in relation to a Landmark_feature, either as a particular part, or as an immediately bordering entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location that the Focal_feature is a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Formational_cause: Indicates the action (or causer) which brings the features of the Focal_feature about.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_feature: A stable bounded area defined by its relation to another landform. This FE is incorporated in each LU in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: This FE is used for the Names of Focal_features.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that a Focal_feature is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The Type is the subtype of natural feature.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description, characteristic, or property of the Focal_feature which is not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_feature: The landform with respect to which the Focal_feature is defined.

annotations.definition: A Locale is defined politically to be the head of state of another, larger political locale, the Container_possessor.

frameElementDefinitions.Constituent_parts: The materials or Constituent_parts that make up a Locale.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The political locale that the Locale is a the head of.
frameElementDefinitions.Locale: The politically or administratively-defined locale that functions as the head of state for the containing entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: This FE is used for the Names of Locales.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: A place that the Locale is located with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description, characteristic, or property of the Locale which is not covered by more specific FEs.
frameElementDefinitions.Population: The people living in the jurisdiction of the locale, typically expressed with a PP-of.

annotations.definition: A quantity, the Numerator, can be understood in terms of how it relates to another quantity, the Denominator; the comparison of these two numbers is called the Rate. The nouns in this frame may denote the Rate unless the referent is specifically expressed. This frame is a general description of the relationship between any two dependent quantities; children of this frame may describe situations in which the scale of the Numerator and/or the Denominator is fixed.

frameElementDefinitions.Rate: The number which represents the relative quantities of the Numerator and the Denominator.
frameElementDefinitions.Numerator: Identifies the quantity whose magnitude is determined relative to the Denominator.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_entity: The set of things to which the Rate pertains.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characteristic of the Rate that is not covered by another more specific FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: A name of the Rate of or description of the particular category the Rate belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Denominator: The quantity whose magnitude is determined relative to the Numerator.

annotations.definition: These words relate to the relative ordering of two events or times. There are two major types of relations evoked by the words of this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Focal_occasion: The Focal_occasion is the event which is being located in time with respect to some other event: The Focal_occasion is profiled, in contrast to the Landmark_occasion, which is backgrounded.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_occasion: The Landmark_occasion is the event with respect to which the Focal_occasion is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Interval: Frequently, the Interval between the Reference and Focal Occasions is expressed: The article in the Times closely followed the singer's announcement that he was retiring.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE is used for the Explanation or explanation for the Focal_occasion being early or late.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_participant: The Focal_participant is the participant in the Focal_occasion which is profiled as being in a relation of relative time to the Landmark_occasion.

annotations.definition: A Captor ends the captivity or inhibition of the motion of a Theme from the Location_of_confinement. The release is in accord with the plans of the Captor.

frameElementDefinitions.Captor: The Captor is the individual who ends the confinement or captivity of the Theme. The release of the Theme is in accord with the plans of the Captor.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the person or object in motion released by the Captor.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the release which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Captor that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Captor which brings about the release.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation identifies the reason for which the Captor are releasing the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose identifies the purpose for which the Captor release the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Theme ceases to be confined.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time identifies when the Captor release the Theme .
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_confinement: The location to which the Theme's motion was formerly inhibited.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the Releasing event occurs.

annotations.definition: The Authorities release a Suspect who has been held under suspicion of having committed a crime.

frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities are those who have the official authority to release a Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: The Suspect is the person released by the Authorities, having been held under suspicion of having committed a crime.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the Authorities release the Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means identifies the means by which the Authorities release the Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation identifies the reason for which the Authorities are releasing the Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose identifies the purpose for which the Authorities release the Suspect.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place identifies the place where the Authorities release the Suspect .
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time identifies when the Authorities release the Suspect .
frameElementDefinitions.Holding_location: The place that the Suspect is no longer confined to.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist needs a Means action performed for their Benefit. The relevant Means action is often evoked only by reference to an Intermediary who performs it. Also, if the Protagonist performs the Means action themselves, the Instrument that they use may be referred to in place of the Means Note that when one of the three interrelated FEs Means, Instrument and Intermediary is realized overtly, the other two are blocked from overt realization as clause-level arguments of the target.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The person or thing whose needs are served by the Means action.
frameElementDefinitions.Benefit: An action or state that the Means action brings about and from which the Protagonist profits.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the achievement of the Benefit depends on the Means action.
frameElementDefinitions.Intermediary: The person or thing that performs a Means action to the Benefit of the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An object that the Protagonist uses to realize a Benefit.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action or state that the Protagonist wants to bring about. It is more or less the Benefit viewed in relation to the Protagonist rather than the Means.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed either by the Protagonist or an Intermediary that has a Benefit for the Protagonist attached to it.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The conditions when the Protagonist needs to rely on an Instrument or Intermediary.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer takes a certain Expectation to be a reliable fact, such that their plans can be based on it.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: An individual that considers the Expectation certain.
frameElementDefinitions.Expectation: The fact which the Cognizer depends on in planning for the future.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The situation in which the Cognizer can count on the Expectation.

annotations.definition: A Believer has faith in a piece of Content or an Element which fits into a religious belief system made up of many articles of faith. The Content is not necessarily tied to direct sensory experience. The system of beliefs may be enshrined by the members of a social group

frameElementDefinitions.Believer: The Believer is the person whose religious beliefs are in question.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The Content is the object of the Believer's religious conviction. Content can be expressed as a finite clause or in a in-PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Believer is convinced of the usefulness and validity of their religious beliefs .
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Believer knows or thinks something.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is a function that an Element of the faith is believed to serve.
frameElementDefinitions.Element: A predefined, fixed idea to which reference is made throughout the belief system.

annotations.definition: A Resource is depleted by some process, resulting in there being only a Remainder in existence some time into or after the process. The original Resource may be indicated metonymically via reference to an Original_owner. The Location where the Resource is found may be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Remainder: The part of the Resource that the Process has not yet applied to.
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The original set of items to which the Depletive_process applies one after the other.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The place where the Resource is found.
frameElementDefinitions.Original_owner: The individual who owned the Resource that is being depleted.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the amount of the Resource that is let is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: An individual who may use the Remainder for his benefit.
frameElementDefinitions.Original_context: A temporal setting or event which metonymically stands for the entities that were in existence then. The Remainder is part of the Original_context. This FE occurs especially when abstract entities such as social institutions are seen as Resources.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer calls up an episodic memory of past Experience or an Impression of a Salient_entity formed on the basis of past experience. The Cognizer may also remember the Salient_entity in a particular State, which serves as a frame of reference in the Cognizer's mind. When attention is focused on a Salient_entity, then mention of a global Experience is excluded and typically, but not always, either a State or Impression of the Salient_entity is presented.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: A person who recalls memories of past experience or impressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: A person or thing that the Cognizer remembers as being involved in some state or event or as having some property.
frameElementDefinitions.Experience: A past event or state that the Cognizer witnessed and is now recalling. The Experience may be a specific event or state or a vaguer period that is treated as a unit.
frameElementDefinitions.Impression: A property that the Cognizer attributes to the Salient_entity based on their past experience of the Salient_entity or a category to which the Salient_entity is assigned.
frameElementDefinitions.State: A property or role that a Salient_entity actually held and which frames the memories that the Cognizer has of the Salient_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Vividness: The degree of detail and/or immediacy of a remembered Experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: A situation in which the Cognizer encountered the Salient_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Cognizer remembers the experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Cognizer that affect the action (fondly).
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Cognizer remembers the experience.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer retains facts in memory and is able to retrieve them. The Mental_content may be presented in clearly propositional form as a finite clause. It may also take the form of an embedded question or be a concealed question in the form of a simple NP.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The person who retains and retrieves information.
frameElementDefinitions.Mental_content: Mental_content stored by and accessible to the Cognizer. It may derive from experience or activity or simply from being informed of it.
frameElementDefinitions.Accuracy: The extent to which the remembered Mental_content matches the information or experience that it is based on.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Cognizer retrieves Mental_content or forgets it.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: An entity, concrete or abstract, or an event that the remembered Mental_content is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Context: A situation or a text from which the Mental_content was acquired.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer thinks of and performs an Action that is a self- or other-imposed task or some other kind of desirable behavior. The Action may involve a Salient_entity in some way affected by the Cognizer. If a Salient_entity is mentioned, the Action is left unexpressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The person who thinks of or fails to think of an obligatory or desireable Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: An Action that the Cognizer is supposed to carry out. This may be a Task imposed specifically on the Cognizer or it may be self- or socially imposed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A Time when the Cognizer remembers or fails to carry out an Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: The Salient_entity is a person or thing that the Cognizer needs to affect or effect when carrying out the Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE describes how often the Action is performed by the Cognizer.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Theme to move away from a location, the Source. The Sourceis profiled by the words in this frame, just as the Goal is profiled in the Placing frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person (or other force) that causes the Theme to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: Theme is the object that changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the initial location of the Theme, before it changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the location where the Theme ends up. This Frame Element is not profiled by words in this frame, though it may occasionally be expressed, as with the word remove.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an act whereby the Agent achieves the removal.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The non-Agentive cause of the removing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion. This frame element occurs throughout the motion domain but is very infrequent in Motion_Noise.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_motion: The Means_of_motion expresses the conveyance with which the Agent effects the motion of the Theme. The Means_of_motion holds and conveys the Theme. Means_of_motions can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: The Cotheme is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the removal takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the removal takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the state of affairs expressed by the target, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The tool the Agent uses to remove the Theme from its Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
