frameElementDefinitions.Co-timed_event: An event the punctuates the larger Removing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the Agent or Cause removes the Theme from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: To what end the Agent or Cause removes the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE is used to indicate the Direction that the Theme travels when it is removed.

annotations.definition: The abstract scene in which an Agent affects a Source and a Theme so that the Theme ends up not co-located with the Source.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the entity that does the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme, when overtly expressed, invariably occurs in a PP Complement headed by "of".
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the region or container which is emptied of something. In this frame, the Source occurs as an NP Object.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is any expression which tells where the Theme ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depicitive phrase describing either the Agent or Theme of the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the Agent emptying the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This is the object with which the Agent empties the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-sentient force which instigates the emptying.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the emptying takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the emptying takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Agent affects an Artifact so that it is no longer capable of performing its inherent function.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity causes the Artifact to not function.
frameElementDefinitions.Artifact: The device that no longer works.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An event that the Agent hopes to bring about by breaking an Artifact.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that the Agent performs which results in the Artifact being inoperable.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An inanimate entity or force that renders the Artifact non-functional.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place at which the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A holistic description of the event (e.g. the same way), including depictions of the Agent in terms of how the state of the Agent affects the overall action (e.g maliciously).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: Meaning: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often. It is to be distinguished from Iteration, which pertains simply to the number of times an event occurs--Iteration expressions answer the question how many times.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Artifact is (or has been) rendered nonfunctional.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describing the state of particpant while lack of functionality is being rendered.

annotations.definition: A Lessee gains the use of Goods owned by a Lessor by payment to the Lessor.

frameElementDefinitions.Lessee: The individual that gains temporary use of the Goods by paying the Lessor
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is an item which is temporarily exchanged for Money in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for tempory use of the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In isome cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Lessor: The individual who has permanent ownership of the Goods and allows them to be used by the Lessee temporarily.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location the renting takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the renting is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state-of-affairs from which the renting logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the purchasing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Lessee that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The individual intended by the Lessee to receive the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Goods are (or have been) in the Lessee's possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Lessee's intended purpose for the Goods.

annotations.definition: A Lessor who owns some Goods allows a Lessee to use the Goods temporarily in exchange for Money.

frameElementDefinitions.Lessee: The Lessee has Money and wants the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is the item which is temporarily exchanged for Money.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Rate: In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Lessor: The Lessor has possession of the Goods and temporarily exchanges them for Money from a Lessee.
frameElementDefinitions.Unit: This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured. Generally, it occurs in a by-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the leasing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state-of-affairs from which the renting logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the renting takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the renting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Lessor that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Lessor after the sale has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Goods are (or have been) in the Lessee's possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Lessee's intended purpose for the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Back: This FE indicates that the selling act reverses an earlier separate act in which the current Lessor rented or bought the Goods from the current Lessee.
frameElementDefinitions.Relay: This FE indicates that the current leasing constitues a re-lease by the current Lessor of Goods that they bought or rented earlier from a third person (not the Lessee of the current leasing act).

annotations.definition: A Speaker declares that they will no longer be involved with the Content. Such a declaration implies that the Speaker has decided that association with the Content is somehow undesirable.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the person who communicates their cessation of association with the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the Speaker is communicating. When this FE is expressed, it often appears in a prepositional phrase introduced by "to", or as a direct object.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The idea that the Speaker no longer associates with, whether it be an activity, a propostion, or a group.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the renunciation which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Speaker that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Speaker makes a statement of renunciation.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or physical state of the Speaker that leads to their renunciation.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Speaker to transmit the statement of renunciation.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Speaker during the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the statement is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The FE specifies the place where the statement takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe the process of making amends for some (wrongful) action (the Injury) committed by the Wrongdoer. In this process, in order to appease the Injured_party or to follow laws or customs, the Wrongdoer performs some Benefiting_action for the Injured_party or gives the Injured_party some Gift.

frameElementDefinitions.Injured_party: The Injured_party was affected by some previous wrongful act of the Wrongdoer and who should benefit from the reparations.
frameElementDefinitions.Injury: The Injury is the injurious action that the Wrongdoer committed against the Injured_party. Although conceptually present, this FE need not always be realized.
frameElementDefinitions.Wrongdoer: The Wrongdoer makes amends to the Injured_party for the Injury.
frameElementDefinitions.Benefiting_action: The Benefiting_action is performed by the Wrongdoer to make amends.
frameElementDefinitions.Gift: Gift identifies the entity that the Wrongdoer gives to the Injured_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner marks any description of the Wrongdoer's making of reparations not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification, (e.g. probably), and general descriptions comparing events (e.g. the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of a Wrongdoer that also affect the action ( e.g. presumptuously).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Wrongdoer makes amends.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when amends are made.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Wrongdoer makes restitution to the Injured_party.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Wrongdoer makes amends for the Injury .

annotations.definition: A Debtor gives a Loaner Money in return for having borrowed Money at an earlier time. Alternatively, the type of Loan can be expressed as the object of repayment.

frameElementDefinitions.Loaner: The person who is owed Money by the Debtor.
frameElementDefinitions.Debtor: The person who is giving Money to the Loaner to return an earlier borrowed amount.
frameElementDefinitions.Money: The amount of Money that the Debtor gives to the Loaner, which may or may not be the full amount of the Loan.
frameElementDefinitions.Loan: A situation or a representative of a situation prior to repayment in which the Debtor uses the Loaner's money.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The level to which the Loan can be considered repaid in its entirety.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Debtor gives the Loaner money.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the Money is transfered.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: How the Debtor gives Money to the Loaner.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The physical location where the repayment takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: What the Debtor does in order to repay the Loaner.

annotations.definition: A Defender successfully repels the aggressive attempt of takeover by an Enemy.

frameElementDefinitions.Enemy: The Enemy is the entity that fails to overtake the Defender.
frameElementDefinitions.Defender: The Defender is the land, government, and its people that the Enemy fails to seize.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the intensity and extent of the repelling event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner in which the Defender repels the Enemy.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action by which the repelling event is made possible through the efforts of the Defender.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Defender repels the Enemy.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Defender succeeds to repel the Enemy.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason why the Defender repels the Enemy.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The tool used by the Defender to repel the Enemy.
frameElementDefinitions.Invasion_act: This FE refers to the Invasion_act perpetrated by the Enemy.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE describes the outcome of the repelling event.

annotations.definition: An Agent changes the filler of a Role by placing a New filler in the position after the Old filler ceases to occupy the position. Often this entails the Agent removing the Old filler. In most cases the Role is implicit.

frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the replacement.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The conscious entity, generally a person, that performs actions resulting in the New entity occupying the position.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that interacts with an New entity so that it ends up in the position.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies where the Agent installs the New entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent places the New entity in a position.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent installs the New entity.
frameElementDefinitions.New: The New entity is the person or thing that the Agent sets to fill a Role.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics or states of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in placing the New filler in a position.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the New entity is affected by the Agent. Degrees of replacement are generally only possible when there are multiple New and Old entities.
frameElementDefinitions.Old: The entity that formerly occupied the position.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The category that the New and Old entities fit into as a result of the function they serve.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Informer informs the Authorities of the illegal or otherwise improper Behavior of the Wrongdoer.

frameElementDefinitions.Informer: The Informer reports the activities of the Wrongdoer to the Authorities.
frameElementDefinitions.Wrongdoer: The Wrongdoer has his or her activities reported to the Authorities.
frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities are the officials, government or otherwise, who handle misbehavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The Behavior is the action or actions for which the Wrongdoer is reported.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Informer reports the Behavior of the Wrongdoer.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location where the reporting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose is the purpose for which the Informer reports the Wrongdoer.

annotations.definition: A Representative is authorized by a Represented to communicate on behalf of the Represented.

frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The Origin is the place where the Representative was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.
frameElementDefinitions.Persistent_characteristic: The Persistent_characteristic is a physiological characteristic or personality trait of the Representative which is concieved of as persisting over time.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor is a temporary condition of the Representative.
frameElementDefinitions.Age: The Age is the length of time the Representative has been alive.
frameElementDefinitions.Ethnicity: The Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the Representative belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Context_of_acquaintance: An expression indicating the context with which the Representative is associated.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: Rank is the position in a hierarchy that a Representative occupies within an organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Place_of_employment: This FE identifies the Place where the Representative works.
frameElementDefinitions.Represented: The Represented authorizes the Representative to communicate on its behalf.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE identifies the Type of Representative.
frameElementDefinitions.Compensation: The Compensation is the payment that the Representative receives for performing a task.
frameElementDefinitions.Contract_basis: The Contract_basis describes the conditions of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period, or payment basis.

annotations.definition: An Entity represents some Phenomenon through its existence and/or defining characteristics. Sometimes a Cognizer, a people or group of people who believe in the representation, is expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The event, idea, or emotion evoked by the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes the Degree to which the Entity represents the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period in which the representing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE describes the means through which the representing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: An object that has some association with a Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person or group of people who believe in the representation.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency denotes how often the Entity is associated with the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the represenation logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the representing event occurs.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Speaker asks an Addressee for something, or to carry out some action.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker, as in other communication frames, is generally an External Argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: In this frame the Addressee can either occur as an NP Object (usually with a following complement clause) or as a PP Complement of verbs or nouns.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message is the content of the request that can be expressed as a quote, in a finite or non-finite clausal Complement, an NP Object or PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is rarely expressed in this frame but can occur as a PP Complement headed by about.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is the physical entity or channel used to transmit the Message. The Medium is frequently expressed as a PP Complement headed by on or in.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner indicates the manner in which the Message is delivered.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Speaker that results in a request being made and received.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The interval during which the Speaker makes the request.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This extra-thematic FE applies to participants that are expected to derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs, or a state of affairs that holds, at a time that includes some time that the request was occuring. This often makes us of "as" and "while".
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.

annotations.definition: A Customer requests an Entity from a Supplier. The Customer can order the Entity himself or via a Medium.

frameElementDefinitions.Customer: The Customer is the person or business ordering the Entity from the Supplier.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The object ordered by the Customer.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The channel used by the Customer to transmit the request.
frameElementDefinitions.Supplier: The person or organization from whom the Customer orders the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Customer requested the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The person or organization which benefits from the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the request is placed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Customer that results in a request being made.

annotations.definition: Unless a particular Required_situation obtains, Negative_consequences will follow. Alternatively, the Required_situation is required to achieve a Purpose (which avoids Negative_consequences). A set of Circumstances may be specified under which the requirement holds.

frameElementDefinitions.Negative_consequences: An event or state of affairs that results if the required Required_situation does not occur and which impacts some deprofiled entity in a way that the speaker finds undesirable.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The FE Circumstances encodes a setting, often temporal or spatial, within which the requirement holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The event or state of affairs that is achieved by bringing about the Required_situation and which entails the non-occurrance of Negative_consequences.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period during which the Required_situation is necessary to prevent negative consequences.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location within which the Required_situation is required.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why the Required_situation is necessary.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Required_situation is necessary. The Degree may either imply a modification of the direness of the Negative_consequences, the probability of the Negative_consequences (if the Required_situation is not made to hold), or both.

annotations.definition: One state of affairs, the Dependent, cannot occur without another state of affairs, the Requirement, also obtaining. This Requirement is construed to be under the control of some implicit force or actor. In an alternative construal, the Dependent state of affairs may be presented as depending on the presence of a Required_entity. Individual that profit from the occurrence of the Dependent state, Dependent_individuals, are backgrounded. The same goes for individuals (Required_individual) that are required to provide any Required_entity or to see to the occurrence of the Dependent state of affairs. The nexus between Requirement and Dependent can be qualified further in terms of the Degree of strength of the link between the two eventualities, the Place and Time where this nexus obtains, the Reason for its existence, or a specification of Condition when it applies.

frameElementDefinitions.Dependent: The state-of-affairs which cannot hold without the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Requirement: The state-of-affairs that must be present or obtain in order for the Dependent to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Dependent depends on the presence of the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state-of-affairs which militates that the Requirement is necessary for the Dependent to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Requirement is necessary for the Dependent.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: The conditions under which the the Requirement is necessary.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The field of endeavor whose nature imposes the Requirement.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place denotes a location in which the specified relation between Requirement and Dependent holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_individual: The individual in whose interest it is to have the Required_entity or for the Requirement state of affairs to obtain.
frameElementDefinitions.Required_entity: An entity that has to be present for the Dependent state of affairs to obtain
frameElementDefinitions.Required_individual: An individual that has to provide the Required_entity or see to it that the Requirement state of affairs holds.

annotations.definition: An Agent saves a Patient or an Asset from a Harmful_situation.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient being who rescues the Patient or Asset from the Harmful_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The living being who is rescued by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Asset: The entity that is judged to be valueable which might be lost or damaged by the Harmful_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Harmful_situation: An event that occurs which could result in injury or harm to the Patient or Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent rescues the Patient or Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Agent rescues the Patient or Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Agent rescues the Patient or Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The setting in which the rescuing act takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The tool with which the Agent saves the Patient or Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Agent rescues the Patient or Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent rescues the Patient or Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in performing the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Patient or Asset is actually saved.

annotations.definition: A Researcher (an individual a group or institution) attempts to answer a Question by means of consulting literature, observation, or conducting experiments in a particular Field pertinent to the Question. The Question may be underspecified in the form of a Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Researcher: The people engaged in a research endeavor.
frameElementDefinitions.Question: A significant unanswered open proposition which the Researcher is attempting to resolve.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: A phenomenon or idea involved in a Question.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the research is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The experimentations, observations, or other procedures performed by the Researcher in order to find an answer to the Question.
frameElementDefinitions.Population: A group of entities that are studied by the Researcher to answer the Question. The studied group need not be the only kind of entity that provides evidence for answering the research Question.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Researcher that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Purpose indicates an action that the Researcher intends to accomplish by performing research.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_state: The Duration_of_state is the period of time over which the research takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The physical place where the research is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The Type, or subclass of research.

annotations.definition: In this frame, a Booker brings about a situation where an Organization plans to provide Services to a Client at a particular Scheduled_time. The Organization has authority over when Services are provided to whom. The Booker usually has to communicate and negotiate with the Organization to plan the Services for a mutually agreeable Scheduled_time. Usually, there is a Payment as well.

frameElementDefinitions.Client: The entity that is intended to receive the Services of the Organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Services: The goods or labor that the Client requests the Organization preserve for future consumption or rendering.
frameElementDefinitions.Organization: The entity that enters into its records the request of the Client to retain Services in the future.
frameElementDefinitions.Scheduled_time: The future point in time when the Client arranges for the Organization to provide Services.
frameElementDefinitions.Booker: The entity that contacts the Organization directly. In many if not most cases, a Booker is also a Client, but the roles may also be separated, e.g. a travel agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event as a whole ("the same way") or a characteristic of the Booker during the action that holistically affects the event ("sloppily").
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location the Booker performs the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The situation that the Booker intends to bring about by reserving.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action or methodology used by the Booker to bring the reservation about.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the reserving event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Payment: The amount paid to the Organization in exchange for the Services.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Deformer deforms a Patient possibly against a Resistant_surface such that it undergoes a shape-change from its canonical or original shape into the Configuration, a new shape. Some of these words indicate that the Configuration is an undesirable alteration of the norm, and in such cases the lexical unit is marked with the semantic type Negative_judgement. This frame does not include senses that specifically indicate causing harm to a living being.

frameElementDefinitions.Deformer: The Deformer is the one who does the reshaping.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which the Deformer affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The FE Manner identifies the manner in which an Deformer intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The FE Means identifies the means by which the Deformer intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity acted on and that undergoes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place indicates where the Deformer intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Deformer intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The FE Time identifies when the Deformer intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the shape the Patient becomes.
frameElementDefinitions.Resistant_surface: The Resistant_surface is the surface against which a Deformer presses the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: The Locus is the locus of the reshaping action; it is essentially a simultaneous Instrument and Resistant_surface.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is an animate or inanimate entity, a force, or event that produces an effect. Volitionality is not a necessary characteristic of Causes.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The FE Iterations indicates the number of times the reshaping has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation indicates a state of affairs that the Deformer is responding to in performing the reshaping.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Patient is reshaped.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Patient which is directly affected by the reshaping.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The location that the Patient ends up in.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The conditions under which the Patient is shaped.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The state of the Patient with respect to the change that the Deformer brings about by making the adjustment.
frameElementDefinitions.Configuration: A state that holds of the Patient or a relation that holds among the constituent parts of the Patient as a result of the arranging action.

annotations.definition: This frame has to do with people (the Residents) residing in Locations, sometimes with a Co-resident.

frameElementDefinitions.Resident: The individual(s) that reside at the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The place in which somebody resides.
frameElementDefinitions.Co_resident: A person or group of people that the resident is staying with or among.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: Frequency at which event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of a Resident during their residence.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Resident that enables them to reside in the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE refers to the Time when the Resident resides.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Agent resolves an outstanding Problem by finding its solution, explanation, answer, etc. Alternatively, some non-agentive Cause may resolve the Problem. The Means, Degree, Time and Place may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent finds the solution or explanation to the outstanding mystery or Problem.
frameElementDefinitions.Problem: The Problem is the predicament or mystery that is resolved.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-Agentive event or state of affairs that brings about the resolution of the Problem.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an intermediate action taken by the Agent in order to resolve the Problem.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time at which the Problem is resolved.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE indicates the location at which the Problem is resolved.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Agent or Cause resolves the problem.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the resolution, such as secondary effects ( quietly ) or comparisons of events ( the same way ). In agentive cases, Manner may also describe salient characteristics of the Agent which affect the action. ( carefully, dilligently, deliberately etc. )
frameElementDefinitions.Resultant_situation: The Resultant_situation is any situation which arises as a result of resolving a Problem .
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of resolving a Problem .
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The Containing_event denotes an event or state of affairs that occurs or holds at a time that includes the time during which the resolution of the Problem occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Problem is resolved.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is an entity used for resolving the Problem .
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the resolution of a Problem .
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the length of time taken to resolve a Problem . The government resolved the beach ball crisis in just 22 hours.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role is used for the capacity in which the Agent resolves a Problem or the role of the Problem in being resolved, and is expressed in as-PPs.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: Frequency describes how often the Agent or Cause resolves this problem.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This FE identifies the physical or abstract setting in which the Problem is resolved.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: A person who participates in resolving a Problem along with the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the Agent is trying to bring about by solving the Problem in the specified way.

annotations.definition: A Speaker addresses a Proposal made by an Interlocutor, either agreeing to it or rejecting it. Agreeing entails a social responsibility on the Speaker to bring about some state of affairs encoded in the Proposal.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the person who communicates the reply or response.
frameElementDefinitions.Interlocutor: This is the person who made the Proposal to the Speaker and to whom the Speaker communicates their response. This frame element stands in for the Proposal and is only licensed when the Proposal can be understood from the context.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: As with Medium in other Communication frames, the physical entity or channel used to communicate can be expressed, usually as a PP Complement headed by "on" or "in".
frameElementDefinitions.Proposal: The Proposal is the prior communication or action to which a response is given.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Speaker that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by the Speaker responds to the Proposal .
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The response may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause, the Speaker mental or emotional state.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Speaker makes a verbal response.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time interval during which the Speaker makes their verbal response.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Speaker intends to accomplish by making a verbal response.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of response events.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: Role denotes the capacity in which the Interlocutor is judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group is the set of people within which the Respond_to_proposal is occuring.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the success or failure logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the success or failure, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: An Agent performs a Response action in consequence of a Trigger event. In many cases, a non-agentive Responding_entity causes the Response after the Trigger occurs.

frameElementDefinitions.Response: The means action whereby the Agent responds to the Trigger.
frameElementDefinitions.Responding_entity: A non-agentive entity that is impinged upon by a Trigger event to which they respond.
frameElementDefinitions.Trigger: An event that impinges upon the Agent or Responding_entity and produces a Response from it.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point in time when the Agent or Responding_entity manifests its Response to the Trigger .
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A characterization of the Responding_entity, Agent, or their Response action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Response action takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by performing the Response action.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The intentional Agent that is impinged upon by a Trigger event to which they respond.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: A particular feature of the Responding_entity or Agent that is involved in the Response action.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The capacity of of the Agent is their Response act.

annotations.definition: A Trigger elicits a Response by some Responding_entity or an Agent.

frameElementDefinitions.Responding_entity: A non-agentive entity that is impinged upon by a Trigger event to which they respond.
frameElementDefinitions.Trigger: An event that impinges upon the Agent or Responding_entity and produces a Response from it.
frameElementDefinitions.Response: The means action whereby the Agent responds to the Trigger.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The intentional Agent that is impinged upon by a Trigger event to which they respond.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A characterization of the Responding_entity, Agent, or their Response action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Response action takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by performing the Response action.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The point in time when the Agent or Responding_entity manifests its Response to the Trigger .

annotations.definition: An Agent is responsible for having intentionally performed an Act or for being the or a primary instigator behind the Act. There is often a sense of negative evaluation of the Agent for having done so.

frameElementDefinitions.Act: This FE identifies the Act that the Agent performs intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent performs the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the intentional act occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent acts intentionally.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain within which the Agent acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the Agent does the Act in a given period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time over which an Act was committed multiple times.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the Agent performing the Act.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the state of affairs expressed by the target, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE gives a description of the Intentionally_act event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Agent bears responsibility for performing the Act.

annotations.definition: One Part of a larger Whole has been mentioned, is inferrable, or is available in the discourse setting and the previously unmentioned part, the Rest, is now mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Part: A Part of a larger Whole that has been mentioned in prior discourse or is available in the discourse setting.
frameElementDefinitions.Rest: The previously unmentioned part of the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The larger entitiy or group that is fully made up of the Part and the Rest.

annotations.definition: An Agent and their Action succeed or fail inasmuch as the result of an Action matches the Goal that they hope to bring about by the Action.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is what the Agent attempts to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: This FE identifies the means by which the Goal is achieved (or not).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt succeeds or fails.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent during the Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an attempt succeeds or fails..
frameElementDefinitions.Role: A participant function in a particular event or in events of a particular kind.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE is applied to expressions which indicate the prevailing physical conditions for the event, or some concurrent states of affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the success or failure logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the success or failure, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event or state of affairs that occurs or holds at a time that includes the time during which the success or failure reported by the target occurs and of which the success or failure is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Action succeeds or fails.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist which had been dead returns to life.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The being or entity that becomes living again.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the resurrection occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An explanation of how the state-of-affairs came to pass.
frameElementDefinitions.Prior_state: The state of the Protagonist before resurrection.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Protagonist which results from resurrection.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Protagonist during the resurrection.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Protagonist returns from the dead.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Agent continues to possess a Theme for a Duration of time.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who has possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The entity which the Agent possesses for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Agent is in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent keeps the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way the Agent retains possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent has possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the Agent retains the Theme.

annotations.definition: A Speaker reveals Information that was previously secret to an Addressee. In some cases, the Addressee is expected to keep the Information from other parties. The Information may be damaging to the reputation of the Speaker. Instead of (or in addition to) a Speaker, a Medium may also be mentioned. Likewise, a Topic may be stated instead of Information. Some lexical units in this frame imply that the Addressee has already been confronted with the Information.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the sentient entity that reveals the Information (whether spoken or written).
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the Information is revealed.
frameElementDefinitions.Information: Information identifies the content that the Speaker reveals to the Addressee. It can be expressed as a clause or as a noun phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject matter about which the Speaker is speaking. It normally only appears with noun targets, or in special constructions.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner is any description of the revelation which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Speaker that also affect the revelation (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully, maliciously).
frameElementDefinitions.Completeness: The degree to which Information is revealed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Speaker makes a revelation.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or physical state of the Speaker that leads to the revelation.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Speaker to transmit the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Speaker during the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the revelation is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The FE specifies the Place where the Information is revealed.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: An alternative description of expressed action.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_protagonist: This FE is used to indicate the location of a participant in cases where the multiple participants of an event are not co-located in the typical fashion. In Release 1.2, this FE has various frame-specific names including Location of perceiver, or Location of participant; it was even (incorrectly) labelled Place in some frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies an event during which the Speaker reveals Information.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_material: The medium in which the secret Information origninally resided.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole especially any descriptions of the state of affairs denoted by the target as role fillers in other frames.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE describes the length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: Frequency is defined as the number of times the Reveal_secret event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question "how often."
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the revealed secret.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns the infliction of punishment in return for a wrong suffered. An Avenger performs a Punishment on a Offender as a consequence of an earlier action by the Offender, the Injury. The Avenger inflicting thePunishment need not be the same as the Injured_Party who suffered the Injury, but the Avenger does have to share the judgment that the Offender's action was wrong. The judgment that the Offender had inflicted an Injury is made without regard to the law.

frameElementDefinitions.Avenger: The Avenger exacts revenge from the Offender for the Injury.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive identifies a phrase describing the actor of an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Offender: The Offender has committed the earlier Injury for which the Avenger seeks revenge.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument with which the revenge is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This frame element refers to the Manner in which the Avenger exacts their revenge from the Offender.
frameElementDefinitions.Punishment: The Avenger carries out a Punishment in order to exact revenge on the Offender.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the revenge occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the revenge is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Injury: The Injury is the injurious action committed by the Offender against the Injured_Party. This Frame Element need not always be realized, although it is conceptually necessary.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of inflicting punishment for a wrong suffered.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the revenge occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Injured_party: This frame element identifies the constituent that encodes who or what suffered the Injury at the hands of the Offender. Sometimes, an abstract concept such a person's honour or their blood is presented as the element that has suffered the Injury. These also constitute instances of Injured_party
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Avenger seeks revenge for the Injury.

annotations.definition: An Agent brings about a radical change in leadership by removing the Current_leadership and potentially dismantling the Current_order, outside recognized means of doing so (e.g., elections).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual or group within a society that overturns the Current_leadership.
frameElementDefinitions.Current_leadership: The people currently in power, whom the Agent attempts to overthrow.
frameElementDefinitions.Current_order: The currently-existing system of authority or government.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the revolution.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the revolution takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means by which the revolution is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Agent defeats and removes the Current_leadership or removes the Current_order.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the Agent or revolution not covered by more specific FEs.

annotations.definition: An Agent (the punisher or rewarder) performs a Response_action on an Evaluee for a Reason, the Evaluee's actions or beliefs. Means and Instrument may also be indicated. The goal of the punishment/reward is to discourage/encourage the actions or beliefs. Words in this frame presuppose that a judgment of the Evaluee has occurred and that the Evaluee is (or becomes) aware of the judgment. This judgment was performed by a cognizer which is either the same as the Agent, or, minimally, a representative of the same institution.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: Evaluee is the person or thing about whom/which a judgment has made and to whom reward/punishment is dealt. With verbs, the Evaluee is typically expressed as Object.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing the reward or punishment.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that is taken that results in punishement/reward.
frameElementDefinitions.Reason: Typically, there is a constituent expressing the Reason for the Agent's judgment. It is usually a 'for'-PP, e.g.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of the reward or punishment.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person doing the rewarding or punishing.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the reward or punishment is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose of the reward or punishment.
frameElementDefinitions.Response_action: The reward or punishment given to the Evaluee by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree of reward or punishment.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Agent of the reward or punishment.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Theme is moved by a Vehicle which is not directly under their power. The Source, Path, Goal, or Area of the motion may be indicated. The Distance traveled or the Speed of motion may also be indicated. A Route or Roadmay be present and the Manner in which the Theme moves may be given.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity which moves by means of the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Vehicle is the means of conveyance of the Theme. It is usually expressed in a PP-complement or the direct object.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes a property of the motion not related to its trajectory.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path describes the trajectory of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the endpoint of the trajectory of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The Distance is the extent of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the amount of time the motion takes.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is the location where the motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Road: The Road is the thoroughfare on which the Vehicle travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Route: The Route is the usual path that the Vehicle travles.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Theme or Vehicle during the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the riding takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: A theme that rides in the same Vehicle as the main Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Theme rides a vehicle.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist, by exerting great effort, comes to meet the pre-conditions for participating in an Activity that is judged to be difficult for them to participate in, either due to their inherent properties or due to temporary Circumstances.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist who is exerting effort to meet the pre-conditions for participating in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity that the Protagonist expends efforts towards.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Protagonist exceeds or falls short of the minimum requirements for participating in the Activity as a result of expending effort to get themselves ready.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the Protagonist exerts effort to meet the pre-conditions necessary for engaging in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An entity, event, or state of affairs that causes the Protagonist to expend the effort necessary to meet the pre-conditions for participating in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Relevant_feature: A characteristic or behavior of the Protagonist that is considered when judging whether the Protagonist might participate in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Protagonist which enables them to overcome the difficulties and engage in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Protagonist through expenditure of effort becomes capable of engaging in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Protagonist expends effort to become capable of engaging in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Protagonist that also affect the action (eagerly).

annotations.definition: An Asset is in a particular Situation, which has a likelihood of leading to or inviting a Harmful_event which will negatively affect the Asset.

frameElementDefinitions.Asset: Something judged to be desirable or valuable which might be lost or damaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Harmful_event: An event that may occur or a state which may hold which could result in the loss of or damage to the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation under which the Asset is safe or unsafe.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the actual level of security from the expected value given the Asset, the Situation, and state indicated by the target itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the degree of safeness holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place at which the degree of safeness holds.

annotations.definition: A particular Situation is likely (or unlikely) to result in a harmful event befalling an Asset. The Situation may be a state, an activity, or some focal entity that must be inferred to be part of some larger Situation involving both it and the Asset. Although the idea of a harmful event is crucial to the understanding of this frame, it is not expressible as an argument of the LUs in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Situation: The Situation which may lead to a harmful event.
frameElementDefinitions.Asset: Something judged to be desirable or valuable which might be lost or damaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the actual level of security from the expected value given the Situation and the state indicated by the target itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A particular location for which the Situation is safe. Frequently, it is to be inferred that the characteristics of the location make various Situations safe or unsafe.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Situation has the specified level of safety.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain in which the Situation is safe.
frameElementDefinitions.Dangerous_entity: A concrete or abstract entity which may come to cause the loss of, or damage to the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Asset encounters the Risky_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances in which an Asset is at risk.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns rituals performed in line with religious beliefs or tradition.

frameElementDefinitions.Leader: This is the religious or cultural leader who performs and/or leads the rite.
frameElementDefinitions.Member: The FE Member describes the member of the religious or cultural group who participates in/undergoes the rite.
frameElementDefinitions.Organization: Organization identifies the institution in which the rite is prescribed.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that is taken to perform the ritual.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing the rite
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the rite is performed
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the rite takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE identifies the Instrument used to perform a particular rite.
frameElementDefinitions.Desired_state_of_affairs: This FE identifies the state which the rite is hoped or intended to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The type of ritual or ceremony being performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Object: This FE indicates an object on which a rite is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.New_status: Rites may confer a New_status on a participant (e.g. rank, membership).
frameElementDefinitions.Guardian: The Guardian is the member of the Organization who instigates the rite for someone of which they are a guardian.

annotations.definition: This frame involves stable Roadways which connect two stable Endpoints, the Source and the Goal. Being physical objects, these Roadways may have Names and Characteristics; they are also made of a Material. They also cover ground, so they may have a Path or a Relative_location that the travel occurs in.

frameElementDefinitions.Roadway: The Roadway is the roadway that connects locations.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is one of the Endpoints treated as a source.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is one of the Endpoints of the Roadway treated as a goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Endpoints: The Endpoints are two (or more) named endpoints of the Roadway.
frameElementDefinitions.Characteristic: The Characteristic is a characteristic(s) of the Roadway.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The Name is the name of the Roadway.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: This is the setting through which the Roadway goes.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The Use is the purpose of the Roadway.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The Direction is the direction or directions the Roadway runs.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: The Relative_location is where the Roadway is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The Material is what the Roadway is constructed from.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency_of_use: The Frequency_of_use is the state of the Roadway with regard to the number of people who are using the Roadway or typically use it.
frameElementDefinitions.Abundant_entities: The Abundant_entities are the entities which occupy the space denoted by the Roadway.
frameElementDefinitions.Length: The Length is the distance the Roadway extends.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: A subpart of the Roadway.

annotations.definition: These are words describing situations in which a Perpetrator wrongs a Victim by taking something (Goods) from them. In some cases, the Source location from which the Goods were taken is used to metonymically refer to the Victim. The robbery can be done in a particular Manner (for example, forcefully) and via a specific Means (for example, by threatening the Victim).

frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: The Perpetrator is the person (or other agent) that takes the Goods away by force or threats.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the person (or other sentient being or group) that owns the Goods before they are taken away by the Perpetrator.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The Goods are anything (including labor, time, or legal rights) that can be taken away.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is the initial location of the goods, before they change location.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means indicates the means by which the Robbery takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes the way in which the Robbery took place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the Robbery occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time indicates when the Robbery occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The action that the Perpetrator is trying to accomplish by the robbery.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state of affairs that the Perpetrator has in mind which leads to the decision to commit the robbery.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times a robbery event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co-participant denotes a person that participates in a coordinated way in robbery event as the primary protagonist, the Perpetrator.

annotations.definition: An Agent manipulates a linear entity (the Rope) to form a Knot.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the sentient being who manipulates the Rope.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument with which the Rope is manipulated.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Rope: The basically linear object which is manipulated by the Agent to produce a Knot.
frameElementDefinitions.Knot: The stable configuration of a Rope which results from tying.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which the manipulation of the Rope takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Locus: Where the manipulation of the Rope takes place.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Patient, which is an organic substance, undergoes the natural process of decaying from an Initial_state to a Result.

frameElementDefinitions.Patient: The Patient is the entity that undergoes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the rotting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time indicates when the rotting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation indicates the reason for which the Patient rots.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the result of the change of phase of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: The Initial_state is the original state of the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration indicates the amount of time for which the Patient rots.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the Patient changes.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The Subregion describes which part of a larger whole is being affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree expressions that indicate to what degree the Patient has changed.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that a rotting event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Rotting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (horribly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: A state of affairs that entails the Patient decaying.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist is described as being exposed to a potentially dangerous situation that may end in a Bad_outcome for him- or herself. An Asset which is in danger of loss may stand in for the Bad_outcome. There is no implication that the Protagonist intentionally exposes themself to the risky situation. The Protagonist may be attempting to achieve some Purpose, which involves being in a dangerous situation. The Severity of risk involved may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The person who is at risk of some Bad_outcome
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where there is risk.
frameElementDefinitions.Severity: The probability of something bad happening to the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the risk pertains.
frameElementDefinitions.Bad_outcome: A situation that the Protagonist would like to avoid.
frameElementDefinitions.Asset: Something desirable possessed by or directly associated with the Protagonist which might be lost or damaged.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action that creates the risk.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the Protagonist runs risks.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The desired action or goal that the Protagonist hopes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An Explanation for why the Protagonist has put the Asset is in a dangerous situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that the Protagonist risks an Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances indicates the state of affairs under which the risky situation holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The Protagonist intends for his/her actions to benefit Beneficiary in some way.

annotations.definition: An Agent sacrifices an (often abstract) Asset in exchange for a Benefit which is deemed better or more worth having.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent gives up the Asset in hopes of receiving a greater Benefit.
frameElementDefinitions.Asset: The item which is sacrificed by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: State of the Agent or Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Agent sacrifices the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation (the one denoted by the target) to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time when the Agent gives up the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Agent gives up the Asset.
frameElementDefinitions.Benefit: The Agent hopes to receive or gain advantage from the Benefit as a result of giving up the Asset.

annotations.definition: This frame describes an Agent or an Entity meeting or not meeting a Standard. Alternatively, an action or occurrence may be construed as an Event that meets or fails to meet the Standard.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The volitional Agent who is in line with a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The non-agentive Entity that is in line with a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: An occurrence or action that brings about the satisfaction or nonsatisfaction of a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: The condition or requirement that the Agent, Entity, or Event satisfy (or not).
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Standard is satisfied.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Standard is satisfied.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time in which satisfaction of the Standard occurs.

annotations.definition: The Quantity of a Collection occurring in a Place has a particular Measure, which is related to the amount generally needed.

frameElementDefinitions.Collection: The Collection whose Quantity is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Quantity: The feature of an Collection which is under discussion. This FE is incorporated by all the lexical units in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Measure from the norm or from the value for another Collection.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Collection is in the state of having a particular Measure for the Quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Collection has a particular Measure for the Quantity.
frameElementDefinitions.Measure: The measure of the Quantity, which is incorporated by the lexical units.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Collection occurs.

annotations.definition: An Agent schedules an Event to take place at a specified Scheduled_time and Place.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient being who schedules an Event to take place at a Time and Place.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The incident that the Agent has scheduled to take place at a Time and Place.
frameElementDefinitions.Scheduled_time: The time at which the Event is scheduled to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent has scheduled the Event to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The person for whom the Event was scheduled by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The frequency with which the Agent schedules and Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The means used by the Agent in order to schedule the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Agent's motivation for scheduling the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The cause for the scheduling of the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Agent schedules the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE describes the time at which the scheduling occurs.

annotations.definition: An Event is described in terms of the diversity of entities that are affected or considered in the Event. Alternatively a Salient_entity which enables or brings about the Event can be similarly described.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: The state-of-affairs that affects a set of entities.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Salient_entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: The entity which enables or causes the Event being judged.

annotations.definition: A Searcher looks all over a Ground in order to find a Sought_entity. The Sought_entity is not presupposed to exist. The search activity is not concerned with the state of the Ground, which is the case in the Inspecting frame, but only with the finding of the Sought_entity, which is desirable.

frameElementDefinitions.Searcher: The person who is looking all over the Ground for the Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The location that the Searcher covers in looking for the Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Sought_entity: The entity that the Searcher wants to find.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Searcher scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE specifies the length of the interval during which the scouring activtiy takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Searcher scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity manipulated by the Searcher to examine the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Searcher that allows them to search the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Searcher hopes to bring about by searching a Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Searcher scoures the Ground.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer examines a Ground closely, alert to the presence of a Phenomenon.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer pays attention to an entity in order to discover something about it.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Cognizer pays attention to the Ground, so named because it serves as the background or context for the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The entity that the Cognizer is alert for in the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Cognizer scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Cognizer which allows them to scrutinize the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Cognizer scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The event that the Cognizer hopes to bring about by looking for the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Cognizer that interacts with the Ground to accomplish the scrutiny.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns a Cognizer (a person or other intelligent being) paying close attention to something, the Ground, in order to discover and note its salient characteristics.
