The Cognizer may be interested in a particular characteristic or entity, the Phenomenon, that belongs to the Ground or is contained in the Ground (or to ensure that such a property of entity is not present). Some words in this frame allow alternate expressions of the Groundand the Phenomenon:

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer pays attention to an entity in order to discover something about it. The FE is normally expressed as an External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Cognizer pays attention to the Ground, so named because it serves as the background or context for the Phenomenon. This FE can be expressed as an NP Object or as a PP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: Phenomenon identifies the property that belongs to an entity or that the entity contains. Typically, it is expressed as a for-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Cognizer scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE is used for the Means by which the Cognizer to scrutinize the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Cognizer scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: Direction is used for all path-like expressions, except those indicating the location of the perceiver, that describe how the perceiver's attention is directed during the act of perception.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Some of the words in this frame occur with a constituent that expresses the desired outcome of the Scrutiny. Normally this constituent is a PP Complement headed by for or a to-marked VP Complement.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is the piece of text or work in which the Cognizer pays attention to an entity in order to discover something about it. The FE is normally expressed as an External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Cognizer that interacts with the Ground to accomplish the scrutiny.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Cognizer is scrutinizing the Ground.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer seeks a Phenomenon within the Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer pays attention to the Ground in order to find instances of a type of Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Cognizer pays attention to the Ground, so named because it serves as the background or context for the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The entity that the Cognizer is alert for in the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Cognizer scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Cognizer which allows them to scrutinize the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Cognizer scrutinizes the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The event that the Cognizer hopes to bring about by looking for the Phenomenon.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Cognizer that interacts with the Ground to accomplish the scrutiny.

annotations.definition: A Phenomenon, which may be an activity, state or object, is purposefully hidden from the awareness of a potential Cognizer. The person responsible for the hiding of the Phenomenon is not part of this frame and cannot be syntactically realized. The potential Cognizer may be realized, but is in fact rarely expressed. A period of Time during which the Phenomenon is secret may be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Phenomenon: The Phenomenon is the activity, state or object that is purposefully hidden from the awareness of a potential cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Phenomenon is hidden from cognizers.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: A period of Time during which the Phenomenon is hidden from the awareness of potential cognizers.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person who might become aware of the Phenomenon if it was not concealed.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the target operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).

annotations.definition: A Cognizer realizes that a State_of_affairs (or Focal_entity within the State_of_affairs) is meant to deceive (the Cognizer is not necessarily the intended victim of the deception). The Cognizer comes to this belief by perception and reasoning, and not, for instance, by being informed of the true state of affairs.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer realizes the existence of a deception and is not misled by it.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The State_of_affairs is intended to be perceived and taken to be true even though it is not.
frameElementDefinitions.Focal_entity: The Focal_entity is a part of the deception (possibly also the agent behind it) which is perceived by the Cognizer to be misleading.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by the Cognizer which enables them to figure something out.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the text created by the Cognizer to communicate that they came to believe a specified State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Some verbs in this frame may occur with postverbal Topic expressions
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Cognizer comes to understand the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Cognizer realizes the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Cognizer discovers the State_of_affairs.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer_agent attempts to find some Sought_entity by examining some Ground. The success or failure of this activity (the Outcome) may be indicated. NB: This frame should be compared to the Scrutiny frame, in which the primary focus is on the Ground.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer_agent: The person who attempts to find the Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: This is the entity to which the Cognizer_agent pays attention. It is referred to as Ground because it serves as the background or context for the Sought_entity. This FE can be expressed as an NP Object (first example) or as a PP Complement (second example).
frameElementDefinitions.Sought_entity: This is the entity that the Ground may contain. Typically, it is expressed as a for-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Cognizer_agent that allows them to look for the Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A spatial setting larger than the Ground in which the Seeking event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Seeking occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Cognizer_agent intends to accomplish by performing the search.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the amount of effort put into an attempt.

annotations.definition: An Agent intends and takes steps towards bringing about a State_of_affairs or, metonymically, towards acquiring a Sought_entity .

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes an attempt to achieve a State_of_affairs or to obtain a Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Sought_entity: The entity that the Agent attempts to acquire.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt to acquire a Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an attempt at achieving a State_of_affairs or at acquiring the Sought_entity is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the amount of effort put into an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Agent is seeking to achieve a particular State_of_affairs or to acquire a Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent makes the attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time period over which the attempts at achieving a State_of_affairs or acquiring the Sought_entity take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by bringing about a particular State_of_affairs or acquiring the Sought_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: A state or event that the Agent wants to bring about. The Agent may but need not be a participant in that State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A proposition from which the occurrence of the seeking event follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: An individual or organization that helps the Agent bring about the State_of_affairs or delivers the Sought_entity to the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE introduces the underlying situation(s) in which seeking occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the Sought_entity or State_of_affairs .

annotations.definition: An Agent actively (yet not always with success) attempts to keep themselves from participating in an Event. The Agent is usually pushed to the Event because of an internal urge.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent who tries to keep an Event from taking place.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the action or state of affairs that the Agent wishes to prevent.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the Agent attempts to prevent the Event from taking place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Agent prevents the Event from taking place.

annotations.definition: The Self_mover, a living being, moves under its own direction along a Path. Alternatively or in addition to Path, an Area, Direction, Source, or Goal for the movement may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Self_mover: Self_mover is the living being which moves under its own power. Normally it is expressed as an external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is used for any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is used for any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal. This includes "middle of path'' expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is used for any expression which tells where the Self_mover ends up as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory or rate of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Any expression which characterizes the extent of motion expresses the frame element Distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: Area is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path. Note that this FE should not be used for cases when the same phrase could be used with the same meaning with a non-motion target, since these should be annotated with the Place FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: An entity whose motion is correlated with that of the Self_mover, following and tracking the Self_mover, being followed and tracked, or with both parties mutually matching Paths.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The rate at which the location of the Self_mover changes with respect to time.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Self_mover achieves movement.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: Result of an event
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or mental state of the Self_mover which motivates them toward motion or the particular manner of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which a Self_mover moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location within which a defined motion takes place. Any specified or implied Source, Path or Goal should designate a location contained with the Place. This FE can be differentiated from Area by the fact that the annotated phrase can be used in the same meaning with non-motion targets.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state of affairs that leads to the particular motion that the Self_mover adopts.
frameElementDefinitions.External_cause: External_cause is something in the external environment that causes the Self_mover to move.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction that the Self_mover heads in during the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Coordinated_event: The label Coordinated_event is to be used for phrases denoting an event-it does not allow states-that the Traversing is rhythmically aligned with. The Coordinated_event is conceived of as independent: it would occur regardless of the event expressed by the target, which is not even an incidental or optional sub-part of the Coordinated_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The shape of the overall Path travelled by the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: An event or circumstance that would not be expected given the nature of the particular Self_motion event.

annotations.definition: A Sender plans the Path (along with Source and Goal) of a Theme and places it in circumstances such that it travels along this Path under the power of some entity other than the Sender. This frame also has a Recipient distinct from the Goal, as both can be present:

frameElementDefinitions.Sender: This is the person who initiates the movement of the Theme and, unlike Carrying, does not accompany it.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path along which movement occurs. I shipped the books along Interstate 65.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The end of the path and intended goal of the sending. I sent the logs to the shed.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The beginning of the path, similar to source except that it serves to define the path. I sent wood from Illinois to California.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The objects being sent. I mailed the books to Alaska. Note that Theme may be multiply instantiated. They maileda questionnaire with a return envelope.
frameElementDefinitions.Transport_means: The mode of sending employed. I shipped the crops by bigrig .
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the Sender or Theme
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is any expression characterizing the extent of motion of the Theme. Pat threw the Javelin 50 meters.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: This is the recipient of the sent Theme. Tess mailed a letter to Abby
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose of the sending.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for the sending.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the sending takes place
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: An additional theme sent along with the main Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: A container in which the Theme is sent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A spatial setting encomnpassing the Source location, where the Sending event takes place.

annotations.definition: This frame contains nouns that refer to sensations in different modalities. The FE Source is used for the phenomenon that gives rise to the sensation in question. The FE Percept is used for the characteristic quality of the sensation. In cases of veridical perception these are not typically distinguished from one another; we use the FE Percept as the default in these cases. With some nouns in this frame it is possible to express the being who experiences the sensation, or the part of the body of such a being. We mark such expressions with the FEs Perceiver_passive and Body_part, respectively. (The -passive part of this label serves to distinguish this FE from the Perceiver-Agentive FE used in other frames.)

frameElementDefinitions.Perceiver_passive: This FE is only expressed with certain nouns in this frame, and then only with the help of a support verb, such as have:
frameElementDefinitions.Source: This is the entity or phenomenon which gives rise to the sensation. In cases of veridical sensation, this FE is not typically distinguished from Percept. Most of the phrases that express this FE can be paraphrased as from-phrases, e.g.
frameElementDefinitions.Percept: The FE Percept is used for phrases that express the characteristic property of a sensation. Typically this characteristic property is described with reference to the entity or phenomenon that produces the sensation---that is why this FE can be difficult to distinguish from Source. It is possible for Source and Percept to be expressed separately, however:
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: This FE is assigned to phrases expressing the body part in which a sensation is located. Since touch is the least localized of sense modalities, words relating to haptic sensations are the ones most likely to occur with this FE:
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the sensation not covered by a more specific FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: This FE is the perceptual background against which the Phenomenon is experienced by the Perceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the sensation is perceived.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE describes how strong the sensation is.

annotations.definition: A Sent_item is prepared for, received after, or involved in a process of Sending, which may involve a particular kind of Transport_means. The Sent_item has an (intended or actual) Sender and Recipient (or a Source and Goal). As a physical object, the Sent_item can be located in a Place.

frameElementDefinitions.Sender: This is the person or organization who initiates the movement of the Sent_item and, unlike in the case of Carrying, does not accompany it.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The end of the path and intended goal of the sending.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The beginning of the path, similar to source except that it serves to define the path.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: This is the recipient of the sent Sent_item
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When an interaction with the Sent_item occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place in which the Sent_item is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterizations or evaluations of the the Sent_item that introduce another frame, in which the Sent_item figures.

annotations.definition: During a court appearance, a Sentence, generally a punishment, is imposed on the Convict by a Court, usually represented by a judge.

frameElementDefinitions.Convict: The Convict is given a Sentence by the Court.
frameElementDefinitions.Court: The Court imposes a Sentence on a Convict.
frameElementDefinitions.Sentence: The Sentence is imposed on the Convict by the Court.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE denotes the type of sentence.
frameElementDefinitions.Term_of_sentence: This FE denotes the duration of the sentence.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Convict is sentenced.
frameElementDefinitions.Offense: The illegal action of which the Convict has been found guilty and for which they are punished.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location of the sentencing Court.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state that the Convict is in at the time of their sentencing.

annotations.definition: These words refer to separating a Whole into Parts, or separating one part from another. The separation is made by an Agent or Cause and may be made on the basis of some Criterion.

frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole is a single entity or an aggregate of entities which is separated into Parts. It is usually the NP Object of a target verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Parts: This refers collectively to the Parts resulting from separation of a Whole:
frameElementDefinitions.Part_1: Part-1 is the first of two Parts mentioned. This is most often the Object of a verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_2: Part_2 is the second of two Parts mentioned. This is usually a PP Complement. First,
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: With causative uses of these verbs, an Agent separates or divides something. The Agent is usually expressed as the External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Criterion: This frame element expresses a property of the Parts or components of the Whole which is used as the basis for separation. Criterion is typically expressed as a PP headed by by, or as a complex PP of the form on the basis of....
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the Agent who separates or the undergoers of the separation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Agent that accomplishes the separation.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-agent that brings about the separation.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipients: The Recipients are the groups who come into possession of the Parts of the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the separation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the separation occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument used to cause the separation.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Agent's intent for separating the Whole.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe entities that occur in some temporally-ordered sequence. The entities thus have some sort of relation between them that might be described by the Relative_time frame. However, at this time, this frame has no Frame Relation with that frame (though this is still under discussion). Additionally, it should be noted that the words in this frame have a metaphorical link to the words in Shape.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity_1: This entity occurs in some sequence with Entity_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity_2: This entity occurs in some sequence with respect to Entity_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Entities: Entities that occur in some temporal order.
frameElementDefinitions.Relation_type: A characterization of the sequence in which the entities occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE identifies a characteristic or description of the sequence.

annotations.definition: An Agent occupies a Role in some institution. This Roleis (usually) temporary and can be occupied by other entities at different periods of time. It is common for a Duration of the Role to be mentionned.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the entity that occpuies the Role.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The Role that the Agent occupies.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration of time that the Agent occupies the Role.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: The Descriptor modifies the Agent .
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent served in the Role.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Agent served in the Role.

annotations.definition: A group of Component_entities along with their interrelations is viewed as a Complex.

frameElementDefinitions.Component_entities: The entities that function together as a unit.
frameElementDefinitions.Complex: The unit formed by the Component_entities.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterization or evaluation of the Complex.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: A person or organization that possesses or owns the Complex or a larger entity of which the Complex itself is a part.

annotations.definition: A Superset has several members, a part of which is the Subset. This frame exists largely for the purposes of inheritance.

frameElementDefinitions.Superset: The Superset is the larger collection of entities that includes the Subset.
frameElementDefinitions.Subset: The Subset comprises a part of the Superset.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the inclusion event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the set relation holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the set relation holds.

annotations.definition: A Firefighter sets a controlled fire to remove fuel from a currently incompletely controlled fire.

frameElementDefinitions.Firefighter: The Firefighter is the person making the firebreak.
frameElementDefinitions.Flame: The Flame is what is created by the Firefighter.
frameElementDefinitions.Flammables: The Flammables is the the object that is burned in the back fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Firefighter that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event brought about by the Firefighter that actually makes the Flammables catch fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Firefighter as being in some state during the action.

annotations.definition: This frame describes either the creation of a Flame by a Kindler or non-sentient Cause, or the igniting of Flammables by a Kindler or a Cause.

frameElementDefinitions.Kindler: The Kindler is the person performing the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Flame: The Flame is what is created by the Kindler.
frameElementDefinitions.Flammables: The Flamables is the the object that is set on fire by the Kindler.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The cause of the fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Kindler that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event brought about by the Cause or the Kindler that actually makes the Flammables catch fire.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Kindler as being in some state during the action.

annotations.definition: A Self_mover begins a journey at a Source location, in a particular Directiongenerally setting out with an Intended_goal in mind.

frameElementDefinitions.Self_mover: This is the entity which moves under its own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element Source. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. In particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: A relation to a landmark that describes the trajectory in the midst of the motion. (It is thus neither a Source nor the Intended_goal.)
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_goal: Any expression which tells where the Self_mover intends to end up as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event involving the Self_mover that results in the Self_mover moving in the specified way.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the Self_mover moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: A second moving object which the Self_mover moves to follow.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Self_mover during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the motion (which is specified by a Source, Path or Goal) is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The configuration formed by the entire Path of the Self_mover, taken simultaneously.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction in which the Self_mover goes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Self_mover wishes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The device or bodypart that serves to move the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of time that the Self_mover spends away from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Self_mover at the end of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A factor which explains the particular motion or manner of motion of the Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Reciprocation: An event paired with the motion, which explains why the motion is expected. In particular, Reciprocation is used to indicate an action that is being rewarded or punished by the motion, and/or is paired with the motion as the other side of an agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at the time of departure and which the departure is taken to be a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that an action occurs in a given time.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: A larger conceptualization that includes the departing as an integral part. The departure expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action in this larger conceptualization.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: An unexpected event that affects some agent (usually the Self_mover), which is enabled by, and occurs after, the departing.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the departing event itself and any of its participants, and is merely peripherally related to the event as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Coordinated_event: The label Coordinated_event is to be used for phrases denoting an event (it does not allow states) that the departing is rhythmically aligned with. (This would then further require that the departing be iterated.) The Coordinated_event is conceived of as independent: it would occur regardless of the event expressed by the target, which is not even an incidental or optional sub-part of the Coordinated_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance from the Source that the Self_mover ends up.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Offense is described in terms of the legal or other punishing action that can be taken in response to the offense.

frameElementDefinitions.Offense: The Offense is the action, crime, or event which is described in terms of what legal or punishing action can be taken against the offender.
frameElementDefinitions.Code: The legal Code under which the severity of the offense is determined.

annotations.definition: Participants in a Sex act engage in or are subjected to sexual contact. Often the LUs in this frame are suggestive euphemisms which refer to non-sexual physical contact, Giving/Receiving scenarios, frames related to travel, physical orientation, body metonymy, biological necessity, or a simple completion of Activity.

frameElementDefinitions.Participants: The set of entities engaged in or subjected to sexual contact.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE describes the length of time that an entire sexual encounter can last, including but not necessarily implying the length of all stages of the encounter.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the sex act which is not covered by more specific FEs. In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of one or more Participants that also affect the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: To be clear: the place FE describes the overall location of the Participants of the sexual activity and nothing else.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE relates the relative time of occurrence of the sexual activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: For a sexual act, the Domain describes the specific realm of the contact between Participants; for example, phone sex.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the justification of a sexual act, for at least one of the Participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which Participants accomplish a sexual act.

annotations.definition: This frame embodies the process of procreation.

frameElementDefinitions.Parents: The parents who will receive offspring.
frameElementDefinitions.Father: The participant in a sexual relationship who fertilizes an egg.
frameElementDefinitions.Mother: The participant in a sexual relation whose egg is fertilized.
frameElementDefinitions.Offspring: The zygote, in some stage of development.

annotations.definition: An entity is a shaped Part of a larger Whole. The Part may correspond in form and function to a part of another entity. For instance, the leg of a table supports the weight of a table like the leg of an animal or person supports their weight. The Orientation of the Part relative to the Whole may be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Part: A shaped Part of a larger Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: A Whole which has a shaped Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_prop: A property relating to the shaped Part of a Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Orientation: This is a property of the shaped Part that situates the Part within the Whole.

annotations.definition: This frame contains words which describe the dimensional extent and Shape of a Substance. These words do not make reference to a Part-whole relationship. There can also be a descriptor of the Shape, Shape_prop.

frameElementDefinitions.Substance: A Substance of which pieces can be extracted.
frameElementDefinitions.Shape: This is the Shape that the Substance is in.
frameElementDefinitions.Shape_prop: This describes a property of the Shape, e.g. it's size.
frameElementDefinitions.Count: The number of units of the measured Substance.

annotations.definition: In this frame Protagonist_1 and Protagonist_2, collectively Protagonists, allow one another to partake jointly the use of an Entity .

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonists: The jointly expressed participants in the use of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist_1: The participant that is encoded as the subject of an active-form sentence or as a by-PP in a passive, who partakes in the joint usage of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist_2: The participant in the reciprocal event that is coded in a with-PP, who partakes in the usage of the Entity along with Protagonist_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The object that is shared by the Protagonists.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Protagonists share the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Protagonists partake jointly in the use of the Entity.

annotations.definition: An Entity has a Sharpness characterized by its capability to cut. The Sharpness is a value of the Attribute of sharpness.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity to which the Sharpness attribute is related.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The sharpness feature of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Sharpness from the norm or from the value for another Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular value for Sharpness.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the Circumstances under which the Entity has a particular value for the Sharpness.
frameElementDefinitions.Sharpness: The Sharpness value of the Entity. This FE is denoted by the adjectives of the frame.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Firearm to discharge which induces the rapid motion of the Projectile.

frameElementDefinitions.Firearm: The device used by the Agent to cause the motion of the Projectile.
frameElementDefinitions.Projectile: The entity whose rapid, dangerous motion is caused by the Agent and/or Firearm.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the intentional act causing the motion of the Projectile.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is any expression characterizing the extent of motion of the Theme. Pat threw the Javelin 50 meters.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE is the point at which the Theme ends up as a result of the motion. Kim threw the cat into the garden.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Agent whereby the rapid motion of the Projectile is achieved.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: This FE is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor Goal. I pushed the trolley along the street.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: This FE is the starting point of motion. Pat dragged the box out of the cupboard.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the state of the Agent or Projectile during the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: This frame element is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path. Locative setting adjuncts may also be assigned this frame element. Kim pushed the lawnmower around the garden.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the shooting event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the shooting event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Agent's purpose in launching the Projectile

annotations.definition: A firearm is discharged, causing the motion of a projectile. This frame exists primarily to relate frames.

frameElementDefinitions.Firearm: The device used by the Agent to cause the motion of the Projectile.
frameElementDefinitions.Projectile: The entity whose rapid, dangerous motion is caused by the Agent and/or Firearm.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the intentional act causing the motion of the Projectile.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE is any expression characterizing the extent of motion of the Projectile.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE is the point at which the Theme ends up as a result of the motion. Kim threw the cat into the garden.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Agent whereby the rapid motion of the Projectile is achieved.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: This FE is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor Goal. I pushed the trolley along the street.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: This FE is the starting point of motion. Pat dragged the box out of the cupboard.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the state of the Agent or Projectile during the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: This frame element is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular or not to consist of a single, linear path. Locative setting adjuncts may also be assigned this frame element. Kim pushed the lawnmower around the garden.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the shooting event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the shooting event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Agent's purpose in launching the Projectile

annotations.definition: A Shopper looks for Goods in order to purchase them.

frameElementDefinitions.Shopper: The person who attempts to find the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: This is the entity to which the Cognizer pays attention. It is referred to as the Ground because it serves as the background or context for the Goods. This FE is usually expressed as a preposition phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: This is the entity that the Ground may contain. Typically, it is expressed as afor-PP.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the shopping action is done.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Shopper that allows them to look for (and perhaps purchase) the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The failure or success of an attempt.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A spatial setting larger than the Ground in which the Shopping event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the shopping occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Shopper intends to accomplish by performing the search.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the amount of effort put into shopping.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of shopping scenario as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: The Co-participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the shopping event with the Shopper.

annotations.definition: Short selling, a common financial market concept, involves an Intermediary selling Assets to the Market, having borrowed these Assets from a (generally implicit) Lender. The Intermediary is obligated to rebuy the Assets later, so that they may be returned to the Lender. The Intermediary's intent is to repurchase the Assets from the Market at a lower price than when he sold them, and so return them to the Lender for a profit.

frameElementDefinitions.Lender: The original owner and soon to be Lender of the Assets.
frameElementDefinitions.Intermediary: The entity that is the borrower to the Lender and the seller and later repurchaser to the Market.
frameElementDefinitions.Market: The aggregate selling place of many types of financial stocks, bond, securities, etc. The Market is also a metonym for the people who buy the Assets.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The situation that the Intermediary wishes to bring about by short selling. Because this concept is associated with finance, the Purpose is normally to earn a profit.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time at which the Intermediary engages in short selling.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place at which the short selling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Intermediary that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Intermediary acts intentionally.

annotations.definition: An Astronomical_entity comes into view above the horizon as part of a regular, periodic process of (apparent) motion of the Astronomical_entity across the sky. In the case of the sun, the appearance begins the day.

frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the the state of the Astronomical_entity during the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the location above the horizon that the Astronomical_entity ends up in.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the motion of the Astronomical_entity takes place. It covers any description of the appearance which is not covered by more specific FEs such as Speed, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way), and aesthetic judgements of the event .
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The sequence of locations that the Astronomical_entity traverses as it comes over the horizon.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This FE identifies the Speed at which the Astronomical_entity comes into view.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Astronomical_entity appears.
frameElementDefinitions.Astronomical_entity: An entity which is periodically visible from the earth's surface.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location the Astronomical_entity is construed to occupy before coming above the horizon.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of the Astronomical_entity's motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place from which the motion of the Astronomical_entity is viewed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Astronomical_entity comes into view above the horizon.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: A setting, often temporal or spatial, within which the appearance of the Astronomical_entity takes place
frameElementDefinitions.Correlated_event: An event that takes place simultaneously with the Astronomical_entity's appearance.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: FN: The amount of times the Astronomical_entity appears per unit of time.

annotations.definition: A phenomenon, the Indicator, exists as an indication of another phenomenon, the Indicated. The relationship is asymmetric: the Indicated is not necessarily a sign for the existence or presence of an Indicator. The Indicated can be a past, present, or future entity or eventuality. The relationship between Indicator and Indicated is not one of symbolization or iconicity. Rather the Indicator is an index of the Indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Indicator: The phenomenon that signifies the Indicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Indicated: The phenomenon that is signified by the Indicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Reliability: This FE indicates the Reliability of the Indicator as a sign of the Indicated.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description of the Indicator not covered by a more specific FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE depicts how often the Indicated manifests itself.

annotations.definition: A Signatory signs an Agreement document, thereby taking on a commitment encoded in the Agreement.

frameElementDefinitions.Signature: The entity signifying the identity of the Signatory, resulting from the act of writing on the Agreement document.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: An expression of the state of the Signatory at the time of signing.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Signatory that interacts with the Agreement document so as to produce the Signature.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the signing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Signatory that also affect the action (happily, eagerly).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the action whereby the Signatory achieves the signing of the Agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Signatory commits to the Agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which signature is given.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for putting signature to paper.
frameElementDefinitions.Agreement: The document which encodes the commitment of the Signatory.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: The Co-participant signs the Agreement along with the Signatory.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: his extrathematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate. Further, the target predicate should involve some sort of Agent that intends that the benefit go to the Beneficiary.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The category of the Signatory in the Sign_agreement event.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Speaker to become silent, sometimes about a particular Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the intentional act.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Agent silences the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The sentient entity that is not communicating.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: This is the subject matter to which the Message pertains. It is typically expressed as a PP Complement headed by about.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result is the final state of the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Expressor is the Speaker's voice or other communicative noise emitted by the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the silencing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Agent succeeds in creating silence.

annotations.definition: Two or more distinct entities, which may be concrete or abstract objects or types, are characterized as being similar to each other. Depending on figure/ground relations, the entities may be expressed in two distinct frame elements and constituents , Entity_1 and Entity_2, or jointly as a single frame element and constituent, Entities. The similarity may be based on appearance, physical properties, or other characteristics of the two entities. However, no such Dimension has to be specified explicitly. The Entities may be like each other to a greater or lesser Degree. Rather than specifying the Dimension of difference, a Differentiating_fact may be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Entities: This FE marks constituents that express the set of objects or types whose similarity is at issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity_1: When there is an asymmetry, Entity_1 is the entity characterized by its similarity to Entity_2, whose characteristics are assumed to be known. Entity_1 is often an external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity_2: When the Entities are expressed separately, Entity_2 is the one whose characteristics are assumed to be known; it serves as a basis for establishing characteristics of Entity_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: This FE marks constituents which express a property in respect to which the similarity of the entities is assessed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which entities are similar to each other, in general or with respect to some Dimension(s)
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the similarity occurs or exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the general area in which similarity occurs or exists
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which Dimension is similar or different
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An inanimate entity or process that causes the similarity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describing the state of the Entities
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Differentiating_fact: A fact about Entity_1 or the Entities that reveals how Entity_1 is the same or different from other entities. (Note the contrast with 'as to'.)

annotations.definition: A Term is the linguistic sign conventionally used by a Speaker to refer to a type of Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Term: The linguistic sign that is used for referring to a type of Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The type of Entity that is conventionally referred to by a Term.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: A speaker or speech community that uses a Term to refer to a particular Entity.

annotations.definition: A Speaker conventionally uses a particular Term to refer to an Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The type of Entity that is conventionally referred to by a Term.
frameElementDefinitions.Term: The linguistic sign that is used for referring to a type of Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The speaker or speech community that uses a Term to refer to a particular Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A reason for why the Term used for the Entity is the one chosen.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Speaker uses the Term referring to the Entity.

annotations.definition: Two Events happen at the same time. This may either be construed symmetrically as a relation holding between the Events, or asymmetrically as holding between a Landmark_event whose known occurrence is used to describe when a Profiled_event took place.

frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_event: The event which is asserted to occur at the same time as the Landmark_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_event: The event or time period whose timing the Profiled_event is judged against.
frameElementDefinitions.Events: The states-of-affairs which are asserted to be co-timed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Profiled_event and Landmark_event coincide.

annotations.definition: The size of an Entity is assessed in terms of the Degree by which it deviates from the norm. The size generally refers to the amount of three-dimensional space that is occupied by the Entity, not primarily to its weight, density, or length, which is why it is peculiar to refer to a bullet as "large", even if it is heavy, unless it actually has larger dimensions than usual, or to a rope as "big", even if it is long, unless its other, cross-sectional dimensions are also significant.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The thing whose size is being considered.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the size of the Entity deviates from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: The size of the Entity may be compared to the size of a Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: This FE denotes a specific subregion being sized.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: The feature of an (actual or possible) Entity that is measured. In literal examples, it essentially must be size, or a word equivalent to size such as bulk.

annotations.definition: The Sleeper stays in an altered state of consciousness with greatly reduced external awareness.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the sleep occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Sleeper is asleep.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the sleeping event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, noisily), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).

annotations.definition: A Sleeper enters a state of consciousness of reduced external awareness, remains in this state for some amount of time, and (normally) returns to full wakeful consciousness.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Sleeper: The Sleeper is the entity that is sleeping.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe situations in which the Perpetrator secretly takes Goods into or out of a country or other area which are prohibited by law or on which one has not paid the required duty.

frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: This is the person (or other agent) that illegally takes the goods into or out of a country.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: The FE Goods is anything (including labor, time, or legal rights) that can be illegally taken into our out of a country.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which a state holds or a process is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The source is the location the goods occupy initially before change of location.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The path refers to (a part of the) ground the Goods travel over or to a landmark the Goods travel by.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is the location the Goods end up in.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Perpetrator which allows them to smuggle the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The unlawful movement of Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the Event not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In most cases, it indicates salient characteristics of a Perpetrator that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The action that the Perpetrator is trying to accomplish by the act of smuggling.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that a smuggling event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Constant_location: The container, vehicle, or other entity that the Goods move with and remain stationary with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance travelled by the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The amount of time per distance travelled by the Goods.

annotations.definition: In this frame, an Agent places the Theme in the Medium for an extended period of time, with the intent that the Theme will be affected by and absorb some of the Medium.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person performing the soaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity that changes location.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the Theme is soaked.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent soaks the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the substance in which the Theme is submerged.
frameElementDefinitions.Container: This FE identifies the Container that holds the Theme which is submerged and the Medium that it is in.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the submerging occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent soaks the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Agent that brings about the soaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The situation that the Agent hopes to bring about by soaking the Theme.

annotations.definition: An Item becomes filled with a Substance as a result of movement of liquid into visible or invisible interstices in the Item.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: The Item is the absorbant friendly thing that takes in the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Substance: The Substance is the material that is taken in by the Item.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Item takes in Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Item takes in Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: For what length of time the Item is imbued with Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Item soaks in the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree describes the extent to which a particular absorption event deviates from the norm.

annotations.definition: These words describe how outgoing or sociable a Protagonist is judged to be by a (usually implicit) Judge, often in a particular context, either with certain people (the Company), or about a particular situation, topic or event (the Content). As opposed to the Social_interaction_evaluation frame, this frame talks about how much someone interacts, not how well.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist is the person whose sociability is being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Company: The Protagonist may be sociable, shy, outgoing, etc. when with particular Company (specific people or types of people). Company usually occurs in a PP Complement with a target adjective:
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The Protagonist may be sociable, shy, outgoing, etc. when in a particular situation, or about performing some action.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: In this frame the Behavior is judged by the speaker (or, in some cases, a separate Judge) against pre-existing social standards for that type of behavior. An Individual who is involved in the Behavior is assigned the same evaluation as the Behavior. This is an abstract frame that encodes the relationship between such frames as Mental_property, Sociability, Social_interaction_evaluation, Stinginess, and Volubility.

frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The Behavior is the action of the Individual on which the evaluation of the Individual's character is based.
frameElementDefinitions.Individual: The Individual is the person whose Behavior is being evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the described Behavior deviates from the norm.

annotations.definition: A mutual social and personal relationship that keeps people involved in each other's lives comprises a Connection of Individual1 to Individual2 or between Individuals.

frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: Individuals have a Connection with each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Individual_1: Individual1 has a social Connection to Individual_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Individual_2: Individual2 has a relationship to Individual_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Connection: Connection is the personal relationship between Individuals that keeps them involved in each other's lives.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type is the sub-category to which the Connection belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree (strength or closeness) of the Connection.

annotations.definition: This frame contains LUs which are like those of the Desirability frame except the Evaluee incorporates the Parameter as "associated with a percieved place in a social heirarchy" for all LUs. LUs are not restricted to positive or negative implications.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: The Evaluee is being judged for its quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Evaluee is judged good or bad.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances marks expressions that indicate a set of conditions under which the quality of the Evaluee is being judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: This FE marks expressions that indicate what class of things or events the Evaluee is being judged against.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Frame Element Depictive encodes a state that the Evaluee is in and which does not represent a temporary or hypothetical set of Circumstances.

annotations.definition: A Social_event occurs at which Attendees are present to conduct a social function or joint activity. The Social_event is often a party or celebration, in which case it is possible to express someone who is being honored (the Honoree) or its function may be to celebrate an Occasion at which Attendees are present. Often, a specific Attendee, the Host, provides a venue for people to gather.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the Social_event is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Social_event: This FE identifies the Social_event itself.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Social_event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Social_event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host is the person or organization that hosts the Social_event. In cases where an individual stands in for an organization, the host may be marked twice.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The person for whom the Social_event is held.
frameElementDefinitions.Occasion: The Occasion for which the Social_event is held.
frameElementDefinitions.Attendee: Attendee is used for the people who come to the Social_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Social_event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Social_event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Refreshment: Refreshment is used for the meal, food or drinks that are served at the Social_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Style: This FE identifies the Style of the Social_event and its level of formality, indicating appropriate dress for participants and/or the kind of activities in which they engage.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: COD: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the social_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The Name of the Social_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE describes how often the Social_event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason the Social_event is being held.

annotations.definition: This frames describes a social event where there may or may not be a permanent or semi-permanent relationship between participants. Additionally, participants in this frame are expressed in one representative FE, Attendees. Note the difference between this frame and Social_event_individuals, where participants are grammatically separated.

frameElementDefinitions.Attendees: The Attendees are the collectively construed participants of the social event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the social event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the social event is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the social event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the social event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the social event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with Particular_iteration modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.

annotations.definition: This frames describes a social event where there may or may not be a permanent or semi-permanent relationship between participants. Additionally, participants in this frame are expressed separately, as Party_1 and Party_2. Note the difference between this frame and Social_event_collective, where participants are grouped into one collective grammatical constituent and FE.

frameElementDefinitions.Party_1: Party_1 is the grammatically more prominent participant in the social event. Party_1 can be one or more individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Party_2: Party_2 is the grammatically less prominent participant in the social event. Party_2 can be one or more individuals.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the social event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time for which the social event is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the social event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the social event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the social event occurs.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Evaluee is judged by a (usually implicit) Judge to be of a certain character based on her or his Behavior towards other human beings; a specific Affected_party may be overtly mentioned along with the Degree to which the Evaluee behaves appropriately towards others. TheBehavior alone may be mentioned with the understanding that these behaviors characterize a property of the unmentioned Evaluee. The Evaluee's Behavior may be in the context of a Topic.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: The Evaluee is the person whose Behavior towards other human beings is being evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Affected_party: The Affected_party is the entity which is positively or negatively treated by the Evaluee.
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The Behavior is the action of the Evaluee on which the evaluation of the Evaluee's character is based.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is the extent to which the Evaluee exhibits the particular Behavior towards others.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the situation or person that the Evaluee's Behavior responds to or is related to.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Expressor is the body part or action by a body part which indicates the character of the Evaluee.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the Evaluee embodies the characteristic ascribed to her or him.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the evaluation denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: A series of Events are important to humankind and its culture.

frameElementDefinitions.Event: This FE identifies the event which occurs to create history.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain is (a characterization of) the type of event that is part of the Event's history.

annotations.definition: An Item is the only instance of its Type.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: An Item is alone in its category.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type specifies a category which in principle could apply to multiple items, but which is limited to the Item.

annotations.definition: An Entity is judged by a sound Attribute of some perceived level of Volume intensity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which the sound level Attribute is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under consideration. This FE is incorporated in each LU in the frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Volume: A quantity or characterization of the Entity's sound output with repspect to its Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Volume from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Entity has a particular Volume for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular Volume for the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an Entity is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.

annotations.definition: A Sound emitted by a Sound_source, which construed as a single point, moves along a Path. Rather than the Sound_source itself, the Location_of_sound_source may be mentioned. Essentially, this frame denotes the (semi-)fictive motion of the the Sound. Given the spreading nature of sound vibrations, it is not surprising that there usually is no expression of any particular Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Sound: A percept produced in hearing organs by vibrations which travel through the air or another medium.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_sound_source: The location of the Sound_source from which the Sound travels
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path of the Sound's movement is any description of its trajectory of motion which is neither a Location_of_sound_source or a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A setting in which Sound movement happens.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Sound movement occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Sound_source: The point-like entity which produces the Sound.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Sound moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: A description of the location where the Sound ends up.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with the percepts that vibrations which travel through the air or another medium produce in hearing organs. Sounds are a subtype of sensation. In talking about sounds, speakers often provide information about how the sound was produced. A Noisy_event may be mentioned which gives rise to the sound. Alternatively, the sound may be attributed to a Sound_source, which is construed as a point source emitting the sound. A further possible specification of what gives rise to the sound is for speakers to talk about two physical entities coming into contact by motion, resulting in either friction or impact. Although this frame is defined in terms of a phenomenon of objective reality, many instances of the LUs in this frame are metonymic references to purely psychological phenomena due to a strong metaphorical relationship between external and internal reality. Furthermore, many of the LUs in this frame such as chuckle.n or whimper.n require a perceiver's analysis of a sound rather than being purely descriptive.

frameElementDefinitions.Noisy_event: An event during which sound is produced.
frameElementDefinitions.Sound_source: An entity that is construed as a single point source emitting sound.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: One of two entities which is construed as moving relative to the other, resulting in either friction or impact. This entity is given greater syntactic prominence--it is either a possessor or an of-complement of the noun phrase head.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: An entity construed as stationary on or against which the Theme moves, resulting in either friction or impact. This entity is given less grammatical prominence compared to the Theme and is usually encoded in a spatial prepositional phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Themes: Two entities that are both moving against each other, resulting in either friction or impact.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_sound_source: The place from which the sound emanates.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which sound is produced. This FE differs from Location_of_sound_source in that it does not highlight the sound's spreading from its source to the perceiver's location, which is usually indicated by the preposition "from".
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The noise-making may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause, that is the mental or emotional state of an animate Sound_source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Certain properties of the sound relating to its production, loudness, etc may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of a sounds as such: loudly, shrilly, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Component_sound: The sound percept may be characterized in terms of Component_sounds.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Expressions that indicate the loudness of the sound.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE idntifies the Explanation that the Sound_source produces the sound. Generally, this is some external stimulus that prompts the Sound_source to make noise.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path along which the sound travels away from the Sound_source.

annotations.definition: A Goal has a Source from which it has gotten a Theme. The Goal may be a sentient recipient who has actively obtained the Theme but it may also be a non-sentient entity that gets the Theme as a result of natural or other processes that it does not control.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The abstact or concrete thing acquired by the Goal from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: A concrete or abstract entity from which the Goal has gotten the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: An entity or individual that has acquired a Theme from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any evaluative or descriptive characterization of the Source, pertaining either to temporary or permanent properties.

annotations.definition: Basic Image Schema: the Trajector moves (or is conceived as moving) from the Source along the Path to the Goal using the Means.

frameElementDefinitions.Trajector: The entity that moves (or is conceived of as moving) from the Source, along the Path to the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: A description of the initial location of the Trajector, before a motion event begins.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: A description of the intermediate position of the Trajector, after departing the Source and before arriving at the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: A description of the location that the Trajector ends up.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action or state-of-affairs that causes the Trajector to change location.

annotations.definition: A Figure is located essentially at the same location as a Ground, which is viewed as point-like, or having no important internal or external structure.
