Typically, the Ground is viewed as point-like due to its small size with respect to a conversationally relevant spatial scale; this scale may be provided by reference to other, distant entities or full paths of travel that make the size of the Ground seem relatively small. Another use of this frame is to specify a location via a Ground that is primarily considered for its function, rather than its shape; this second use explains why "at the store" is typically used even when a Figure is inside the Ground, but only in cases where the store is considered, according to its prototype, as a place to buy and sell, rather than an architectural entity (in which case "in the store" would be more usual).

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as located relative to a certain Ground location. The Figure can be an entity or an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: An indication of how accessible the Figure is to some participant in the speech context (either the speaker or the addressee, or some other highly salient viewpoint in a narrative). This FE normally describes availability for manipulation and use, but may simply describe how easily seen or difficult to see the Figure is. There is a closed class of items that can fill this slot, including only 'right' indicating ease of access, and 'way', 'all the way', or (dialectally) 'clear' indicating distance and difficulty of access.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure. This FE is filled by one of a closed class of adverbs: up, down, out (meaning 'beyond the boundary around the viewpoint'), off (meaning 'disconnect between landmark and figure'), back (meaning 'along a line from front to back of landmark, and relatively inaccessible'), and, more rarely, away, along, and dialectally, back down (meaning 'down and behind').
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the locative relation between Figure and Ground obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'.
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Ground interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class of adverbials back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Figure before) and on.adv (which indicates a continued path). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, in which case it either refers to fictive, imagined motion (as in the first example below), or it backgrounds motion entirely (as in the second example).

annotations.definition: A Figure is located in contact with a Ground. With some words that evoke this frame, the Figure is also asserted to be fully or partially supported by the Ground (e.g. on), while in others a support relation is either denied (e.g. to, as in "she put her hand to the wall") or unspecified (e.g. against). In addition, some LUs assert a direction in which to find the Figure from the Ground (e.g. atop).

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as located relative to a certain Ground location. The figure can be an entity or an event.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: An indication of how accessible the Figure is to some participant in the speech context (either the speaker or the addressee, or some other highly salient viewpoint in a narrative). This FE normally describes availability for manipulation and use, but may simply describe how easily seen or difficult to see the Figure is. There is a closed class of items that can fill this slot, including only 'right' indicating ease of access, and 'way', 'all the way', or (dialectally) 'clear' indicating distance and difficulty of access.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure. This FE is filled by one of a closed class of adverbs: up, down, out (meaning 'beyond the boundary around the viewpoint'), back (meaning 'along a line from front to back of landmark, and relatively inaccessible'), and, more rarely, away, along, and dialectally, back down (meaning 'down and behind').
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The period of time during which the locative relation between Figure and Ground obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Figures: The Figures are items which mutually serve to identify the location of each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Deixis: An indication of how the locative phrase relates to the grounding of the speech situation, either locating it near (here) or far (there, yonder), or specifically separated from the speech context by distance or a barrier (over). Fillers of this FE are restricted to this closed class of items.
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, directly, and due (and some combinations of these), but also occurs with rarer items like 'smack dab'.
frameElementDefinitions.Temporal_profile: A description of how the access path to the Ground interacts with time. This FE is filled only by the closed class adverbial back.adv (which indicates that the position was occupied by the Figure before). While this FE in its most basic use refers to cases of actual motion, it can be used with descriptions of static position, where motion is backgrounded.

annotations.definition: A Speaker addresses an Audience on a particular Topic. The Audience is generally passive, although for many types of address (including academic talks and press conferences), a discussion or question-answer period is virtually always required.

frameElementDefinitions.Audience: The person or persons to whom the Speaker delivers their text.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person who orally delivers a text about a Topic to a passive Audience.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: What the text that the Speaker produces is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is a description of the Speaker's speech.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This frame element is the Medium through which the Speaker communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Occasion: The Occasion is the reason a public statement is given.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time describes when the speaking occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place describes the location where the speaking occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically followes. This often means that the Explanation causes the complaint, but not in all cases. In particular, there are cases when the connection is a bit weaker than normal for causation. In these instances, cause is mediated by some actor responding in a complex way to the situation. Such cases are not paraphrasable in the language of direct causation.

annotations.definition: LUs in this frame denote that the Instance of a Type is contextually notable. This frame typically requires dual FE layer annotation; see below.

frameElementDefinitions.Type: The class of items within which the Instance is made salient.
frameElementDefinitions.Instance: The notable case of the Type.

annotations.definition: An Entity is in motion at a particular Speed, a momentaneous description of distance traveled per time.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity whose Speed is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is a function of the distance that the Entity moves in a given duration. With the adjectives of this frame this Frame Element is generally incorporated.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: A quantity or other characterization of the Entity's speed.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Speed from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular Speed.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the Circumstances under which the Entity has a particular Speed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why the Entity or Attribute is ata certain speed.

annotations.definition: A Speaker realizes a Sign, usually a linguistic one, in some Formal_realization. The Formal_realization may be described as correct or incorrect in a Judgment expression, or it may be partially indicated as a Part_of_form. Manner may be used as a more general indication of the Formal_realization

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The language user who produces a Sign in some modality.
frameElementDefinitions.Judgment: An evaluation of whether the Formal_realization that the Speaker produced matches the standard or standard range.
frameElementDefinitions.Formal_realization: The written string, gesture, or vocalized sound sequence that the Speaker produces.
frameElementDefinitions.Part_of_form: A constituent part of the Formal_realization.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way that the Speaker produces the Formal_realization.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Speaker produces the Formal_realization.
frameElementDefinitions.Textual_location: The context wherein the Speaker produces a particular Formal_realization.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The person that the Speaker is producing the Formal_realization for.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically followes.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The amount of times the Sign is spelled or pronounced per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE marks any state that the Speaker is in while spelling or pronouncing.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.

annotations.definition: This frame contains LUs that have to do with the terminology used in competitive sports until a more complete, domain specific series of frames are created for the respective sport. This frame is a placeholder for these terms, as they properly belong is more specific frames related to their particular sports. However, these sports would require many complex frames and as such are low-priority for FrameNet.

frameElementDefinitions.Competition: This FE is used for the name of the competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE is used for plural NP participants in a competition.

annotations.definition: An Entity is at a particular stage of its development.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The object that has reached a particular stage of its development.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the progress occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the stage of progress, frequently an evaluation.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of advancement that the Entity has attained.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant dimension of development.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist waits for an Expected_request, and is prepared to respond to the request when it comes by engaging in an Activity. Alternatively, the Protagonist waits for a Salient_entity involved in the request, most often realized as the person making the request.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The sentient being who delays action until an Expected_request has been received.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: A concrete or abstract entity that the Protagonist expects to participate in an Expected_request, typically the party that makes the request of the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Protagonist is waiting for the request.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Protagonist is waiting.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of the waiting to its end.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the waiting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of the Protagonist that also affect the action (patiently, anxiously).
frameElementDefinitions.Expected_request: The Expected_request is what triggers the Protagonist to proceed with an appropriate action.
frameElementDefinitions.End_point: The point in time, defined either as a clock time or in terms of an event that takes place, at which the Protagonist ends the waiting.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the occurrence of the waiting event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Protagonist as being in some state during the waiting.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Protagonist intends to accomplish by standing by.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co-participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the waiting event with the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Protagonist is prepared to conduct an Activity in response to the Expected_request.

annotations.definition: An Entity persists in a stable situation called a State

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Concrete or abstract entity
frameElementDefinitions.State: State of an Entity.

annotations.definition: Despite some implication that a State would be interrupted, the Entity remains in the specified State. Note that State includes locative relations.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: A concrete or abstract Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State of an Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the State is found to still persist.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Entity is in the State.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The state of affairs that explains why the State persists.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time period over which the State continues to hold of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the State continues.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.

annotations.definition: An Entity is in a specified State. When not explicitly specified, the State usually concerns the Entity's health, repair, or functioning. The State is also often assessed with an Evaluation and it may be specified to apply only to a particular Parameter of the Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The entity whose State is specified and/or evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluation: An assessment or characterization of the Entity's State.
frameElementDefinitions.State: A relation in which the Entity participates or a property that the Entity possesses.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: A subpart or characteristic of the Entity that is in a specified State.

annotations.definition: This frame contains verbs and nouns that communicate the act of a Speaker to address a Message to some Addressee using language. Instead of (or in addition to) a Speaker, a Medium may also be mentioned. Likewise, a Topic may be stated instead of a Message. A number of the words can be used performatively, such as declare and insist.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the sentient entity that produces the Message (whether spoken or written). It is normally expressed as the External Argument of predicative uses of the target word, or as the Genitive modifier of the noun.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee is the person to whom the Message is communicated. When this FE is expressed, it often appears in a prepositional phrase introduced by "to", or as a direct object.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message is the FE that identifies the content of what the Speaker is communicating to the Addressee. It can be expressed as a clause or as a noun phrase.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the subject matter to which the Message pertains. It is normally expressed as a PP Complement headed by "about", but in some cases it can appear as a direct object.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner of performing an action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act whereby the Speaker makes a Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The emotional or physical state of the Speaker that leads to their speaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the Speaker to transmit the statement.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Speaker during the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Message is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The FE specifies the location where the Message takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Occasion: The Occasion is the reason a public Message is given.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE indicate that the particular Message event referred to is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. Most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the Message is expressed (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The Group of Speakers or Mediums within which the Message is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes the Message event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The FE Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies an event during which the Speaker produces the Message.

annotations.definition: In this frame either a Stimulus brings about a particular emotion or experience in the Experiencer or saliently fails to bring about a particular experience. Some words indicate that the Stimulus is characterized by the experience it is likely to evoke in an Experiencer and for these, the Experiencer may rarely be present. There may also be a Degree to which the Stimulus affects the Experiencer and Circumstances under which the experience occurs. There may also be a Comparison_set to which the Stimulus is compared and a Parameter that indicates the area in which the Stimulus has its effect.

frameElementDefinitions.Stimulus: The Stimulus is the object or event which brings about the emotion in the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer experiences the emotion brought about by the Stimulus
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Stimulus evokes the emotion in the Experiencer
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE marks expressions that indicate a set of conditions under which the Stimulus is able to bring about the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is the area in which the Stimulus brings about an effect on the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: The Comparison_set is the set of individuals the Stimulus is compared to when determining the Degree of the emotional reaction.
frameElementDefinitions.Property: This FE indicates a Property of the Stimulus which causes a particular experience.

annotations.definition: In this frame, a Resource_controller is judged by a Judge (who is usually implicit) according to how freely they allow the Recipient access to their Resource. The Resource_controller may exhibit this attitude with an Expressor.

frameElementDefinitions.Resource_controller: The Resource_Controller is the individual who controls access to the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The Resource_controller controls the Recipient's access to the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The Recipient is the individual or individuals who receive or should receive the Resource from the Resource_controller.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree is how generous or stingy the Resource_controller is judged to be.
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: The Behavior is an action of the Resource_controller which is judged to be generous or stingy.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The Expressor is the body part or action by a body part which indicates the character of the Resource_controller.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A characterization of the way in which the Resource_controller exhibits stinginess.

annotations.definition: A Supply of a Resource is kept safe and available for a future Use. The Supply may be under the control of a Possessor.

frameElementDefinitions.Supply: This FE denotes the Supply of Resources. It is incorporated into each LU in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The goods or commodity that is being stored in a Supply.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The future Use for the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: The entity that possesses the Supply.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Supply itself, rather than the Resource.

annotations.definition: An Agent has placed a Theme in an accessible but somewhat out of the way Location for the purposes of maintaining it free from harm and illegitimate use while it is not being used.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who has placed the Theme in the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the object that is kept in a Location by the Agent
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The location where the Theme is kept. Some, though not all, LUs in this frame incorporate this FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the storing state which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (deliberately, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE identifies any Depictive phrase describing the Agent or Theme of the storing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the amount of time for which the Theme remains in the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place indicates where the Storing occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the storing event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Time is the time period during which the Theme is stored.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is the setting into which a mass Theme is placed.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co-participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the storing event either as a co-agent or a co-theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Agent intends to accomplish by perform the storing action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: These adjectives describe the degree of strictness or tolerance of an Agent, usually with regard to another person (the Controllee) and often with regard to a particular Issue. The strictness/lenience with respect to the Issue may be manifested in (expressed by) an Agent or by some Medium (e.g., laws), or by some behavior of the Agent, i.e., the Controlling_act.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the person who is strict or liberal in his/her control over another. This FE is usually the External Argument with predicate adjectives, or the head noun if the adjective is used attributively: The school is very liberal in approach and curriculum. I attended a very liberal school.
frameElementDefinitions.Controlled_entity: The Agent's strictness may be directed towards or limited to some particular person or group over whom he/she has control. The Controlled_entity generally occurs in a PP Complement. Grandparents are often indulgent with their grandchildren.
frameElementDefinitions.Controlling_act: The Agent's strictness may be manifested in particular behavior: The judge was lenient in dealing with the offender.
frameElementDefinitions.Issue: The Agent may be strict or lenient with respect to a particular matter or situation. Issue occurs in PP Complements, usually headed by about: The city council is strict about pollution control.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: A Medium of control, such as rules, may also be described as strict or lenient. Medium may occur with or without the Agent who ultimately has control (i.e. the one who implements or enforces the rules). The club has strict rules about who can become a member. Immigration law is very strict.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is the arena in which the Agent is or is not strict.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: The body part or expression of the Agent that indicates their strictness.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the way in which the Agent embodies the strictness associated with him or her.

annotations.definition: A Student enrolls and then remains at an Institution for the purposes of education within a Subject. They may receive instruction from a particular Teacher at the Institution. The Place, Time, and Duration of the study may also be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Teacher: One who instructs a Student in some area of knowledge or skill.
frameElementDefinitions.Student: One who receives instruction from a Teacher or Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Institution: An educational establishment, such as a school or college.
frameElementDefinitions.Subject: The area of knowledge or skill which is taught to a Student.
frameElementDefinitions.Level: This FE identifies the Level of a student in his/her education.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A state which describes the Student during the process of study.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The manner of studying that the Student has.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the student is studying.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location within which the studying takes place. NOTE: Consider whether a possible Place phrase is really where the training took place or is rather part of a description of the Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time for which the process of study is ongoing.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Student studies the Subject.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co_participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the Studying event.

annotations.definition: A Speaker succeeds in getting an Addressee to believe or plan to execute the Content the Speaker has in mind. In most cases, the information is related to some course of action for the Addressee, and the words of this frame imply that the Addressee has formed an intention to act consistently with the Content. There is no implication that the Addressee actually acts upon the intention formed. In the case of convince and persuade, the act that the Speaker wishes the Addressee to perform can be the adoption of a belief, which may be expressed as a that-clause or as an of-PP complement. This frame differs from Attempt_suasion in that in Suasion, the Addressee has changed their mind in accord with the Speaker's wishes, whereas in Attempt_suasion, the only implication is that the Speaker communicates with the intent of changing the mind of the Addressee. This Frame has unique Frame-to-Frame Relations: it inherits from and uses the Eventive_cognizer_affecting frame. This is atypical of FrameNet's frame relations.

frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The person that the Speaker seeks to influence to act.
frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The person who intends through the use of language to get another person to act.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The wish for action on the Addressee's part that the Speaker expresses.
frameElementDefinitions.Text: A piece of written or spoken material that changes the mind of the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The general item or items that are the focus of the Content of the Speaker's message.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act of the Speaker that accomplishes the persuasion.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Speaker suades the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Speaker suades the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Speaker suades the Addressee.

annotations.definition: An Agent, Situation or Entity has an influence on a Cognizer. The influence may be general; or it may be manifested in the Cognizer's engaging in an Action as a consequence of the influence; or the Cognizer may be influenced in how they carry out a Behavior that they are engaged in already. Alternatively, a Product may be specified whose production or design was influenced by the Cognizer's experience of the Situation or Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The person who is influenced by the Situation or Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Situation: A state of affairs--- which can be an event, state or conditions---that has an influence on the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: A person or thing that has an influence on the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: An Action that the Cognizer decides to carry out as a consequence of the influence of a Situation or Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Behavior: A Behavior that the Cognizer is engaged in independently but whose execution is subsequently influenced by the Situation or Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Product: An entity that stands metonymically for the actions and considerations that produced it.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Subjective_influencing event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE applies to appraisals of the Subjective_influence event.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent acts to influence a Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which one has influence.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The field in which one maintains influence.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This is typically an adjective describing a nominal target.

annotations.definition: An Experiencer senses different degrees of warmth that may or may not be related to the ambient temperature. The level of warmth is usually compared against the Experiencer's subjective standard of comfort.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The entity which perceives a level of warmth.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: How much the actual feeling of temperature deviates from a certain level of warmth.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: A part of the body of the Experiencer in which the sensation of a temperature is located.

annotations.definition: A Submittor gives Documents to an Authority so that they can be processed as part of an application, evaluation or other official or bureaucratic process.

frameElementDefinitions.Submittor: The person that begins in possession of the Documents and gives them over into the possession of the Authority for processing.
frameElementDefinitions.Authority: The individual or organization that ends up in possession of the Documents and processes them.
frameElementDefinitions.Documents: The artifacts containing content, typically in the form of text and images, that the Submittor gives to the Authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Submittor gives the Documents to the Authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which event of the Submittor's giving the Documents to the Authority logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Submittor gives the Documents to the Authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Submittor gives the Documents to the Authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Submittor gives the Documents to the Authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the manner in which the Submittor gives the Documents to the Authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Authority's intended purpose for the Documents.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: This extra-thematic FE applies to participants that derive a benefit from the occurrence of the event specified by the target predicate. Further, the target predicate should involve some sort of agent that intends that the benefit go to the Beneficiary.

annotations.definition: Two individuals, the Subordinate and the Superior have a relationship such that the Subordinate generally follows orders given by the Superior and has the best interests of the Superior in mind.

frameElementDefinitions.Superior: The individual in charge of the Subordinate.
frameElementDefinitions.Subordinate: The individual under the command of the Superior.

annotations.definition: An Entity survives a situation with some Support, which tends to be far below the accustomed or standard level expected. The Degree to which the Support is responsible for the Entity's survival may be mentioned, as well as the Duration for which the situation lasts.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The living organism which requires the Support to survive.
frameElementDefinitions.Support: The resource that the Entity relies on to survive through a situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the Entity survives with the help of the Support.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree to which the Support is responsible for the survival of the Entity.

annotations.definition: This frame concerns internally undifferentiated Substances.

frameElementDefinitions.Constituents: The frame element expresses substances/ingredients that make up the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Substance: The undifferentiated entity which is presented as having a permanent existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Substance belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Origin: The location that the Substance comes from, including both extraction sites and emission/production sources.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Inherent_purpose: The purpose for which the Substance is intended.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Patient is in a phase brought about after a change of phase. This can be its inherent phase or indicate that it is returned to its inherent state.

frameElementDefinitions.Substance: The Substance is the entity which has had its phase altered.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: The part of the Patient that is in an altered state.

annotations.definition: A Counter_actor or Cause impedes an Agent in an Activity or impedes the unfolding or continuation of a State_of_affairs. The opposing force applies without first being noticed, often in a secret or an underhanded fashion.

frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event or state_of_Affairs that successfully impedes an Agent or a State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Counter_actor: A person who intentionally or not acts in such a way that an Agent or State_of_affairs is impeded.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: A person who is intentionally engaged in some Activity and who is impeded in that Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: An Activity that is being impeded.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: A state or event that is ongoing and whose continuance or unfolding is impeded.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Cause or Counter_actor impede the Agent or the State_of_affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Counter_actor hopes will result from his undermining.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Counter_actor subverts an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Counter_actor or Cause works agains the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Counter_actor that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE denotes the number of times the Counter_actor or Cause subverts the Agent per unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: This FE denotes the number of times the subversion event has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Counter_actor or an event related to the Cause that enables the subversion.

annotations.definition: An Agent has attempted to achieve a Goal, and the actual outcome of the Agent's action has been resolved, so that it either specifically matches the Agent's intent (e.g. success) or does not match it (e.g. failure).

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent makes an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is what the Agent attempts to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Goal is achieved (or not).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt succeeds or fails.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where an attempt succeeds or fails.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: A participant function in a particular event or in events of a particular kind.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE is applied to expressions which indicate the prevailing physical conditions for the event, or some concurrent states of affairs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the success or failure logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the success or failure, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies an event during which the Agent attempts to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: Re-encoding presents the success or failure event as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.

annotations.definition: The Means action by which a deprofiled agent (the Protagonist) has attempted to achieve a Goal, is finished and the actual outcome of the Means action has been resolved, so that it either specifically matches the Protagonist's intent (e.g. success) or does not match it (e.g. failure). In an alternative construal, an Instrument stands for the Means action that the deprofiled agent undertakes in order to achieve the Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action that the agent undertakes to achieve the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is what the agent attempts to achieve by performing the Means action.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the agent as part of the Means action in order to accomplish the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where Means action succeeds or fails.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the attempt succeeds or fails
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The FE Circumstances encodes a setting, often temporal or spatial, within which the successful action expressed by the target takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist is the deprofiled individual who makes an attempt to achieve a Goal by performing the Means action, which may involve using an Instrument.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an attempt involving a particular Means or Instrument succeeds.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: A participant function of an Instrument or Means in a particular event or in events of a particular kind.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the success or failure logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the success or failure, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event or state of affairs that occurs or holds at a time that includes the time during which the success or failure reported by the target occurs and of which the success or failure is taken to be a part

annotations.definition: A Communicator speaks to an Addressee in such a way that the Addressee understands and fully appreciates the truth and/or relevance of the Message.

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The person who uses language in the written or spoken modality to convey a Message to another person.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee receives a Message from the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: Message A proposition or set of propositions that the Communicator wants the Addressee to believe or take for granted.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive describes the state of the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Communicator communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Communicator communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the communication takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Adequacy: The degree to which the Communicator's efforts are effective at making the Addressee understand and appreciate the Message.

annotations.definition: An Item is located on a Scale relative to a critical value which is determined by some Enabled_situation. Generally, when the Enabled_situation is left unexpressed the Enabled_situation is interpreted to be the desirability of the Item being located within a particular range on the Scale. With many of the adjectives of this frame, the Scale is not explicitly mentioned; in such cases, the Scale is usually the quantity, incorporated in the meaning of the targets themselves, though in the case of adequacy, etc. the Scale referred to is quality.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: The entity whose scalar property is specified.
frameElementDefinitions.Scale: The Scale is the attribute of the Item that is being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Enabled_situation: This FE identifies the Enabled_situation by which the critical value is judged.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree frame element indicates a modification of the value indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: An Assailant attacks a Victim, intending to die in the process. In the prototypical case, the attack is designed to kill the Assailant, as when a pilot deliberately crashes a plane into the target.

frameElementDefinitions.Victim: This Victim is the being or entity that is injured by the Assailant's suicide attack.
frameElementDefinitions.Weapon: An entity used by the Assailant to cause damage to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Assailant: The person (or other self-directed entity) that is attempting physical harm to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Assailant that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Assailant that accomplishes the assault.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Assailant makes an assault on the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Assailant attacks the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Assailant attacks the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The starting-point of the motion of the Assailant or projectiles of the Assailant as they move toward the the Place that the Victim is occupying in the attack. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. With particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE is used for any Depictive phrase describeing the state of the Assailant or Victim while the Attack occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the attack on the Victim by the Assailant.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the Assailant travels.

annotations.definition: This Frame is concerned with evaluating an Evaluee as being suitable for a Purpose or a User, presupposing some choice that is being made about which Evaluee to use for the Purpose or User. An LU like suitable.a thus implies that, given a particular Purpose or User, the Evaluee is one which could be or ought to be chosen. The Evaluee may be deemed suitable to a greater or lesser Degree. This frame has some similarity to the compatibility frame where two Items are evaluated with respect to their compatibility with each other against some background.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: This Frame Element marks constituents expressing the entity that is judged to be more or less suitable for some Purpose or User.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This Frame Element marks constituents that express the Degree to which the Evaluee is suitable for the Context.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This Frame Element marks constituents that express the Purpose for which the Evaluee is judged to be suitable or unsuitable.
frameElementDefinitions.User: This FE marks expressions that indicate a User to whom

annotations.definition: A Communicator processes some information (either from textual or real-world/perceptual sources) regarding a State_of_affairs into a concise form (a summary) that contains the essential content in briefer form, and communicates this summary to an Addressee, or records it in a Medium. The State_of_affairs may be represented directly (e.g., outline what happened, the situation abroad), or as a textual source (summarizing the documents), or metonymically as some focal entity (a synopsis of the candidates).

frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The Communicator creates and communicates the summary of the Information.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Communicator communicates the summary to the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The State_of_affairs is summarized by the Communicator.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The textual or other Medium through which the Communicator summarizes.
frameElementDefinitions.Summary: The content that results from the summarization.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Communicator makes the Summary.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Communicator communicates.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the details of the action beyond the identity of the participants and the setting, especially descriptions of the Communicator that affect the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Communicator that brings about the communication.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Communicator is attempting to bring about by communicating.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time taken by the Communicator in creating and conveying the Summary.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: A Supplier gives a Theme to a Recipient to fulfill a need or purpose (Imposed_purpose) of the Recipient.

frameElementDefinitions.Supplier: The person that begins in possession of the Theme and causes it to be in the possession of the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that is given to the Recipient .
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Supplier gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Supplier gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Supplier gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Supplier gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Supplier acquires or gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the supplying event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Supplier that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the conditions under which the Theme is supplied.
frameElementDefinitions.Imposed_purpose: The Recipient's intended purpose for the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The period over which the Supplier engages in (multiple) supplying acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: An additional theme which is supplied along with the main Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The source of the Theme.

annotations.definition: A Supporter assists to strengthen the Supported by lending itself in material aid (or abstract assistance in the case of metaphorical uses).

frameElementDefinitions.Supporter: The Supporter acts to benefit the Supported by providing additional strength or stability.
frameElementDefinitions.Supported: The Supported is given strength through the assistance of the Supporter.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The measure to which the Supporter's assistance brings the Supported added strength and support.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time period during which the Supporter supports the Supported.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity used by the Supporter in service of the Supported's strength by support.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which a Supporter supports the Supported.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action that the Supporter undertakes in service of the Supported's strengthening.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the support occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies when the support occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The situation that the Supporter wishes to bring about by supporting the Supported.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state-of-affairs from which the accomplishment would be predicted.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: A clause describing the event as a whole.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Profiled_item is higher that its Standard_item on a scale with respect to some Attribute. The Extent of the difference between the Standard_item and the Profiled_item may also be mentioned. Furthermore, the particular value of the standard or profiled item on a certain scale may be given by the Standard_attribute of the Profiled_attribute respectively.

frameElementDefinitions.Standard_item: The FE Standard_item denotes the entities that are exceeded by the Item with regard to the Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_item: The Profiled_item is the entity that is located closest to the end of the scale evoked by some Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The Attribute is a property that evokes a scale along which the Item is located closer to one extreme in comparison to the Standard_item.
frameElementDefinitions.Extent: The Extent is the degree to which the Item surpasses the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Item is higher on the scale than the Standard.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard_attribute: The Standard_attribute is used when the standard is a specific value on a scale.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_attribute: The Profiled_attribute is a particular value on a scale which is being compared to an Attribute or Standard_item.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: The Comparison_set includes the Standard and is what the Item is judged with respect to.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Fugitive presents himself or herself to the Authorities to be subject to the criminal process.

frameElementDefinitions.Fugitive: The Fugitive is wanted by the Authorities and turns himself or herself in to the Authorities.
frameElementDefinitions.Authorities: The Authorities are the officials, government or otherwise, who are in charge of initiating the criminal process.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location where the surrendering occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Fugitive surrenders.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: The Charges is the offense for which the Fugitive is wanted.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Fugitive surrenders to the Authorities.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the surrendering event logically follows.

annotations.definition: A Surrenderer is compelled to transfer a Theme to a Recipient.

frameElementDefinitions.Surrenderer: The person compelled to transfer the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that changes possession.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the Surrenderer gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE denotes a proposition from which the surrendering event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the Surrenderer gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Surrenderer gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means by which the Surrenderer gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner is the manner in which the Surrenderer gives the Theme to the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the Surrendering event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE modifies a clause with an iterative interpretation, which may be signified by the simple present tense on the main verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole. There are two somewhat distinct subgroups of sentence modifiers that cover this function.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the target operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often. It is to be distinguished from Iteration, which pertains simply to the number of times an event occurs-Iteration expressions answer the question how many times.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: This FE identifies the Result of an surrendering event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: A Figure surrounds a Ground. The Ground location is presented as more likely to be a known location than the location of the Figure. Some lexical units entail more-or-less complete surrounding in two or three dimensions (e.g. surround.v), while others entail surrounding on only two sides (e.g. astride.prep, straddle.v). Like other locative relations, the Figure may be any of a movable entity, an immovable entity, or an event.

frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is the entity located with respect to the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground is the area that the Figure surrounds.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Figure is located around the Ground.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE identifies the Direction from a reference location (generally, the deictic center) of a path to the Figure.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance between the Figure and some reference location (generally, the deictic center or the Ground). WARNING: Probably not relevant for this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Profiled_region: The area or zone immediately external to the Ground. This FE excludes Figure and is only used in particular constructions such as the following:
frameElementDefinitions.Directness: An indication of how closely the position of Figure matches the prototype expected from the image schema associated with the target. This FE is normally filled by closed-class, dedicated adverbs like right, straight, and directly.
frameElementDefinitions.Accessibility: An indication of how accessible the Figure is to some participant in the speech context (either the speaker or the addressee, or some other highly salient viewpoint in a narrative). This FE normally describes availability for manipulation and use, but may simply describe how easily seen the Figure is. There is a closed class of items that can fill this slot, including only 'just' and 'right' indicating ease of access.

annotations.definition: A Survivor manages to avoid being negatively affected, despite encountering a Dangerous_situation. The negative effect is most often death, destruction, or a metaphorical construal of one of these.

frameElementDefinitions.Survivor: The Survivor lives through a Dangerous_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Dangerous_situation: The Dangerous_situation is one in which people are likely or expected to be negatively affected.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Survivor (primarily) lives during the Dangerous_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the surviving takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Survivor that enables them to live throug hthe Dangerous_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which the Survivor survives.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive is a description of the Survivor, during or after the Dangerous_situation.

annotations.definition: A person or group, the Authority, believes that another person, the Suspect is a participant in a criminal Incident.

frameElementDefinitions.Authority: The Authority is the group or person who suspects the Suspect of criminal activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: The Suspect is the target of the Authority's suspicion.
frameElementDefinitions.Incident: The (typically criminal) Incident in which the Suspect is suspected of being involved.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the suspecting takes place.

annotations.definition: Perspective of Reciprocality in which the Protagonists are viewed as essentially identically participating in the Reciprocal event, relation, or situation, not differing in agency, affectedness, or presumed cognitive or conversational accessibility.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonists: All the participants in an event or situation, viewed as symmetrically contributing or participating in the situation.

annotations.definition: A Complex with a particular Function is formed out of Component_entities. The function may be expressed by reference to a Salient_entity involved in the working of the Complex.

frameElementDefinitions.Component_entities: The entities that function together as a unit.
frameElementDefinitions.Complex: The unit formed by the Component_entities.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: The activity that the Complex entity performs by virtue of its Component_entities interacting.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterization or evaluation of the Complex.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: A person or organization that possesses or owns the Complex or a larger entity of which the Complex itself is a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: The kind of entity that the Complex operates on as part of its Function.

annotations.definition: A System is perceived as complex depending on the intricacy of the interconnectivty of its parts or components. These individual parts or components do not function as frame elements in this frame, even though they are implied by its definition. A Dimension may also be defined which situates the System within a certain domain.

frameElementDefinitions.System: The complete entity or system whose complexity is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Dimension provides domain specific information about the System.
frameElementDefinitions.Dimension: This frame element situates the System within a certain domain.

annotations.definition: A New filler occupies a Role or serves a Function after the Old filler ceases to occupy the position. In many cases, the Role or Function is implicit.

frameElementDefinitions.New: The new filler of the Role that was previously occupied by the Old filler.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The category that the New and Old entities fit into as a result of the Function they serve.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: The (generally desirable) state of affairs that the New and Old entities are involved in bringing about.
frameElementDefinitions.Old: The entity that occupied the Role or served the Function before the New filler.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the New takes up the Role previously occupied by the Old.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A Degree of replacement is generally only possible when New entities replace Old entities in multiple contexts.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). The rapidity of replacement is also indicated as Manner.
frameElementDefinitions.Textual_location: The domain in which the New and Old entities function.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows.

annotations.definition: An Agent removes a Theme from a Source so that the it is in the Agent's possession.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who takes possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Agent takes possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The location of the Theme prior to the taking.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the taking takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the taking takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner of the taking.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The intermediate action taken by the Agent to take the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for the taking.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for the taking.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The larger event which contains the Taking event.

annotations.definition: An Agent begins preventing a Captive from moving despite a tendency or desire on the part of the Captive to do so.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent brings the Captive into custody.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The instrument with which the capturing is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Agent takes the Captive into custody.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Agent that accomplishes the action indicated by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Agent that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that the Agent is responding to in capturing the Captive.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause is the non-sentient entity which is responsible for restricting the movement or location of the Captive.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The description of one of the participants in the capturing event.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: The larger event which contains or during which occurs the event of taking the Captive captive.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer has a relatively fixed positive or negative point of view towards an Issue. A Side in a debate concerning an Issue or an Action of a Side may stand in for the Issue. The Cognizer's Degree of alignment may also be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The individual that holds an opinion on an Issue or Side.
frameElementDefinitions.Issue: The matter, usually concerning a course of action, which the Cognizer has a positive or negative opinion about.
frameElementDefinitions.Side: The party or individual (generally associated with a particular position on an Issue) that the Cognizer has an opinion about.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Cognizer supports or opposes something.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Cognizer has the indicated point of view towards the Issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is a proposition denoting a state of affairs from which the Cognizer's taking the indicated view on the Issue logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Action: An action taken by a Side or a view that the Side takes on an issue which the Cognizer either supports or opposes.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes the person or element that is taking a side.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Taking_sides which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Cognizer that affect the action (reluctantly, meekly).
frameElementDefinitions.Concessive: This FE signifies that the state of affairs expressed by the main clause (containing the target) occurs or holds, and something other than that state of affairs would be expected given the state of affairs in the concessive clause.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Cognizer takes Sides on an Issue .

annotations.definition: An Activity takes some Time_length to complete. If an Agent is involved they may stand in metonymically for the Activity. For some LUs (e.g., fast, speedy) the Degree to which the Activity is conceived of as taking less time than usual can be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The event which requires some Time_length for completion.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The durational feature of the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_length: The amount of time required for the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Time_length from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Activity takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the Circumstances under which the Activity takes a particular amount of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The individual who performs the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration is the length of time from the beginning of the Activity to its end.

annotations.definition: A Speaker expresses through language his wish to get the Addressee to act. The Addressee forms an intention to act and does so.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker intends through the use of language to get another person to act.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The wish for action on the Addressee's part that the Speaker expresses.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The person that the Speaker seeks to influence to act.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: Act of the Speaker that enables them to persuade the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: An entity that the Speaker gets the Addressee to give to them.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the Speaker hopes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity that the Speaker manipulates to get the Addressee to perform the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Speaker talks the Addressee into performing the Content.

annotations.definition: An Agent ingests or begins to ingest a Food item that they have not had before with the intention of determining its quality on some scale. A Purpose for trying the Food may be explicitly mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who eats or drinks the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Food: The entity being tasted or ingested by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The method in which the Food is eaten or drunk if not covered by the FE Instrument.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The object that the Agent uses to try the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: A more specific description of what the Agent hopes to determine by trying the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Agent tries the Food.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the scene of an Agent trying Food occurs.\
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A characterization of the Agent as they try the Food.

annotations.definition: A Team is a group with shared goals and works toward them together in a coordinated fashion.

frameElementDefinitions.Members: This FE identifies the people or things that form the Aggregate.
frameElementDefinitions.Team: This FE identifies the collection of entities that are the Team.
frameElementDefinitions.Aggregate_property: This FE describes a property of the Aggregate rather than the Members and is used to tag the adjective that modifies the aggregate noun when it is unambiguous.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to the Aggregate.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location or political locale that the Organization is contained within.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The relevant Domain as it pertains to the Aggregate.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The job, obligation, or goal worked towards jointly by the Members of the Team.
frameElementDefinitions.Parent_organization: The broader organization (if any) that members of a team belong to.

annotations.definition: A Speaker addresses an Addressee with a Message, which may be indirectly referred to as a Topic. Instead of (or in addition to) a Speaker, a Medium may also be mentioned.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker is the sentient entity that produces the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee receives the Message from the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message is the communication produced by the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is a general description of the content of the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes the way in which the Speaker delivers the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means describes the way the Speaker accomplishes the task of giving the Addressee the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This FE is the Medium in which the Message is expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Speaker informs the Addressee of the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place is the location in which the Speaker informs the Addressee of the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the Message is expressed. This FE is often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE identifies a characteristic or description of the telling event.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The FE Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.

annotations.definition: An Entity has a Temperature characterized by the target. The Temperature is a value of the temperature Attribute.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity for which the temperature Attribute is under consideration.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: The feature of an Entity which is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Temperature: A quantity or other characterization of the Entity's state with respect to the temperature Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Temperature from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity is in the state of having a particular Degree for the temperature Attribute.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the Circumstances under which the Entity has a particular Temperature.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: A part of the Entity that has the specified Temperature value.

annotations.definition: A Trajector is identified as occurring at the same time as some better-known Landmark. We consider sequenced calendric units (e.g. Tuesday, 1987, January) as periods for the purposes of this frame. Some lexical items allow certain types of frame elements while disallowing others (e.g. *During Tuesday...). Several words of this frame (e.g. now.adv) have deictic incorporation of the Landmark_period.

frameElementDefinitions.Trajector_entity: The entity that occurs at some time.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_event: The event which designates the time at which a trajector occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE describes the state of affairs reported by the target is understood to play a role in the state of affairs of the modifier.
frameElementDefinitions.Trajector_period: The period that occurs with respect to some other time.
