frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_period: The period in which a trajector takes place. This period can be cyclical and, in that case, is interpreted contextually.
frameElementDefinitions.Trajector_event: The event that occurs at some time.
frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_entity: The entity which designates the time of some trajector. Note: this FE is infrequent or non-existent in English and is included for completeness.

annotations.definition: A salient Subevent has a Pattern of recurrence within a larger Process.

frameElementDefinitions.Process: The larger event that is characterized by the recurring Subevents.
frameElementDefinitions.Subevent: The salient, punctual event that is construed as part of a Process.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Pattern in which the Subevents recur.
frameElementDefinitions.Pattern: The Pattern that can be detected in the recurrence of the Subevents of the Process .
frameElementDefinitions.Type: An indication of a conventionally recognized subtype that the Pattern belongs to.

annotations.definition: A Subpart of a Time_period is picked out. In some cases, the part (as opposed to the entire Time_period) may be described by a Subpart_property. The Time_period may be indicated by an event which takes place during the Time_period.

frameElementDefinitions.Subpart: The part of the Time_period which is profiled.
frameElementDefinitions.Subpart_property: Some description which pertains specifically to the Subpart (rather than the Time_period).
frameElementDefinitions.Time_period: A temporal span conceived of as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE indicates the Time that is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: A Temporary_group is formed from members of a Parent_organization for a particular Purpose. The Temporary_group does not exist independently within the Parent_organization. It is under the control of a Container_possessor, which also controls the Parent_organization.

frameElementDefinitions.Temporary_group: The group formed temporarily from a larger group for a particular purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Members: The people belonging to the Temporary_group.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which an Temporary_group is formed.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_existence: The interval during which the Temporary_group exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: A name given to the Temporary_group for identification.
frameElementDefinitions.Container_possessor: The location or political locale that the Temporary_group is contained within or is responsible to.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE describes the general location where the Temporary_group operates, but does not indicate the possessor of the Temporary_group.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any description of the Temporary_group not covered by a more specific frame element.
frameElementDefinitions.Parent_organization: The Parent_organization is the larger group from which members are selected to be part of the Temporary_group.

annotations.definition: A Person takes leave from his or her routine Activity (such as work or school) for a Duration of time. The Duration can be any length of time, as long as the absence is temporary.

frameElementDefinitions.Person: The person who is absent from his or her Activity for a Duration of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: This FE describes the activity or responsiblilities from which the Person is taking a temporary leave.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time the Person is absent from his or her Activity. This can also be expressed as a description of a duration, as shown below:
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The place in which the Person spends his or her leave.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: The kind of leave being taken by the Person.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A characterization of the absence.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the absences occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for the Person's absence.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Person's intended purpose for the temporary leave.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the leave occurs.

annotations.definition: A Guest stays for a time at a Location, which is not his or her permanent residence, for some Duration. The Location is often the permanent residence of a Host.

frameElementDefinitions.Guest: The Guest remains for a relatively short period of time in the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Location: The Location where the Guest spends a short amount of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Guest remains at the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Another person who, along with the Guest, is temporarily living at the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the temporary stay takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host is living at the Location on a permanent basis, and is allowing the Guest to stay there.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The activity that the Guest hopes to achieve by the stay.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Guest that brings about their ability to stay at the Location.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the type of event by comparison to other events (the same way) or by characterizing a state of the Guest that affects the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The average number of times the event occurs over a period.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of one of the participants in the temporary stay, or of the staying state-of-affairs in general.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place indicates the larger physical setting of the temporary stay.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A state of affairs that provides an explanation for the temporary stay.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time of the temporary stay, and which is taken to be a part of the stay.

annotations.definition: In this scenario, the Theme begins in possession of the Lender and ends up in the possession of the Borrower, with the expectation that the Borrower will return the Theme after a Duration of time.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that is transferred from the Lender to the Borrower for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Lender: The person or institution who gives the Theme to the Borrower for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Borrower: The person or institution who receives the Theme from the Lender for a Duration.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time in which the Borrower has possession of the Theme.

annotations.definition: A condition that is set out in an Agreement imposes Requirements on a Party or parties, with respect to some Regulated_entity. If the conditions are not met, the Agreement as a whole ceases to be valid or, more specifically, a Desired_state_of_affairs cannot occur. (The Desired_state_of_affairs typically is an interaction between the parties that benefits one or both sides.)

frameElementDefinitions.Agreement: A mutual commitment to an obligation made by two or more parties.
frameElementDefinitions.Regulated_entity: A salient entity, an activity or behavior by the parties to the agreement to which a particular stipulation pertains.
frameElementDefinitions.Requirements: The Requirements that a particular stipulation of the Agreement imposes on one or both of the parties.
frameElementDefinitions.Desired_state_of_affairs: An event or state of affairs that is desirable to some or all of the parties to the Agreement and which is conditional on the Requirements being met.
frameElementDefinitions.Party: A party who demands that certain Requirements be met before they will enter into an Agreement or allow a Desired_state_of_affairs to be realized.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of the Requirements that are imposed by the Agreement.

annotations.definition: A Terrorist commits a violent or otherwise harmful Act upon a Victim in order to coerce or terrorize a government or populace. The Act may be committed in particular for political or ideological reasons, and is often directed at civilians, as opposed to government or military officials. It is most often in violation of one or many laws.

frameElementDefinitions.Terrorist: The Terrorist commits the Act of terror for political or ideological gains.
frameElementDefinitions.Act: The Act is the harmful action performed by the Terrorist or terrorist organization.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is most directly affected or harmed by the Act of terrorism.
frameElementDefinitions.Organization: A Terrorist may be part of a larger group or Organization of such people.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterization or evaluation of the Terrorist or Act.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Terrorist commits the Act.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The intermediate action performed by the Terrorist in order to comit the Act.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The location in time when the Act takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Act takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The action or state-of-affairs that the Terrorist is trying to accomplish by commiting the Act.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The Instrument is used by the Terrorist in order to inflict harm or commit the Act.

annotations.definition: A Text is an entity that contains linguistic, symbolic information on a Topic, created by an Author at the Time_of_creation. It may be a physical entity that is made of a certain Material etc. It may be constructed for an Honoree.

frameElementDefinitions.Author: The Author is the person who produced a particular Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Text: The Text is the entity that contains linguistic information. This an incorporated FE for each LU in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The person for whom the Text is produced.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The Time at which the Text was created.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: What a Text is made of (if applicable).
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_text: This is a text in which the Text is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Genre: The Genre or sub-Genre of the Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The use for which the text was produced; its purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Subpart: The smaller texts that make up the Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: Topic is what the Text is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Title: The Title of a Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is the language in which a text is composed, or the physical medium (radio, ink) in which it is distributed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The region where the Text is sold, circulated, or otherwise popular.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic of the Text

annotations.definition: An Author creates a Text, either written, such as a letter, or spoken, such as a speech, that contains meaningful linguistic tokens, and may have a particular Addressee in mind. The Text may include information about its topic, although the latter is not an FE in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Author: The Author produces a particular Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Components: The Components are the parts that the Author uses to construct the Text.
frameElementDefinitions.Text: The entity which results from the act of writing or speaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: The instrument with which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action. This frame includes some peculiar examples of manner phrases. Write it in pen. Write it in ink.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Text is created.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time at which the text is created.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: This is the person to whom the Message is communicated. When this FE is expressed, it often appears in a prepositional phrase introduced by to, or as the direct object, or as the first object in a double object construction.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: Beneficiary identifies the person in whose honor or for whose benefit the Text is created.
frameElementDefinitions.Form: This FE describes the form in which the text is presented.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for creating the text.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The language in which the Text is represented or the physical materials upon which the Text is recorded.

annotations.definition: These are words describing situations in which a Perpetrator takes Goods from a Victim or a Source. The Means by which this is accomplished may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Perpetrator: Perpetrator is the person (or other agent) that takes the goods away.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: Victim is the person (or other sentient being or group) that owns the goods before they are taken away by the perpetrator.
frameElementDefinitions.Goods: Goods is anything (including labor, time, or legal rights) that can be taken away.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is the initial location of the goods, before they change location.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the theft occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Perpetrator takes the Victim's Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the theft occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which a theft occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE identifies the Explanation for why a theft occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the theft occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An object used by the Perpetrator in taking possession of the Goods.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that a theft occurs.

annotations.definition: This frame describes the attributes of a thermodynamic Phase of Matter.

frameElementDefinitions.Matter: The Matter is the physical substance or entity being described in terms of a Phase.
frameElementDefinitions.Phase: The state of the Matter being described.

annotations.definition: An Entity is in a state such that it participates in a preponderance of states and events which are desirable for it. For living entities, this may entail a proliferation of instances of the entity, or of desirable properties associated with the (single) entity. For non-living entities (economies, political locales, academic topics, etc.), this entails a proliferation of instances, or of activities that lead to instances, or any attribute that would prevent the entity from disappearing. This state is conceived of as temporary, or at least contingent on a number of changeable factors. Words expressing the opposite state, i.e., a lack of such positive activity, are also in this frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity is experiencing a period of desirable events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Entity thrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place where the thriving takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: The category in which the Entity thrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for why or how the Entity thrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Desirability: This FE describes the state of the Entity. It is often incorporated into the LUs of this frame.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Preventing_cause interferes with the ability of a Protagonist to complete an intended Action. The Protagonist may not be mentioned in the sentence, in which case the Action is an endeavor that an individual or group of individuals hopes to accomplish. In this frame it is necessary that the Protagonist is unable to successfully complete the Action.

frameElementDefinitions.Preventing_cause: The Preventing_cause is the agent or force who keeps the Protagonist from completing her or his planned Action.
frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The Protagonist is the individual or group of individuals who have a planned Action which is prevented by the Preventing_cause
frameElementDefinitions.Action: The Action is the action or event certain individuals desire to bring about which is prevented by the Preventing_cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time is when the thwarting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the thwarting occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Preventing_cause thwarts the Action of the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Action is thwarted.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for why or how the Protagonist and/or Action was thwarted by the Preventing_cause.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question how often.

annotations.definition: LUs in this frame denote a period of time in which an Enabled_action becomes possible. The Duration of this time period can be both exact and inexact. An Agent who performs the Enabled_action may be mentioned as well.

frameElementDefinitions.Enabled_action: The action that becomes possible through the existence of the period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: A quantity or other characterization of the period of the time in which an Enabled_action is allowed. In this frame, the duration may be exact or inexact.
frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is the one who performs the Enabled_action.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole is the larger time period to which target is a part of.

annotations.definition: An Event is identified as occurring at a particular Distance and in a particular Direction from a Landmark_event. The lexical units incorporate the Direction and, in some cases, the Landmark_event. This frame builds on the Direction frame, further specifying the Domain as Time. This frame will receive some of the lexical units currently found in Relative_time.

frameElementDefinitions.Landmark_event: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate the reference point in which the Event is anchored.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE marks expressions that indicate the amount of time that lies between the Target and the Landmark_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: This FE marks expressions that indicate whether the Target is prior or subsequent to the Landmark_event on the time line.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: This FE indicates the Event temporally located with respect to the Landmark_event.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe a period of time, as opposed to a point in time.

frameElementDefinitions.Duration: A quantity or other characterization of the State's duration. In this frame, the duration can be inexact.
frameElementDefinitions.State: An ongoing state whose duration can be measured.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole is the larger moment in time of which the target is a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Any characterization or evaluation of the timespan not covered by a more specific FE.

annotations.definition: A Timetable of Events is a mental object deliberately created, modified or kept by a Cognizer. The Timetable is often modified by an adjective depicting a Period_of_iterations or a dependent noun expressing the Events of the Timetable. Note that this frame does not include the physical representation of a timetable.

frameElementDefinitions.Timetable: The mental schedule of times at which the Events are set to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Events: The events which are to take place at specified times acording to the Timetable.
frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The sentient entity who keeps the Timetable.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The time period within which the Timetable repeats.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_implementation: The time at which the Events on the Timetable transpire.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose for which the Cognizer keeps the Timetable.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterization of the Timetable.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe an Experiencer's feelings of tolerating with respect to some Content. A Reason for the toleration may also be expressed. Although the Content may refer to an actual, current state of affairs, quite often it refers to a general situation which causes the emotion.

frameElementDefinitions.Experiencer: The Experiencer experiences tolerance with respect to the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: Content is what the Experiencer's feeling of tolerance is directed towards.. The Content differs from a stimulus because the Content is not construed as being direcly responsible for causing the emotion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is the explanation for why an Experiencer tolerates the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the toleration occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner of performing an action
frameElementDefinitions.Parameter: The Parameter is a domain in which the Experiencer tolerates the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the area about which the Experiencer has the tolerated experience(s).
frameElementDefinitions.State: The State is the abstract noun that describes a more lasting experience by the Experiencer.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: The Event is the occasion or happening that Experiencers in a certain emotional state participate in.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances FE encodes the circumstances or conditions under which the Experiencer experiences the emotion. Unlike Content it does not give the specific content of the emotion, but the Content can often be inferred from the Circumstances.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Experiencer is in the state of toleration.

annotations.definition: A living entity intends a Tool to be able to fulfill a generic Purpose. The material from which the tool is created can be something natural or something manmade, including another tool.

frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: A Purpose is a generic goal associated with the Tool.
frameElementDefinitions.Tool: A Tool is the object or process that has been designed specifically to achieve a Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: A description of the Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The sphere of activity in which the Tool is typically used.

annotations.definition: A stretch of linguistic discourse or a Text that a Communicator produces has a Topic that it is about.

frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is what a Text is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Text: A set of propositions that is coherent in being about a Topic
frameElementDefinitions.Communicator: The Communicator is the person who has produced a Text on a Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate to which Degree the Text is about the Topic
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE marks expressions in which Manner the Topic is treated.
frameElementDefinitions.Status: The Frame Element Status is used for expressions that indicate the relative importance of a Topic with respect to the other topics that a Text is about.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Communicator produces Text on a Topic.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Text or Communicator presents the Topic.

annotations.definition: A Tourist experiences an Attraction, a location with a unique history or other societally recognized individual character, with the goal of seeing and learning about it. Typically, the Attraction has a source of information about the Attraction--a guide, pamphlets, or displays.

frameElementDefinitions.Tourist: The individual that is seeking an experience at an Attraction.
frameElementDefinitions.Attraction: The societally-recognized unique location that the Tourist experiences.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Tourist or the Attraction during the tour.
frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The perceptual background against which the Attraction is experienced by the Tourist.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the details of the event in terms of how they compare to other events (the same way) or in terms of how the state of the Tourist affects them (happily, obliviously).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means indicates a method the Tourist is using to participate in a tour.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time that the touring takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Some action that the Tourist is trying to accomplish with touring. This is generally (at a minimum) for the purposes of entertainment and learning.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the touring takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time the touring activity lasts.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: The Co-participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the touring event with the Tourist.

annotations.definition: A Toxic_substance causes death or harm when introduced into or absorbed by a living organism, the Victim. The Toxic_substance may be specified to be produced by or contained in a Toxin_source. The Toxic_substance may affect the Victim as a whole or a particular Body_part (understood here to include organs and other sub-systems). A Degree of harmfulness can be specified and the Toxic_substance may develop its effect only under particular Circumstances.

frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the being or entity that is capable of being harmed by the Toxic_substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree is the degree to which the Toxic_substance is harmful to the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The Body_part defines the part of the Victim's body that is harmed.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A fact or action that explains why the Toxic_substance has a harmful effect on the Victim .
frameElementDefinitions.Toxic_substance: This FE marks expressions that indicate the substance which potentially causes harm to a Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: A set of circumstances, often the relative amount of the Toxic_substance that is present, under which the Toxic_substance is specified to have or lack a toxic effect.
frameElementDefinitions.Toxin_source: A living organism, object or process that produces a Toxic_substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: Type identifies the kind or sort of Toxic_substance.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Temporal period for which the substance or source remains toxic to the victim.

annotations.definition: This frame involves a Donor transferring a Theme to a Recipient.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that changes ownership.
frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person that begins in possession of the Theme and causes it to be in the possession of the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which an event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the transfer occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Transferors: The Donor and Recipient conceived of as a unit.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action whereby the transfer of the Theme from the Donor to the Recipient is effected.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the transfer which is not covered by more specific FEs
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which a Theme is transferred

annotations.definition: In this scenario, the Theme begins in the possession of the Donor and ends up in the possession of the Recipient. Alternatively, these two participants may be jointly construed as Transferors.

frameElementDefinitions.Recipient: The entity that ends up in possession of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The object that changes ownership.
frameElementDefinitions.Donor: The person that begins in possession of the Theme and causes it to be in the possession of the Recipient.
frameElementDefinitions.Transferors: The Donor and Recipient conceived of as a unit.

annotations.definition: An Entity ends up with a particular new Final_quality.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of time that the Entity retains the Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In cases where the Entity is intentional, Manner may indicate salient characteristics of an intentional Entity that also affect the event (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity which undergoes a change so that it possesses the Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_quality: The quality that the Entity has at the end of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Transitional_period: The period during which the Entity is in transition.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE marks the set of conditions under which the Entity adopts Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: A characterization of the kind of Group members that the Entity pertains to, and which are saliently affected by its change.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Any eventuality or entity which brings about the change of the Entity.

annotations.definition: An Entity ends up in a Final_situation.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In cases where the Entity is intentional, Manner may indicate salient characteristics of an intentional Entity that also affect the event (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity which undergoes a change, ending up in the Final_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Transitional_period: The period during which the Entity is in transition.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE marks the set of conditions under which the Entity enters the Final_situation.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The type of individual things that have an instance of the Entity and which are saliently affected by its change.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Any eventuality or entity which brings about the change of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_situation: The Entity's situation after the change.

annotations.definition: An Entity ends up in a Final_category, Final_situation, or Final_quality which it was not in before. Some members of the frame (e.g. grow.v) tend to emphasize a gradual change, while most members of the frame do not indicate whether a change is gradual or not.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the change occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: For how long the Entity remains in the Final_category, Final_situation, or Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In cases where the Entity is intentional, Manner may indicate salient characteristics of an intentional Entity that also affect the event (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The Entity which undergoes a change, ending up in the Final_category, Final_situation, or Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_quality: The quality of the Entity after the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_category: The category that the Entity ends up in after the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Transitional_period: The period during which the Entity is in transition.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_state: This FE describes the state of the Entity before becoming occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE marks the set of conditions under which the Entity enters the Final_category, Final_situation, or Final_quality.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_category: This FE describes the category of the Entity before becoming occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The individual entities related to the Entity which are saliently affected by its change.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: Any eventuality or entity which brings about the change of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_situation: The Entity's situation after the change.

annotations.definition: This frame characterizes, at a very abstract level, an Agent or Cause affecting a Patient. This frame is inherited by many lower-level frames.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: Agent is the entity that acts on another entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Patient: Patient is the entity that is being acted on and that may but need not undergo a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: An Event in which an Agent acts on an entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing a participant in the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of a transitive action.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An act performed by an Agent that enables them to act upon the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event which causes the Patient to be affected in some way.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer produces a Target_symbol which represents, in the Target_representation format, a Content that pre-exists in the form of a Source_symbol in a Source_representation format.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: This is the person who carries out the translation.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_symbol: A symbol or set of symbols representing the Content in the Source_representation format.
frameElementDefinitions.Source_representation: The representation system in which the Content pre-exists.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the translation event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of the Cognizer that also affect the action (deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Cognizer which accomplishes the translation of the Content.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This Frame Element marks expressions that indicate when a certain Categorization was made.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are the set of conditions in which the translation is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_symbol: A symbol or set of symbols representing the Content in the Target_representation format.
frameElementDefinitions.Target_representation: The representation system into which the Content is rendered by the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The information that is meant to be represented by the Source_symbol and Target_symbol.
frameElementDefinitions.Equivalence: An assessment of how equivalent the Source_representation and the Target_representation are.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is a text produced by the Cognizer in which she uses a Target_symbol to express Content from a Source_representation system.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the translation takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Cognizer hopes to bring about by translating.

annotations.definition: A Rider is being transported in a Vehicle, or is moving on foot.

frameElementDefinitions.Rider: The Rider is being transported by a Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Vehicle carries the Rider.

annotations.definition: A Deceiver sets up a Trap in order to cause a Victim to have an incorrect understanding about a situation. The Deceiver anticipates that this will lead the Victim to take a course of action (including doing nothing) which causes the Victim harm.

frameElementDefinitions.Trap: The Trap is a device, strategy, or system designed to mislead and cause harm to a Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Deceiver: The Trap is orchestrated by a Deceiver.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Deceiver intends to mislead and cause harm to the Victim by means of the Trap.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description of the Trap.

annotations.definition: In this frame a Traveler goes on a journey, an activity, generally planned in advance, in which the Traveler moves from a Source location to a Goal along a Path or within an Area. The journey can be accompanied by Co-participants and Baggage. The Duration or Distance of the journey, both generally long, may also be described as may be the Mode_of_transportation. Words in this frame emphasize the whole process of getting from one place to another, rather than profiling merely the beginning or the end of the journey.

frameElementDefinitions.Area: This is the Area in which the traveling takes place. This frame element describes the enclosed area inside which travelling, of unspecified Source, Path or Goal takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: The Co-participant is the person or persons who accompany the Traveler on the journey.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE dientifies the Distance traveled.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: This FE identifies the Duration of time during which the trip occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the location where the travelers end up.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the traveling takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action done by the Traveler by which the traveling is accomplished.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path is the route along which the travel takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Traveler: This is the living being which travels. Normally, the Traveler is expressed as an external argument.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the starting point of the trip.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the travel takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Traveler is effected, by their body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Traveler. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the traveling occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the travel is undertaken.
frameElementDefinitions.Baggage: The Baggage are the items necessary for travel that accompany the Traveler.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the Traveler makes the journey.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Traveler travels, i.e. the future state of affairs that will hopefully come about.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times the trip is traveled by the Travelers.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The Time throughout which the traveling repeatedly takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Travel_means: This FE applies to expressions that indicate documents, money, tickets, etc. which allow people to engage in travel.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Traveler during the journey.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The affect of travel on a Traveler.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location of the motion. It describes the area in which motion (with a specified Source, Path or Goal) takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction in which the Traveler goes.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characterisitic of the traveling event.

annotations.definition: A Theme changes location with respect to a salient location, which can be expressed by a Source, Path, Goal, Area, Direction, Path_shape, or Distance, as shown in the examples below:

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: This is the object which moves. In many sentences this FE will be expressed as something which moves under its own power. However, this FE is not required to be an animate mover, so it is different from Self_mover.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element Source. In prepositional phrases, the prepositional object expresses the starting point of motion. In particles, the starting point of motion is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal (see below) expresses the frame element Path, including directional expressions:
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Any expression which tells where the Theme ends up as a result of the motion expresses the frame element Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: Any expression which characterizes the extent of motion expresses the frame element Distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: This frame element is used for expressions which describe a general area in which motion takes place when the motion is understood to be irregular and not to consist of a single linear path.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which event occurs
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event involving the Theme that results in the Theme moving in the specified way.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the Theme moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: This is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique:
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depictive phrase describing the actor of an action
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is where the motion (which is specified by a Source, Path or Goal) is located.
frameElementDefinitions.Path_shape: The configuration formed by the entire Path of the Theme, taken simultaneously.
frameElementDefinitions.Direction: The direction in which the Theme goes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Theme wishes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_motion: The device or bodypart that serves to move the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The length of time that the Theme spends in motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state of the Theme at the end of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A factor which explains the particular motion or manner of motion of the Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Reciprocation: An event paired with the motion, which explains why the motion is expected. In particular, or an action that Reciprocation is used to indicate an action that is being rewarded or punished by the motion, is paired with the motion as the other side of an agreement.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at the time the Traversing occurs and which the Traversing is taken to be a part of.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The number of times that an action occurs in a given time.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: A larger conceptualization that includes the Traversing as an integral part. The Traversing expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action in this larger conceptualization.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: An unexpected event that affects some agent (usually the Theme), which is enabled by, and occurs after, the Traversing.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the Traversing event itself and any of its participants, and is merely peripherally related to the event as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Endpoints: The Source and Goal of either a single or a repeated motion taken together as Endpoints of the Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Coordinated_event: The label Coordinated_event is to be used for phrases denoting an event-it does not allow states-that the Traversing is rhythmically aligned with. The Coordinated_event is conceived of as independent: it would occur regardless of the event expressed by the target, which is not even an incidental or optional sub-part of the Coordinated_event.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The time period within which the motion event occurs repeatedly.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: In general, this FE is used for phrases that describe the clause of the target as a whole. There are two somewhat distinct subgroups of sentence modifiers that cover this function.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.

annotations.definition: In this frame an Agent is evaluated as behaving toward an Affected_Party in some Manner, which may or may not be incorporated into the target LU.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This FE marks expressions that denote the person whose conduct is being evaluated.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE marks expressions that characterize the way that the Agent behaves toward the Affected_Party. In the case of mistreat.v, this FE is incorporated.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE marks expressions that indicate a set of Circumstances under which the Agent acts in a particular way.
frameElementDefinitions.Affected_party: The person to whom behavior is directed by the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE marks expressions that indicate why an Agent behaved in a certain way.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Agent behaves in a certain way.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Agent exhibits their behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The way in which the treating or mistreating is done.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The Domain of human behavior in which the Agent acts in a certain Manner.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the (usually 'good' or 'bad') conduct of the Agent deviates from some standard or norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Standard: An explicit point to which the conduct of the Agent is compared.

annotations.definition: A Behavior, attributed to an Evaluee, conforms with or is heavily influenced by a popular trend or practice, often in the realm of fashion, design, though extending social and interactional behavior in general. Trendiness differs from Popularity in adding a factor of aesthetic or other judgment to behaviors and individuals, and judging these with respect to social behavior; Popularity is about amount of acceptability or utilization based on general (often non-aesthetic) desirability.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the degree of trendiness from the norm or from the value for another Behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which the Behavior is trendy or fashionable.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Some specification of the circumstances under which the Behavior is trendy or not.
frameElementDefinitions.Evaluee: The Evaluee is judged to conform to some degree with popular fashion with respect to his or her behavior.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment of trendiness.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Behavior is trendy..

annotations.definition: A trial is a process conducted in a Court before a Judge and (usually) a Jury in which the participants meet in order to decide a Case. The Prosecution attempts to prove that the Defendant is guilty of the Charges and the Defense tries to prove that the Defendant is innocent. Trials have a rich schematic structure which is only partially represented here. The Trial frame is the part of the Criminal Process frame that occurs after a crime, Arrest of a suspect, Arraignment, jury selection, etc. and before Sentencing and punishment or release. The Trial is made up of court appearances. The beginning of the Trial generally involves opening arguments, in which the Prosecution and the Defense present their respective points of view. Then during the course of the Trial, both sides present evidence, some of which is provided by witnesses with testimony, and this is then drawn upon as support for their arguments. Following the presentation of evidence, testimony, and arguments, generally culminating in closing arguments for each side, the Jury (if present) deliberates and either comes to no sufficient consensus or produces a Verdict.

frameElementDefinitions.Charges: The legal label for the crime that the Defendant is accused of.
frameElementDefinitions.Court: The specific room where the participants in a Trial meet.
frameElementDefinitions.Defense: The individual or group which is responsible for promoting the welfare of the Defendant during a Trial, usually involving the contention that the Defendant is innocent of the Charges.
frameElementDefinitions.Jury: The group of individuals who observe the Trial and ultimately attempt to come to a verdict.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the trial occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Prosecution: The individual or group responsible for promoting the interests of the state in the Trial, prototypically including presenting arguments for the guilt of the Defendant.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the trial occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Defendant: The person charged with an offence.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The official who directs and presides over the Trial.
frameElementDefinitions.Case: The Case which a Trial is convened to decide.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The way in which the Trial is conducted.

annotations.definition: A Cause triggers a System to operate in accordance with its intended purpose. Alternatively, an Actor (acting volitionally or not) which participates in the Cause, stands in for the Cause. The System often has the capability to detect and signal the presence of an object or the occurence of an event (metal, an earthquake). It may also be or contain an explosive device. The Means by which the Cause activates the System may also be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause interacts with the System such that conditions are met for the System to operate.
frameElementDefinitions.System: The System interacts with or detects the Cause and activates.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is a subevent which the Cause or Actor participates in which results in triggering the System .
frameElementDefinitions.Actor: An entity which participates in a Cause, perhaps volitionally and perhaps not.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any holistic description of the event, including overall depictions (the same way) and descriptions pertaining to the Actor's or Cause's influence on the character of the event (eagerly, quietly).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the triggering takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the triggering takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The purpose of the Actor in setting off the System.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer thinks that the Information given by a particular Source is correct. The specific Content or Topic of the Information may also be described.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person who has an opinion about the reliability of a Information_source or the Information from the Information_source.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is the entity that a Cognizer trusts information about.
frameElementDefinitions.Content: The Content denotes the mental content that the Cognizer is certain or uncertain about.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE indicates the Degree to which the Cognizer trusts the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of an aspect of the Cognizer that also affects the nature of the trusting, or a general comparison of the state-of-affairs to others.
frameElementDefinitions.Expressor: An object or action associated with the Cognizer which is so affected by the Cognizer's belief that it reveals the Cognizer's state.
frameElementDefinitions.Information_source: The individual that gives Information to the Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Information: The text or speech which the Cognizer has heard and evaluates the truth of.

annotations.definition: A Defendant is tried by a Jury or Judge in a Court for Charges. This frame perspectivalizes the general Trial frame.

frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The Judge heads the Court where the case is tried. An expression referring to the Court may also stand in for the Judge.
frameElementDefinitions.Court: This FE identifies the Court in which a case is tried.
frameElementDefinitions.Defendant: The Defendant is being tried for the Charges in Court.
frameElementDefinitions.Jury: This FE identifies the people who are charged by the Court to listen to the testimony, evidence, and arguments, and come to a consensus about the guilt or innocence of the Defendant.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the case is tried.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the case is tried.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: This FE identifies the Charges brought against the Defendant.
frameElementDefinitions.Governing_authority: The Defendant is tried under the laws of the Governing_authority.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner of the trial of the Defendant.

annotations.definition: An Evaluator participates in an Experience (or metonymically, a salient Entity of the Experience), often for the first time, in order to form an opinion about its quality. The aspect of quality that is being judged is often implicit but may be expressed as the Purpose of the Evaluator's participation.

frameElementDefinitions.Evaluator: The person (or metonymic manifestation thereof) that participates in the Experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Experience: The activity or event that the Evaluator participates in.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: Entity is something that the Evaluator uses to try an Experience, and metonymically stands in for the Experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of how the Evaluator acts in having the Experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: A description of what the Evaluator does in order to participate in the Experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: A more explicit description of what the Evaluator seeks to learn by having the Experience.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The temporal location during which the Experience occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The physical location in which the Experience occurs.

annotations.definition: A State_of_affairs turns out to be true in someone's knowledge of the world. This may be recognized with evidence or in certain circumstances. The cognizer whose beliefs are affected is not expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The state of affairs that a cognizer realizes.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the State_of_affairs is realized.

annotations.definition: This frame has to do with nouns denoting types of entities. The Subtype refers to a collection of members of a more general Category which have certain defining characteristics; alternatively, the Subtype refers only to the type itself, i.e. a more restrictive set of characteristics than those of the more general Category. The properties of the items that are included in the collection may be specified by the Item_Property. Sometimes the Subtypes themselves are described by some Type_Property.

frameElementDefinitions.Subtype: The entity which denotes a subtype of the more general Category.
frameElementDefinitions.Category: The more general type that the target denotes a subtype of. This FE is obligatory and should be labeled DNI when not present.
frameElementDefinitions.Type_property: A property of the Subtype itself rather than the members of the Category.
frameElementDefinitions.Item_property: A property of the members of the Category that helps identify (directly or indirectly) the particular set of members that are referred to by the Subtype.

annotations.definition: A State_of_affairs is generally, or with regard to a particular Feature, representative of its class, which may be narrowed to a specific Comparison_set.

frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the Entity varies from other members of the Comparison_set.
frameElementDefinitions.State_of_affairs: The state of affairs which is evaluated with regard to whether it exhibits the essential characteristics of a Comparison_set.
frameElementDefinitions.Comparison_set: The set of indviduals, which are of similar kind to the Entity, and to which the Entity is compared.
frameElementDefinitions.Feature: A property of the State_of_affairs wherein it differs or is similar to members of a Comparison_set.

annotations.definition: A Reported_fact is presented by the speaker as deriving from statements (made directly to them or to others) of third parties.

frameElementDefinitions.Reported_fact: An assertion of a state, event, property or existence or the assignment to a category that the speaker attributes to unnamed others.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Reported_fact was reported.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The span of time for which the Reported_fact has been circulating.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which or the apparant cause of the Reported_fact.

annotations.definition: An Entity changes, either in its category membership, its situation, or in terms of the quality of an Attribute. In the first case, where category membership is changing, an Initial_category and a Final_category may be expressed. In the second case, where a situation as a whole is changing, an Initial_situation and a Final_situation can be present. And in the last case, where some characterization of the Entity is changing, an Initial_quality and a Final_quality can be specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The concrete or abstract entity that undergoes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_category: The type of Entity that results from the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_category: The category that the Entity belongs to before it undergoes the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Entity undergoes the transformation.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_quality: The value of the Attribute before the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_quality: The quality of the Attribute that results from the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Attribute: A characteristic of the Entity for which a quality can be assigned or determined by calculation, measurement, or observation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Change event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the initial and final categories, situations, or qualities differ from each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The physical or abstract Circumstances under which the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event or state that causes the change to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Intermediate_value: A value that the Attribute assumes before the Final_value obtains.
frameElementDefinitions.Value_range: The Initial_value and Final_value taken together as the range through which the Entity changes. The main faith of the church has changed between Orthodox, Protestant and Greek Catholic several times.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location that the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_situation: The Entity's situation that results from the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_situation: The situation that the Entity began in.

annotations.definition: An Entity changes from one kind of thing (Initial_category) to another kind of thing (Final_category). Whether the Initial_category is mentioned or not, the change is viewed as negating some Initial_category, and not simply adding an additional Final_category to the Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The concrete or abstract entity that undergoes a change.
frameElementDefinitions.Final_category: The type of Entity that results from the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Initial_category: The category that the Entity belongs to before it undergoes the change.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Entity undergoes the transformation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Transformation event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The extent to which the initial and final categories differ from each other.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The physical or abstract Circumstances under which the change takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: An event or state that causes the change to take place.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location that the change takes place.

annotations.definition: An Entity is affected by an Event.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The thing which is affected by the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: A change in the world which affects the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Entity undergoes the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Entity undergoes the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Entity undergoes the Event. The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Entity undergoes the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: In this scene, an Event occurs, and an Entity is affected by it.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The thing which is affected by the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Event: A change in the world which affects the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the Entity undergoes the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Entity undergoes the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for which the Entity undergoes the Event. The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which the Entity undergoes the Event.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: A Wearer removes an item of Clothing from a Body_location (the part of the body that the Clothing is prototypically worn on.)

frameElementDefinitions.Wearer: The individual who begins the action wearing the Clothing and ends the action without it.
frameElementDefinitions.Clothing: The item that the Wearer is initially wearing but then removes.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_location: The initial location of the Clothing on the body, before it changes location. Quite often, the location is not overtly specified and is implicitly supplied by the location on the body that the Clothing is prototypically associated with.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the clothing removal which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of a Wearer that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means is an act whereby the Wearer achieves the removal.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the event.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the Wearer undresses an item of Clothing.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which a Wearer undresses.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The Purpose for which a Wearer undresses.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.

annotations.definition: Based on how many Individuals are employed, the Individuals as a whole are described with a Value (either a percentage or absolute number) representing the Employment of the Individuals.

frameElementDefinitions.Individuals: The group of people whose employment status is considered.
frameElementDefinitions.Employment: The property of a group of Individuals describing how many of them are employed or unemployed.
frameElementDefinitions.Value: The count or percentage of people among the group of Individuals that are of the specified employment status.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the Value from the norm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time during which Employment level of the Individuals has a specified Value.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: The number or rate of individuals that are either employed or unemployment may be contrasted, implicitly or explicitly, with different groups.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Individuals under consideration reside.

annotations.definition: An Agent causes a Firearm to discharge, usually directing the projectile from the barrel of the Firearm (the Source), along a Path, and to a Goal.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The self-directed entity that uses and activates the Firearm.
frameElementDefinitions.Firearm: This FE identifies the Firearm that an Agent activates so that it rapidly propels a projectile.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the means by which an Agent uses the Firearm.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the manner in which an Agent uses the Firearm.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent intentionally affects the Patient.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent uses the firearm.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Agent fires the Firearm.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is the location the Theme ends up in, or is intended to end up in.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: This is the path along which the projectiles travel.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: The Source is the location of the Firearm from which projectiles originate.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: Area is the setting in which the projectile's movement takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: This FE identifies the number of times a firearm is used.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: Depicitive phrase describing the state of a participant in the action.

annotations.definition: A Driver transports themselves and possibly other passengers (the Theme) to another location.

frameElementDefinitions.Theme: The Theme is the entity which moves by means of the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Vehicle is the means of conveyance of the Theme. It is usually expressed in a PP-complement or the direct object.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion expresses the frame element Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes a property of the motion not related to its trajectory.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path describes the trajectory of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is the endpoint of the trajectory of motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The Distance is the extent of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The Duration is the amount of time the motion takes.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: The Speed is the rate at which the motion occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Area: The Area is the location where the motion takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Road: The Road is the thoroughfare on which the Vehicle travels.
frameElementDefinitions.Route: The Route is the usual path that the Vehicle travles.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the riding takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: A theme that rides in the same Vehicle as the main Theme.
frameElementDefinitions.Driver: The individual that directs the motion of the Vehicle

annotations.definition: Through use, a Resource is diminished in quality or quantity (up to complete attrition) such that it becomes less capable of sustaining future use.

frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The Resource is a physical entity or an ability to perform work that was used in an earlier process, typically in pursuit of a goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A measure of the amount of the Resource that is gone, or of the degree to which its quality is reduced.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes an event or state from whose occurrence the Resource's being used up logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Excess: This FE indicates that the presence of an overwhelming amount of a kind of entity or the high intensity of an event is sufficient to bring about the used up state of the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Subregion: Subregion denotes a sub-part of the Resource in which the it is specifically exhausted or worn out.

annotations.definition: An Entity aids in the successful completion of a Purpose. Its Degree of utility may also be expressed. Some of the lexical units in this frame (e.g. great, good) can only express permanent, generic states of the Entity.

frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The item which is useful for some purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The kind of task which is promoted by the use or presence of the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount that the presence of the Entity aids in the completion of the (desirable) Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: This Frame Element marks expressions of a Domain or parameter with respect to which the Entity is useful.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Entity is useful for a Purpose.

annotations.definition: An Agent manipulates an Instrument in order to achieve a Purpose.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent uses the Instrument to achieve their Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent uses the Instrument.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which an Agent uses the Instrument.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent uses the Instrument.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent uses the Instrument.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action ( deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: An entity directed by the Agent that serves to bring about the Purpose of the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Role: An Instrument that has a function associated with it.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of the Agent's using the Instrument to achieve their Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: An event within which the Agent uses the Instrument. Often, the Containing_event will also give an indication of the Agent's Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time. A Frequency expression answers the question "how often."
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: This FE modifies a clause with an iterative interpretation, which may be signified by the simple present tense on the main verb.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes a participant of the state of affairs introduced by the target as being in some state during the action. The depicted state does not necessarily or usually facilitate or cause the state of affairs reported by the target.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the target's proposition, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Group: This FE identifies the population within which the Using event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute of the Instrument or the Using act and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of a continuous situation to its end. In many cases, the continuous situation is a dynamic action which is ongoing, while in others it is simply an undifferentiated state.

annotations.definition: An Agent has access to a finite amount of some Resource and uses it in some way to complete a Purpose. The specific Portion of the Resource may be stated, and after the Resource is spent, it is no longer available to the Agent. The Means by which the Resource is used may be mentioned, but it is often implied by the stated Purpose or understood based on what the Resource is.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The person who depletes a Resource to achieve some Goal
frameElementDefinitions.Resource: The exhaustible commodity that the Agent has access to.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: What the Agent is attempting to achieve by using the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Portion: The amount of the Resource that the Agent uses to achieve the Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: A more specific description of how the Purpose is achieved by spending the Resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action ( deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The physical location where the Resource is used.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which the Resource is used.

annotations.definition: The frame concerns the vehicles that human beings use for the purpose of transportation.

frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE identifies a characteristic or description of the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: Vehicle is the transportation device that the human beings use to travel.This FE is incorporated into each LU in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Possessor: This Frame Element denotes the Possessor of the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Itinerary: The frame element tells about the time and route of the service of the transportation device.
frameElementDefinitions.Means_of_propulsion: The Means_of_propulsion designates how the vehicle is moved.
