frameElementDefinitions.Use: The task which the Vehicle is put to. Normally, this includes the transportation of some kind.

annotations.definition: A Vehicle moves into position or otherwise changes configuration or state as preparation for normal operation. The focus of the event is on the state of the Vehicle rather than any agent which is manipulating the Vehicle.

frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The vehicle which moves into position for typical operation.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any expression which describes the location that the Vehicle has been stationed at.
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_goal: The location that the Vehicle is bound for.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the preparation for motion which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the Vehicle that affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Vehicle during the preparations.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An event in which the Vehicle participates which causes or enables it to travel.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of the Vehicle's motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: An individual or vehicle that is also involved in the preparations.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: This FE indicates the Circumstances under which the preparation of the Vehicle takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place describes the location where the Vehicle prepares for motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration_of_final_state: The length of the time that the Vehicle remains in the state of readiness.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time when the Vehicle gets ready for motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation is a proposition from which the main clause logically follows.

annotations.definition: A flying Vehicle comes to the ground at a Goal in a controlled fashion, typically (but not necessarily) operated by an operator.

frameElementDefinitions.Vehicle: The Vehicle comes to the ground, usually (but not necessarily) under the control of an operator.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Source is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion. While Source expressions are possible in this frame, they are relatively infrequent.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Source nor a Goal. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is any expression that tells where the Vehicle ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A Manner expression describes a property of landing which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which a Vehicle lands.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Vehicle is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Vehicle.
frameElementDefinitions.Cotheme: Cotheme is the second moving object, expressed as a direct object or an oblique.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the aircraft lands.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: The New_situation are the state of the Goal when the Vehicle arrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the state of the Vehicle upon arrival.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The time period within which the landing event repeats multiple times.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the landing event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Vehicle lands at the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE marks the state of affairs reported by the landing event as understood to play a role in the state of affairs of Event_description
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: This FE presents the current frame as an integral part of a larger conceptualization expressed by another frame. The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Vehicle lands at the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the landing occurs.

annotations.definition: An entity is integrated into a vehicle as a Part of a (possibly unexpressed) Whole which may refer to the whole vehicle or merely to a sub-part of the vehicle.

frameElementDefinitions.Part: Some Part of the larger vehicle, the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Whole: A vehicle (the Whole) which includes the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Function: The function of the part within the vehicule as a whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Orientation: This property of the part that situates the part within the whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A property relating to the part.

annotations.definition: In this frame, which is a part of the Trial sequence, a Judge gives a Finding with respect to the Charges against the Defendant in a particular Case.

frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The Judge (which may be the jury, judge, magistrate or court) decides on the Finding.
frameElementDefinitions.Finding: This FE identifies the outcome of the Judge's deliberation or consideration. Many LUs in this frame incorporate this FE.
frameElementDefinitions.Defendant: The person who stands accused of a crime and is subject to the verdict.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: This FE refers to the Charges with respect to which the verdict is being given.
frameElementDefinitions.Case: The Case on which the verdict is being given.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Where the event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Legal_basis: This FE indicates the basis in law of a verdict.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances under which the verdict was reached.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: This FE identifies the Manner in which the Judge acts.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Judge returns a particular verdict.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies an action that the Judge performs in order to return the verdict.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation for the verdict given.

annotations.definition: An Inspector attains a degree of certainty in the Unconfirmed_content, generally by inspecting some evidence.

frameElementDefinitions.Inspector: The individual or individuals that ascertain that the Unconfirmed_content is true.
frameElementDefinitions.Unconfirmed_content: An open proposition that the Inspector decides by examining evidence. It is usually a propostion put forward which some parties would disbelieve or contest.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the verification which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), force (hard, softly), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an Inspector that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action performed by the Inspector by which the Inspector manages to establish the truth of the Unconfirmed_content.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The amount of certainty which the Inspector achieves after the process of verification.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the Inspector hopes to bring about by going through the verification.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The Medium is the piece of text or work in which the Inspector verifies the Unconfirmed_content:
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: A tool or instrument used by the Inspector in the verification process.
frameElementDefinitions.Condition: The conditions under which the the verification will occur.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: This FE describes a characteristic of the verification event.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the target operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE describes the Time at which the verification event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE describes where the verificiation event takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for the state of the verification event.

annotations.definition: An entity goes through several versions, from initial to final.

frameElementDefinitions.Item: The Item is one version among several versions (which may or may not yet exist), progressively getting closer to a final version.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree of the distance the Item is from the final version.

annotations.definition: An improvised explosive device, the Bomb, is placed so as to be hidden from view or disguised as something not harmful. It is rigged to detonate when triggered by actions of an intended Victim. Possible triggering actions include stepping on a pressure-sensitive plate, activating a trip-wire, or moving an object which activates a spring-loaded device.

frameElementDefinitions.Bomb: The explosive device which activates when set off by the victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The type of activity that the Bomb is generally involved in. Often this can be considered a kind of Type specification also.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype the Bomb belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The substance that makes up the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Bomber: The person who sets the bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: This FE identifies a part of the Bomb. This includes permanent, obligatory parts and mere attachments.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to a Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which the Bomb comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The individual or group responsible for the creation of the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description or characteristic of the Bomb.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim inadvertantly causes the Bomb to denotate.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame describe acts (or situations characterized by acts) that cause injury or harm. The acts may involve an Aggressor or Cause injuring a Victim, or Aggressors causing each other harm.

frameElementDefinitions.Aggressor: The Aggressor is the person causing the Victim's injury.
frameElementDefinitions.Victim: The Victim is the being or entity that is injured.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree is the degree to which violence is done.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Aggressor acts upon the Victim.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An intentional action performed by the Aggressor that results in the committing of a violent act.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: Place identifies the place where the violence occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: Time identifies the time when the harm-causing event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Instrument: This FE is any object used to cause the injury.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: Purpose identifies the purpose for which a harm-causing action is carried out.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE identifies the event in which the harm is caused.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: A fact or action related to one of the participants in the volient act that the Aggressor responds to in doing violence.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: Iterations refers to the number of times the violent act occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Cause: The Cause marks expressions that indicate some non-intentional, typically non-human, force that inflicts harm on the Victim
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the violent event and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the violence occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Aggressors: The Aggressors commit acts of violence upon each other.

annotations.definition: A Visitor matches location with a Host by going to a place strongly associated with a Host, the Host_location (often a place of residence, employment, or government), in order to achieve a Purpose. The Host allows the use of his/her resources. The visit is often social in nature.

frameElementDefinitions.Visitor: The sentient entity that heads toward and is temporarily with the Host in order to fulfil some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Host: An individual that allows the use of his or her resources for the benefit of the Visitor.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The activity that is enabled by the hospitality of the Host and that the Visitor wishes to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: The location that the Visitor normally resides in or is (permanently) associated with.
frameElementDefinitions.Host_location: The location associated with the Host, where the Visitor arrives at and remains during the visit.

annotations.definition: The Visitor arrives at the Host_location to visit the Host.

frameElementDefinitions.Visitor: The Visitor goes to the Host
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: Normal_location is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion. While Normal_location expressions are possible in this frame, they are relatively infrequent. If present, they often express a general direction from which a Visitor moves, rather than a landmark away from which it moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Normal_location nor a Host_location. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host welcomes the Visitor as a guest. The location strongly associated with the Host serves as the goal of the Visitor's motion.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A Manner expression describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Visitor arrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Visitor is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Visitor. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when arriving occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Visitor arrives at the Host or Host_location.
frameElementDefinitions.Host_location: The Host_location is a location associated with the Host, and is commonly the place of residence or employment of the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the arrival event occurs.

annotations.definition: A Visitor departs from a Host_location after having visited a Host.

frameElementDefinitions.Visitor: The Visitor departs from the location of the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Host_location: The Visitor departs from the Host_location, a location strongly associated with the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of the trajectory of the departure which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a sense meaning 'via'.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE labels any expression which tells where the Visitor ends up, or would end up, as a result of departing from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and many other things count as Manner expressions, though Speed and Means should be tagged using the more specific FE labels.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Visitor which results in their departure.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This frame element labels words or phrases that describe how quickly the Visitor is moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Visitor is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Visitor. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. In this frame, Mode_of_transportation is often expressed obliquely in a prepositional phrase headed by 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance that the Visitor has traveled.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the departure takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: >The Place is the location in which the departing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Visitor departs from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host allows the Visitor to use his/her resources, and to remain temporarily at the Host_location.

annotations.definition: A Visitor is located with a Host for a visit.

frameElementDefinitions.Visitor: The sentient entity that is temporarily with the Host in order to fulfil some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Host: The Host welcomes the Visitor to his/her location, with which he/she is permanently associated. The Host allows the Visitor use of resources to accomplish a Purpose. During the visit, the Visitor matches location with the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_state: A state (of the Visitor) that results as a consequence of the Visitor being situated with the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The activity that is enabled by the Host and that the Visitor wishes to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: The location that the Visitor normally resides in or is (permanently) associated with.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which a visit is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the Visitor spends with the Host.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any overall description of the details of the visit, either in terms of how it matches other events (the same way) or in terms of states of the Visitor that affect the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A description of the position of the Visitor during the visit.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Visitor that enables their visit.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of instances of the described event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: A characterization of the average number of times a visit happens over a period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Host_location: The Host_location is the location permanently associated with the Host, and which the Visitor stays at temporarily for the acheiving of some Purpose.

annotations.definition: An Agent matches location with an Entity in order to fulfill some Purpose. Quite often, the Purpose is social- or entertainment-oriented.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity that is temporarily with the Entity in order to fulfil some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The physical entity that is permanently associated with entities or characteristics that the Agent wishes to use for some Purpose. During the visit, the Agent matches location with the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_state: A state (of the Agent) that results as a consequence of the Agent being situated at the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The activity that is enabled by the Entity and that the Agent wishes to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: The location that the Agent normally resides in or is (permanently) associated with.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which a visit is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the Agent spends with the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any overall description of the details of the visit, either in terms of how it matches other events (the same way) or in terms of states of the Agent that affect the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A description of the position of the Agent during the visit.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Agent that enables their visit.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of instances of the described event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: A characterization of the average number of times a visit happens over a period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: A description of the state of Agent or Entity during the action.

annotations.definition: An Agent and an Entity become co-located. The Agent in pursuit of a Purpose, and temporarily remains with the Entity to fulfill this Purpose and then departs again, usually back to their Normal_location.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity that heads toward and is temporarily with the Entity in order to fulfil some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: A place that is permanently associated with entities or characteristics that the Agent wishes to use for some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The activity that is enabled by the Entity and that the Agent wishes to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: The location that the Agent normally resides in or is (permanently) associated with.

annotations.definition: An Agent arrives at a location that should help them achieve their Purpose. This situation is point-of-view-neutral.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: Agent is the object that moves under its own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: Normal_location is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion. While Normal_location expressions are possible in this frame, they are relatively infrequent. If present, they often express a general direction from which a Agent moves, rather than a landmark away from which it moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Normal_location nor a Goal. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is any expression that tells where the Agent ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion. Although always conceptually present and specific, Goal may sometimes be understood from context, rather than expressed by any separate constituent.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A Manner expression describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent arrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Agent is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Agent. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when arriving occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Agent arrives at the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the visiting occurs

annotations.definition: An Agent departs from the Source, normally bound for their normal location. This is a point-of-view-neutral conception of the departure.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This is the individual which moves under their own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any constituent that expresses the initial position of the Agent, before the change of location, is tagged with Source. Often the Source is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of the trajectory of the departure which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE labels any expression which tells where the Agent ends up, or would end up, as a result of departing from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and many other things count as Manner expressions, though Speed and Means should be tagged using the more specific FE labels.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Agent which results in their departure.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This frame element labels words or phrases that describe how quickly the Agent is moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Agent is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Agent. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance that the Agent has traveled.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the departure takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location in which the departing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent departs from the Source.

annotations.definition: An Agent matches location with an Entity in order to fulfill some Purpose. Quite often, the Purpose is social- or entertainment-oriented. This scene is point-of-view-neutral.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity that is temporarily with the Entity in order to fulfil some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: The physical entity that is permanently associated with entities or characteristics that the Agent wishes to use for some Purpose. During the visit, the Agent matches location with the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Dependent_state: A state (of the Agent) that results as a consequence of the Agent being situated at the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The activity that is enabled by the Entity and that the Agent wishes to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: The location that the Agent normally resides in or is (permanently) associated with.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at which a visit is made.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The amount of time that the Agent spends at the Entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any overall description of the details of the visit, either in terms of how it matches other events (the same way) or in terms of states of the Agent that affect the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: A description of the position of the Agent during the visit.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Agent that enables their visit.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of instances of the described event.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: A characterization of the average number of times a visit happens over a period of time.

annotations.definition: A Visitor and a Host become co-located at a location strongly associated with the Host. The Visitor is allowed by the Host to use the Host's location and/or resources for the achievement of a Purpose. The visit is often social in nature.

frameElementDefinitions.Visitor: The sentient entity that heads toward and is temporarily with the Host in order to fulfil some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Host: An individual that allows the use of his or her resources for the benefit of the Visitor.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The activity that is enabled by the Host and that the Visitor wishes to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: The location that the Visitor normally resides in or is (permanently) associated with.
frameElementDefinitions.Host_location: A location strongly associated with (in the territory of) the Host, often a place of residence, government, or employment. The Visitor is allowed onto the Host_location and may be invited to make use of it or resources contained within it.

annotations.definition: An Agent arrives at a Goal that should enable their Purpose.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: Agent is the object that moves under its own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: Normal_location is any expression which implies a definite starting-point of motion. While Normal_location expressions are possible in this frame, they are relatively infrequent. If present, they often express a general direction from which a Agent moves, rather than a landmark away from which it moves.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a Normal_location nor a Goal. In this frame, Path expressions almost always have a via-sense.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: Goal is any expression that tells where the Agent ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion. Although always conceptually present and specific, Goal may sometimes be understood from context, rather than expressed by any separate constituent.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A Manner expression describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which an Agent arrives.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Agent is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Agent. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium. They are usually expressed obliquely with 'in' or 'by'.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when arriving occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Agent arrives at the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the arrival occurs.

annotations.definition: After a visit is complete, the Agent departs the Source, normally bound for their normal haunts.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: This is the individual which moves under their own power.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: Any constituent that expresses the initial position of the Agent, before the change of location, is tagged with Source. Often the Source is understood from context.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: Any description of the trajectory of the departure which is neither a Source nor a Goal expresses the frame element Path.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: This FE labels any expression which tells where the Agent ends up, or would end up, as a result of departing from the Source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element Manner. Descriptions of steadiness, grace, and many other things count as Manner expressions, though Speed and Means should be tagged using the more specific FE labels.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The action of the Agent which results in their departure.
frameElementDefinitions.Speed: This frame element labels words or phrases that describe how quickly the Agent is moving.
frameElementDefinitions.Mode_of_transportation: The Mode_of_transportation expresses how the motion of the Agent is effected, by its body or by a vehicle which holds and conveys the Agent. Vehicles can move in any way and in any medium.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: The distance that the Agent has traveled.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the departure takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The Place is the location in which the departing takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the Purpose for which the Agent departs from the Source.

annotations.definition: An Agent leaves their Normal_location and matches location with an Entity in pursuit of a Purpose. The Agent temporarily remains with the Entity to fulfil this Purpose and then departs again, usually back to the Normal_location.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The sentient entity that heads toward and is temporarily with the Entity in order to fulfil some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Entity: A place that is permanently associated with entities or characteristics that the Agent wishes to use for some Purpose.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The activity that is enabled by the Entity and that the Agent wishes to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Normal_location: The location that the Agent normally resides in or is (permanently) associated with.

annotations.definition: This frame is concerned with the sounds produced by animate entities by means of their vocal tracts. Descriptions of sounds from artifacts meant to emulate such sounds, or to act as vocalizing animals or people, are also treated here as figurative language.

frameElementDefinitions.Sound_source: An entity that is construed as a single point source emitting sound.
frameElementDefinitions.Location_of_sound_source: The place from which the sound emanates.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which sound is produced. This FE differs from Location_of_sound_source in that it does not highlight the sound's spreading from its source to the perceiver's location, which is usually indicated by the preposition "from".
frameElementDefinitions.Internal_cause: The noise-making may be prompted by either some outside phenomenon or occurrence, or by an Internal_cause, that is the mental or emotional state of an animate Sound_source.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Certain properties of the sound relating to its production, loudness, etc may be of lexicographic interest if they describe properties of a sounds as such: loudly, shrilly, etc.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Expressions that indicate the loudness of the sound.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: This FE idntifies the Explanation that the Sound_source produces the sound. Generally, this is some external stimulus that prompts the Sound_source to make noise.
frameElementDefinitions.Path: The Path along which the sound travels away from the Sound_source.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Vocalization event occurs.

annotations.definition: These words describe the size of message that a Speaker is judged by a (usually unexpressed) Judge to give, either in a specific occasion or Topic or in general. As in other communication frames, Medium may also be expressed.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: This is the person who is characterized as talkative, reticent etc. It is usually the External Argument:
frameElementDefinitions.Company: The Company in which the Speaker exhibits a certain communicative behavior. In this frame, Company occurs in PP Complements headed by with:
frameElementDefinitions.Text: Text is rarely expressed because the quantity of information given is the focus of the frame, rather than the information content. Texts can occur as NP objects, but cannot be either direct quotes or complement clauses.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: This FE is the Topic on which much or little information is communicated. Topic is far more frequent than Message in this frame.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: Medium is the physical entity or channel used to communicate, as in other Communication frames. It is usually a PP Complement headed by in or on.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: Degree to which the Speaker withholds or does not withhold information and conversation.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Manner in which the speaker is reticent or voluble.
frameElementDefinitions.Judge: The individual whose point of view is taken in assigning the judgment denoted by the target.

annotations.definition: A Gambler commits an Asset to a prediction that an Uncertain_situation will have a particular Outcome (or class of outcomes). He or she loses the Asset if the prediction ends up being incorrect, and gains it back plus additional winnings if the prediction ends up correct.

frameElementDefinitions.Gambler: The person who wages the Asset on a certain Outcome.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the Gambler makes the wager.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time when the Gambler makes the wager.
frameElementDefinitions.Asset: Something desirable possessed by or directly associated with the Gambler which is lost if the Gambler loses the wager.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the Gambler makes wagers.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The desired action or goal that the Gambler hopes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: An Explanation for why the Gambler has put the Asset up in a wager.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The number of times that the Gambler makes wagers.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances indicates the state of affairs under which the Gambler makes a wager.
frameElementDefinitions.Beneficiary: The Gambler intends for his/her actions to benefit Beneficiary in some way.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Gambler predicts that a certain Outcome will hold.
frameElementDefinitions.Uncertain_situation: The Gambler predicts how an Uncertain_situation will resolve.

annotations.definition: A Protagonist delays a planned action because they cannot or do not want to proceed until an Expected_event occurs. The Expected_event may be evoked by reference to a Salient_entity that participates in it. If a Salient_entity is expressed, the Expected_event is typically the arrival of the Salient_entity at the Place where the Protagonist is located.

frameElementDefinitions.Protagonist: The sentient being who cannot proceed with their planned activities until a Salient_entity has arrived or an Expected_event has occurred.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: A concrete or abstract entity that the Protagonist expects to participate in an Expected_event, typically that of arriving at the Place of the Protagonist.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Protagonist is waiting.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Protagonist is waiting.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of the waiting to its end.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the waiting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of the Protagonist that also affect the action (patiently, anxiously).
frameElementDefinitions.Expected_event: The Expected_event is an event that needs to take place or a state that needs to begin to hold before the Protagonist can proceed with their planned activities.
frameElementDefinitions.End_point: The point in time, defined either as a clock time or in terms of an event that takes place, at which the Protagonist ends the waiting.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the occurrence of the waiting event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: This FE describes the Protagonist as being in some state during the waiting.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: An action that the Protagonist intends to accomplish by waiting.
frameElementDefinitions.Co-participant: Co_participant is an entity that participates in a coordinated way in the same event as the primary protagonist, regardless of whether the protagonist, and hence the Co-participant, is more agent- or more undergoer-like.

annotations.definition: A Sleeper transitions from a state of consciousness where they are largely unaware of their environment to a wakeful state.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Sleeper: This FE identifies the sleeping entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the event occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Sleep_state: This describes the state out of which the Sleeper awakens.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Sleeper awakens.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The state of the Sleeper upon waking up.
frameElementDefinitions.New_situation: The New_situation is the situation the Sleeper finds him or herself in upon awaking.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: This FE indicates an instance of the Waking_up event within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason for which the Sleeper awakes from the Sleep_state.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This frame element is defined as the number of times an event occurs per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: A Suspect has been investigated and has a warrant issued for his or her arrest on certain Charges.

frameElementDefinitions.Suspect: The Suspect is suspected of having committed a crime by the authorities who want the Suspect to enter the criminal process.
frameElementDefinitions.Charges: The Charges are the offenses the Suspect will be accused of committing.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This frame element selects some gradable attribute and modifies the expected value for it.

annotations.definition: A Speaker warns an Addressee by giving a Message that describes an undesirable situation that may affect the Addressee. The warning may alternatively be described as about Topic pertaining to the undesirable situation, or issued via a specific Medium.

frameElementDefinitions.Speaker: The Speaker produces the Message to alert the Addressee as to possible danger.
frameElementDefinitions.Addressee: The Addressee receives the warning from the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Message: The Message is the communication produced by the Speaker.
frameElementDefinitions.Topic: The Topic is a general description of the content of the Message.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner describes the way in which the Speaker delivers the warning.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The Means describes the way the Speaker accomplishes the task of warning the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: This frame element is the Medium in which the Message is expressed.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time period during which the Speaker warns the Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location in which the Speaker warns Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the Message is expressed (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A characteristic or description of the telling event or of the Speaker or Addressee.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: This FE indicates the number of times an event or state (of the kind denoted by the target in its clause) has taken place or held.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer faces a choice on some Issue. They have several Options available but keep changing their mind between Option_1 and Option_2. The Cognizer may in fact act on their choice at some point but they need not; the Options can simply be considered for some time with one being favored for a while and then the other.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the person who repeatedly changes their mind about which of the Options to choose.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which it logically follows that the Cognizer keeps changing their mind about the Issue.
frameElementDefinitions.Options: The Options are the alternative courses of action or the alternative physical or concrete entities that the Cognizer can choose from.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Cognizer keeps changing their mind between the Options that present themselves.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE denotes the Time interval during which the Cognizer keeps changing their mind about which of the Options to select.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the selecting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions comparing events (the same way) and salient characteristics of the Cognizer that also affect the action.
frameElementDefinitions.Issue: The Issue is a question about a course of action or a choice between items.
frameElementDefinitions.Option_1: When there is an explicitly separate construal of the Options, Option_1 is the first-mentioned alternative, typically in the form of a from-PP. When it is clear that the Cognizer actually acted on their choice, Option_1 is the alternative they embraced first before pursuing Option_2.
frameElementDefinitions.Option_2: When there is an explicitly separate construal of the Options, Option_2 is the second-mentioned alternative, typically in the form of a to-PP. When it is clear that the Cognizer actually acted on their choice, Option_2 is the alternative they embraced after abandoning Option_1.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: In the cases where the Cognizer does not act on their choice, the Duration denotes the length of time from the beginning of the Cognizer's deliberations to the end. In situations where the Cognizer does act on the Options, the Duration denotes the length of the interval during which the Cognizer repeatly changes from one option to the other.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE describes the number of times within a given period that the Cognizer wavers.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The Degree to which the Cognizer wavers.
frameElementDefinitions.Containing_event: This FE denotes an event that occurs or state of affairs that holds at a time that includes the time during which the event or state of affairs reported by the target occurs and of which it is taken to be a part.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: Circumstances describe the state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is specifically independent of the event itself and any of its participants.

annotations.definition: A Person or Institution is being described in terms of their wealthiness, in other words the amount of money in their possession.

frameElementDefinitions.Person: The individual or individuals whose wealthiness is being described.
frameElementDefinitions.Institution: A group which has (or lacks) possession of some resource.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: The degree of wealthiness (or poverty) of the Person or Institution.
frameElementDefinitions.Type_of_possession: A characterization of the entity (usually money) that the Person (or Institution possesses.

annotations.definition: A Weapon is an artifact created specifically to cause harm or damage. In some cases, a Use, Material of construction, Part or Type may be more narrowly specified.

frameElementDefinitions.Weapon: An entity prototypically used by a Wielder to cause damage.
frameElementDefinitions.Use: The type of activity that the Weapon is generally involved in. Often this can be considered a kind of Type specification also.
frameElementDefinitions.Type: This FE indicates what subtype of the general category the Weapon belongs to.
frameElementDefinitions.Material: The substance that makes up the Weapon.
frameElementDefinitions.Wielder: The person (or other sentient entity) that is using the Weapon.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: This FE identifies a part of the Weapon. This includes permanent, obligatory parts and mere attachments.
frameElementDefinitions.Name: The term used to refer to an Weapon.
frameElementDefinitions.Time_of_creation: The time at which a Weapon comes into existence.
frameElementDefinitions.Creator: The individual or group responsible for the creation of the Weapon.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: A description or characteristic of the Weapon.

annotations.definition: The words in this frame refer to what Clothing the Wearer (or a specific Body_part of the Wearer) has on.

frameElementDefinitions.Wearer: The person whose clothing is under discussion.
frameElementDefinitions.Clothing: This FE identifies the Clothing that the Wearer wears.
frameElementDefinitions.Body_part: The body part of the Wearer which is covered by the Clothing.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A characterization of how completely the clothing status holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A characterization of the impression given by the Wearer's clothing status.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The time at or during which the Wearer has the Clothing on.
frameElementDefinitions.Source: NB: This FE will be mapped to Intended_wearing_location The location that the Wearer exits while wearing the Clothing.
frameElementDefinitions.Intended_wearing_location: The location whose characteristics or state the Clothing of the Wearer was intended to suit. This FE is only possible with wear.v.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Wearer wishes to bring about.
frameElementDefinitions.Relative_location: The location relative to the body of the Wearer that the Clothing occupies. Note that in many cases, the location is defined relative to other clothing.

annotations.definition: Ambient conditions of temperature, precipitation, windiness, and sunniness pertain at a certain Place and Time. Further Specification of the conditions that pertain may also be indicated.

frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the weather occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the weather occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Specification: This FE identifies a further Specification of the weather conditions.

annotations.definition: A Whole is made up of multiple Parts, which themselves may not be independent of each other or of the Whole. Wholes_and_parts is contrasted with Set_relation, where the Whole is simply a set of a number of (usually independent) parts.

frameElementDefinitions.Whole: The Whole is the larger entity that includes the Part.
frameElementDefinitions.Part: The Part is either a subset or a constituent part of the Whole.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the inclusion event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: The Time at which the inclusion relation holds.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location at which the inclusion relation holds.

annotations.definition: A Cognizer would engage in an Activity if asked or otherwise prompted to do so.

frameElementDefinitions.Cognizer: The Cognizer is the individual that is willing to engage in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The Activity is what the Cognizer would do under certain circumstances.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the Cognizer's willingness logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Cognizer is willing to engage in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Cognizer is willing to engage in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state of affairs that the Cognizer wants to achieve by being willing to engage in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: A modifier expressing the deviation of the willingness from the norm or from the value for another Cognizer.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: This FE denotes the number of times the Cognizer is willing per some unit of time.

annotations.definition: A Competitor claims a Prize as a result of the outcome of their participation in a Competition. Competition is meant to be construed broadly to include cases where there is no skill involved as well as cases where there is no ranking of participants, and indeed does not require that there be multiple pariticipants or a definable end of game, as in the case of winning prizes at a carnival game. All that is required to be this kind of Competition is that there is some way of determining whether a Competitor will receive the Prize.

frameElementDefinitions.Competitor: The individual or individuals involved in the Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Score: A quantity whereby progress is measured during the game, and Rank and Prizes are awarded to the Competitor afterward.
frameElementDefinitions.Margin: The amount by which the Competitor 's and Scores differ.
frameElementDefinitions.Rank: The rank (according to Score) that a Competitor acheives.
frameElementDefinitions.Prize: A socially and/or monetarily significant entity which is given to a Competitor according to Score or Rank.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: An action of the Competitor that results in the outcome of the Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the Competitor which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously), secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of a Competitor that also affect their action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Time: When the Competition takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The location where the Competition takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Venue: This FE is for the named venue of a competition, particularly when the place has been built for the purpose of a Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Competition: The event in which the Competitor competes.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The reason why the Competitor wins the Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when winning events began to be repeated to when they stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Opponent: The individual that the Competitor has been competing against.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world when the Competitor wins the Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Re-encoding: The frame expressed by the target typically corresponds to a Means action within the frame expressed by the Re-encoding, but not necessarily.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The state-of-affairs that prevails as a result of the Competitor winning the Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Iterations: The frame element Iterations is used for expressions that indicate the number of times the Competitor wins the Competition.
frameElementDefinitions.Communicative_force: This FE indicates the Communicative_force or authority with which the winning event operates (often denoted by words such as "officially," "formally," "technically," etc.).
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness, or a description of the state-of-affairs introduced by the winning event.

annotations.definition: An individual that is scheduled to be a Participant, or already is a Participant in an Activity or an Organization, indicates that they will no not or no longer participate in that Activity or Organization. Note that the Organization often is one that is constituted by a treaty or agreement.

frameElementDefinitions.Participant: The individual or individuals that are engaged or are scheduled to be engaged in an Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Activity: The event that the Participant is engaged in or was scheduled to be engaged in.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the withdrawing event logically follows.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the Place where the Participant ends the participation in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the Participant ends their participation in the Activity.
frameElementDefinitions.Organization: The Organization is an intentionally formed human social group with some definite structure to which the Participant belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The state-of-affairs that the Participant hopes to bring about by withdrawing
frameElementDefinitions.Means: The act done by the Participant that accomplishes their withdrawal.
frameElementDefinitions.Medium: The document that the Participant uses to communicate their withdrawal.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: A description of the attitude of the Participant which also affects the character of the withdrawal.

annotations.definition: As is usual for locative relations, the location of the Figure is specified as being inside a region defined relative to the Ground. In this case, the region measures outward from the Ground with a width specified by the Distance.

frameElementDefinitions.Ground: The Ground serves as a basis for describing the location of the Figure, as inside a region measured outward from the Ground and of a width specified by Distance.
frameElementDefinitions.Figure: The Figure is perceived as located relative to a certain Ground location.
frameElementDefinitions.Distance: This FE identifies the Distance between the Ground and the borders of the region within which the Figure is located.

annotations.definition: One linguistic unit, Sign_1, has a relation to another linguistic unit, Sign_2. This relation may be semantic, phonological, orthographic, or distributional in nature. The parameter along which the two signs are set in relation to each other is incorporated in the meaning of the target words in this frame. When the relation is symmetrical, the two linguistic units can be expressed jointly, Signs. Otherwise they have to be expressed disjointly. Ordinarily it is assumed that one linguistic unit has exactly one meaning but in cases where this is not so, a set of Circumstances may be specified that helps pick out the relevant sense.

frameElementDefinitions.Sign_1: FN: a linguistic unit that carries semantic, phonological or syntactic information.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The Circumstances are conditions under which the relationship holds between Sign_1 and Sign_2.

annotations.definition: An Agent expends effort towards achieving a Goal. Alternatively, a Salient_entity involved in the Goal can be expressed in place of a Goal expression.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent puts effort into reaching Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Goal: The Goal is what the Agent expends effort to achieve.
frameElementDefinitions.Outcome: The Outcome is the failure or success of an attempt to achieve a Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the effort is done.
frameElementDefinitions.Degree: This FE identifies the Degree to which an attempt occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the Time when the effort occurs.
frameElementDefinitions.Depictive: The Depictive is a phrase which describes the Agent.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: The place where the work is done.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: The Manner in which the Agent puts in effort in acheiving the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Duration: The time peiod over which the work takes place.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: The reason for which the Agent puts effort into achieving the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states. In addition, most expressions of Particular_iteration indicate which instance of the series is being referred to.
frameElementDefinitions.Circumstances: The state of the world (at a particular time and place) which is independent of the the attempt of the Agent to achieve the Goal.
frameElementDefinitions.Domain: The domain or area within which the work towards the Goal belongs.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: An entity that is centrally involved in the Goal that the Agent is attempting to acheive.
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: How often the Agent works.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE applies to appraisals of the event, typically a judgment of its expectedness.
frameElementDefinitions.Descriptor: Descriptor covers any characterization or description of the target that is not covered by another more specific FE.

annotations.definition: The Agent is assigned to a Post where he or she performs a task associated with that Post. Alternatively, the Agent is expressed as acting with respect to a Salient_entity associated with the task. In most cases the Salient_entity is located at the Post.

frameElementDefinitions.Agent: The Agent is assigned to a Post to perform a task involving a Salient_entity.
frameElementDefinitions.Place: This FE identifies the place where the Agent staffs the Post.
frameElementDefinitions.Purpose: This FE identifies the purpose for which the Agent works at the Post.
frameElementDefinitions.Time: This FE identifies the time when the Agent works at the Post.
frameElementDefinitions.Means: This FE identifies the Means by which the Agent works at the Post.
frameElementDefinitions.Manner: Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (quietly, loudly), and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an Agent that also affect the action (presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully).
frameElementDefinitions.Frequency: The Frequency with which the Agent works in a given period of time.
frameElementDefinitions.Period_of_iterations: The length of time from when the event denoted by the target began to be repeated to when it stopped.
frameElementDefinitions.Result: The Result of working at the Post.
frameElementDefinitions.Particular_iteration: Expressions marked with this extra-thematic FE modify a non-iterative use of the target, and indicate that it is conceived as embedded within an iterated series of similar events or states.
frameElementDefinitions.Explanation: The Explanation denotes a proposition from which the main clause (headed by the target) logically follows. This often means that the Explanation causes the state of affairs expressed by the target, but not in all cases.
frameElementDefinitions.Event_description: This FE gives a description of the working event.
frameElementDefinitions.Post: The Post is a location or station assigned to the Agent where a task or tasks are to be performed.
frameElementDefinitions.Salient_entity: The Salient_entity is centrally involved in the task the Agent is assigned to perform. It is normally located at the Post.
